Existence Justification: In travel hospitality loyalty programs, promotion enrollments represent a true many-to-many operational relationship. A single guest profile enrolls in multiple active promotions simultaneously (e.g., Double Points Summer, Stay 3 Get Bonus, Birthday Bonus), and each promotion has thousands of enrolled members. The business actively manages these enrollments as distinct entities, tracking enrollment-specific data such as progress toward qualifying criteria, completion status, and bonus award fulfillment that belongs neither to the profile nor the promotion alone.'
}

Table guest.segment_membership {
  segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this guest-to-segment membership record. Primary key.']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing segment definition']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the guest master profile record']
  assignment_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the strength of the segment assignment, particularly relevant for ML-model-based assignments. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date on which this guest was assigned to this segment. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this segment membership became active and eligible for campaign targeting. Supports point-in-time segment membership queries. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  assignment_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this segment membership expired or was removed. Null for currently active memberships. Supports historical segment membership tracking. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this guest was assigned to this segment: rule-based (business rules engine), manual (marketing team override), or ml-model (machine learning prediction). Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  assignment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason or business rule identifier explaining why this guest was assigned to this segment. Supports audit trail and segment definition refinement. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was created in the system.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when this specific membership record was last evaluated and confirmed during segment refresh cycles. Enables tracking of stale memberships.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this segment membership: active (in use for campaigns), inactive (temporarily disabled), expired (past expiry date), suspended (manually paused by marketing team).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was last updated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this segment membership record. Supports audit trail for manual assignments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of guest profiles to marketing segments for campaign targeting and personalization. It captures the many-to-many relationship between guest profiles and marketing segments, where a single guest can belong to multiple segments simultaneously (e.g., High-Value Leisure + Spa Enthusiast + West Coast Traveler), and each segment contains thousands of guest profiles. Each record links one profile to one segment with assignment metadata including confidence scores, effective dates, assignment methods (rule-based vs ML-predicted), and reason codes that exist only in the context of this membership relationship.. Existence Justification: In the travel hospitality business, guest profiles are simultaneously assigned to multiple marketing segments for campaign targeting and personalization (e.g., a single guest can be tagged as High-Value Leisure + Spa Enthusiast + West Coast Traveler), and each segment contains thousands of guest profiles. Marketing teams actively manage these multi-segment memberships with assignment confidence scores, effective dates, assignment methods (rule-based vs ML-predicted), and reason codes. This is an operational many-to-many relationship that the business actively creates, updates, and queries for campaign audience list generation.'
}

Table guest.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for household']
  merged_household_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on household (merged_household_id)']
  merged_into_household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target household ID if this household record has been merged into another household.']
  preferred_property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property most frequently visited or designated as preferred by the household.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary guest who represents or manages the household account.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the household\'s primary mailing or residential address.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the household\'s address (apartment, suite, unit number, etc.).']
  adult_count varchar [note: 'Number of adult members (typically 18+ years) in the household.']
  average_daily_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average daily rate paid by the household across all historical stays, used for segmentation and pricing strategies.']
  child_count varchar [note: 'Number of child members (typically under 18 years) in the household.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the household\'s primary address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the household\'s primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was first created in the system.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this household to the corresponding account in the CRM system (e.g., Salesforce).']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External or third-party identifier for the household used for integration with partner systems or external platforms.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'The name or label assigned to the household for identification purposes.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record (active, inactive, merged into another household, or archived).']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household structure (e.g., family, couple, single traveler, group, corporate).']
  last_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent stay by any member of the household at any property.']
  lifetime_nights varchar [note: 'Total cumulative number of nights stayed across all household members and all properties.']
  lifetime_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative revenue generated by the household across all bookings, stays, and ancillary services.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty program tier or status level for the household based on cumulative stay activity and spend.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the household has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional offers.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual guests associated with this household.']
  merge_date date [note: 'Date when this household record was merged into another household.']
  next_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the next confirmed or upcoming stay by any member of the household at any property.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the household\'s primary address.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the household\'s preferred billing and transaction currency.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the household\'s preferred communication language.']
  preferred_room_type varchar [note: 'Room type preference for the household (e.g., king bed, double queen, suite, accessible room).']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for household communications and correspondence.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the household.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the operational system from which this household record originated (e.g., PMS, CRM, loyalty platform).']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing recurring special requests or preferences for the household (e.g., high floor, near elevator, hypoallergenic bedding).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the household\'s primary address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was last modified or updated.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the household is designated as VIP, requiring special handling and elevated service standards.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for household. Referenced by household_id.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_booking {
  reservation_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reservation booking record. Primary key for the reservation_booking product. System-generated surrogate key used across all downstream systems and analytics.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the booking source channel through which this reservation was created (direct website, OTA, GDS, phone, walk-in, corporate portal, etc.). Used for channel attribution and commission calculation.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the corporate account if this reservation is booked under a negotiated corporate rate agreement. Used for corporate billing and rate compliance auditing.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the market segment classification for this reservation (transient, group, corporate, leisure, government, etc.). Used for revenue management segmentation and forecasting.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest master record who is the primary guest on this reservation. Links to guest profile containing contact information, preferences, and loyalty status.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property where this reservation is booked. Links to property master containing location, brand, segment, and operational details.']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the group block if this reservation is part of a group booking (conference, wedding, corporate event, etc.). Null for individual FIT reservations.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the rate plan applied to this reservation (BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate negotiated rate, promotional rate, package rate, etc.). Determines pricing rules and cancellation policies.']
  travel_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.travel_agent. Business justification: Reservation_booking currently references travel agent via travel_agent_iata_number (string business key). Normalizing to FK using travel_agent_id allows joining to get full agent details (agency_name,']
  accessibility_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested an ADA-compliant accessible room. Used for room assignment and compliance with Americans with Disabilities Act requirements.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the guest is expected to check in to the property. Used for occupancy forecasting, revenue projections, and operational planning (housekeeping, staffing).']
  average_daily_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average nightly room rate for this reservation, calculated as total_room_revenue divided by (length_of_stay * number_of_rooms). Core KPI for revenue management and competitive benchmarking.']
  booking_date date [note: 'Date the reservation was originally created in the system. Used to calculate booking lead time, pickup analysis, and demand forecasting accuracy.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reservation. Tracks progression from initial booking through completion or cancellation. Updated by PMS based on guest actions and property operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|tentative|waitlisted|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the reservation was created, including timezone. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Used for channel performance analysis and real-time availability updates.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the cancellation policy applicable to this reservation (e.g., 24HR, 72HR, NRR for non-refundable). Defines penalties and deadlines for cancellation.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the reservation was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active reservations. Used to calculate cancellation rates and revenue displacement analysis.']
  commission_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total commission payable to the booking channel or travel agent for this reservation. Calculated based on commission schedule and total room revenue.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-facing alphanumeric confirmation code provided to the guest at time of booking. Used by guests to retrieve, modify, or cancel their reservation. Unique across all properties and channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail. Distinct from booking_timestamp which represents the business event time.']
  crs_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number generated by Sabre SynXis CRS for reservations originating through the central reservation system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and channel attribution.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this reservation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency reporting and financial consolidation.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the guest is expected to check out from the property. Used to calculate length of stay and room availability for subsequent bookings.']
  early_checkin_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested early check-in (before standard check-in time). Used for operational planning and guest service recovery.']
  guarantee_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the reservation is guaranteed against no-show. Determines cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.']
  late_checkout_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested late check-out (after standard check-out time). Used for housekeeping scheduling and room availability management.']
  length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of nights the guest is booked to stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Key metric for revenue management, ALOS calculation, and yield optimization.']
  loyalty_member_number varchar [note: 'Loyalty program member number of the guest, if enrolled. Used for points accrual, tier benefits, and personalized guest recognition. Personally identifiable information.']
  modification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this reservation (date change, rate change, guest change, etc.). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  number_of_adults varchar [note: 'Count of adult guests (typically age 18+) included in this reservation. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B forecasting, and compliance with fire safety regulations.']
  number_of_children varchar [note: 'Count of child guests (typically under age 18) included in this reservation. Used for amenity planning, F&B forecasting, and family-friendly service delivery.']
  number_of_rooms varchar [note: 'Count of rooms reserved under this booking. Typically 1 for individual reservations; may be greater than 1 for multi-room bookings or small groups.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Code representing a bundled package offering if this reservation includes additional services beyond room only (e.g., breakfast included, spa credit, parking, resort fee). Null for room-only bookings.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used to guarantee or settle this reservation. Distinct from payment channel (web, mobile, front desk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|bank_transfer|mobile_payment|loyalty_points|corporate_billing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pms_reservation_code varchar [note: 'Native reservation identifier from Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for operational lookups and integration with front desk, housekeeping, and cashiering modules.']
  points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this reservation. Calculated based on room revenue, member tier, and promotional multipliers.']
  room_type_requested varchar [note: 'Code representing the room type requested by the guest at time of booking (e.g., KING, QUEEN, SUITE, DELUXE). May differ from actual room type assigned at check-in.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing guest special requests at time of booking (e.g., high floor, near elevator, extra pillows, early check-in). Used by operations to enhance guest experience.']
  total_room_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room revenue for this reservation across all nights, before taxes and fees. Calculated as nightly rate multiplied by length of stay and number of rooms. Key component of RevPAR and ADR calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture.']
  vip_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reservation is flagged for VIP treatment (high-value guest, loyalty elite member, celebrity, executive, etc.). Triggers enhanced service protocols and amenities.']

  Note: 'Core master record for an individual guest reservation across all booking channels (direct, OTA, GDS, CRS via Sabre SynXis). Captures the full booking lifecycle from inquiry through confirmation, modification, cancellation, and no-show. Stores arrival/departure dates, LOS (Length of Stay), room type requested, rate code, BAR/LRA/NRR rate plan, guarantee method, booking status, source channel, CRS confirmation number, OPERA PMS reservation ID, number of adults/children, and special request flags. SSOT for reservation identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table reservation.booking_status_history {
  booking_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for booking_status_history']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee or system user who triggered this event. Null for system-automated events.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent reservation record. Links this status history event to the specific booking being tracked.']
  agent_name varchar [note: 'The name of the agent or system user who performed the action. Denormalized for audit trail completeness and reporting convenience.']
  booking_source_code varchar [note: 'The specific source within the channel (e.g., EXPEDIA, BOOKING_COM, BRAND_WEBSITE, CALL_CENTER). Provides granular attribution for the event.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'For cancellation events, the specific reason code (e.g., GUEST_CANCEL, WEATHER, EMERGENCY, DUPLICATE_BOOKING, RATE_ISSUE). Null for non-cancellation events.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which this event was initiated (e.g., DIRECT, OTA, GDS, CRS, VOICE, WALK_IN, MOBILE_APP). Denormalized for audit completeness.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique confirmation number for the reservation. Denormalized from parent reservation for audit trail completeness and query performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this history record was created in the data warehouse. This is the ETL load time, distinct from event_timestamp.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is associated with a guest dispute or service recovery case. True if disputed, False otherwise.']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the event, extracted from event_timestamp for simplified date-based reporting and partitioning.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this lifecycle event occurred in the source system. This is the business event time, not the ETL load time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the category of lifecycle event: status_change (status transition only), modification (booking amendment), cancellation (booking cancelled), no_show (guest did not arrive), check_in (guest arrived), check_out (guest departed).']
  guest_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated notification (email, SMS, push) was sent to the guest for this event. True if sent, False otherwise.']
  guest_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest notification was sent. Null if no notification was sent.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the event was initiated. Captured for security audit, fraud detection, and compliance purposes. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  modification_field_name varchar [note: 'For modification events, the specific field or attribute that was changed (e.g., arrival_date, room_type_code, rate_code, number_of_adults). Enables granular change tracking.']
  modification_type varchar [note: 'For modification events, specifies the category of amendment: date_change (arrival/departure dates), room_type_change (upgrade/downgrade), rate_plan_change (rate code switch), occupancy_change (number of guests), special_request_update (preferences), guest_name_change (name correction), payment_method_change (guarantee method), package_change (package modification), add_on_change (ancillary services). Null for non-modification events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: date_change|room_type_change|rate_plan_change|occupancy_change|special_request_update|guest_name_change|payment_method_change|package_change|add_on_change — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The reservation status after this event. Represents the current state of the booking at the time of this history record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inquiry|tentative|waitlisted|confirmed|guaranteed|modified|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out|due_in|due_out — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For modification events, the value of the field after the change. Stored as string to accommodate various data types. Null for non-modification events.']
  no_show_reason_code varchar [note: 'For no-show events, the reason code (e.g., NO_CONTACT, FLIGHT_DELAY, GUEST_FORGOT, DUPLICATE_BOOKING). Null for non-no-show events.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For modification events, the value of the field before the change. Stored as string to accommodate various data types. Null for non-modification events.']
  penalty_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For cancellation or modification events, any penalty or change fee assessed per the rate plan rules. Zero if no penalty applies.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The reservation status immediately before this event. Null for the initial status record. Values align with Oracle OPERA PMS reservation status codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inquiry|tentative|waitlisted|confirmed|guaranteed|modified|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out|due_in|due_out — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  property_code varchar [note: 'The unique code identifying the hotel or resort property where this reservation event occurred. Denormalized for partition key and audit completeness.']
  rate_difference_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For modification events, the monetary difference between the original and new rate (positive for increase, negative for decrease). Null for non-rate-change events.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the status change or modification (e.g., GUEST_REQUEST, RATE_CHANGE, OVERBOOKING, NO_SHOW, EARLY_DEPARTURE, SYSTEM_AUTO). Aligns with property-defined reason code tables.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or additional context for the status change or modification. Captures agent notes or system-generated descriptions.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated revenue impact of this event (positive for revenue gain, negative for revenue loss). Calculated based on rate changes, cancellations, and penalties.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual processing time in minutes from event initiation to completion. Used to measure SLA performance.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event met the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) for processing time. True if compliant, False if SLA was breached.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'The target processing time in minutes defined by the SLA for this event type. Used to calculate SLA compliance.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The source system or interface that generated this event (e.g., OPERA_PMS for front desk, SYNXIS_CRS for central reservations, MOBILE_APP for guest self-service).']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The unique transaction or session identifier from the source system. Enables correlation with source system logs for troubleshooting and dispute resolution.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string for web/mobile-initiated events. Supports device analytics and troubleshooting.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of all reservation lifecycle events including status transitions (Inquiry → Tentative → Confirmed → Modified → Cancelled → No-Show → Checked-In → Checked-Out) and all booking modifications. For status changes, captures previous status, new status, transition timestamp, triggering agent/system, and reason code. For modifications (the SSOT for all amendment records), captures amendment type (date change, room type upgrade/downgrade, rate plan switch, occupancy change, special request update), original values, new values, modification channel, modification timestamp, agent ID, and any associated rate difference or penalty fee. Enables lifecycle analytics, SLA compliance tracking, revenue impact analysis, dispute resolution, and guest service recovery workflows. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS status change and modification events.'
}

Table reservation.cancellation {
  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cancellation or no-show event. Primary key.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Cancellations trigger revenue reversals, refund processing, and penalty charges that must link to the original AR invoice for accurate revenue recognition, refund authorization, and financial reportin']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Cancellations require booking source attribution for channel-specific cancellation policy enforcement, commission reversal calculations, and OTA penalty assessment. Currently only links to ota_partner']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Cancellation currently references policy via cancellation_policy_code (string). Normalizing to FK allows joining to get full policy details (policy_name, policy_description, penalty_type, penalty_amou']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the agent or system user who processed the cancellation or no-show event.']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA partner if the cancellation was initiated by or processed through an OTA channel.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who held the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the cancellation or no-show occurred.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation that was cancelled or resulted in a no-show.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known reservation confirmation number associated with the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was first created in the system.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the guest initiated a dispute of the cancellation penalty or no-show fee.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has disputed the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute resolution process (open, resolved in guest favor, resolved in property favor, or withdrawn by guest).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation or no-show event occurred. For cancellations: when the cancellation was processed. For no-shows: the date/time the guest failed to arrive.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of non-fulfillment event: guest-initiated cancellation, property-initiated cancellation, OTA-initiated cancellation, or no-show (non-arrival).']
  guarantee_charge_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guarantee charge (penalty or no-show fee) was successfully processed against the guarantee method.']
  guarantee_method varchar [note: 'Method used to guarantee the reservation (credit card, deposit, corporate guarantee, travel agent guarantee, or none). Critical for no-show charge processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was last updated.']
  original_arrival_date date [note: 'The scheduled arrival date of the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  original_departure_date date [note: 'The scheduled departure date of the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  ota_chargeback_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged back to the OTA for commission recovery on this cancellation or no-show.']
  ota_chargeback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property is eligible to charge back the commission to the OTA for this cancellation or no-show per the OTA contract terms.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty or fee charged for the cancellation or no-show, in the property\'s base currency.']
  penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cancellation penalty or no-show fee is applicable based on the policy and timing of the event.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the penalty charge posting to the guest folio or account (posted, pending, reversed, or failed).']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the cancellation or no-show was processed (PMS - Property Management System, CRS - Central Reservation System, OTA - Online Travel Agency, GDS - Global Distribution System, call center, mobile app, web). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pms|crs|ota|gds|call_center|mobile_app|web — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for cancellation (e.g., CHANGE_PLANS, DUPLICATE_BOOKING, PRICE_ISSUE, EMERGENCY, OTHER). Null for no-show events.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or additional details about the cancellation reason provided by the guest or agent.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded to the guest after applicable penalties, in the property\'s base currency.']
  refund_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest is eligible for a refund of any prepaid amounts after penalty deduction.']
  revenue_lost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room revenue lost due to the cancellation or no-show, calculated from the original reservation rate and length of stay.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the penalty charge was reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge was subsequently reversed.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason for reversing the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge.']
  room_nights_lost varchar [note: 'Number of room nights lost due to the cancellation or no-show, calculated as the difference between arrival and departure dates.']
  waiver_authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or agent who authorized the penalty waiver.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation penalty or no-show fee was waived by the property.']
  waiver_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for waiving the penalty (e.g., LOYALTY_STATUS, EMERGENCY, SERVICE_RECOVERY, MANAGER_DISCRETION).']
  window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before arrival that the cancellation occurred, used to evaluate penalty applicability per the cancellation policy.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all reservation non-fulfillment events including guest cancellations, property-initiated cancellations, OTA-initiated cancellations, and no-show (non-arrival) events. For cancellations: records event date/time, reason code, applicable cancellation policy, penalty/fee amount, cancellation window evaluation, refund eligibility, and processing channel. For no-shows: records no-show date, no-show fee charged, guarantee method used for charge, automatic guarantee charge processing, and OTA chargeback eligibility. Common fields: waiver flag, waiver reason, posting status to guest folio, processing agent/channel, and any subsequent dispute or reversal. Critical for revenue leakage analysis, NRR policy enforcement, revenue recovery operations, OTA chargeback management, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) reporting.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_group_block {
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the group room block record. Primary key.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel or booking source through which the group block was created.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account or travel agency associated with this group block, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or reservations staff member responsible for managing this group block.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated event or meeting record if this group block is linked to a MICE event managed in the event domain.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the group block is held.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'The first date of the group block period when guests are expected to check in.']
  attrition_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group block contract includes an attrition clause requiring minimum room pickup or penalties.']
  attrition_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of contracted rooms that must be picked up to avoid attrition penalties, if applicable.']
  available_room_count varchar [note: 'The number of rooms remaining available in the block for booking, calculated as contracted minus pickup.']
  billing_instruction varchar [note: 'Instructions for billing and invoicing the group, including payment terms and responsible party details.']
  block_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the group block, used for booking and reference purposes across systems.']
  block_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the group block, typically reflecting the event, corporate account, or travel agency name.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group block indicating its booking stage and commitment level.']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group block by business segment or purpose, such as corporate meeting, MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions), tour operator, or wedding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|leisure_group|mice|tour_operator|wholesale|airline_crew|wedding — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Description of the cancellation terms and conditions applicable to the group block.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or code describing the reason for group block cancellation.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the group block was cancelled, if applicable.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission rate percentage payable to the booking agent or intermediary for this group block.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary contact person for the group block.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the primary contact person for the group block.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary contact person for the group block.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the group block contract was signed by both parties.']
  contracted_room_count varchar [note: 'The total number of rooms contracted or committed in the group block agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the group block record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the group rate amount.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which the group must pick up rooms from the block before unbooked rooms are released back to general inventory.']
  departure_date date [note: 'The last date of the group block period when guests are expected to check out.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary deposit amount required for the group block, if applicable.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the deposit payment must be received.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a deposit is required to secure the group block.']
  group_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The negotiated nightly room rate amount for the group block, typically in the property\'s base currency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the group block record was last updated or modified.']
  lra_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group block has Last Room Availability (LRA) status, meaning rooms are available at the group rate even when the property is sold out.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or comments related to the group block for internal reference.']
  pickup_room_count varchar [note: 'The number of rooms from the block that have been booked or picked up by the group as of the current date.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'The rate code or rate plan identifier associated with the group block pricing.']
  revenue_forecast_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The forecasted total revenue expected from this group block based on contracted rooms and rates.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special requests or requirements from the group, such as room setup, amenities, or services.']
  wash_schedule_date date [note: 'The date on which the group block inventory is scheduled to be washed or adjusted based on pickup performance.']

  Note: 'Master record for a group room block associated with a corporate account, travel agency, or event. Manages the contracted room block size, pickup count, cutoff date, group rate code, block status (tentative/definite/cancelled), attrition clause, and wash schedule. Links to the event domain for MICE group blocks and to the channel domain for wholesale/tour operator blocks. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS Group Block module and Delphi by Amadeus for event-linked groups.'
}

Table reservation.group_block_pickup {
  group_block_pickup_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the group block pickup transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the group block pickup occurred. Enables property-level group performance analysis.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual reservation that consumed inventory from the group block. Links to the transactional reservation record.']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent group block from which this pickup was made. Links to the master group block allocation.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled check-in date for the pickup reservation. Used for occupancy forecasting and group block utilization analysis.']
  block_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total group block consumed at the time of this pickup. Calculated as (total_block_rooms - remaining_block_rooms) / total_block_rooms * 100.']
  booking_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the channel through which the pickup was made (e.g., DIRECT, GDS, OTA, CRS). Enables channel attribution analysis for group blocks.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'Specific source system or platform that originated the pickup (e.g., OPERA, SynXis, Delphi). Used for system integration tracking.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when this pickup reservation was cancelled, if applicable. Used for attrition analysis and wash factor calculation.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for cancellation. Supports root cause analysis of group block attrition.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company or organization associated with this pickup. Links individual reservations to corporate accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this pickup record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Essential for multi-currency group block management.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'Date by which the group block inventory must be picked up or released back to general inventory. Critical for attrition management.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled check-out date for the pickup reservation. Used to calculate Length of Stay (LOS) and room night consumption.']
  group_attendee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the attendee role within the group event. Supports rooming list segmentation and billing allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: attendee|speaker|organizer|exhibitor|sponsor|staff|guest — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  guarantee_type varchar [note: 'Method used to guarantee the pickup reservation. Critical for no-show risk assessment and revenue assurance.']
  guest_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the guest on this pickup reservation. Used for confirmation and pre-arrival communication.']
  guest_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the guest associated with this pickup reservation. Used for rooming list management and guest communication.']
  guest_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the guest. Critical for day-of-arrival communication and service recovery.']
  loyalty_member_number varchar [note: 'Loyalty program membership identifier for the guest. Enables points accrual and tier benefit application for group pickups.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for this pickup (e.g., CORPORATE, LEISURE, GOVERNMENT). Supports segmentation analysis within group blocks.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this pickup record was last updated. Enables change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights consumed by this pickup. Calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Key metric for group block utilization.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest failed to arrive for this pickup reservation. Critical for no-show rate tracking and revenue recovery.']
  pickup_before_cutoff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pickup occurred before the group block cutoff date. Used to assess on-time pickup performance.']
  pickup_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the pickup transaction, typically generated by the Property Management System (PMS) or Central Reservation System (CRS).']
  pickup_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pickup reservation. Tracks progression from initial booking through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|tentative|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out|waitlisted — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the individual reservation was booked against the group block inventory. Critical for tracking pickup velocity and cutoff date management.']
  rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Nightly room rate applied to this pickup reservation. Used to calculate total room revenue and compare against Best Available Rate (BAR).']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Rate plan code applied to this pickup reservation. Typically the negotiated group rate code from the master group block contract.']
  remaining_block_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms remaining in the group block after this pickup. Snapshot metric for real-time utilization monitoring.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific room type consumed from the group block inventory (e.g., KING, QUEEN, SUITE). Enables room type mix analysis.']
  room_type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the room type consumed (e.g., Deluxe King, Executive Suite). Supports reporting and guest communication.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated this pickup transaction. Supports data lineage and system integration troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this pickup record in the source operational system. Enables cross-system reconciliation and data traceability.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing guest special requests for this pickup (e.g., high floor, accessible room, early check-in). Supports service delivery.']
  total_room_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room revenue generated by this pickup, calculated as rate_amount multiplied by nights. Excludes taxes and fees.']
  vip_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this pickup is for a VIP guest requiring special handling. Drives service prioritization and amenity allocation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking individual reservation pickups against a group block, capturing the booking reference, pickup date, room type consumed, rate applied, and remaining block inventory. Enables real-time group block utilization monitoring, attrition risk assessment, and cutoff date management. Supports revenue management decisions on releasing unsold group inventory back to transient availability.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_rate_plan {
  reservation_rate_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reservation rate plan record. Primary key for this entity.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Reservation_rate_plan currently references policy via cancellation_policy_code (string). Normalizing to FK allows joining to get full policy details. Rate plans are defined with specific cancellation ']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit trail for rate plan authorship is critical revenue management and compliance requirement. Tracks which revenue manager created rate plans for pricing accountability and regulatory audit. No exis']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Common for promotional and non-refundable rates (e.g., 7-day, 14-day, 21-day advance purchase). Null or 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement.']
  booking_window_end_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before arrival when this rate plan is no longer available for booking. Used to close last-minute bookings for certain rate plans. Null indicates no end restriction.']
  booking_window_start_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before arrival when this rate plan becomes available for booking. Used to control early booking windows for promotional rates. Null indicates no start restriction.']
  channel_corporate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is available for booking through corporate direct billing channels. Requires corporate account setup and negotiated rate agreement.']
  channel_direct_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is available for booking through direct channels (property website, call center, front desk). True for most rate plans; false for channel-exclusive rates.']
  channel_gds_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is available for booking through GDS channels (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan). Typically used for corporate and travel agent bookings.']
  channel_group_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is available for group block bookings through MICE and event sales channels. Managed via Delphi by Amadeus for group inventory allocation.']
  channel_ota_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is available for booking through OTA channels (Expedia, Booking.com, etc.). Subject to rate parity agreements and channel management rules.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the room rate paid as commission to the booking channel or agent. Typically 10-25% for OTAs, 10% for travel agents. Null if commissionable_flag is false.']
  commissionable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for travel agent or OTA commission. True for most OTA and GDS rates; false for net rates and direct promotional rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  display_sequence varchar [note: 'The order in which this rate plan should be displayed to guests and agents in the booking interface. Lower numbers appear first. Used to prioritize high-value or strategic rate plans.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this rate plan is no longer available for booking or stay dates. Nullable for evergreen rate plans like BAR. Used for promotional and seasonal rate plan expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this rate plan becomes available for booking and stay dates. Aligns with revenue management strategy and seasonal pricing windows.']
  guarantee_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the reservation must be guaranteed. Credit card: hold on card; Deposit: partial payment required; Prepayment: full payment required; Direct bill: corporate account billing; None: no guarantee required.']
  guarantee_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a payment guarantee (credit card, deposit, or prepayment) is required at the time of booking for this rate plan. True for most rate plans; false for select corporate or group rates with direct billing arrangements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate plan record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization across systems.']
  loyalty_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and elite benefits. True for most direct and qualified rates; false for opaque and deeply discounted rates.']
  loyalty_points_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'The multiplier applied to base loyalty points earned for stays under this rate plan. Standard rates typically have 1.0x; promotional rates may have 0.5x or 2.0x multipliers. Null if loyalty_eligible_flag is false.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the market segment this rate plan targets. Examples: TRAN (transient), CORP (corporate), GROUP (group), LEISURE (leisure). Used for revenue reporting and STR benchmarking.']
  maximum_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for a booking under this rate plan. Used to prevent long-stay bookings from blocking high-value short-stay demand. Null indicates no maximum restriction.']
  member_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is exclusively available to loyalty program members. True for member-exclusive rates; false for public rates.']
  minimum_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for a booking under this rate plan. Used for revenue optimization during high-demand periods. Null or 1 indicates no minimum restriction.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the amenities, services, or F&B components included in the package rate. Examples: Daily breakfast for two, $50 spa credit, complimentary parking, welcome amenity. Null for non-package rates.']
  package_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan includes bundled amenities, services, or F&B components (e.g., breakfast included, spa credit, parking). True for package rates; false for room-only rates.']
  rate_plan_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the rate plan type for segmentation and reporting. Standard includes BAR and rack rates; corporate includes negotiated business rates; promotional includes limited-time offers; package includes bundled F&B or amenities; group includes MICE block rates; wholesale includes tour operator allotments; opaque includes non-branded OTA rates; member includes loyalty program rates; government includes public sector rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|corporate|promotional|package|group|wholesale|opaque|member|government — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the rate plan as configured in the CRS and PMS. Examples: BAR, CORP, AAA, PKG, NRF. This is the externally-known code used across all distribution channels.']
  rate_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the rate plan, including terms, conditions, restrictions, and benefits. Used for guest communication and agent training.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and agents. Examples: Best Available Rate, Corporate Negotiated Rate, AAA Member Rate, Romance Package, Non-Refundable Rate.']
  rate_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate plan. Active: available for booking; Inactive: not available for new bookings; Suspended: temporarily disabled; Pending: awaiting approval or activation; Expired: past effective end date.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the system of record where this rate plan was originally configured. SYNXIS: Sabre SynXis CRS; OPERA: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDEAS: IDeaS G3 RMS strategy output; MANUAL: manually configured rate plan.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal terms and conditions governing the rate plan, including cancellation penalties, modification rules, no-show charges, and guest obligations. Must comply with consumer protection regulations.']

  Note: 'Reservation-scoped reference master for the bookable rate plan catalog presented to guests and agents via CRS and PMS. Includes BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), corporate negotiated rates, package rates, and promotional rates. Stores rate plan code, rate plan name, rate category, cancellation policy reference, guarantee requirements, minimum LOS restrictions, advance purchase requirements, and channel eligibility flags. SSOT BOUNDARY: This domain owns the bookable rate plan catalog (what can be sold and under what terms). The revenue domain owns rate strategy, pricing optimization, yield management, and dynamic rate amounts. Rate plan records here are synchronized from Sabre SynXis CRS rate configuration and reflect IDeaS G3 RMS rate strategy outputs as read-only pricing inputs.'
}

Table reservation.cancellation_policy {
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cancellation policy. Primary key.']
  allows_modification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reservations under this policy allow date or room type modifications without cancellation. False for most non-refundable policies.']
  applies_to_corporate_bookings boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy can be applied to corporate negotiated rate bookings. Corporate accounts may have special cancellation terms.']
  applies_to_group_bookings boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy can be applied to group block reservations. Group policies often have different terms than individual FIT (Free Independent Traveler) bookings.']
  channel_restrictions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of booking channels where this policy applies or is restricted. Examples: direct_only, ota_excluded, gds_only. Null indicates no channel restrictions.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this cancellation policy. Part of audit trail for policy governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cancellation policy record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for policy lifecycle management.']
  deposit_due_days_before_arrival varchar [note: 'Number of days before arrival date by which the deposit must be received. Null indicates deposit due at time of booking.']
  deposit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total reservation value required as deposit when deposit_required is true. Value stored as decimal (e.g., 100.00 for full prepayment).']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an advance deposit payment is required at time of booking under this policy. Common for non-refundable and group bookings.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence for ordering policies in user interfaces and booking engines. Lower numbers appear first. Used for presenting policies from most to least flexible.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this cancellation policy is no longer active for new reservations. Null indicates policy is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this cancellation policy becomes active and can be applied to new reservations. Part of policy lifecycle management.']
  free_cancellation_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before scheduled arrival date during which a guest can cancel without penalty. Alternative to hours-based window for longer lead-time policies.']
  free_cancellation_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled arrival time during which a guest can cancel without penalty. Null indicates no free cancellation window.']
  guarantee_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a payment guarantee (credit card, deposit, or prepayment) is required to book under this cancellation policy. Typically true for stricter policies.']
  guest_facing_summary varchar [note: 'Concise, guest-friendly summary of the cancellation policy displayed during booking process. Typically 1-2 sentences highlighting key terms.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal staff notes and operational guidance for applying this cancellation policy. Not visible to guests. Used for training and exception handling.']
  is_non_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy represents a non-refundable rate where no refund is provided under any circumstances. True for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) policies.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this cancellation policy. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cancellation policy record was last updated. Tracks policy changes for revenue management analysis and compliance audit.']
  legal_terms_text varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions text for the cancellation policy, including jurisdiction-specific consumer protection disclosures required by GDPR, CCPA, and local regulations.']
  modification_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed fee charged for modifying a reservation when allows_modification is true. Null if no modification fee applies. Currency is property base currency.']
  no_show_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary penalty charged for no-show when no_show_penalty_type is flat_fee. Currency is property base currency per USALI standards.']
  no_show_penalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total reservation value charged as penalty for no-show when no_show_penalty_type is percentage_of_stay. Value stored as decimal.']
  no_show_penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty applied when a guest fails to arrive (no-show). May differ from standard cancellation penalty to account for lost revenue opportunity.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary penalty amount charged for cancellation when penalty_type is flat_fee. Currency is property base currency per USALI standards.']
  penalty_nights_count varchar [note: 'Number of nights charged as penalty when penalty_type is first_night or nights_based. Typically 1 for first night penalty, can be higher for stricter policies.']
  penalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total reservation value charged as penalty when penalty_type is percentage_of_stay. Value stored as decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty applied when cancellation occurs outside the free cancellation window. Determines how the cancellation charge is calculated.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the cancellation policy used in PMS (Property Management System) and CRS (Central Reservation System) systems. Examples: FLEX, MOD, STRICT, NRF.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cancellation policy terms and conditions, including guest-facing language explaining cancellation rules, deadlines, and penalties.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the cancellation policy displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Flexible Cancellation, Moderate Cancellation, Strict Cancellation, Non-Refundable.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cancellation policy. Only active policies can be assigned to rate plans and reservations.']
  policy_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier of the cancellation policy indicating the level of flexibility offered to guests. Aligned with revenue management strategy and rate plan positioning.']
  seasonal_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy can be overridden by seasonal or event-specific cancellation rules during high-demand periods.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record where this cancellation policy was originally configured. Typically Oracle OPERA PMS (Property Management System) or Sabre SynXis CRS (Central Reservation System).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this cancellation policy in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with PMS (Property Management System) or CRS (Central Reservation System).']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the terms and conditions for reservation cancellation, including the policy code, policy name, free cancellation window (hours/days before arrival), penalty structure (first night, percentage of stay, flat fee), non-refundable flag, and applicable rate plan associations. Manages multiple policy tiers (flexible, moderate, strict, non-refundable) aligned with revenue management strategy. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS policy configuration.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_special_request {
  reservation_special_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the special request record. Primary key for the reservation special request entity.']
  primary_reservation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member assigned to fulfill the special request. Enables accountability tracking and performance measurement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile who made the special request. Enables tracking of guest preferences across multiple stays.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where the special request is to be fulfilled. Supports multi-property operations and reporting.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent reservation to which this special request is attached. Links the request to the booking record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the special request was acknowledged by the assigned department or staff member. Tracks response time for Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred by the property to fulfill the special request. Supports Cost Per Occupied Room (CPOR) analysis and operational efficiency measurement.']
  assigned_department varchar [note: 'Operational department responsible for fulfilling the special request. Routes the request to the appropriate team for action. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_desk|housekeeping|food_beverage|concierge|maintenance|guest_services|spa|transportation|events|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged to the guest for fulfilling the special request. Posted to the guest folio in the Property Management System (PMS).']
  charge_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount. Supports multi-currency operations for international properties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the special request record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Internal estimated cost to the property for fulfilling the special request. Used for operational expense tracking and profitability analysis.']
  failure_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for request non-fulfillment. Enables root cause analysis and operational improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unavailable|capacity_constraint|policy_restriction|cost_prohibitive|timing_conflict|guest_cancelled|system_error|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the special request could not be fulfilled or was only partially fulfilled. Supports service recovery and guest communication.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the special request in the fulfillment workflow. Tracks progress from initial submission through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|acknowledged|in_progress|fulfilled|partially_fulfilled|unable_to_fulfill|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the special request was completed or fulfilled. Used for cycle time analysis and guest experience measurement.']
  guest_feedback_text varchar [note: 'Free-form guest comments or feedback regarding the special request fulfillment. Captured through post-stay surveys or direct communication.']
  guest_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has been notified of the fulfillment status or any issues with the special request. Tracks communication completion.']
  guest_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Guest-provided satisfaction rating for how the special request was handled, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10. Feeds into Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) calculations.']
  impacts_loyalty_points boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fulfillment or non-fulfillment of this special request affects the guest loyalty program points or status. Used for loyalty program integration.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Staff-only notes and comments regarding the special request fulfillment process. Not visible to guests; used for operational coordination.']
  is_pre_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the special request was submitted before the guest arrival date. Pre-arrival requests enable proactive preparation and enhanced guest experience.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a recurring preference that should be applied to future reservations for the same guest. Supports loyalty program personalization.']
  is_vip_request boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the special request is associated with a VIP (Very Important Person) guest requiring elevated attention and priority handling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the special request record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify the guest about the special request status. Supports omnichannel guest communication tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|phone_call|mobile_app|in_person|letter|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification of the special request indicating the importance for guest satisfaction and operational sequencing. Critical requests require immediate attention.']
  request_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the request type for operational planning and resource allocation. Used for departmental workload distribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_setup|service_timing|accessibility|amenity|food_beverage|transportation|special_event|general — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of special request in the Property Management System (PMS). Used for system processing and reporting.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the special request was submitted. Supports channel performance analysis and guest experience tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guest_direct|reservation_agent|online_booking|mobile_app|call_center|email|walk_in|loyalty_program|group_coordinator|travel_agent|ota|gds|other — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_text varchar [note: 'Free-form text description of the special request as entered by the guest or reservation agent. Captures detailed instructions and context for fulfillment.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the special request was originally submitted by the guest or agent. Represents the business event time for the request creation.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of the special request indicating the nature of the guest need. Supports operational routing to appropriate departments (housekeeping, front desk, food and beverage, concierge). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bed_configuration|floor_preference|accessibility_need|early_check_in|late_check_out|amenity_delivery|dietary_requirement|room_location|view_preference|temperature_preference|pillow_preference|transportation|special_occasion|other — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requires_charge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fulfilling the special request incurs an additional charge to the guest folio. Used for revenue tracking and billing integration.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record where the special request was originally captured. Supports data lineage and system integration tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OPERA_PMS|SYNXIS_CRS|SALESFORCE_CRM|MOBILE_APP|WEB_PORTAL|CALL_CENTER|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the special request in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  target_fulfillment_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the special request should be fulfilled. Often aligned with guest arrival date or specific event timing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing guest special requests attached to a reservation, including request type (bed configuration, floor preference, accessibility needs, early check-in, late check-out, amenity delivery, dietary requirement), request text, fulfillment status, assigned department, fulfillment timestamp, and failure reason if unmet. Supports pre-arrival operations, housekeeping coordination, and guest experience personalization. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS traces and requests module.'
}

Table reservation.deposit_ledger {
  deposit_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the deposit ledger entry. Primary key for the deposit ledger transaction record.']
  ar_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_payment. Business justification: Deposit payments must link to AR payment records for cash application, reconciliation, and refund processing. Essential for front desk operations matching deposits to guest payments and finance reconc']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the booking channel through which the reservation and deposit were made. Supports channel performance analysis and commission tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user ID of the staff member who processed the deposit transaction. Supports audit trails and operational accountability.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Deposits post to specific GL accounts (advance deposits liability, forfeited deposits revenue). Required for nightly audit GL reconciliation, balance sheet accuracy, and SOX compliance for cash handli']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who made the deposit payment. Links deposit to guest profile for reconciliation and loyalty tracking.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the reservation and deposit are recorded. Supports multi-property operations.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation against which this deposit is recorded. Links the deposit to the booking lifecycle.']
  applied_to_folio_date date [note: 'The date when the deposit was applied to the guest folio. Marks the transition from advance deposit liability to revenue or accounts receivable offset.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'The originating source of the reservation booking. Used for channel attribution, commission calculation, and marketing ROI analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|ota|gds|crs|corporate|group|travel_agent|wholesaler — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cancellation policy governing deposit refund or forfeiture. Linked to rate plan and booking channel terms.']
  card_last_four_digits varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the payment card used for the deposit. Stored for guest reference and dispute resolution while maintaining PCI compliance.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known confirmation number for the reservation. Used for guest communication and deposit tracking across channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deposit ledger record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deposit amount. Supports multi-currency operations and foreign exchange tracking.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the deposit collected or due. Represents the base deposit amount before any adjustments or fees.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the deposit payment is required. Used for payment reminders, cancellation policy enforcement, and revenue forecasting.']
  deposit_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the deposit policy applied to this reservation. Determines deposit amount, due date, and cancellation terms.']
  deposit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deposit. Tracks progression from due through collection, application to folio, or refund/forfeiture.']
  deposit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deposit based on its business purpose. Determines handling rules, refund policies, and revenue recognition treatment.']
  folio_number varchar [note: 'The guest folio number to which this deposit was applied. Links deposit to final billing and checkout reconciliation.']
  forfeiture_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the deposit that was forfeited due to cancellation policy or no-show. Recognized as revenue per cancellation terms.']
  forfeiture_date date [note: 'The date when the deposit was forfeited. Triggers revenue recognition and updates reservation status to cancelled or no-show.']
  forfeiture_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for deposit forfeiture. Used for policy compliance tracking, revenue analysis, and guest communication.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the deposit transaction. Captures special circumstances, guest requests, or operational exceptions.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'The date when the deposit payment was actually received by the property. Critical for cash flow tracking and revenue recognition.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value refunded to the guest if the deposit was returned. May differ from original deposit amount due to cancellation fees or partial refunds.']
  refund_date date [note: 'The date when the deposit refund was processed. Used for cash flow tracking and guest satisfaction monitoring.']
  refund_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the refund transaction from the payment processor. Used for refund reconciliation and audit trails.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date when the deposit is recognized as revenue per ASC 606 guidelines. May differ from payment received date based on performance obligation fulfillment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deposit ledger record was last modified. Tracks changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional ledger tracking all advance deposits collected, applied, or refunded against a reservation. Records deposit amount, due date, payment method, payment reference, status (pending/received/applied/refunded/forfeited), and posting date. Supports pre-arrival cashiering, revenue recognition compliance (ASC 606), and guest folio reconciliation. Distinct from the finance domain\'s general ledger — this is the reservation-scoped deposit lifecycle record.'
}

Table reservation.travel_agent {
  travel_agent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for travel_agent']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Travel agent commissions generate AP invoices for payment processing. Essential for commission accounting, agent payment reconciliation, and tracking commission expense against booking revenue for pro']
  ap_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_payment.
