Business justification: Links agent commission payments to travel agent records for payment tracking, dispute resolution, and commission expense reconciliation. Required for agent relationship management and accurate commiss']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Travel agents operate through specific distribution channels (GDS, direct connect, portal) requiring channel assignment for connectivity fee allocation, commission schedule application, and channel-sp']
  parent_agency_reservation_travel_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent travel agency if this agent is part of a franchise, consortium, or multi-branch organization. Enables hierarchical reporting of agency performance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the travel agency\'s physical or mailing address, typically containing street number and street name.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the travel agency\'s address for suite, floor, building, or additional location details.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the travel agency or travel management company (TMC). Primary human-readable identifier.']
  agency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the travel agency business model. Retail agencies serve individual travelers, corporate TMCs manage business travel programs, OTAs operate online platforms, tour operators package travel products, consortiums are agency networks, and wholesalers distribute bulk inventory.']
  arc_number varchar [note: 'Seven or eight-digit ARC accreditation number for US-based travel agencies. Used for commission processing and booking attribution in the North American market.']
  booking_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered classification based on the travel agent\'s historical booking volume or revenue contribution. Higher tiers may receive preferential commission rates, priority support, or marketing benefits.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the travel agency is located.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default commission percentage paid to the travel agent on qualifying bookings. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). May be overridden by specific rate plans or negotiated contracts.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the travel agency contact. Used for booking confirmations, commission statements, and operational notifications.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the travel agency for operational communication, booking inquiries, and relationship management.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the travel agency contact. Used for urgent booking issues, reservation modifications, and relationship management.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the travel agent contract expires or was terminated. Null for open-ended relationships. Used to enforce commission eligibility windows.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the travel agent relationship or contract became effective. Marks the beginning of the commission-earning period.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the travel agency\'s location (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel agent record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for the travel agent on direct bill or net remit payment terms. Expressed in the property\'s base currency. Used for credit risk management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for commission payments and credit limits (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  gds_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or pseudo city code (PCC) assigned to the travel agent within the Global Distribution System (Amadeus, Sabre, Galileo, Worldspan). Used to attribute bookings originating from GDS channels.']
  iata_number varchar [note: 'Eight-digit IATA accreditation number assigned to the travel agency by the International Air Transport Association. Primary industry identifier for accredited travel agencies.']
  last_booking_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent reservation created by this travel agent. Used to identify inactive agents and trigger re-engagement campaigns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, relationship history, or internal comments about the travel agent. Used by sales and reservations teams.']
  onboarding_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the travel agent completed all onboarding requirements (contract signing, profile setup, training, system access). Marks readiness to begin booking.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the travel agency\'s address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the travel agent\'s preferred communication language (e.g., en, es, fr, de).']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Default payment instrument used by the travel agent for booking guarantees and deposits. Credit card for immediate authorization, bank transfer for wire payments, check for paper payments, direct bill for invoiced accounts, virtual card for single-use card numbers, net remit for commission offset against payment.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the travel agency is located.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (TIN, EIN, VAT number) for the travel agency. Used for commission payment reporting and tax compliance.']
  total_bookings_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all reservations created by this travel agent since relationship inception. Used for performance reporting and tier qualification.']
  total_revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative room revenue generated by all bookings attributed to this travel agent since relationship inception. Expressed in the property\'s base currency. Used for tier qualification and ROI analysis.']
  travel_agent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the travel agent relationship. Active agents can make bookings and earn commissions. Inactive agents are temporarily disabled. Suspended agents are under review. Pending approval agents await onboarding completion. Terminated agents have ended their relationship. Blacklisted agents are permanently banned.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this travel agent record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel agent record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the travel agency. Used for agency verification and marketing partnership opportunities.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or process that created this travel agent record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for travel agencies and travel management companies (TMCs) authorized to book on behalf of guests, including IATA/ARC number, agency name, agency type, commission rate, preferred payment method, GDS identifier, and active status. Manages the agency relationship for commission processing, booking attribution, and channel performance reporting. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS travel agent profile module.'
}

Table reservation.waitlist {
  waitlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the waitlist entry. Primary key.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Waitlist entries need channel tracking for conversion rate analysis by channel, automated notification routing through originating channel, and channel-specific rate/availability offers when rooms bec']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate account associated with this waitlist entry, if applicable. Used for priority handling of corporate bookings.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest profile requesting the waitlist entry.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the waitlist entry was created.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reservation that was converted from this waitlist entry, if applicable. Null if not yet converted.']
  waitlist_converted_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that converted the waitlist entry to a reservation. Null if not yet converted.']
  waitlist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created the waitlist entry.']
  waitlist_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified the waitlist entry.']
  auto_convert_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the system should automatically convert this waitlist entry to a confirmed reservation when inventory becomes available (True) or require manual confirmation (False).']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason the waitlist entry was cancelled. Null if not cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waitlist entry was cancelled by the guest or system. Null if not cancelled.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the booking channel through which the waitlist request was received (e.g., DIRECT, OTA, GDS, CRS).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the guest for waitlist status notifications and conversion opportunities.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number provided by the guest for waitlist status notifications and conversion opportunities.']
  converted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waitlist entry was converted to a confirmed reservation. Null if not yet converted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waitlist entry was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quoted rate amount.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the waitlist entry will automatically expire if not converted. Typically set to the requested arrival date or a configured number of days after creation.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from the originating system or channel, used for cross-system reconciliation and tracking.']
  guarantee_type_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the guarantee method provided by the guest for the waitlisted reservation.']
  guest_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Loyalty tier or priority classification of the guest, used to determine waitlist position and conversion priority.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the market segment classification for this waitlist entry (e.g., LEISURE, CORPORATE, GROUP).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waitlist entry was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal staff notes regarding the waitlist entry, conversion attempts, or guest communication history.']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred method for receiving waitlist status updates and conversion notifications.']
  position varchar [note: 'Numeric position of this entry in the waitlist queue for the requested room type and dates. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  quoted_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The room rate amount quoted to the guest at the time of waitlist creation, in the property\'s base currency.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the rate plan the guest requested for the waitlisted reservation (e.g., BAR, CORP, AAA).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason the guest was placed on the waitlist rather than confirmed immediately.']
  requested_arrival_date date [note: 'The arrival date requested by the guest for the waitlisted reservation.']
  requested_departure_date date [note: 'The departure date requested by the guest for the waitlisted reservation.']
  requested_nights varchar [note: 'Number of nights requested by the guest, calculated as departure date minus arrival date.']
  requested_room_count varchar [note: 'Number of rooms requested by the guest on the waitlist.']
  requested_room_type_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the room type requested by the guest (e.g., KING, SUITE, DELUXE).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that originated this waitlist entry.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special requests or preferences noted by the guest at the time of waitlist creation (e.g., high floor, near elevator, accessible room).']
  waitlist_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the waitlist entry, typically formatted as WL followed by numeric sequence.']
  waitlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waitlist entry. Active indicates the guest is still waiting; converted means a reservation was created; expired means the requested dates have passed; cancelled means the guest withdrew; fulfilled means inventory was allocated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing reservation waitlist entries when requested room types or dates are unavailable. Captures waitlist position, requested arrival/departure dates, requested room type, rate plan, guest priority (loyalty tier, corporate account), waitlist status (active, converted, expired, cancelled), and conversion timestamp if fulfilled. Supports inventory optimization and guest recovery when rooms become available through cancellations.'
}

Table reservation.room_assignment {
  room_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for room_assignment']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'FK to reservation.booking.booking_id — MUST-HAVE: Room assignment must link to the reservation it fulfills. Core operational join for check-in, room move, and folio management workflows.']
  connected_room_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the room assignment ID of the connecting room, if applicable. Null if not part of a connecting room arrangement.']
  primary_room_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the staff member who performed the room assignment. Null for automated assignments.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the room is located. Links to the property master data.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the confirmed reservation that this room assignment belongs to. Links to the parent reservation record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific physical room assigned to this reservation. Links to the room inventory master data.']
  tertiary_room_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the staff member who last modified this room assignment record. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Business date on which the room assignment was made. Used for operational reporting and day-level analytics.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the room was assigned. Indicates whether assignment was automated, manual, or driven by guest preference.']
  assignment_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or channel through which the room assignment was made (e.g., OPERA PMS, Mobile Check-In, Kiosk, Front Desk Terminal).']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the room assignment. Tracks progression from initial assignment through guest departure.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room was assigned to the reservation. Represents the moment the assignment was committed in the PMS.']
  bed_configuration varchar [note: 'Bed type and configuration in the assigned room. Critical for matching guest preferences and group requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double_queen|twin|double_twin|murphy|sofa_bed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  building_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the building or tower within a multi-building property (e.g., MAIN, TOWER-A, VILLA). Null for single-building properties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this room assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  early_checkin_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this assignment was made to accommodate an early check-in request (arrival before standard check-in time).']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'The floor level where the assigned room is located. Used for guest preferences and operational logistics.']
  guest_preference_match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score (0-100) indicating how well this room assignment matches the guest\'s stated preferences (floor, view, location, amenities). Used for service quality measurement.']
  is_accessible_room boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the assigned room meets ADA accessibility requirements. Used for compliance reporting and guest matching.']
  is_connecting_room boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this room is part of a connecting room pair or group. Used for family and group bookings.']
  is_guest_requested boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the guest specifically requested this room or room type during pre-arrival or at check-in.']
  is_upgrade boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this assignment represents an upgrade from the originally booked room type.']
  key_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room key (physical or digital) was created for this assignment. Indicates actual guest check-in readiness.']
  key_type varchar [note: 'Type of room key issued to the guest for this assignment. Used for technology adoption tracking and guest preference analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this room assignment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  late_checkout_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this assignment includes a late check-out accommodation (departure after standard check-out time).']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the room assignment is locked (cannot be changed) or unlocked (can be reassigned). Used to prevent accidental changes during check-in.']
  locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room assignment was locked. Null if assignment is not locked.']
  original_room_type_code varchar [note: 'The room type code that was originally booked by the guest. Used to calculate upgrade value and track displacement.']
  previous_room_number varchar [note: 'The room number from which the guest was reassigned, if applicable. Null for initial assignments. Used for reassignment tracking.']
  reassignment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this reservation has been reassigned to a different room. Used to track operational disruptions and guest experience impact.']
  reassignment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for room reassignment. Null if no reassignment has occurred. Used for root cause analysis.']
  reassignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent room reassignment. Null if no reassignment has occurred.']
  room_condition_at_assignment varchar [note: 'Housekeeping status of the room at the time of assignment. Ensures only ready rooms are assigned to arriving guests.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the room type assigned (e.g., KING, DBLQ, STE, PTHS). Used for inventory management and rate application.']
  smoking_preference varchar [note: 'Smoking designation of the assigned room. Used to match guest preferences and comply with local regulations.']
  special_request_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing any special requests related to the room assignment (e.g., high floor, away from elevator, connecting rooms, accessible features).']
  upgrade_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the upgrade indicating whether it was complimentary, paid, loyalty-driven, or operationally necessary.']
  upgrade_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for the room upgrade. Null if is_upgrade is false. Used for revenue management and guest satisfaction analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: loyalty_status|operational|paid_upgrade|service_recovery|availability|overbooking|vip|group_courtesy — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  view_type varchar [note: 'Type of view available from the assigned room. Used for guest preference matching and upsell opportunities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|city|mountain|garden|pool|courtyard|partial_ocean|no_view — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking a confirmed reservation to a specific physical room at the property, capturing the assigned room number, room type, floor, assignment timestamp, assignment method (auto-assigned, manually assigned, guest-requested), upgrade flag, upgrade reason, and assignment status. Tracks room reassignments and upgrades throughout the pre-arrival and check-in process. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room assignment module.'
}

Table reservation.booking_package {
  booking_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the booking package record. Primary key.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Booking_package currently has cancellation_policy (string) field. Normalizing to FK allows joining to get full policy details (penalty terms, free cancellation windows, etc.). Packages have specific c']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the package is offered and fulfilled. Used for multi-property reporting and revenue allocation.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent reservation to which this package is attached.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan under which this package is offered. Links package to pricing strategy and market segment.']
  cancellation_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which the package can be cancelled without penalty. Used for revenue management and guest communication.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or free-text explanation for package cancellation. Used for service recovery and trend analysis.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the package was cancelled. Null if package is still active.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package revenue is eligible for commission payment to Online Travel Agency (OTA), Global Distribution System (GDS), or other intermediaries.']
  component_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of individual components included in the package (e.g., breakfast for 2, spa credit $100, parking, airport transfer). Used for operational fulfillment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the package record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and booking velocity analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for package pricing. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the package expires or is no longer available for consumption. Typically aligns with check-out date.']
  fb_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of package revenue allocated to the Food and Beverage (F&B) department for departmental profit analysis.']
  fulfillment_instructions varchar [note: 'Operational instructions for property staff on how to deliver and track package components. May include Point of Sale (POS) codes, voucher numbers, or service delivery notes.']
  is_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package rate is included in the room rate (true) or charged separately (false). Affects Average Daily Rate (ADR) calculation.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package is mandatory for the reservation (e.g., resort fee, breakfast inclusion) or optional add-on.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package charges are refundable upon cancellation or modification of the reservation.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for the package (e.g., leisure, corporate, group, Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE)). Used for revenue management and segmentation analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the staff member or system that last modified the package record. Used for audit and quality control.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the package record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments about the package. Used for internal communication and service delivery coordination.']
  other_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of package revenue allocated to other departments (spa, golf, activities, parking, etc.) for Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) calculation.']
  package_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the package type for reporting and revenue allocation purposes.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the package type in the Property Management System (PMS). Examples: BRKFST, ROMANCE, GOLF, SPA.']
  package_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost to the property for delivering the package components. Used for Gross Operating Profit (GOP) calculation.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the package contents, inclusions, terms, and conditions. Used for guest communication and operational fulfillment.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the package. Examples: Bed and Breakfast, Romance Package, Golf Getaway, Spa Retreat.']
  package_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the package charged to the guest. Represents the package price component of the reservation.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the package within the reservation. Tracks from reservation through fulfillment or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|reserved|confirmed|consumed|cancelled|expired|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  posting_rhythm varchar [note: 'Frequency and timing rule for posting package charges to the guest folio. Determines revenue recognition timing.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of package units included in the reservation. Typically 1 per guest or per room depending on package type.']
  redemption_date date [note: 'Date when the package was fully redeemed or consumed by the guest. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the guest has consumed or redeemed the package components. Used for fulfillment tracking and service recovery.']
  revenue_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method for allocating package revenue across departments (rooms, Food and Beverage (F&B), spa, etc.) per Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (USALI) standards.']
  rooms_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of package revenue allocated to the Rooms department for Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) and departmental reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the package was added to the reservation. Used for channel attribution and commission tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OPERA|SynXis|Delphi|Direct|OTA|GDS|CRS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Guest-specific requests or modifications to the standard package (e.g., dietary restrictions for breakfast, preferred spa treatment time).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the package becomes active and available for guest consumption. Typically aligns with check-in date.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package rate amount includes applicable taxes (true) or taxes are calculated separately (false).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the staff member or system that added the package to the reservation. Used for audit and training purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing package components bundled with a reservation, such as breakfast inclusion, spa credits, parking, airport transfers, romance packages, and F&B vouchers. Stores package code, package description, included components, package rate, component value, posting instructions, and redemption status. Supports revenue allocation across departments (rooms vs. F&B vs. spa) and package fulfillment tracking. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS package module.'
}

Table reservation.walk_record {
  walk_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the walk record transaction. Primary key.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Walk events require originating channel tracking for OTA penalty calculations, service recovery cost allocation by channel, and channel-specific SLA compliance reporting. Currently only links to ota_p']
  destination_hotel_property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alternative property where the guest was relocated. The property that accommodated the walked guest.']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA partner if the original reservation was booked through an online travel agency. Critical for managing OTA penalties and chargebacks.']
  primary_walk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who processed the walk transaction. Used for training, quality assurance, and accountability tracking.']
  primary_walk_property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the original reservation was made but could not be honored due to overbooking or other operational constraints.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who was walked. Links to the guest profile who experienced the relocation.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original reservation that was walked. Links to the reservation that could not be honored at the original property.']
  brand_standard_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the walk was handled in compliance with brand standards for guest relocation and service recovery. Used for quality assurance and brand audit purposes.']
  cash_compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary cash compensation provided to the guest for the walk inconvenience. Tracked separately from other compensation types for financial reporting.']
  compensation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary compensation amounts. Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'The primary type of compensation provided to the guest for the walk inconvenience. May include complimentary nights, transportation, loyalty points, cash, vouchers, room upgrades, or a combination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: complimentary_night|transportation|loyalty_points|cash|voucher|room_upgrade|multiple — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  complaint_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest filed a formal complaint regarding the walk event. Used for tracking service recovery effectiveness and brand reputation management.']
  complaint_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any complaint filed by the guest. Tracks the complaint lifecycle from initial filing through final resolution.']
  complimentary_nights_count varchar [note: 'Number of complimentary room nights provided as compensation for the walk. Typically one night but may vary based on brand standards and guest status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the walk record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  guest_consent_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit consent was obtained from the guest before the walk. Critical for compliance with brand standards and guest rights policies.']
  guest_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when guest consent was obtained. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  guest_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-walk guest satisfaction score collected through follow-up survey. Used to measure effectiveness of service recovery and track Guest Recovery Rate (GRR).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the walk record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  loyalty_points_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points awarded to the guest as compensation for the walk. Used for guest retention and satisfaction recovery.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special circumstances, or contextual information about the walk event. Used for operational documentation and future reference.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected from the guest after the walk event. Measures likelihood of guest to recommend the brand despite the walk experience.']
  original_arrival_date date [note: 'The scheduled arrival date from the original reservation that was walked. Used for tracking the intended stay period.']
  original_departure_date date [note: 'The scheduled departure date from the original reservation that was walked. Used for calculating the total nights impacted by the walk.']
  original_room_type_code varchar [note: 'The room type code that was originally reserved at the source property. Used to ensure comparable or upgraded accommodation at the destination property.']
  ota_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner was notified of the walk event as required by contractual obligations.']
  ota_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the OTA partner was notified of the walk event. Used for compliance verification and audit trail.']
  ota_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount charged by the OTA partner for the walk event. Tracked for financial reporting and channel cost analysis.']
  ota_penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the walk event triggers a contractual penalty with the OTA partner. Used for financial accrual and channel relationship management.']
  provided_room_type_code varchar [note: 'The room type code that was provided at the destination property. Used to track whether the guest received a comparable, upgraded, or downgraded room.']
  revenue_lost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue lost from the original reservation that was walked. Used for revenue management analysis and overbooking strategy optimization.']
  room_nights_lost varchar [note: 'Number of room nights lost from the original reservation due to the walk. Used for occupancy impact analysis and revenue management reporting.']
  service_recovery_action_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all service recovery actions taken beyond standard compensation. May include manager apology calls, welcome amenities, special requests honored, or other personalized gestures.']
  total_compensation_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all compensation provided to the guest, including complimentary nights, transportation, cash, and estimated value of loyalty points and vouchers.']
  transportation_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary cost of transportation provided to the guest. Used for cost tracking and operational expense analysis.']
  transportation_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transportation was arranged and provided to relocate the guest to the destination property.']
  walk_date date [note: 'The date on which the walk event occurred. Typically corresponds to the original scheduled arrival date when the guest was informed of the relocation.']
  walk_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the walk event. Common reasons include overbooking, maintenance issues, operational constraints, VIP displacement, group block conflicts, or system errors.']
  walk_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the guest was walked. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for service recovery and operational analysis.']
  walk_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the walk event was recorded in the system. Captures the exact moment the guest was notified and the walk transaction was initiated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record documenting guest walk events where a confirmed guest is relocated to an alternative property due to overbooking. Captures walk date, original booking reference, destination property, room type provided, compensation (complimentary night, transportation, loyalty points), guest consent, and service recovery actions. Supports Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) tracking, brand standard compliance, and OTA penalty management.'
}

Table reservation.program_enrollment {
  program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this wellness program enrollment record. Primary key.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the guest reservation booking record']
  wellness_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the wellness program master record']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the guest cancelled their wellness program enrollment. Null for active enrollments.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or free-text explanation for program cancellation. Used for program improvement and refund processing.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the guest\'s participation in the wellness program. Tracks progression from enrollment through completion or cancellation.']
  confirmed_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was confirmed (moved from waitlist or initial enrollment confirmed). Used for operational planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this enrollment record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the enrollment price.']
  customization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing guest-specific customizations to the standard wellness program (e.g., treatment substitutions, schedule adjustments, intensity modifications).']
  dietary_preferences varchar [note: 'Guest\'s dietary preferences and restrictions specific to this wellness program enrollment (e.g., vegan, gluten-free, allergies). Used to coordinate F&B for program meals.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest enrolled in this wellness program. Used for enrollment tracking and reporting.']
  enrollment_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual price paid for this wellness program enrollment. May differ from program_price due to discounts, packages, or seasonal pricing.']
  medical_clearance_date date [note: 'Date when medical clearance was obtained for programs requiring physician approval or health screening. Required for programs with medical_supervision_required_flag = true.']
  medical_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of medical clearance for this enrollment. Controls whether guest can proceed with program participation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this enrollment record.']
  program_end_date date [note: 'Actual end date of the wellness program for this guest. Used to calculate program duration and completion.']
  program_start_date date [note: 'Actual start date of the wellness program for this guest. May differ from reservation arrival date if program begins mid-stay.']
  progress_notes varchar [note: 'Wellness coordinator notes tracking guest progress through the program (e.g., treatment attendance, fitness session participation, goal achievement).']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to guest upon cancellation, based on cancellation policy. Null if no refund issued.']
  satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Guest satisfaction rating for the wellness program experience (1-5 scale). Collected post-program for quality tracking.']
  waitlist_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment was initially placed on a waitlist due to program capacity constraints.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Position in the waitlist queue if waitlist_flag is true. Null if not waitlisted.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between a guest reservation and a wellness program. It captures the guest\'s participation in structured multi-day wellness programs, including medical clearances, dietary customizations, program scheduling, and completion tracking. Each record links one reservation to one wellness program with enrollment-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this participation relationship.. Existence Justification: In resort and destination spa operations, guests frequently enroll in multiple wellness programs during a single stay (e.g., a 7-night stay might include a 3-day detox program followed by a 2-day fitness bootcamp), and each wellness program cohort has multiple concurrent participants each with their own reservation. The enrollment itself is an actively managed business entity with program-specific data including medical clearances, dietary customizations, progress tracking, waitlist management, and completion status that belongs to neither the reservation nor the program master record alone.'
}

Table reservation.group_spa_package_contract {
  group_spa_package_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this group spa package contract record. Primary key.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa package included in the group block contract']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the group room block that has contracted this spa package offering']
  advance_booking_deadline date [note: 'Deadline date by which group members must book their spa package appointments to receive the contracted group rate and guarantee availability.']
  billing_arrangement varchar [note: 'Defines how spa package charges are billed for this group. Values: master_account (all charges to group master account), individual_folio (each guest pays separately), split_billing (partial group subsidy), prepaid (included in group package price).']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this spa package contract within the group block lifecycle. Tracks progression from tentative offer through fulfillment.']
  contracted_date date [note: 'Date when this spa package was added to the group block contract and terms were agreed upon.']
  contracted_package_count varchar [note: 'Number of spa package instances contracted or committed as part of the group block agreement. Represents the minimum guaranteed bookings.']
  coordinator_contact varchar [note: 'Name and contact information for the group coordinator or event planner responsible for managing spa package bookings for this group block.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this group spa package contract record was first created.']
  customization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special customizations, modifications, or requirements for this spa package as contracted for this specific group block (e.g., extended duration, treatment substitutions, VIP amenities).']
  group_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount off the spa package rack rate negotiated for this group block. Applied to all bookings of this package by group members.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this group spa package contract record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between reservation_group_block and spa_package. It captures the negotiated spa package offerings included in group room blocks for events, corporate retreats, weddings, and conferences. Each record links one group block to one spa package with contracted pricing, advance booking requirements, billing arrangements, and coordinator details that exist only in the context of this group-specific spa offering.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality operations, group blocks for weddings, corporate retreats, and conferences routinely contract multiple spa packages as part of their amenity offerings (bride/groom packages, team wellness sessions, VIP treatments). Simultaneously, popular spa packages are offered across multiple concurrent group blocks with different negotiated terms. The relationship represents an operational contract that sales coordinators actively create and manage, with group-specific pricing, booking deadlines, billing arrangements, and customization requirements that belong to neither the block definition nor the package catalog alone.'
}

Table property.property {
  property_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for property']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each property operates in ONE currency; each currency used by MANY properties. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string from property - will be retrieved via ']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.ownership_entity. Business justification: Each property has ONE ownership entity; each ownership entity can own MANY properties. This is a core master data relationship. Adding ownership_entity_id FK allows removal of ownership_type string (o']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address of the property including street number and street name. Used for guest communications, GDS/OTA display, emergency services, and regulatory filings.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite number, building name, or floor designation. Supplements address_line1 for complete physical address representation.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the hotel brand affiliation (e.g., HH for Hilton Hotels, MXY for Marriott, etc.). Used for brand-level reporting, loyalty program alignment, and STR competitive benchmarking. Maps to the brand reference product.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the hotel brand (e.g., Hilton Hotels and Resorts, Marriott Hotels). Denormalized for reporting convenience and to support brand-level analytics without requiring a join to the brand reference product.']
  brand_tier varchar [note: 'The STR-aligned brand tier classification indicating the market positioning of the property brand. Used for RevPAR benchmarking, competitive set definition, and revenue strategy segmentation. Aligns with STR chain scale categories.']
  city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality in which the property is located. Used for geographic reporting, market segmentation, STR competitive set definition, and OTA search indexing.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the property permanently closed or was converted. Null for active properties. Used for portfolio lifecycle reporting, asset disposal accounting under IFRS/GAAP, and STR historical benchmarking exclusion.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the property is located (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for international reporting, currency determination, regulatory jurisdiction, and GDPR/CCPA applicability assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the property master record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']
  dos_email varchar [note: 'The business email address of the Director of Sales (DOS) for this property. Used for group lead routing, RFP notifications, and sales performance reporting distribution via Salesforce CRM and Delphi by Amadeus.']
  dos_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the Director of Sales (DOS) responsible for group, corporate, and MICE sales for this property. Used for Delphi by Amadeus event sales routing, Salesforce CRM account ownership, and sales performance reporting.']
  franchise_agreement_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the franchise license agreement governing the property\'s brand affiliation. Populated only when is_franchised is true. Used for franchise compliance tracking, royalty fee calculation, and brand standards audit management.']
  gds_property_code varchar [note: 'The property code used within Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport for travel agent bookings and corporate rate distribution. Critical for channel distribution management and OTA/GDS rate parity monitoring.']
  gm_email varchar [note: 'The business email address of the current General Manager (GM). Used for operational communications, escalation routing, and executive reporting. Classified as confidential PII as it identifies a specific individual.']
  gm_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the current General Manager (GM) responsible for the property\'s overall operations. Used for operational escalations, regulatory correspondence, and executive reporting. Classified confidential as an internal business contact.']
  is_franchised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property operates under a franchise agreement with the brand. When true, franchise fee structures, brand standards compliance, and PIP obligations apply. Drives franchise royalty reporting and brand audit scheduling.']
  largest_meeting_room_sqft varchar [note: 'The square footage of the largest single meeting or ballroom space at the property. Used for group sales qualification, event capacity planning, and MICE marketing collateral.']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent significant renovation or Property Improvement Plan (PIP) was completed. Used for FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures and Equipment) lifecycle tracking, CapEx planning, brand standards compliance, and franchise renewal assessments.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate of the property in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for mapping, proximity-based search, competitive set geographic analysis, and mobile guest experience features.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate of the property in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for mapping, proximity-based search, competitive set geographic analysis, and mobile guest experience features.']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date on which the property first opened for guest operations. Used for property age calculations, depreciation schedules under IFRS/GAAP, STR comp set historical analysis, and anniversary-based marketing campaigns.']
  opera_property_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric property code assigned within Oracle OPERA PMS. This is the operational system-of-record identifier used for front desk, reservations, cashiering, and housekeeping transactions. Serves as the primary integration key between the lakehouse and OPERA.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'The formal lifecycle state of the property. Governs whether the property is bookable, reportable in STR benchmarks, and included in revenue management optimization. Valid states: pre_opening (under development/fit-out), active (fully operational), suspended (temporarily closed), closed (permanently closed), converted (rebranded or repurposed).']
  parent_brand_group varchar [note: 'The name of the parent hotel company or brand group that owns the brand (e.g., Hilton Worldwide, Marriott International, Hyatt Hotels Corporation). Supports portfolio-level reporting and corporate hierarchy analytics.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'The main public-facing telephone number for the property in E.164 international format. Used for guest communications, GDS/OTA display, emergency contact, and regulatory filings.']
  pip_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the Property Improvement Plan (PIP) for this property. PIPs are typically required at franchise renewal, ownership transfer, or brand standard updates. Drives CapEx budget planning and franchise compliance monitoring.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the property location. Used for geographic analytics, tax jurisdiction mapping, OTA search, and regulatory reporting.']
  property_name varchar [note: 'The full official trading name of the hotel, resort, or vacation property as displayed to guests, on signage, and in all guest-facing communications. This is the authoritative display name used across all channels.']
  property_type varchar [note: 'The physical and operational type of the property asset. Drives applicable operating procedures, amenity expectations, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hotel|resort|extended_stay|vacation_property|boutique|conference_center — promote to reference product]']
  revenue_manager_email varchar [note: 'The business email address of the Revenue Manager for this property. Used for IDeaS G3 RMS notifications, rate strategy communications, and revenue performance reporting distribution.']
  revenue_manager_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the Revenue Manager responsible for pricing, forecasting, and inventory controls for this property. Used for IDeaS G3 RMS access provisioning, escalation routing, and revenue strategy accountability.']
  segment_classification varchar [note: 'The operational segment classification of the property indicating service level and market positioning. Used for USALI-aligned financial reporting, revenue management strategy, and portfolio segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: luxury|premium|upper_upscale|upscale|select_service|extended_stay|resort — promote to reference product]']
  star_rating decimal(2,1) [note: 'The official star rating of the property on a 1.0 to 5.0 scale as awarded by the applicable national or international rating authority (e.g., AAA, Forbes Travel Guide, national tourism boards). Used for guest expectation setting, OTA display, and competitive benchmarking.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or administrative region in which the property is located. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory compliance (CCPA for California properties), and regional performance reporting.']
  str_property_code varchar [note: 'The unique property identifier assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) for inclusion in the STAR Report competitive benchmarking program. Required for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) reporting.']
  synxis_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier assigned within Sabre SynXis CRS used for central reservations, channel management, and rate distribution. Required for reconciling CRS bookings to the property master record.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The IANA time zone identifier for the property\'s local time zone (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Critical for accurate check-in/check-out timestamp processing, night audit scheduling, and revenue management rate activation timing.']
  total_floor_count varchar [note: 'The total number of floors in the main hotel tower or building. Used for housekeeping scheduling, elevator capacity planning, and emergency evacuation planning per local fire safety and building codes.']
  total_meeting_space_sqft varchar [note: 'The total square footage of all meeting, conference, and event spaces within the property. Key metric for MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) sales, group evaluation, and event revenue forecasting in Delphi by Amadeus.']
  total_room_count varchar [note: 'The total number of guest rooms and suites in the property inventory. This is the denominator for RevPAR, OCC (Occupancy Rate), and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room inventory configuration.']
  total_suite_count varchar [note: 'The number of suite-category rooms within the total room inventory. Used for premium inventory management, upsell strategy, and suite-specific RevPAR reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the property master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), ETL incremental load processing, and audit trail compliance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical hotel, resort, or vacation property in the portfolio. Captures the authoritative identity including brand affiliation (brand code, brand tier, parent brand group), segment classification (luxury, premium, select-service), ownership type, franchise status, operational status with formal lifecycle states (pre-opening, active, suspended, closed, converted), star rating, total room count, geographic coordinates, full physical address (street, city, state/province, postal code, country), time zone, currency, PMS system identifiers, and key operational contacts (GM name/email, DOS name/email, revenue manager name/email). This is the SSOT for property identity, location, brand affiliation, and operational state across all domains. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.'
}

Table property.hierarchy {
  hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the property organizational and operational hierarchy. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each hierarchy node reports in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  fnb_revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Hierarchical F&B reporting requires linking revenue centers to regional/brand nodes. Corporate rollup of F&B KPIs (covers, RevPAR, food cost %) by hierarchy level is core multi-property operations rep']
  parent_node_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the hierarchy tree. Null for the corporate root node. Enables recursive parent-child traversal for consolidated reporting of RevPAR, ADR, OCC, and GOP across organizational tiers.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: A hierarchy node CAN represent a single property (leaf node) or a grouping (branch node). Link leaf nodes to their property master record. This FK will be nullable - branch nodes (regions, portfolios)']
  brand_portfolio varchar [note: 'Name of the brand portfolio or brand family associated with this hierarchy node (e.g., \'Luxury Collection\', \'Premium Brands\', \'Select Service\'). Applicable to division and region nodes that manage a specific brand segment. Supports brand-level performance reporting and portfolio analytics.']
  chain_scale varchar [note: 'STR-defined chain scale classification for this hierarchy node, indicating the service and price tier of properties within this node. Used for competitive benchmarking, STR STAR Report alignment, and brand portfolio segmentation. Values align with STR\'s standard chain scale categories.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the geographic location of this hierarchy node (e.g., USA, GBR, SGP). Applicable primarily to property and cluster nodes. Used for regulatory compliance reporting, tax jurisdiction mapping, and geographic analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance for financial reporting hierarchy changes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this hierarchy node and its parent-child relationship became operationally effective. Used for point-in-time hierarchy reconstruction and historical reporting aligned with management reporting periods.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this hierarchy node or its parent-child relationship ceases to be effective. Null for currently active nodes. Supports temporal hierarchy versioning for historical consolidated reporting and audit compliance.']
  fiscal_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) indicating the start of the fiscal year for this hierarchy node. Supports USALI-aligned financial period reporting and SAP S/4HANA fiscal year variant configuration for nodes that operate on non-calendar fiscal years.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Chain code used to identify this portfolio or brand in Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. Applicable to corporate and brand-level hierarchy nodes. Supports channel distribution management and GDS booking analytics.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Major geographic region classification for this hierarchy node, aligned with STR geographic hierarchy and internal management reporting structures. Used for regional KPI roll-ups and competitive benchmarking via STR STAR Reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Americas|EMEA|APAC|GREATER_CHINA|MEA|EUROPE|NORTH_AMERICA|LATIN_AMERICA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this node within the organizational hierarchy tree, starting at 1 for the corporate root. Enables efficient tree traversal, level-based filtering, and KPI aggregation at each tier (e.g., Level 1 = Corporate, Level 2 = Region, Level 3 = Division, Level 4 = Area Office, Level 5 = Cluster, Level 6 = Property).']
  is_reporting_node boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node is designated as a formal reporting node for consolidated KPI roll-ups (RevPAR, ADR, OCC, GOP). True for nodes that appear in management reporting packages; False for intermediate structural nodes used only for hierarchy traversal.']
  is_str_market_node boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node corresponds to a defined STR competitive market for benchmarking purposes. True for nodes that are directly mapped to an STR market for RGI, MPI, and ARI reporting via STR STAR Reports.']
  kpi_rollup_method varchar [note: 'Method used to aggregate KPIs (RevPAR, ADR, OCC, GOP) from child nodes to this parent node. \'sum\' for absolute metrics (room revenue, GOP); \'weighted_average\' for rate-based metrics (ADR, RevPAR) weighted by available rooms; \'simple_average\' for index metrics; \'none\' for leaf nodes with no children.']
  management_type varchar [note: 'Operational management structure classification for this hierarchy node. \'owned\' indicates company-owned assets; \'managed\' indicates third-party owner with management contract; \'franchised\' indicates franchise agreement; \'leased\' indicates leasehold arrangement; \'joint_venture\' indicates shared ownership structure. Critical for financial consolidation and USALI reporting.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy node across systems (e.g., CRS, PMS, ERP). Used as the business key for cross-system integration and reporting roll-ups. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA profit center codes and Oracle OPERA PMS property codes.']
  node_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about this hierarchy node, including its geographic scope, strategic purpose, or operational characteristics. Used for documentation, onboarding, and BI tool tooltips.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the hierarchy node (e.g., \'Americas Region\', \'EMEA Division\', \'Southeast Asia Cluster\', \'Grand Hyatt New York\'). Used in management reporting, dashboards, and consolidated KPI roll-ups.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchy node. \'active\' indicates the node is operational and included in reporting; \'inactive\' indicates it has been decommissioned; \'pending\' indicates a newly created node awaiting activation; \'dissolved\' indicates a node that has been merged or restructured out of the hierarchy.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchy node indicating its organizational level. \'corporate\' is the top-level portfolio node; \'region\' represents major geographic divisions (Americas, EMEA, APAC); \'division\' is a sub-regional grouping; \'area_office\' is a local management unit; \'cluster\' is a grouping of nearby properties; \'property\' is an individual hotel or resort asset.']
  path varchar [note: 'Materialized full path string from the root node to this node using delimiter-separated node codes (e.g., \'CORP/AMER/USEAST/NYC-CLUSTER\'). Enables efficient subtree queries and breadcrumb navigation in BI tools without recursive joins.']
  property_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual property assets directly or indirectly under this hierarchy node. Maintained as a denormalized count for reporting efficiency. Applicable to corporate, region, division, area office, and cluster nodes. Used in portfolio-level KPI reporting and capacity planning.']
  reporting_segment_code varchar [note: 'Internal financial reporting segment code assigned to this hierarchy node, aligned with SAP S/4HANA profit center and segment reporting structures. Used for USALI-compliant financial consolidation, EBITDA and NOI reporting, and SOX-compliant segment disclosures.']
  responsible_executive varchar [note: 'Name of the senior executive accountable for this hierarchy node (e.g., Regional Vice President, Area General Manager, General Manager). Used for management reporting accountability, escalation routing, and organizational governance.']
  responsible_executive_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the executive accountable for this hierarchy node. Used for automated reporting distribution, escalation notifications, and governance communications.']
  responsible_executive_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the executive accountable for this hierarchy node (e.g., \'Senior Vice President - Americas\', \'Area General Manager - Southeast Asia\'). Supports organizational chart generation and governance documentation.']
  restructure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business reason for any hierarchy restructuring event affecting this node (e.g., regional reorganization, brand portfolio realignment, merger, acquisition, or divestiture). Supports audit trail and change management documentation.']
  sap_profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA profit center code mapped to this hierarchy node for financial consolidation, USALI-aligned P&L reporting, and management accounting. Enables direct linkage between the property hierarchy and SAP financial reporting structures for EBITDA, NOI, and GOPPAR analysis.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display order for this node among its siblings within the same parent node. Controls the sequence in which nodes appear in management reports, dashboards, and organizational charts. Lower values appear first.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy node record originated (e.g., OPERA_PMS for property-level nodes, SAP_S4HANA for financial segment nodes, SYNXIS_CRS for distribution hierarchy nodes). Supports data lineage and ETL audit tracking in the Silver Layer.']
  str_geographic_code varchar [note: 'STR-assigned geographic market code for this hierarchy node, used to align internal organizational hierarchy with STR competitive benchmarking markets. Enables direct mapping to STR STAR Report data for Revenue Generation Index (RGI), Market Penetration Index (MPI), and Average Rate Index (ARI) benchmarking.']
  str_market_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable STR market name corresponding to the STR geographic code (e.g., \'New York City\', \'London\', \'Singapore\'). Used in competitive benchmarking reports and STR STAR Report alignment for RevPAR and ADR market comparisons.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary geographic location of this hierarchy node (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Singapore\'). Used for scheduling, reporting period alignment, and operational communications across multi-property portfolios.']
  total_room_count varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of guestrooms across all properties under this hierarchy node. Used for portfolio-level RevPAR and OCC calculations, capacity planning, and STR competitive set benchmarking. Maintained as a denormalized aggregate for reporting performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this hierarchy node record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this hierarchy node record, incremented each time the node\'s attributes or parent-child relationship is modified. Supports temporal versioning, audit compliance, and point-in-time hierarchy reconstruction for historical reporting.']

  Note: 'Defines the organizational and operational hierarchy of properties including corporate portfolio, regional divisions (e.g., Americas, EMEA, APAC), area offices, and property clusters. Captures parent-child relationships with hierarchy level, node name, node code, responsible executive, and effective date. Supports multi-property consolidated reporting with KPI roll-ups (RevPAR, ADR, OCC, GOP) across geographic and organizational tiers. Aligned with STR geographic hierarchy and internal management reporting structures.'
}

Table property.property_facility {
  property_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical facility or amenity record within the property domain. Primary key for the facility data product.']
  cleaning_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to housekeeping.cleaning_standard. Business justification: Facilities (pools, spas, fitness centers) require specific cleaning protocols defined by brand standards. Operations managers assign cleaning standards to facilities to ensure compliance with health c']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Facilities (pools, fitness centers, spas) are cost centers for expense allocation in USALI. Real operations track maintenance, utilities, and labor costs by facility for departmental profitability ana']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each facility usage fee is denominated in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Facilities (pools, spas, fitness centers) operate as distinct organizational units with dedicated staff, labor budgets, and scheduling. Required for labor cost allocation, productivity tracking, and o']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent property at which this facility is located. Links the facility to its owning hotel or resort asset in the property master.']
  property_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property_outlet. Business justification: A facility CAN be associated with a revenue-generating outlet (e.g., restaurant facility linked to restaurant outlet). This FK will be nullable - not all facilities are outlets (e.g., pool, gym). Remo']
  ada_features varchar [note: 'Descriptive text listing specific ADA-compliant accessibility features available at the facility (e.g., \'Wheelchair ramp, pool lift, accessible restroom, braille signage\'). Supports guest accessibility requests and ADA compliance audit documentation.']
  age_restriction varchar [note: 'Age-based access restriction for the facility. Adults_only applies to facilities such as adult pools, bars, and certain spa areas; minimum_age values apply to facilities with safety or legal age requirements. Used for guest communication, liability management, and regulatory compliance.']
  area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the facility measured in square feet. Used for event space marketing, FF&E (Furniture Fixtures and Equipment) planning, CapEx budgeting, and PIP documentation. Meeting and event spaces use this for per-square-foot revenue analysis.']
  av_equipment_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether built-in audio-visual equipment (projectors, screens, microphones, video conferencing systems) is available in the facility. Primarily relevant for meeting rooms, ballrooms, and business centers. Used in MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) event sales and BEO configuration.']
  building_wing varchar [note: 'Named wing, tower, or zone of the property building where the facility is physically located (e.g., \'North Tower\', \'Pool Deck\', \'Conference Level\'). Supports wayfinding, housekeeping scheduling, and multi-building resort navigation.']
  capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests or persons the facility can accommodate simultaneously under normal operating conditions. For meeting rooms this is the maximum seating capacity; for pools it is the maximum bather load; for restaurants it is the total cover count. Used in event sales, operational scheduling, and safety compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage and compliance with SOX financial controls and GDPR data management requirements.']
  dress_code varchar [note: 'Required dress code for guests accessing the facility. Relevant for restaurants, bars, spas, and pool areas. Used in guest-facing amenity descriptions and front desk communication to set expectations and enforce property standards.']
  facility_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the facility into a business category for reporting and guest-facing amenity segmentation. Recreation covers pools, gyms, and sports; food_beverage covers restaurants and bars; meetings_events covers ballrooms and conference rooms; wellness covers spas and health centers; business_services covers business centers; transportation covers parking and valet. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recreation|food_beverage|meetings_events|wellness|business_services|transportation|retail|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the facility within the property management and channel distribution systems (e.g., POOL-01, SPA-MAIN, GYM-24H). Used in OTA feeds, PMS configuration, and guest-facing amenity display.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the facility as presented to guests and staff (e.g., \'Infinity Pool\', \'The Serenity Spa\', \'Grand Ballroom\'). Used in guest-facing amenity listings, housekeeping schedules, and event coordination.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the facility by its primary function (e.g., pool, spa, fitness_center, restaurant, bar, meeting_room, ballroom, business_center, parking, recreational_area, retail_outlet). Drives amenity display grouping, operational scheduling, and ADA compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pool|spa|fitness_center|restaurant|bar|meeting_room|ballroom|business_center|parking|recreational_area|retail_outlet|lounge — promote to reference product]']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Floor level within the property building where the facility is located. Used for wayfinding, housekeeping zone assignment, and emergency evacuation planning. Negative values indicate below-grade levels (e.g., basement parking).']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection for this facility. Pass indicates full compliance; pass_with_conditions indicates compliance with minor corrective actions required; fail indicates non-compliance requiring closure or remediation; pending indicates inspection scheduled but not yet completed.']
  is_24_hour boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility operates continuously 24 hours per day without a defined closing time (e.g., fitness centers, parking structures, business centers). When True, operating_hours_open and operating_hours_close are informational only.']
  is_ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility meets ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements including wheelchair access, accessible entrances, accessible restrooms, and assistive equipment. Critical for regulatory compliance reporting and guest accessibility communication.']
  is_fee_based boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility charges a separate usage fee beyond the room rate (e.g., resort fee, spa access fee, parking fee). When True, the associated outlet_code links to POS revenue tracking.']
  is_reservation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests must make a prior reservation to use the facility (e.g., spa treatment rooms, cabanas, private dining rooms). Drives guest-facing booking prompts and operational capacity management.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is only available during specific seasons of the year (e.g., outdoor pools, ski facilities, beach clubs). When True, seasonal_open_date and seasonal_close_date define the operational window.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection conducted for this facility (yyyy-MM-dd). Covers health department inspections for F&B outlets, pool safety inspections, fire safety checks, and ADA compliance audits. Supports regulatory compliance tracking.']
  license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility\'s operating license or permit (yyyy-MM-dd). Triggers renewal workflows to ensure continuous regulatory compliance. Critical for F&B outlets, spas, and pool facilities subject to periodic licensing.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued operating license or permit number for regulated facilities such as restaurants (food service license), bars (liquor license), spas (cosmetology license), and pools (health department permit). Required for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  max_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Regulatory or operational maximum occupancy threshold expressed as a percentage of total capacity (e.g., 80.00 means 80% of capacity). Used for fire safety compliance, COVID-era capacity restrictions, and operational safety management.']
  natural_light boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has access to natural daylight through windows or skylights. A key selling attribute for meeting and event spaces, as natural light is a premium feature in MICE sales. Also relevant for wellness facilities and restaurants.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled regulatory or safety inspection for this facility (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for compliance calendar management and proactive maintenance scheduling to ensure facilities pass inspections without operational disruption.']
