  operating_hours_close varchar [note: 'Standard daily closing time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. For facilities operating past midnight, this may be 23:59 with a separate overnight flag. Used alongside operating_hours_open for guest-facing display and staffing.']
  operating_hours_open varchar [note: 'Standard daily opening time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for guest-facing amenity display, operational staffing schedules, and housekeeping preparation windows. For 24-hour facilities, set to 00:00.']
  outdoor_indoor varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is located indoors, outdoors, or in a semi-outdoor (covered but open-air) environment. Affects seasonal availability, weather contingency planning for events, and guest-facing amenity descriptions.']
  property_facility_description varchar [note: 'Long-form marketing and operational description of the facility for use in guest-facing channels, OTA listings, and property websites. Describes the facility\'s features, ambiance, services offered, and unique selling points. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS for channel distribution.']
  property_facility_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the facility indicating its operational availability. Active means fully operational; seasonal means available only during defined seasonal periods; under_renovation means temporarily closed for PIP (Property Improvement Plan) works; closed means permanently decommissioned; pending_opening means approved but not yet operational.']
  renovation_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date of the most recent or current PIP (Property Improvement Plan) renovation for this facility (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for return-to-service planning, guest communication, and CapEx project closure.']
  renovation_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual start date of the most recent or current PIP (Property Improvement Plan) renovation for this facility (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for operational impact planning, guest communication, and CapEx project tracking in SAP S/4HANA.']
  renovation_status varchar [note: 'Current PIP (Property Improvement Plan) renovation status of the facility. Planned indicates approved but not yet started; in_progress indicates active construction or refurbishment; completed indicates renovation finished and facility returned to service. Supports CapEx tracking and operational impact planning.']
  seasonal_close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which a seasonal facility closes at the end of the season (yyyy-MM-dd). Applicable only when is_seasonal is True. Used for operational planning and guest communication regarding amenity availability.']
  seasonal_open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which a seasonal facility opens for the current or upcoming season (yyyy-MM-dd). Applicable only when is_seasonal is True. Used for operational planning, guest-facing amenity availability display, and housekeeping preparation scheduling.']
  seating_configuration varchar [note: 'Default or primary seating arrangement style for the facility, particularly relevant for meeting rooms and event spaces. Theater style is rows facing a stage; banquet is round tables; boardroom is a single conference table. Used in BEO (Banquet Event Order) management and event sales in Delphi by Amadeus. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theater|classroom|banquet|reception|boardroom|u_shape|cabaret|open_plan|not_applicable — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  smoking_policy varchar [note: 'Smoking policy applicable to this specific facility. Non_smoking indicates a fully smoke-free environment; smoking_permitted indicates smoking is allowed; designated_area indicates a specific smoking zone exists within the facility. Supports guest communication and regulatory compliance.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the display order of facilities within a category on guest-facing amenity listings and property websites. Lower numbers appear first. Managed by property marketing teams to prioritize premium or signature facilities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility record was most recently modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines, data lineage tracking, and audit compliance under SOX and GDPR.']
  usage_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard per-use or per-day fee charged to guests for accessing the facility, expressed in the property\'s local currency. Applicable only when is_fee_based is True. Used for revenue forecasting and guest billing configuration in PMS.']

  Note: 'Catalog of physical facilities and amenities available at each property, including pools, spas, fitness centers, business centers, parking structures, restaurants, bars, meeting spaces, and recreational areas. Captures facility name, facility type, capacity, operating hours, accessibility (ADA compliance), seasonal availability, renovation status, and associated outlet codes. Supports guest-facing amenity display and operational scheduling.'
}

Table property.franchise_agreement {
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the franchise or management contract agreement record in the property domain. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each franchise agreement is denominated in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Franchise agreements impose specific compliance obligations (brand standard audits, quality assurance score minimums, PIP requirements). Linking franchise agreements to their mandated compliance oblig']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.ownership_entity. Business justification: Each franchise agreement has ONE franchisor entity. Normalize franchisor reference to ownership_entity master. This is the first of two FKs to ownership_entity (franchisor and franchisee). Remove fran']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property governed by this franchise or management contract agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the franchisor or legal department. Used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA contract management and legal document repositories. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the franchise or management contract agreement. Active: agreement is in force. Pending: executed but effective date not yet reached. Expired: term ended without renewal. Terminated: early termination invoked. Suspended: temporarily inactive due to breach or force majeure. Under_Renewal: renewal negotiation in progress. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractual relationship governing the property\'s brand affiliation and operational model. Franchise: brand license with owner-operated management. Management Contract: brand operates the property on behalf of owner. Lease: brand leases the physical asset. Owner-Operated: independent ownership and operation. License: limited brand usage rights. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Standardized brand identifier code assigned by the franchisor representing the hotel brand under which the property operates (e.g., HH for Hilton Hotels, MC for Marriott Courtyard). Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS brand configuration and STR benchmarking brand classification.']
  brand_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the brand under which the property operates per STR chain scale segmentation. Drives competitive benchmarking, RevPAR analysis, and STR STAR Report peer group assignment.']
  brand_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the franchisor\'s brand standards manual applicable to this agreement at time of execution. Brand standards govern FF&E specifications, service delivery, guest experience requirements, and quality assurance audit criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this franchise agreement record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR data processing records. Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  crs_connectivity_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement mandates connectivity to the franchisor\'s CRS (Central Reservation System) for rate and inventory distribution. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS integration requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the franchise or management contract agreement becomes legally binding and operational. Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used for portfolio governance, brand compliance tracking, and financial reporting under USALI.']
  exclusive_territory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement grants the franchisee an exclusive geographic territory within which the franchisor agrees not to license competing properties under the same brand. Impacts competitive set analysis and new property development decisions.']
  executed_date date [note: 'Date on which the franchise or management contract agreement was fully executed (signed by all parties). May differ from effective_date if there is a gap between signing and commencement. Used for legal document management and audit trails.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the franchise or management contract agreement is scheduled to expire if not renewed. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Critical for portfolio renewal pipeline management.']
  fdd_registration_number varchar [note: 'Registration number of the Franchise Disclosure Document (FDD) filed with applicable state regulatory authorities. Required in registration states under the FTC Franchise Rule and state franchise laws. Supports regulatory compliance tracking.']
  ff_and_e_reserve_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of gross revenue required to be set aside in a FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures and Equipment) reserve fund for ongoing property maintenance and capital replacement. Mandated under most franchise and management contract agreements per USALI standards.']
  franchisee_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the franchisee or property owner entity entering into the franchise or management contract agreement. Used for ownership structure tracking, portfolio governance, and SAP S/4HANA vendor/partner master alignment.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this agreement. Critical for cross-border portfolio legal compliance and dispute resolution.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'State or province within the governing law country whose laws apply to this agreement. Relevant for US-based agreements where state franchise disclosure laws (FDD requirements) apply.']
  initial_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of the initial franchise or management contract term as specified in the agreement. Typically ranges from 10 to 30 years for franchise agreements. Used for portfolio lifecycle planning and IFRS 16 lease accounting where applicable.']
  last_brand_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent brand standards quality assurance audit conducted by the franchisor at this property. Used to track compliance status and schedule next audit cycle per agreement requirements.']
  liquidated_damages_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Pre-agreed liquidated damages amount payable upon early termination of the agreement by the franchisee or property owner. Expressed in agreement currency. Critical for portfolio risk assessment and financial exposure reporting under SOX.']
  loyalty_fee_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of revenue from loyalty member stays payable to the franchisor for loyalty program administration, points redemption funding, and member benefits. Tracked in Salesforce CRM loyalty management and SAP S/4HANA.']
  management_fee_base_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Base management fee as a percentage of total gross revenue payable to the management company under a management contract agreement. Applicable only when agreement_type is management_contract. Reported under USALI management fee accounting.']
  management_fee_incentive_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Incentive management fee as a percentage of GOP (Gross Operating Profit) or NOI (Net Operating Income) payable to the management company when performance thresholds are exceeded. Applicable for management contract agreements. Aligns with USALI GOP reporting.']
  marketing_fee_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of gross room revenue payable to the franchisor\'s brand marketing fund. Covers national and global advertising, loyalty program marketing, and brand awareness campaigns. Reported under USALI franchise cost accounting.']
  next_brand_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next brand standards quality assurance audit. Supports proactive compliance management and operational readiness planning.']
  pip_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved CapEx (Capital Expenditure) budget for PIP (Property Improvement Plan) obligations under this agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Tracked in SAP S/4HANA asset management and reported under USALI FF&E accounting.']
  pip_completion_date date [note: 'Contractually required completion date for all PIP (Property Improvement Plan) obligations specified in the franchise or management contract agreement. Null if pip_required is false. Tracks CapEx compliance milestones.']
  pip_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIP (Property Improvement Plan) is mandated as a condition of this franchise or management contract agreement. PIP obligations are typically triggered at agreement signing, renewal, or brand standard compliance review.']
  pms_system_required varchar [note: 'Name or code of the Property Management System (PMS) mandated by the franchisor as a brand standard technology requirement under this agreement (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS). Supports technology compliance tracking and integration planning.']
  quality_assurance_score_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum quality assurance (QA) inspection score the property must maintain to remain in compliance with brand standards under this agreement. Failure to meet this threshold may trigger a cure period or termination clause.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by the franchisee to exercise a renewal option before the agreement expiration date. Supports portfolio renewal pipeline management and prevents inadvertent agreement lapse.']
  renewal_option_count varchar [note: 'Number of renewal option periods available to the franchisee or property owner upon expiration of the initial term. Supports portfolio renewal pipeline management and long-term asset planning.']
  renewal_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of each renewal option period as specified in the agreement. Combined with renewal_option_count to determine maximum potential agreement duration for portfolio planning.']
  reservation_fee_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of gross room revenue or per-reservation fee payable to the franchisor for CRS (Central Reservation System) and GDS (Global Distribution System) distribution services. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS fee structures.']
  royalty_fee_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of gross room revenue payable to the franchisor as a royalty fee under the franchise agreement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Core financial obligation tracked in SAP S/4HANA AP and reported under USALI franchise cost accounting.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Null for active or pending agreements. Distinct from expiration_date (scheduled end) — this captures early or involuntary termination events.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by either party to invoke termination of the agreement without cause. Supports legal compliance tracking and portfolio risk management.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for agreement termination. Used for portfolio analytics, brand exit tracking, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mutual_agreement|franchisee_default|franchisor_default|brand_exit|sale_of_property|force_majeure|non_renewal — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the exclusive or protected geographic territory granted under this agreement, if applicable. May reference radius in miles/kilometers, zip codes, or defined market boundaries. Null if exclusive_territory is false.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this franchise agreement record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture, data lineage, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Records of franchise and management contract agreements governing each property\'s brand affiliation and operational model. Captures franchisor entity, franchisee entity, agreement type (franchise, management contract, lease, owner-operated), effective date, expiration date, renewal terms, royalty fee structure, PIP obligations, brand standard compliance requirements, and termination clauses. Supports portfolio governance and brand compliance management.'
}

Table property.pip_plan {
  pip_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Property Improvement Plan record. Primary key for all capital improvement tracking.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each PIP plan is budgeted in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: A PIP plan CAN be triggered by a franchise agreement requirement (brand-mandated property improvement). This FK will be nullable - PIPs can be triggered by other events (renovation, compliance, capita']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property subject to this improvement plan.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the project was completed and all deliverables were accepted, used for performance tracking and variance analysis.']
  actual_spend_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual capital expenditure incurred on the project to date in USD, tracked for budget variance analysis.']
  ada_compliance_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project scope includes ADA accessibility improvements or compliance upgrades.']
  approved_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Formally approved capital budget for the project in USD, authorized by ownership or executive management.']
  brand_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the property\'s compliance with brand standards relative to this PIP: compliant (meets standards), non-compliant (deficiencies exist), pending-review (under brand evaluation), waived (exemption granted), or not-applicable (non-branded property).']
  brand_standard_compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the property must complete the PIP to maintain brand compliance and avoid penalties or franchise termination.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current percentage of project completion (0.00 to 100.00), used for progress tracking and earned value analysis.']
  contractor_license_number varchar [note: 'State or local license number of the general contractor, required for regulatory compliance and vendor management.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary general contractor or construction firm executing the capital improvement work.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PIP plan record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  expected_roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment percentage for the capital improvement project, calculated from expected revenue lift and cost savings against total CapEx.']
  ffe_actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditure on furniture, fixtures, and equipment to date, used for budget variance tracking.']
  ffe_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget specifically for furniture, fixtures, and equipment purchases as part of the capital improvement project, tracked separately from construction costs.']
  fire_safety_upgrade_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project scope includes fire safety system upgrades or compliance work.']
  impacted_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative of physical areas affected by the project, such as guest rooms, lobby, F&B outlets, meeting spaces, back-of-house, or exterior.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this PIP plan record, used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PIP plan record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional project notes, special conditions, risk factors, or stakeholder comments.']
  opex_impact_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated change in annual operating expenses (positive or negative) resulting from the capital improvement, used for ROI and payback period calculations.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Estimated number of months required to recover the capital investment through incremental revenue and cost savings.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the construction permit expires, requiring renewal if work extends beyond this date.']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date the construction permit was issued by local regulatory authorities.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Building or construction permit number issued by local authorities for the capital improvement work.']
  project_manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the project manager for communication and coordination.']
  project_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for managing and coordinating the capital improvement project.']
  project_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the project manager.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the improvement project for reporting and communication purposes.']
  project_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the capital improvement project, used across finance, operations, and brand compliance systems.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capital improvement project: draft (planning), approved (authorized), in-progress (active work), on-hold (temporarily suspended), completed (finished), cancelled (terminated), or waived (brand exemption granted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|waived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator for the kind of capital improvement project: brand-mandated PIP, owner-initiated renovation, infrastructure upgrade, technology upgrade, or regulatory compliance project.']
  projected_completion_date date [note: 'Estimated date for project completion, used for operational planning and room inventory availability forecasting.']
  revenue_displacement_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total revenue loss in USD due to rooms out of service and operational disruption during the project, critical for financial impact analysis and GOP forecasting.']
  rooms_out_of_service_count varchar [note: 'Number of guest rooms temporarily unavailable during the project execution, critical for revenue displacement forecasting and occupancy planning.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for project commencement, used for resource planning and revenue displacement forecasting.']
  scope_of_work_summary varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the planned capital improvements, including areas, systems, and deliverables covered by the project.']
  sustainability_certification_target varchar [note: 'Target environmental or sustainability certification the project aims to achieve (e.g., LEED, Green Key, EarthCheck), if applicable.']
  total_estimated_capex decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial estimated total capital expenditure for the project in USD, used for budgeting and financial planning.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'Business event or condition that initiated the PIP or capital project, such as ownership transition, periodic brand review, guest satisfaction decline, or regulatory mandate.']

  Note: 'Property Improvement Plan (PIP) and capital project records for each property, serving as the single source of truth for ALL capital improvement work. Covers brand-mandated PIPs triggered by ownership transitions or periodic brand reviews, owner-initiated renovations (soft goods refresh, full renovation, infrastructure upgrade, technology upgrade), and infrastructure capital projects. Captures project reference number, project type discriminator (brand_pip, owner_renovation, infrastructure, technology), triggering event, total estimated CapEx, approved budget, actual spend to date, scope of work summary, impacted areas, rooms out of service, project manager, brand standard compliance deadline, scheduled start date, projected completion date, actual completion date, completion percentage, and project status (draft, approved, in-progress, completed, waived). Critical for brand compliance, CapEx planning, revenue displacement forecasting, and OpEx impact analysis. This is the SSOT for all capital improvement tracking — no separate renovation tracking exists.'
}

Table property.pip_item {
  pip_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line-item work scope record within a Property Improvement Plan (PIP). Primary key for this entity.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each PIP item is costed in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: PIP capital projects include F&B outlet renovations (kitchen equipment, dining room refresh, bar upgrades). Capital planning tracks outlet-specific PIP items for budget allocation and ROI analysis. St']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Links PIP items to physical delivery receipts for capital assets. Critical for project milestone tracking, installation scheduling coordination, asset capitalization timing (GAAP/IFRS compliance), and']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: A PIP item CAN target a specific meeting space (e.g., ballroom renovation, AV equipment upgrade). This FK will be nullable - most PIP items target other areas. No columns to remove - property_area is ']
  pip_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Property Improvement Plan under which this line-item work scope belongs. Links the item to its governing PIP document and approved CapEx budget.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: PIP capital improvement items frequently reference master procurement contracts for FF&E purchases. Enables contract compliance verification, negotiated pricing validation, and ensures PIP spending ad']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: A PIP item CAN target a specific facility (e.g., pool resurfacing, restaurant kitchen upgrade). This FK will be nullable - some PIP items are property-wide (e.g., roof replacement, HVAC system). No co']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property at which this PIP line-item work scope is being executed. Enables multi-property portfolio tracking of improvement spend.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Each PIP item generates specific purchase orders for capital asset procurement. Essential for tracking capex spend against approved budgets, monitoring delivery timelines for project scheduling, and r']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP S/4HANA vendor master record for the contractor responsible for this PIP item. Enables linkage to procurement contracts, insurance certificates, and payment terms.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which this PIP line item was fully completed, inspected, and accepted. Used for brand compliance verification, final cost reconciliation, asset capitalization triggering in SAP, and PIP progress reporting to ownership and brand.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual invoiced and posted cost in the property\'s functional currency for this PIP line item upon completion or at current spend date. Compared against estimated_cost for budget variance reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AP invoice postings against the PIP work order.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which work for this PIP line item commenced on-site. Compared against scheduled_start_date to identify schedule slippage and its downstream impact on brand compliance deadlines and hotel operations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the ownership, asset management, or corporate authority who approved the CapEx budget for this PIP item. Required for SOX financial controls documentation and CapEx authorization audit trail.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the CapEx budget for this PIP item was formally approved by the designated authority. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail and for establishing the authorized spend baseline.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA fixed asset number assigned to the asset created or replaced by this PIP item upon capitalization. Links the PIP line item to the property\'s fixed asset register for depreciation scheduling and asset lifecycle management.']
  brand_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PIP item is mandated by the brand franchisor as a condition of franchise agreement compliance (True) or is an ownership-initiated discretionary improvement (False). Brand-required items carry compliance deadlines and potential franchise termination risk if not completed.']
  capex_budget_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA internal order or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element code to which this PIP item\'s costs are allocated. Enables precise CapEx budget tracking, cost center assignment, and financial reporting per USALI and SOX requirements.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'The date by which this PIP item must be completed to satisfy brand franchisor compliance requirements. Failure to meet this deadline may result in franchise agreement penalties, brand standard deficiency notices, or franchise termination proceedings.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'The procurement contract or purchase order number in SAP S/4HANA governing the contractor\'s engagement for this PIP item. Used for invoice matching, lien waiver tracking, and contract compliance verification.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contractor or vendor responsible for executing the work scope for this PIP item. Used for contractor performance tracking, lien waiver management, and procurement compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this PIP item record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  deferral_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why this PIP item has been deferred beyond its original scheduled completion date. Required for brand franchisor approval of deferrals and for ownership reporting on PIP execution delays. Populated only when item_status is \'deferred\'.']
  deferred_completion_date date [note: 'The revised target completion date approved by the brand franchisor for a deferred PIP item. Replaces the original scheduled_completion_date as the binding compliance deadline when a deferral has been formally granted.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted or estimated cost in the property\'s functional currency for completing this PIP line item, as approved in the CapEx budget. Used for budget variance analysis, ownership reporting, and CapEx authorization tracking per USALI and SAP S/4HANA project accounting.']
  ffe_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the PIP item into the FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures and Equipment) taxonomy per USALI and brand standards. Determines CapEx vs OpEx treatment, depreciation schedule, and procurement channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: furniture|fixtures|equipment|soft_goods|technology|signage|artwork|building_infrastructure|os_and_e — promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA General Ledger account code for posting costs associated with this PIP item. Determines whether the expenditure is capitalized (balance sheet) or expensed (P&L) per USALI and IFRS/GAAP accounting treatment.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the brand quality assurance inspector conducted or is scheduled to conduct the formal inspection of this completed PIP item. Required for brand compliance certification and franchise agreement documentation.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the brand quality assurance inspection for this PIP item. A failed or conditional pass outcome triggers a punch list of remediation items that must be resolved before the item can be closed for brand compliance purposes.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PIP item requires a formal brand quality assurance inspection upon completion (True) before it can be marked as accepted and closed. Brand-mandated items typically require inspector sign-off as a condition of compliance certification.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific work scope for this PIP line item, including materials, finishes, quantities, and brand standard requirements (e.g., \'Replace all guestroom soft goods including headboards, drapery, and carpet per brand standard BS-2024-GR-07\').']
  item_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this PIP line item, typically matching the numbering scheme in the brand-issued PIP document or SAP work order. Used for cross-referencing with brand inspectors, contractors, and CapEx approval documentation.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this PIP line item within the execution workflow. Tracks progression from initial identification through approval, active construction, and final completion or deferral. Used for PIP compliance reporting to brand and ownership.']
  life_safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PIP item addresses a life safety, fire code, ADA accessibility, or OSHA regulatory compliance requirement (True). Life safety items are treated as critical priority and may not be deferred without regulatory authority approval.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Brand-assigned or ownership-assigned priority ranking for this PIP item. Critical items typically represent brand standard violations or life-safety issues requiring immediate remediation. Drives sequencing of work execution and resource allocation.']
  property_area varchar [note: 'The physical area or zone of the property affected by this PIP line item. Drives scheduling coordination with hotel operations to minimize guest impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guestroom|lobby|corridor|exterior|food_and_beverage|meeting_space|fitness_center|pool|back_of_house|spa|parking|landscaping — promote to reference product]']
  punch_list_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting outstanding deficiencies, remediation requirements, or conditional items identified during brand inspection or internal quality review. Captures specific corrective actions required before final acceptance of this PIP item.']
  quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'The quantity of units, rooms, fixtures, or items covered by this PIP line item\'s work scope (e.g., 120 guestrooms, 45 chairs, 3 elevators). Used for unit cost benchmarking, procurement quantity planning, and cost-per-unit analysis.']
  rooms_out_of_order varchar [note: 'The number of guestrooms placed out of order (OOO) in the PMS as a direct result of this PIP item\'s work scope. Critical input for RevPAR impact modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS and for coordinating room inventory availability in Oracle OPERA PMS during renovation.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'The planned date by which this PIP line item is expected to be fully completed and ready for inspection. Used for milestone tracking, brand compliance deadline management, and rooms-out-of-order (OOO) planning to minimize RevPAR impact.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned date on which work for this PIP line item is scheduled to commence. Used for construction phasing, contractor mobilization planning, and coordination with hotel operations to manage guest impact and room inventory availability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this PIP item record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Project System, Oracle OPERA PMS, manual entry). Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sustainability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PIP item contributes to the property\'s sustainability or energy efficiency program (True), such as LED lighting upgrades, low-flow plumbing fixtures, or HVAC efficiency improvements. Supports ESG reporting and green certification tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity field on this PIP item (e.g., each, room, sqft, sqm, linear_ft, lot). Required for accurate cost-per-unit benchmarking and procurement specification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this PIP item record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  work_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of work required for this PIP item. Distinguishes between full replacement, renovation/refresh, new installation, repair, technology upgrade, or regulatory compliance work. Drives cost estimation methodology and contractor selection.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item work scope records within a Property Improvement Plan (PIP). Captures item description, area of property affected (guestroom, lobby, F&B outlet, exterior), FF&E category, estimated cost, actual cost, responsible contractor, scheduled start date, scheduled completion date, actual completion date, and item status. Enables granular tracking of PIP execution progress and spend against approved CapEx budget.'
}

Table property.certification {
  certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the property certification or audit record.']
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.food_safety_inspection. Business justification: Property-level ISO 22000 certifications require supporting outlet-level inspection records. Certification audits trace property cert to individual outlet inspections. Regulatory compliance audit trail']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Brand certifications CAN be linked to their governing franchise agreements (e.g., Marriott brand standards certification tied to franchise agreement). This FK will be nullable - many certifications ar']
  permit_fee_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each certification permit fee is denominated in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. FK named permit_fee_currency_id to distinguish from potential other currency FKs. R']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property that holds this certification or was subject to this audit.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the higher-level accreditation body that accredits the issuing authority or audit firm (e.g., ANSI-ASQ National Accreditation Board, UKAS). Provides additional credibility and traceability.']
  audit_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the on-site audit or inspection was completed.']
  audit_section_scores varchar [note: 'Detailed breakdown of scores by audit section or domain (e.g., Front Desk: 92, Housekeeping: 88, F&B: 85). Stored as structured text or JSON for granular analysis.']
  audit_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the on-site audit or inspection began.']
  auditor_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party audit firm or consulting company that performed the audit or certification assessment.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor or inspector who conducted the audit or certification assessment.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The official certificate or audit report number issued by the certifying authority or audit firm.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Level or tier of the certification (e.g., LEED Certified, LEED Silver, LEED Gold, LEED Platinum; Green Key 1-5 stars). Indicates the degree of achievement or compliance.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Full name or title of the certification, accreditation, or audit program (e.g., LEED Gold, Green Key Eco-Rating, ISO 22000 Food Safety, ADA Compliance Certification).']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope or coverage of the certification or audit (e.g., entire property, F&B operations only, specific building or facility). Clarifies what areas or functions are covered.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification. Indicates whether the certification is active, has expired, is suspended, has been revoked, or is pending approval.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification or audit. Covers environmental certifications (LEED, Green Key), food safety (ISO 22000), accessibility (ADA), fire safety, health department permits, liquor licenses, and brand quality assurance audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: green_certification|food_safety|accessibility|fire_safety|health_permit|liquor_license|brand_qa_audit|safety_inspection|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context related to the certification or audit. May include auditor comments, property responses, or follow-up actions.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall compliance or audit score expressed as a percentage or numeric rating. Represents the property\'s performance against the certification or audit criteria.']
  corrective_action_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the corrective action plan (CAP) developed in response to audit findings. Links to the document or system tracking remediation activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system.']
  critical_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical deficiencies or non-conformances identified during the audit or inspection. Critical deficiencies require immediate corrective action.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate, audit report, or permit document stored in the document management system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification expires and must be renewed. Null for certifications with no expiration or for one-time audits.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification was granted or the audit was completed and the report was issued.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, government agency, or audit firm that issued the certification or conducted the audit (e.g., U.S. Green Building Council, local health department, brand corporate QA team).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  minor_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor deficiencies or observations noted during the audit. Minor deficiencies are recommended for improvement but are not critical.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next scheduled inspection or audit is due. Used for proactive compliance tracking and scheduling.']
  permit_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee paid to the issuing authority for the certification, permit, or license. Expressed in the property\'s local currency.']
  reaudit_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which a follow-up audit or re-inspection is scheduled to verify that corrective actions have been implemented and deficiencies resolved.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or administrative jurisdiction under which the certification or permit is issued (e.g., state, county, municipality). Relevant for health permits, liquor licenses, and fire safety inspections.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current renewal state of the certification. Indicates whether the certification is active, awaiting renewal, has expired, or has been successfully renewed.']

  Note: 'Records of property-level certifications, accreditations, regulatory compliance credentials, and brand quality assurance audit results. Covers green certifications (LEED, Green Key), food safety (ISO 22000), accessibility (ADA), fire safety, health department permits, liquor licenses, and brand QA audit scores. Captures certification/audit type, issuing authority or audit firm, certificate number, issue date, expiration date, renewal status, compliance score, audit section scores, critical deficiency count, corrective action plan reference, and re-audit scheduled date. Supports regulatory reporting, brand standard audits, and compliance tracking.'
}

Table property.ownership_entity {
  ownership_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ownership entity record. Primary key.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the ownership entity acquired its first property or entered the portfolio. Used for investment performance tracking, ROI calculations, and ownership tenure analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ownership entity record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership entity structure. REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) for publicly traded entities like Host Hotels or Park Hotels, private equity for PE-backed funds, individual for owner-operators, joint venture for multi-party ownership, institutional investor for pension funds or insurance companies, family office for high-net-worth family investment vehicles.']
  investment_vehicle_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific investment fund, REIT portfolio, or investment vehicle through which the entity holds properties. Used for portfolio segmentation and fund-level performance reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ownership entity record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the ownership entity as registered with governing authorities. Used for franchise agreements, NOI reporting, and legal documentation.']
  management_company_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the property management company or hotel management firm affiliated with or contracted by the ownership entity. Used to identify management relationships and operational oversight structures.']
  ownership_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional ownership details, special arrangements, joint venture terms, or other contextual information relevant to the ownership entity.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ownership entity within the portfolio. Active indicates current ownership, inactive for historical records, pending acquisition for entities in due diligence, divested for sold assets, under review for entities undergoing restructuring or compliance review.']
  portfolio_acquisition_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total acquisition cost in USD for all properties owned by this entity. Aggregated value used for ROI calculations, EBITDA analysis, and investment performance tracking.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the ownership entity contact. Used for financial reporting distribution, asset management updates, and franchise agreement communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for the ownership entity. Used for asset management communications, NOI reporting, and operational escalations.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the ownership entity contact. Used for urgent communications and operational escalations.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the legal registered address for the ownership entity. Used for legal correspondence, tax filings, and franchise agreement documentation.']
  registered_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the legal registered address (suite, floor, building) for the ownership entity. Optional field for additional address details.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City of the legal registered address for the ownership entity.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the legal registered address of the ownership entity (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the legal registered address for the ownership entity.']
  registered_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the legal registered address for the ownership entity. Used for state-level tax reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  reit_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ownership entity is subject to REIT regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., income distribution rules, asset composition tests). True for entities that must comply with REIT regulations.']
  sox_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ownership entity is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act financial controls and reporting requirements. True for publicly traded entities or entities with publicly traded parent companies.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN in USA) or equivalent tax identifier for the ownership entity. Used for tax reporting, franchise agreements, and financial compliance.']
  total_properties_owned varchar [note: 'Current count of properties owned by this entity within the portfolio. Used for portfolio concentration analysis and ownership diversification metrics.']

  Note: 'Master records for property ownership entities including REITs (e.g., Host Hotels, Park Hotels), private equity sponsors, individual owner-operators, and joint venture structures. Captures legal entity name, EIN/tax ID, entity type (REIT, PE fund, individual, JV), ownership percentage per property, acquisition date, acquisition price, investment vehicle, management company affiliation, primary contact, and registered address. Supports franchise agreement counterparty identification, NOI/FFO reporting to owners, asset management communications, and REIT compliance reporting.'
}

Table property.meeting_space {
  meeting_space_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the meeting space. Primary key.']
  cleaning_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to housekeeping.cleaning_standard. Business justification: Meeting spaces require event-specific cleaning protocols (pre-event setup cleaning, post-event breakdown cleaning, turnover between events). Banquet and conference services depend on this link to sche']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Meeting and event spaces are profit centers for MICE revenue tracking. Real hospitality operations attribute event revenue, catering, and AV charges to specific spaces for profitability analysis. Crit']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this meeting space is located.']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting space meets ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements including wheelchair access, accessible restrooms, and assistive listening systems.']
  adjacent_prefunction_space boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting space has an adjacent pre-function or foyer area for registration, breaks, or networking.']
  blackout_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the space can be fully darkened using blackout curtains or shades. Critical for presentations and AV-heavy events.']
  builtin_av_equipment varchar [note: 'Description of permanently installed AV equipment in the space (e.g., projector, screen, sound system, microphones, video conferencing). Comma-separated list.']
  capacity_banquet varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate in banquet-style seating (round tables with 8-10 guests per table).']
  capacity_classroom varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate in classroom-style seating (rows of tables and chairs facing front).']
  capacity_hollow_square varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate in hollow square configuration (tables arranged in square with open center).']
  capacity_reception varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate for standing reception or cocktail-style events.']
  capacity_theater varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate in theater-style seating (rows of chairs facing front).']
  capacity_u_shape varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the space can accommodate in U-shape seating configuration (tables arranged in U formation).']
  catering_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether food and beverage (F&B) catering is mandatory when booking this space. Common for ballrooms and premium spaces.']
  ceiling_height_feet decimal(5,2) [note: 'Height of the ceiling measured in feet. Important for AV equipment setup, lighting design, and event atmosphere.']
  climate_control_type varchar [note: 'Type of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) control available (individual thermostat, shared zone control, or none).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meeting space record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.']
  dedicated_wifi_bandwidth_mbps varchar [note: 'Dedicated internet bandwidth available for the meeting space measured in Mbps. Null if shared bandwidth or not applicable.']
  divisible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting space can be partitioned into smaller sections using airwalls or movable partitions. Enables flexible space utilization.']
  emergency_exit_count varchar [note: 'Number of emergency exits available in the meeting space. Required for fire safety compliance and event planning.']
  entrance_width_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Width of the main entrance door measured in inches. Important for accessibility compliance and equipment load-in.']
  fire_capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum occupancy allowed by local fire safety codes and building regulations. Must not be exceeded regardless of setup style.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Physical floor or level where the meeting space is located within the property (e.g., Ground Floor, Mezzanine, 2nd Floor, Rooftop).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meeting space record was last updated. Part of audit trail for change tracking.']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'Date when the meeting space last underwent renovation or significant refurbishment. Part of PIP (Property Improvement Plan) tracking.']
  loading_dock_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting space has direct or convenient access to a loading dock for equipment and material delivery.']
  meeting_space_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the meeting space. Determines availability for booking and event planning.']
  minimum_catering_spend decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum food and beverage spend required when booking this space. Null if no minimum or catering not required.']
  minimum_rental_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum number of hours the space must be rented for a single event. Used for revenue management and booking policies.']
  natural_light_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting space has windows or skylights providing natural daylight. Important for guest experience and event ambiance.']
  number_of_sections varchar [note: 'Number of separate sections the space can be divided into when partitions are deployed. Null if space is not divisible.']
  rental_rate_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for the meeting space rental. Actual rates are managed in rate tables and vary by season, day of week, and booking terms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this meeting space record originated (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Oracle OPERA PMS).']
  space_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the meeting space within the property. Used in PMS and event management systems for booking and BEO (Banquet Event Order) references.']
  space_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the meeting space as displayed to guests and event planners (e.g., Grand Ballroom, Executive Boardroom, Terrace Garden).']
  space_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the meeting space by functional type. Determines suitability for different event formats and MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ballroom|boardroom|breakout_room|conference_room|meeting_room|outdoor_terrace|pre_function_space — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total area of the meeting space measured in square feet. Critical for capacity planning and event layout design.']
  wifi_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether wireless internet connectivity is available in the meeting space. Essential for modern business events.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of meeting, conference, and event spaces within each property. Captures space name, space code, space type (ballroom, boardroom, breakout room, outdoor terrace), total square footage, ceiling height, maximum capacity by setup style (theater, classroom, banquet, reception, U-shape), natural light availability, AV equipment inventory, divisibility (whether space can be partitioned), and rental rate tier. Feeds the event domain for MICE sales and BEO management via Delphi by Amadeus.'
}

Table property.property_outlet {
  property_outlet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the property outlet. Primary key.']
  cleaning_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to housekeeping.cleaning_standard. Business justification: Restaurant and bar outlets require food-service-specific cleaning standards (health department compliance, grease trap maintenance, dining area sanitation). F&B operations depend on this link for heal']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each outlet prices in ONE currency (average_check_amount). Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Every F&B outlet has an assigned manager tracked for P&L accountability, labor scheduling, operational oversight, and guest service escalations. Replaces denormalized manager contact fields with prope']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Outlets operate as distinct organizational units with dedicated labor budgets, schedules, and cost centers. Required for labor cost allocation, schedule generation, and USALI departmental reporting. R']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where this outlet is located. Links to the property master data.']
  alcohol_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet is licensed to serve alcoholic beverages. True if alcohol service is permitted.']
  average_check_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average guest check amount for the outlet. Used for revenue forecasting and performance benchmarking. Expressed in property base currency.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the outlet was permanently closed. Null if the outlet is still operational or temporarily closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outlet record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cuisine_type varchar [note: 'The primary cuisine or culinary style offered by the outlet. Applicable to Food and Beverage (F&B) outlets. Examples: Italian, Asian Fusion, Steakhouse, Mediterranean, International.']
  delivery_service_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet offers delivery service to guest rooms or external locations. True if delivery is available.']
  dress_code varchar [note: 'Dress code policy enforced at the outlet. Communicates guest attire expectations and supports brand positioning.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Floor number where the outlet is located within the property. Used for wayfinding and facility management.']
  gratuity_policy varchar [note: 'Policy regarding gratuities (tips) at the outlet. Defines whether gratuity is included in pricing, optional, automatically added, or not accepted.']
  health_permit_number varchar [note: 'Health department permit number for food service operations. Required for Food and Beverage (F&B) outlets.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outlet record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major renovation or refurbishment of the outlet. Supports Property Improvement Plan (PIP) tracking and Furniture Fixtures and Equipment (FF&E) lifecycle management.']
  liquor_license_number varchar [note: 'Official liquor license number issued by the local regulatory authority. Required for outlets serving alcohol.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the outlet\'s physical location within the property (e.g., Lobby Level, Poolside, 5th Floor East Wing). Assists guests in finding the outlet.']
  loyalty_points_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchases at this outlet are eligible for loyalty program points accrual. True if loyalty points can be earned.']
  menu_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the outlet\'s current menu can be accessed. Applicable to Food and Beverage (F&B) outlets.']
  mobile_ordering_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet supports mobile ordering through the property\'s mobile application or third-party platforms. True if mobile ordering is available.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the outlet first opened for business. Used for historical tracking and anniversary celebrations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the outlet. Format varies by property but typically includes days of week and time ranges (e.g., Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM).']
  outdoor_seating_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet offers outdoor seating. True if outdoor seating is available.']
  outlet_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the outlet within the property. Used for operational reference and system integration.']
  outlet_name varchar [note: 'The business name of the outlet as displayed to guests and used in marketing materials.']
  outlet_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the outlet. Determines whether the outlet is available for guest use and revenue generation.']
  outlet_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outlet by its primary business function. Determines operational processes and revenue reporting categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restaurant|bar|lounge|cafe|spa|fitness_center|golf_course|retail_shop|pool_bar|room_service|banquet|conference_center|business_center|recreational_facility|other — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pos_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) system instance used by this outlet. Typically references Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS system configuration.']
  private_dining_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet offers private dining rooms or spaces for exclusive events. True if private dining is available.']
  reservation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance reservations are required or recommended for this outlet. True if reservations are mandatory or strongly encouraged.']
  revenue_center_code varchar [note: 'USALI-compliant revenue center code used for financial reporting and general ledger (GL) integration. Maps outlet revenue to the appropriate financial statement line item.']
  seasonal_close_date date [note: 'Annual date when the outlet closes for seasonal operation. Applicable only to seasonal outlets.']
  seasonal_open_date date [note: 'Annual date when the outlet opens for seasonal operation. Applicable only to seasonal outlets.']
  seasonal_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet operates on a seasonal schedule (e.g., summer only, winter only). True if seasonal.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests that can be seated simultaneously in the outlet. Used for reservation management and capacity planning.']
  service_charge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard service charge percentage applied to guest bills at this outlet. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.00 for 18%).']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total square footage of the outlet space. Used for space utilization analysis and capital expenditure (CapEx) planning.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Web address for the outlet\'s dedicated website or property page. Used for online marketing and guest information.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of revenue-generating outlets within each property including restaurants, bars, spas, golf courses, retail shops, and recreational facilities. Captures outlet name, outlet code, outlet type, cuisine type (for F&B), seating capacity, operating hours, POS system identifier (MICROS), revenue center code (USALI), and outlet status. Serves as the SSOT for outlet identity referenced by the foodandbeverage, finance, and event domains.'
}

Table property.gds_profile {
  gds_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the GDS/CRS property profile record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a property listing/distribution profile asset managed and distributed across GDS and CRS channels.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each GDS profile displays rates in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: GDS distribution requires detailed outlet content (cuisine, hours, dress code, reservation policy) for booking channel display. has_restaurant boolean is insufficient; link to outlet master data enabl']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent property master record that this GDS profile describes. Links the distribution profile to the physical hotel or resort asset in the property domain.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address of the property as displayed in GDS and OTA channel listings. Used by guests and travel agents for navigation and correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  amadeus_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier specific to the Amadeus GDS network. May differ from the Sabre GDS property code. Used for Amadeus channel distribution, rate loading, and availability display to travel agents on the Amadeus platform.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand identifier code as registered in the GDS and CRS systems. Identifies the hotel brand (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service) under which the property is marketed in distribution channels. Used for brand-level filtering in GDS searches.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The full marketing name of the hotel brand as displayed in GDS and OTA channels (e.g., \'Luxury Collection\', \'Marriott Hotels\', \'Hilton Garden Inn\'). Supports brand-level reporting and channel distribution analytics.']
  chain_code varchar [note: 'The hotel chain or brand code registered with the GDS networks identifying the parent brand or management company. Used by GDS systems to group properties under a common brand umbrella for travel agent searches and loyalty program linkage.']
  check_in_time timestamp [note: 'The standard guest check-in time for the property in HH:MM (24-hour) format as published in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Displayed to guests and travel agents during the booking process to set arrival expectations.']
  check_out_time timestamp [note: 'The standard guest check-out time for the property in HH:MM (24-hour) format as published in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Displayed to guests and travel agents during the booking process to set departure expectations.']
  city_code varchar [note: 'The IATA or GDS city/airport code associated with the property location (e.g., \'NYC\', \'LON\', \'PAR\'). Used by GDS systems to associate the property with a travel destination for search and booking purposes.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'The full city name of the property location as displayed in GDS and OTA channel listings. Supports geographic search and filtering in distribution channels.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the property location as registered in the GDS profile. Used for geographic filtering in GDS and OTA search results and for regulatory compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this GDS profile record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and profile lifecycle tracking.']
  crs_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier assigned within the Central Reservation System (CRS), specifically Sabre SynXis CRS. This code is the primary key used for rate distribution, inventory allocation, and booking transactions flowing through the CRS. Distinct from the GDS property code.']
  display_name varchar [note: 'The property name as it appears in GDS and CRS search results and booking screens. This is the consumer-facing name used by travel agents and OTA partners. May differ from the legal property name or internal operating name.']
  distribution_channel_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the primary distribution channel scope this profile record covers. \'gds\' = GDS-only profile (Sabre/Amadeus/Galileo); \'crs\' = CRS direct booking profile; \'ota\' = OTA partner profile; \'direct\' = brand.com direct booking; \'all\' = unified profile across all channels.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'The fax number for the property as listed in GDS channel profiles. Used by travel agents and corporate accounts for reservation confirmations and group booking correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  floor_count varchar [note: 'The total number of floors in the main hotel building as published in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Used by travel agents and guests for property orientation and accessibility planning.']
  galileo_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier specific to the Galileo/Travelport GDS network. Used for Travelport channel distribution, rate loading, and availability display to travel agents on the Galileo platform.']
  gds_property_code varchar [note: 'The unique property identifier assigned within the GDS network (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport). This code is used by travel agents and OTA partners to look up and book the property across GDS channels. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration.']
  has_airport_shuttle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property offers complimentary or paid airport shuttle service as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by travel agents and guests to filter properties by transportation amenities.']
  has_fitness_center boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property has a fitness center or gym as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by travel agents and guests to filter properties by wellness amenities. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS amenity configuration.']
  has_meeting_facilities boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property has meeting rooms or conference facilities (MICE — Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by corporate travel agents and event planners to filter properties for group and event bookings.']
  has_parking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property offers on-site parking (self-park or valet) as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by travel agents and guests to filter properties by parking availability.']
  has_pool boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property has a swimming pool as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by travel agents and guests to filter properties by recreational amenities. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS amenity configuration.']
  hero_image_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the primary hero/banner image displayed on GDS and OTA property listing pages. This is the main visual asset representing the property in channel distribution. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL conforming to OTA image specification standards.']
  is_ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property meets Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility requirements as published in GDS and OTA profiles. Used by travel agents and guests requiring accessible accommodations. Supports ADA compliance reporting.']
  is_pet_friendly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property accepts pets as published in GDS and OTA amenity flags. Used by travel agents and guests to filter properties by pet policy. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS amenity configuration.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization of this property profile from the Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration to the GDS networks and downstream distribution channels. Used to monitor data freshness and identify stale profiles requiring re-sync.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the property in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for map-based search and proximity filtering in GDS, OTA, and direct booking channels. Supports geo-analytics and competitive set mapping.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'The full marketing narrative for the property displayed on GDS detail screens and OTA property pages. Covers property overview, location highlights, accommodation types, dining, recreation, and meeting facilities. Supports channel distribution and OTA partnership management.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the property in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for map-based search and proximity filtering in GDS, OTA, and direct booking channels. Supports geo-analytics and competitive set mapping.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'The primary reservations or front desk phone number for the property as listed in GDS and OTA channels. Used by travel agents and guests to contact the property directly. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the property location as registered in the GDS profile. Used for geographic proximity searches in GDS and OTA platforms. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  profile_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this GDS/CRS property profile version became active and live for distribution across GDS and OTA channels. Used for profile versioning, content audit trails, and channel distribution management.']
  profile_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this GDS/CRS property profile version expires or is scheduled for deactivation. Nullable for open-ended active profiles. Used for profile lifecycle management and scheduled content refresh cycles.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GDS/CRS property profile. Controls whether the property is actively distributed and bookable across GDS channels. \'active\' = live and bookable; \'inactive\' = not distributed; \'suspended\' = temporarily removed from distribution; \'pending_review\' = awaiting content approval; \'draft\' = profile under construction.']
  property_category varchar [note: 'The GDS/OTA property category classification used to filter search results. Determines how the property is categorized in GDS and OTA search interfaces. Aligned with OTA (OpenTravel Alliance) property type codes.']
  reservation_email varchar [note: 'The reservations department email address for the property as listed in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Used by travel agents and corporate accounts for booking inquiries and group reservation correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'A concise marketing description of the property (typically 150–250 characters) displayed in GDS search result summaries and OTA listing previews. Highlights key selling points such as location, brand tier, and signature amenities.']
  star_rating decimal(2,1) [note: 'The official star rating of the property as displayed in GDS and OTA channels (e.g., 3.0, 4.0, 5.0). Sourced from recognized rating bodies or brand classification standards. Influences GDS search filtering and OTA ranking algorithms.']
  total_room_count varchar [note: 'The total number of guest rooms and suites at the property as published in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Used by travel agents for property selection and by revenue management for inventory benchmarking. Represents the principal quantitative fact (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
