  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this GDS profile record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Used to track content changes, monitor sync freshness, and support data quality audits.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'The official property website URL as listed in GDS and OTA channel profiles. Directs guests and travel agents to the brand.com direct booking page. Supports direct channel conversion and OTA partnership management.']

  Note: 'GDS (Global Distribution System) and CRS property profile records defining how each property is listed and distributed across Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, and direct booking channels. Captures GDS property code, chain code, CRS property code (Sabre SynXis), property display name, short description, long description, hero image URL, amenity flags, and last sync timestamp. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration. Supports channel distribution and OTA partnership management.'
}

Table property.media {
  media_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the property media asset record. Primary key.']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Media assets CAN be meeting-space-specific (e.g., ballroom photos for sales materials). This FK will be nullable - most media is property-level or facility-level. hotel_id remains for property-level m']
  parent_media_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on media (parent_media_id)']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Media assets CAN be facility-specific (e.g., pool photo, restaurant photo). This FK will be nullable - hero images and property-wide media are not facility-specific. hotel_id remains for property-leve']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property this media asset belongs to. Links to the property master data.']
  alt_text varchar [note: 'Descriptive alternative text for the media asset used for accessibility compliance (ADA) and SEO optimization.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the media asset received final approval for distribution to OTA (Online Travel Agency) and GDS (Global Distribution System) channels.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Brand compliance and quality approval status for distribution to external channels.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the media asset for channel distribution.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'Proportional relationship between width and height (e.g., 16:9, 4:3) used for responsive display optimization.']
  asset_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the media asset used across DAM (Digital Asset Management) system and distribution channels.']
  brand_website_approved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the media asset is approved for display on the brand.com direct booking website.']
  caption varchar [note: 'Marketing caption or description text displayed with the media asset on guest-facing channels.']
  capture_date date [note: 'Date when the photo or video was originally captured, used for content freshness tracking.']
  cdn_path varchar [note: 'CDN-optimized path for fast global delivery of the media asset to OTA (Online Travel Agency) and brand.com channels.']
  copyright_holder varchar [note: 'Legal entity or individual holding copyright ownership of the media asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the media asset record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Sequence number controlling the order in which media assets are displayed in galleries and listings.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the media asset is scheduled to be retired or removed from active distribution channels.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the media asset becomes active and available for distribution to channels.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Technical file format of the media asset (e.g., JPEG, PNG, MP4) used for compatibility validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: JPEG|PNG|GIF|MP4|MOV|WEBP|SVG|PDF — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the media asset as stored in the DAM system.']
  file_size_kb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the media file in kilobytes, used for performance optimization and storage management.']
  file_url varchar [note: 'Full URL path to the media asset file location for retrieval and display.']
  gds_approved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the media asset is approved for syndication to GDS channels (Amadeus, Sabre, Galileo).']
  is_hero_image boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this media asset is the primary hero image for the property used in search results and main property pages.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the media asset is seasonal content (e.g., holiday decorations, summer pool scenes) with limited display periods.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code for media assets with language-specific content (e.g., signage, menus in photos).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the media asset record was last modified, used for change tracking and synchronization across channels.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when the media asset metadata or file was last modified in the system.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the usage license for the media asset expires, requiring renewal or removal from channels.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Type of usage license governing how the media asset can be used and distributed.']
  media_category varchar [note: 'Functional area or space that the media asset depicts, used for organizing content by property feature. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_exterior|lobby|guestroom|suite|pool|fitness_center|restaurant|meeting_space|spa|amenity|event_space|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  media_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the media asset in the content management workflow.']
  media_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the media asset type used for content management and channel distribution.']
  ota_approved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the media asset is approved for syndication to OTA partner channels (Expedia, Booking.com, etc.).']
  photographer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the photographer or content creator who produced the media asset, used for attribution and rights management.']
  resolution_height varchar [note: 'Height of the media asset in pixels, used for quality control and channel distribution requirements.']
  resolution_width varchar [note: 'Width of the media asset in pixels, used for quality control and channel distribution requirements.']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Keywords associated with the media asset for search engine optimization and discoverability on brand.com and OTA platforms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or DAM platform from which the media asset record originated (e.g., Adobe Experience Manager, Bynder).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of descriptive tags for content categorization, search optimization, and channel filtering (e.g., luxury, family-friendly, beachfront).']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the thumbnail version of the media asset used for preview and gallery navigation.']
  upload_date date [note: 'Date when the media asset was uploaded to the DAM (Digital Asset Management) system.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the media asset has been viewed across all distribution channels, used for content performance analytics.']

  Note: 'Catalog of property-level media assets including hero images, gallery photos, virtual tours, floor plans, and video content used for channel distribution, OTA listings, and marketing. Captures media type, file URL, CDN path, alt text, display order, applicable area (exterior, lobby, guestroom, pool), resolution, last updated date, and channel approval status. Sourced from DAM (Digital Asset Management) system and syndicated to GDS, OTA, and brand.com channels.'
}

Table property.seasonal_calendar {
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the seasonal calendar entry. Primary key for the seasonal calendar product.']
  banquet_menu_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_menu_package. Business justification: Seasonal calendars drive banquet package pricing and availability. High-demand seasons trigger premium packages; low seasons activate promotional packages. Revenue management requires linking seasonal']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each seasonal calendar uses ONE currency for ADR/RevPAR estimates. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.']
  prior_seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on seasonal_calendar (prior_seasonal_calendar_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this seasonal calendar entry applies. Links to the property master data.']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days in advance that guests book for this seasonal period. Used for forecasting accuracy and inventory management. Based on historical booking patterns.']
  blackout_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this period is designated as a blackout date (e.g., Major Convention, Peak Holiday Demand, Special Event). Null if not a blackout period.']
  cancellation_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical cancellation rate for reservations during this seasonal period expressed as a percentage. Used for overbooking strategy and revenue forecasting.']
  climate_description varchar [note: 'Description of typical weather and climate conditions during this seasonal period (e.g., Warm and Sunny, Rainy Season, Snow Season, Hurricane Risk). Used for guest communication and operational planning.']
  competitive_set_position varchar [note: 'Property\'s expected market position relative to its STR competitive set during this seasonal period. Values: leader (outperforming), competitive (at par), lagging (underperforming), premium (high-end positioning), value (budget positioning).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this seasonal calendar entry was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  demand_classification varchar [note: 'Categorization of expected demand level during this seasonal period. Drives pricing strategy, staffing models, and inventory controls. Values: peak (high demand), shoulder (moderate demand), off-peak (low demand), super-peak (exceptional demand). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: peak|shoulder|off-peak|super-peak|low|moderate|high — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  end_date date [note: 'Last date of the seasonal period (inclusive). Defines the conclusion of the season for rate strategy and operational planning.']
  estimated_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Average Daily Rate for this seasonal period in the property\'s local currency. Used for revenue forecasting and rate strategy validation.']
  estimated_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted occupancy rate for this seasonal period expressed as a percentage. Used for revenue forecasting, staffing planning, and inventory management. Based on historical performance and market trends.']
  estimated_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Revenue Per Available Room for this seasonal period in the property\'s local currency. Key performance indicator for revenue management strategy validation.']
  facility_closures varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of facilities or outlets that are closed during this seasonal period (e.g., Outdoor Pool, Beach Bar, Tennis Courts). Used for guest communication and operational planning.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'Name of the holiday or special event if this is a holiday period (e.g., Christmas, New Year, Independence Day, Local Festival). Null if not a holiday period.']
  is_blackout_date boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is designated as a blackout date for promotional rates, loyalty redemptions, or discounted packages. Used to protect revenue during high-demand periods.']
  is_holiday_period boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this seasonal period includes major holidays or special events that drive exceptional demand. Used for premium pricing and staffing adjustments.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who most recently updated this seasonal calendar entry. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this seasonal calendar entry was most recently updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  market_segment_focus varchar [note: 'Primary market segment(s) targeted during this seasonal period (e.g., Leisure, Business, Group, MICE, FIT). Comma-separated if multiple segments. Drives marketing and sales strategy.']
  maximum_los_restriction varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for reservations during this seasonal period. Used to prevent long-stay bookings from blocking high-demand dates. Null if no maximum restriction applies.']
  minimum_los_restriction varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for reservations during this seasonal period. Used to optimize revenue during high-demand periods. Null if no minimum restriction applies.']
  no_show_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical no-show rate for reservations during this seasonal period expressed as a percentage. Used for overbooking strategy and revenue optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special considerations, or operational instructions for this seasonal period. Used for communication between revenue management, operations, and workforce teams.']
  operating_status varchar [note: 'Operational mode of the property during this seasonal period. Indicates whether full services are available or if there are seasonal closures or reduced operations.']
  rate_season_code varchar [note: 'Rate season identifier used by the Revenue Management System (RMS) to apply appropriate Best Available Rate (BAR) levels and dynamic pricing rules. Maps to IDeaS G3 RMS rate season configuration.']
  reduced_operating_hours varchar [note: 'Description of any reduced operating hours for facilities or services during this seasonal period (e.g., Restaurant: Dinner Only, Spa: Weekends Only). Null if no reductions apply.']
  rgi_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Revenue Generation Index for this seasonal period. RGI measures property\'s RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set (100 = at par, >100 = outperforming, <100 = underperforming).']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the season (e.g., PEAK_SUM, SHOULDER_SPR, OFF_WIN). Used for rate plan mapping and operational planning.']
  season_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the seasonal period (e.g., Summer Peak Season, Winter Off-Peak, Holiday Super Peak). Human-readable label for reporting and communication.']
  season_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which this seasonal period applies. Supports year-over-year comparison and multi-year planning.']
  seasonal_calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this seasonal calendar entry. Values: active (in use), draft (being planned), archived (historical), cancelled (no longer applicable).']
  seasonal_hiring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional seasonal staff hiring is required for this period. Used by HR and workforce planning teams to initiate recruitment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this seasonal calendar entry originated (e.g., IDeaS G3 RMS, OPERA PMS, Manual Entry). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_events varchar [note: 'Description of major local or property-specific events occurring during this seasonal period that impact demand (e.g., Annual Music Festival, Marathon Weekend, Convention Center Trade Show). Null if no special events.']
  staffing_level varchar [note: 'Planned staffing level for this seasonal period. Drives workforce scheduling and seasonal hiring decisions. Values: full (standard staffing), reduced (lower than standard), minimal (skeleton crew), peak-augmented (additional seasonal staff).']
  start_date date [note: 'First date of the seasonal period (inclusive). Defines the beginning of the season for rate strategy and operational planning.']
  yoy_demand_trend varchar [note: 'Trend classification comparing this season\'s expected demand to the same period in the prior year. Used for strategic planning and market positioning. Values: increasing (demand up), stable (demand flat), decreasing (demand down), volatile (unpredictable), new-season (no historical data).']
  yoy_demand_variance_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage change in expected demand compared to the same seasonal period in the prior year. Positive values indicate growth, negative values indicate decline. Null for new seasons without historical data.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the revenue manager or system user who created this seasonal calendar entry. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Defines property-level seasonal periods and operating calendars that drive rate strategy, staffing models, and operational planning. Captures season name, start date, end date, demand classification (peak, shoulder, off-peak, super-peak), applicable rate season code, holiday indicator, blackout date flag, seasonal facility closures, reduced operating hours, and year-over-year demand trend. Used by revenue management (IDeaS G3 RMS) for rate strategy and BAR level setting, by workforce domain for labor scheduling and seasonal hiring, and by operations for outlet hours and facility availability. Each property maintains its own seasonal calendar reflecting local demand patterns, events, and climate.'
}

Table property.currency {
  currency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the currency record. Primary key.']
  base_currency_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on currency (base_currency_id)']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code for the primary country where this currency is the official legal tender (e.g., USA for USD, GBR for GBP). Used for geographic revenue analysis and Smith Travel Research (STR) market benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this currency record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance with Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) requirements for financial data.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 alphabetic currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). This is the standard international identifier for the currency used across all financial systems, property management systems, and revenue management platforms.']
  currency_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the currency (e.g., United States Dollar, Euro, British Pound Sterling, Japanese Yen). Used for display purposes in financial reports and guest-facing materials.']
  currency_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the currency in the system. Active currencies are available for all transactions; inactive currencies are retained for historical reporting; deprecated currencies are being phased out; historical currencies are no longer in circulation (e.g., legacy European currencies replaced by EUR); pending currencies are configured but not yet live.']
  decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for this currency in financial calculations and display. Most currencies use 2 (e.g., USD 100.50), some use 0 (e.g., JPY 1000), and a few use 3 (e.g., BHD 10.250). Critical for accurate revenue calculations, Average Daily Rate (ADR), and Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) reporting.']
  display_format varchar [note: 'The standard display format for this currency including symbol placement, decimal separator, and thousands separator (e.g., $1,234.56 for USD, 1.234,56 € for EUR). Used to ensure consistent presentation across guest-facing systems, reports, and Business Intelligence (BI) dashboards.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this currency configuration became or will become effective in the system. Used for managing currency transitions, redenominations, and historical accuracy in financial reporting.']
  erp_currency_code varchar [note: 'The currency code or identifier used in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, such as SAP S/4HANA. Used for financial consolidation, General Ledger (GL) posting, Accounts Payable (AP), and Accounts Receivable (AR) integration.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'The authoritative source or provider for exchange rate data for this currency (e.g., OANDA, XE.com, Bloomberg, Reuters, Central Bank). Used to ensure consistent exchange rate application across Central Reservation System (CRS), Revenue Management System (RMS), and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems.']
  exchange_rate_update_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which exchange rates are refreshed for this currency. Critical for accurate revenue recognition, dynamic pricing in Revenue Management System (RMS), and financial consolidation under International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this currency configuration expires or is no longer valid for new transactions. Nullable for currencies with indefinite validity. Used for managing currency transitions and ensuring historical data integrity.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is currently active and available for use in transactions, reservations, and financial operations. Inactive currencies are retained for historical reporting but cannot be used for new transactions.']
  is_base_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is the base currency for financial consolidation and corporate reporting. Typically one currency per enterprise (e.g., USD for US-headquartered companies). Used for Gross Operating Profit (GOP), Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA), and consolidated financial statements under Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (USALI).']
  is_crypto_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a cryptocurrency (e.g., Bitcoin, Ethereum) rather than a traditional fiat currency. Used for special handling in payment processing, regulatory compliance, and financial reporting.']
  minor_unit varchar [note: 'The relationship between the major currency unit and its minor unit (e.g., 100 cents = 1 dollar). Defines the subdivision of the currency for precise financial calculations in Property Management System (PMS) and Point of Sale (POS) systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special handling instructions, or contextual information about this currency. May include information about currency restrictions, regulatory requirements, or system-specific configuration details.']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit ISO 4217 numeric currency code used in systems where alphabetic codes are not practical. Provides an alternative identifier for the currency in legacy systems and international payment processing.']
  payment_processing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is enabled for payment processing through Point of Sale (POS) systems, online booking engines, and payment gateways. Must comply with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) requirements.']
  pms_currency_code varchar [note: 'The currency code or identifier used in the Property Management System (PMS), such as Oracle OPERA. May differ from ISO 4217 code in legacy systems. Used for system integration and data mapping between PMS and enterprise data warehouse.']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'The rounding method to be applied when performing calculations with this currency. Standard follows mathematical rounding rules; up always rounds up; down always rounds down; nearest rounds to nearest value; bankers rounding (round half to even) minimizes cumulative rounding errors in large transaction volumes. Critical for accurate Average Daily Rate (ADR) and Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this currency record originated (e.g., OPERA PMS, SAP S/4HANA, Manual Entry). Used for data lineage tracking and Extract Transform Load (ETL) process monitoring.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Standard symbol used to represent the currency (e.g., $, €, £, ¥). Used in user interfaces, reports, and guest-facing displays for quick visual recognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this currency record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Reference master for currencies used across the global property portfolio, including ISO 4217 currency codes, currency names, decimal precision, exchange rate source configuration, and base currency designation for financial consolidation.'
}

Table property.vendor_agreement {
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this property-vendor agreement record. Primary key.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the hotel property that has negotiated terms with this vendor']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor providing goods or services under this agreement']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the formal contract or service agreement governing this property-vendor relationship. Links to contract management systems for compliance tracking.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this property-vendor agreement record was created in the system.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard number of days between order placement and delivery for this vendor to this property. Used for inventory planning and order scheduling.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that last modified this property-vendor agreement record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this property-vendor agreement record was last updated.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent formal performance review conducted by the property for this vendor. Used to schedule periodic vendor evaluations.']
  local_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address for the local vendor representative servicing this property. Used for order confirmations, delivery notifications, and operational communications.']
  local_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the vendor representative assigned to service this specific property. May differ from the vendors primary contact in the vendor master record.']
  local_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The direct phone number for the local vendor representative servicing this property. Used for urgent order inquiries, delivery coordination, and issue escalation.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum order value required by this vendor for deliveries to this property. Used to enforce ordering thresholds and optimize procurement batching.']
  payment_terms_override varchar [note: 'Property-specific payment terms that override the vendors standard payment terms. Examples: Net 30, Net 45, 2/10 Net 30. Null if standard vendor payment terms apply.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'The property-specific performance rating for this vendor on a 1.00 to 5.00 scale. Based on delivery timeliness, product quality, service responsiveness, and invoice accuracy. Used for vendor evaluation and sourcing decisions.']
  preferred_vendor_tier varchar [note: 'The vendor preference tier assigned to this vendor for this specific property. Indicates procurement priority and influences ordering workflows. Values: Preferred, Standard, Approved, Conditional.']
  property_specific_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The negotiated discount percentage that this vendor provides to this specific property, which may differ from corporate-level or other property-level agreements. Used for invoice validation and cost analysis.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this property-vendor relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active relationships. Used for historical analysis and vendor transition planning.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this property began procuring from this vendor under the current agreement terms. Used for relationship tenure analysis and contract renewal planning.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this property-vendor relationship. Governs whether procurement orders can be placed. Values: Active, Suspended, Under Review, Terminated, Pending Activation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that created this property-vendor agreement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between property and vendor. It captures the negotiated commercial terms, service-level agreements, and relationship parameters that govern procurement relationships between individual hotel properties and their suppliers. Each record links one property to one vendor with property-specific pricing, delivery terms, payment conditions, and local contact arrangements that exist only in the context of this specific property-vendor relationship.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality operations, each hotel property maintains procurement relationships with multiple vendors across different supply categories (food & beverage suppliers, FF&E vendors, housekeeping suppliers, maintenance contractors), and each vendor serves multiple properties across the portfolio. Each property-vendor relationship has negotiated commercial terms including property-specific discount rates, delivery lead times, minimum order values, payment term overrides, and dedicated local vendor contacts that vary by property. The business actively manages these relationships for vendor performance evaluation, contract compliance monitoring, and procurement optimization.'
}

Table property.property_space_allocation {
  property_space_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for property_space_allocation']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the event booking receiving the space allocation']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the meeting space being allocated']
  space_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this space allocation record. Primary key.']
  allocation_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this space allocation was created in the system. Used for audit trail and booking history.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The specific date this meeting space is allocated to the event. Critical for multi-day events using the same or different spaces across days.']
  allocation_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this space allocation was last modified. Tracks changes to timing, setup, or status.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this space allocation. Tracks progression from tentative reservation through setup, event execution, and breakdown. Critical for operations coordination.']
  end_time varchar [note: 'The time when the meeting space allocation ends on the allocation date. Used for scheduling and calculating rental duration.']
  guaranteed_attendance varchar [note: 'The number of attendees guaranteed for this specific space allocation. Used for capacity validation, F&B planning, and billing. May differ from overall event attendance if event uses multiple concurrent breakout spaces.']
  rental_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'The rental fee charged for this specific space allocation. Calculated based on space rental_rate_tier, duration, and negotiated event terms. Each allocation may have different charges.']
  setup_style varchar [note: 'The seating/table configuration requested for this specific allocation. Determines capacity and drives BEO setup instructions. Same space may have different setups for different event sessions.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific requests or requirements for this space allocation (e.g., additional AV equipment, special lighting, temperature preferences, accessibility accommodations).']
  start_time varchar [note: 'The time when the meeting space allocation begins on the allocation date. Used for scheduling and avoiding double-booking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation of a specific meeting space to an event booking for a defined time period. It captures the operational assignment of physical function space to MICE events, including setup configuration, timing, attendance guarantees, and rental charges. Each record links one meeting_space to one event_booking with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific space-time allocation.. Existence Justification: In MICE operations, a single multi-day conference event booking routinely requires multiple meeting spaces (e.g., main ballroom for general sessions, multiple breakout rooms for concurrent workshops, boardroom for VIP meetings). Conversely, a high-demand meeting space hosts multiple different event bookings across days and time slots. The allocation itself is an operational entity that event coordinators actively create, modify, and track, with specific attributes like time slots, setup configurations, guaranteed attendance per space, and individual rental charges that belong to neither the space nor the event alone.'
}

Table property.channel_connection {
  channel_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each property-channel connection record']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution channel through which the property sells inventory']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the property that is connected to this distribution channel']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific property was activated on this specific distribution channel and began accepting bookings through it']
  commission_rate_override decimal(5,2) [note: 'Property-specific commission rate override for this channel, expressed as a percentage. Overrides the channel\'s standard commission_rate_pct when negotiated at the property level. Null if standard channel rate applies.']
  connectivity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the connectivity between this property and this channel. Tracks whether the technical integration is live, suspended, or experiencing issues.']
  content_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last content update (room descriptions, images, amenities) pushed from this property to this channel. Used to track content freshness and synchronization status.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document number for the commercial contract governing this property-channel relationship. Links to the legal agreement that defines terms, commission rates, and obligations.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property-channel connection record was first created in the system']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific property was deactivated or disconnected from this specific distribution channel. Null for active connections.']
  inventory_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which room inventory is allocated to this channel for this specific property. May differ from the channel\'s default allocation method based on property-level negotiations or technical constraints.']
  last_booking_received timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent booking received from this channel for this property. Used to monitor channel performance and identify inactive connections.']
  rate_loading_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical protocol used to load rates and availability from the property PMS/CRS to this specific channel. May vary by property-channel combination based on technical capabilities and contractual agreements.']
  rate_parity_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific property has negotiated an exception to the channel\'s standard rate parity requirements. True if property is exempt from rate parity obligations for this channel.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property-channel connection record was last modified']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between property and channel. It captures the commercial and technical relationship governing how a specific property distributes inventory through a specific distribution channel. Each record links one property to one channel with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship: activation dates, connectivity configuration, commission overrides, rate loading protocols, and inventory allocation rules.. Existence Justification: In the travel hospitality industry, properties distribute inventory through multiple distribution channels simultaneously (GDS networks, OTA platforms, direct web, wholesalers), and each channel serves multiple properties across the portfolio. The business actively manages these property-channel connections as operational entities with specific configuration, activation dates, connectivity protocols, and property-specific commission overrides that cannot be stored on either the property or channel master records alone.'
}

Table property.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on legal_entity (parent_legal_entity_id)']

  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for business credit and identification purposes.']
  entity_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification of entity structure (e.g., S-Corp, C-Corp, LLP, GP, LP) specific to jurisdiction.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was most recently updated in the system.']

  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the legal entity indicating whether it is actively conducting business, dormant, suspended, or dissolved.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership held by the parent entity, if applicable. Represents equity stake in subsidiary relationships.']
  parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent legal entity if this entity is a subsidiary or controlled entity within a corporate hierarchy.']
  primary_business_activity varchar [note: 'Textual description of the main business activities and operations conducted by the legal entity.']
  primary_business_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the primary business operating address, which may differ from the registered address.']
  primary_business_city varchar [note: 'City of the primary business operating address.']
  primary_business_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the primary business operating country.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official correspondence with the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for contacting the legal entity for business purposes.']
  registered_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address of the legal entity as filed with the incorporation authority.']
  registered_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered address (suite, floor, building name) if applicable.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered address.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the registered address country.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered address.']
  registered_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the registered address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued registration or incorporation number assigned to the legal entity by the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the primary government or regulatory body that oversees this legal entity\'s operations and compliance.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code used for financial reporting by this legal entity.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the primary industry of the legal entity according to SIC standards.']
  stock_exchange_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary stock exchange where the entity\'s securities are listed (e.g., NYSE, NASDAQ, LSE), if publicly traded.']
  stock_ticker_symbol varchar [note: 'The ticker symbol under which the entity\'s stock is traded, if publicly traded.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number assigned by the tax authority in the primary jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU).']
  ultimate_parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the top-level parent entity in the corporate ownership structure, representing the ultimate controlling entity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for legal_entity. Referenced by legal_entity_id.'
}

Table property.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']

  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the party\'s primary physical address, typically containing suite, floor, building, or apartment information.']
  annual_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reported annual revenue of the party in the preferred currency, used for financial assessment and relationship management.']
  city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality where the party is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the party is located or registered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this party record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating or creditworthiness assessment of the party, typically provided by credit rating agencies or internal risk assessment.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this party record becomes effective and valid for business operations, supporting temporal data management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this party record remains effective and valid. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'The total number of employees working for the party organization, used for sizing and segmentation analysis.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for document transmission, if applicable. Still used in some jurisdictions for legal and compliance documentation.']
  incorporation_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the party is legally incorporated or registered, which may differ from the operational country.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'The date on which the party was legally incorporated or registered with governing authorities. For individuals, this may represent the date of business registration.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code identifying the primary business sector or industry of the party, typically using NAICS or SIC codes.']
  is_publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the party is a publicly traded company listed on a stock exchange.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the party is exempt from certain taxes based on legal status (e.g., non-profit, government entity).']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the party as registered with governing authorities. For individuals, this is the full legal name; for organizations, this is the registered business name.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this party record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional information, special instructions, or contextual details about the party that do not fit structured fields.']
  parent_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent party in a corporate hierarchy, enabling representation of subsidiaries, divisions, and organizational structures.']
  party_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code assigned to the party for operational reference and integration with external systems.']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party indicating whether the party is actively engaged in business, temporarily suspended, pending approval, or dissolved.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party entity indicating whether it represents an individual person, corporate organization, government entity, vendor, partner, or franchise operator.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days allowed for payment settlement with this party, used for accounts payable and receivable management.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code for the party\'s address, used for mail delivery and geographic segmentation.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the preferred currency for financial transactions with this party.']
  preferred_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the preferred language for communication with this party.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contact person at the party organization for business communications and relationship management.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'The job title or role of the primary contact person at the party organization.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for official communication with the party. This is the main contact channel for digital correspondence.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'The primary telephone number for voice communication with the party. Includes country code and area code where applicable.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction where the party is registered. For organizations, this is the company registration number; for individuals operating as sole proprietors, this may be a business license number.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'The nature of the business relationship between this party and the organization, such as property owner, franchise operator, vendor, strategic partner, contractor, or agent.']
  secondary_phone varchar [note: 'An alternate telephone number for the party, used as a backup contact method or for specific departments.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the party is located.']
  stock_ticker_symbol varchar [note: 'The stock exchange ticker symbol for the party if publicly traded, used for financial market identification.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the party. For US entities, this may be an EIN (Employer Identification Number) or SSN (Social Security Number); for international entities, the equivalent national tax identifier.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The time zone in which the party primarily operates, using IANA time zone database naming convention (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'The commercial or trade name under which the party conducts business, if different from the legal name. Also known as DBA (Doing Business As) name.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'The official website URL of the party, used for public information and digital presence.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by operator_party_id.'
}

Table inventory.room_type {
  room_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for room_type']
  ada_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_assessment. Business justification: ADA assessments evaluate room type categories for accessibility standards (suite vs. standard room requirements). Links room type to assessment for compliance certification and accessible room invento']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Marketing campaigns routinely target specific room types for promotions (suite sales, standard room flash deals). Campaign planning, offer eligibility rules, and performance attribution by room catego']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Offers frequently restrict eligibility to specific room types (suites only, exclude standard rooms). While campaign_offer has eligible_room_types text field, proper FK enables booking validation, inve']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this room type is defined. Room types are property-specific configurations.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of accessibility features available in this room type. Examples: roll-in shower, grab bars, visual alarm, hearing kit, lowered fixtures. Used for guest matching and compliance tracking.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the room type. Inactive types are not available for new reservations. Seasonal types are active only during specific periods. Renovation types are temporarily unavailable.']
  bathroom_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of bathroom setup. Examples: single full bath, 1.5 bath, dual vanity, separate tub and shower. Used for guest expectations and competitive positioning.']
  bed_count varchar [note: 'Number of beds in the room type. Used for occupancy calculations and guest preference matching.']
  bed_type varchar [note: 'Primary bed configuration for the room type. Critical for guest preference matching and inventory allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double|twin|california_king|murphy|sofa_bed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this room type record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this room type configuration expires or is replaced. Null for currently active configurations. Used for historical tracking and renovation planning.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this room type configuration becomes effective. Used for managing room type changes, renovations, and new inventory rollouts.']
  floor_level_range varchar [note: 'Typical floor range where this room type is located. Examples: 1-5, 6-10, 15-20. Used for guest preference and accessibility planning.']
  is_ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type meets ADA accessibility requirements including mobility, hearing, and visual accommodations. Required for compliance reporting and guest accessibility requests.']
  is_connecting_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type can be configured as a connecting room. Used for family and group booking allocation.']
  is_suite boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type is classified as a suite with separate living and sleeping areas. Used for inventory segmentation and revenue reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this room type record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across PMS, RMS, and CRS systems.']
  lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type participates in Last Room Availability agreements with OTA partners. Critical for channel distribution and rate parity management.']
  max_occupancy varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests allowed in this room type per fire code and property standards. Used for reservation validation and yield optimization.']
  overbooking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type can be overbooked as part of revenue management strategy. Used by RMS for yield optimization.']
  pseudo_room_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a pseudo room type used for inventory management purposes (e.g., house use, out of order, complimentary) rather than a sellable room type. Used for inventory controls and reporting exclusions.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Rate category classification used for revenue management and pricing strategy. Examples: BAR, Corporate, Government, Group, Promotional. Links to rate plan structures.']
  room_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the room type by service tier. Used for segmentation in revenue management and guest experience programs.']
  room_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of key room features and amenities. Examples: balcony, kitchenette, fireplace, whirlpool, separate shower, walk-in closet. Used for guest communication and OTA distribution.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the room type in PMS and RMS systems. Examples: KING, QQDBL, JSUITE, PRES. This is the operational identifier used across reservations, inventory, and revenue management.']
  room_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing description of the room type including amenities, features, and guest experience highlights. Used for guest-facing channels and OTA distribution.']
  room_type_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the room type as displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Deluxe King, Queen Queen Double, Junior Suite, Presidential Suite.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Property segment classification for this room type. Aligns with brand standards and service level expectations.']
  sellable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type is available for sale to guests. Non-sellable types include staff rooms, maintenance rooms, and owner units.']
  smoking_policy varchar [note: 'Smoking policy for this room type. Most properties are non-smoking; some maintain designated smoking inventory.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence for this room type in PMS screens, reports, and guest-facing channels. Lower numbers appear first.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the room type in square feet. Used for guest communication, competitive positioning, and property planning.']
  standard_occupancy varchar [note: 'Standard number of guests for this room type used for base rate calculations. Additional guests may incur extra person charges.']
  view_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the view from the room. Impacts pricing strategy and guest satisfaction. Used in revenue management for rate differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|city|mountain|garden|pool|courtyard|no_view|partial — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master catalog of room types defined per property, including physical configuration (bed type, bed count, view category, square footage), maximum occupancy, accessibility features, ADA compliance status, connecting room eligibility, and suite classification. Serves as the foundational classification entity for all inventory management — availability, controls, blocks, allotments, channel distribution, and demand forecasting all reference room type as their primary dimension. Defined and maintained in Oracle OPERA PMS across luxury, premium, and select-service segments.'
}

Table inventory.room {
  room_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical room. Primary key. Authoritative SSOT for physical room identity in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  ada_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_assessment. Business justification: ADA assessments evaluate individual rooms for accessibility compliance (barrier identification, remediation tracking). Links room to its most recent assessment for compliance status reporting and reme']
  connecting_room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the adjacent room that connects to this room via connecting door. Null if no connecting room.']
  fire_safety_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.fire_safety_record. Business justification: Fire safety inspections evaluate individual rooms for suppression systems, alarms, extinguishers, evacuation routes. Links room to fire safety record for compliance certification, insurance requiremen']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) where this room is located.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the room type classification (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite, Executive Double) assigned to this room. Determines rate category and inventory grouping.']
  ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the room meets ADA accessibility standards including wheelchair access, grab bars, visual alarms, and accessible bathroom fixtures.']
  balcony_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the room has a private balcony or terrace. Premium feature used for rate differentiation.']
  bed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of beds in the room. Used for occupancy capacity and guest preferences.']
  bed_type varchar [note: 'Primary bed configuration in the room. Critical for guest preferences and room assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double|twin|california_king|murphy|sofa_bed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  building_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the building or tower within a multi-building property. Null for single-building properties.']
  connecting_room_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room has a connecting door to an adjacent room. Used for family bookings and group assignments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this room record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional room features and amenities (e.g., minibar, safe, coffee maker, smart TV, work desk). Used for guest preference matching and marketing.']
  ffe_condition_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Condition assessment score for room furniture, fixtures, and equipment on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Used for CapEx planning and PIP prioritization.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'The floor level where the room is located within the building. Used for housekeeping routing and guest preferences.']
  front_office_status varchar [note: 'Current front office occupancy state. Indicates whether the room is vacant, occupied by a guest, reserved for arrival, or blocked.']
  housekeeping_status varchar [note: 'Current housekeeping state of the room. Updated by housekeeping staff and used for room assignment and availability.']
  kitchenette_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the room includes a kitchenette with cooking facilities. Common in extended-stay properties.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this room record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent renovation or major refurbishment. Used for PIP (Property Improvement Plan) tracking and FF&E (Furniture Fixtures and Equipment) lifecycle management.']
  lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room is eligible for Last Room Availability distribution to OTA partners. Used for channel distribution controls.']
  max_occupancy varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests allowed in the room per fire code and property policy. Used for reservation validation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the room. Determines whether the room is available for sale and occupancy. Lifecycle status field.']
  out_of_order_end_date date [note: 'Expected date when the room will return to service. Used for inventory forecasting and revenue management.']
  out_of_order_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for why the room is out of order. Null when room is in service. Used for maintenance tracking and OCC impact analysis.']
  out_of_order_start_date date [note: 'Date when the room was taken out of order. Used for revenue impact analysis and maintenance duration tracking.']
  overbooking_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room can be included in overbooking calculations. Used by RMS for yield optimization.']
  room_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known room number displayed on the door and used for guest communication. Business identifier for the room.']
  smoking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether smoking is permitted in the room. Used for guest preference matching and inventory segmentation.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total interior square footage of the room. Used for marketing, rate positioning, and guest selection criteria.']
  view_type varchar [note: 'Type of view from the room windows. Impacts rate positioning and guest satisfaction. Used for upsell opportunities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|city|mountain|garden|pool|courtyard|no_view — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wing_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the wing or section within a building. Used for operational routing and guest location preferences.']

  Note: 'Physical room master record representing each individual guestroom or suite at a property. Captures room number, floor, wing, building, room type assignment, physical attributes (square footage, bed configuration, connecting rooms), ADA accessibility status, smoking designation, view type, and current operational status. The authoritative SSOT for physical room identity in Oracle OPERA PMS.'
}

Table inventory.room_status {
  room_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for room_status']
  attendant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the housekeeping staff member currently assigned to clean or service this room.']
  guest_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Group room block management requires tracking which rooms are assigned to which blocks for rooming list fulfillment, pickup tracking, and block-specific housekeeping instructions. Front desk and house']
  health_safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.health_safety_incident. Business justification: Health/safety incidents trigger room status changes (blocked, out of order, requires inspection). Links status to incident for incident response workflow, housekeeping coordination, and guest relocati']
  primary_room_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the housekeeping supervisor or inspector who performed the quality inspection of the room.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest currently occupying the room. Null if room is vacant.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property where the room is located. Links to the property master data.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active reservation currently occupying the room. Null if room is vacant.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical room. Links to the room inventory master data.']
  room_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user (staff member) who last updated the room status.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the room is administratively blocked from sale (e.g., VIP hold, renovation, inventory control, group block).']
  cleaning_type varchar [note: 'Type of cleaning service required or last performed. Checkout: full cleaning after guest departure; Stayover: daily service for occupied room; Deep Clean: comprehensive periodic cleaning; Turndown: evening service; Refresh: light touch-up.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this room status record was first created in the system.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating a mismatch between front desk status and housekeeping status (e.g., front desk shows vacant but housekeeping shows occupied). Requires investigation.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature of any status discrepancy and actions taken to resolve it.']
  do_not_disturb_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the guest has activated the do not disturb indicator, preventing housekeeping and other staff from entering.']
  expected_checkin_date date [note: 'The scheduled check-in date for the next arriving guest. Used for due-in planning and housekeeping prioritization.']
  expected_checkout_date date [note: 'The scheduled checkout date for the current guest occupying the room. Used for due-out planning.']
  front_desk_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status from the front desk perspective. Vacant: unoccupied and available; Occupied: guest currently in residence; Reserved: booked for future arrival; Due Out: guest scheduled to check out today; Due In: guest scheduled to check in today; Out of Order: room unavailable for sale.']
  housekeeping_status varchar [note: 'Current housekeeping state of the room indicating cleanliness and readiness. Dirty: needs full cleaning; Clean: cleaned but not inspected; Inspected: cleaned and quality-checked; Pickup: minor refresh needed; Out of Service: maintenance required; Do Not Disturb: guest requested no service.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the room is administratively blocked from sale (e.g., for renovation, VIP hold, or inventory control).']
  is_clean boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the room has been cleaned and is ready for occupancy. True if housekeeping status is clean or inspected.']
  is_inspected boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the room has passed housekeeping quality inspection after cleaning.']
  is_out_of_order boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the room is out of order and unavailable for sale due to maintenance or other operational issues.']
  last_cleaned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room was last cleaned by housekeeping staff.']
  last_inspected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room last passed housekeeping quality inspection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this room status record was last modified in the system.']
  last_occupied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room was last occupied by a guest (most recent check-in or last known occupancy).']
  last_vacated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room was last vacated by a guest (most recent check-out).']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance condition of the room. Available: no maintenance issues; Minor Repair: small issues not blocking occupancy; Major Repair: significant issues requiring attention; Out of Order: room cannot be occupied; Scheduled Maintenance: planned preventive maintenance.']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Simple binary occupancy indicator. Vacant: no guest assigned; Occupied: guest currently checked in; Reserved: future reservation exists.']
  out_of_order_end_date date [note: 'The expected or actual date when the room will be or was returned to service after being out of order.']
  out_of_order_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the room is out of order (e.g., plumbing issue, HVAC failure, renovation, pest control, water damage).']
  out_of_order_start_date date [note: 'The date when the room was first designated as out of order.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Housekeeping priority level for servicing this room. Urgent: immediate attention required (e.g., due-in within 1 hour); High: priority cleaning (e.g., VIP guest, early check-in); Normal: standard cleaning schedule; Low: can be deferred.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text special instructions for housekeeping staff regarding this room (e.g., guest allergies, VIP preferences, extra amenities required).']
  status_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room status was last updated in the system. Tracks the most recent status transition.']
  turndown_service_status varchar [note: 'Status of evening turndown service for the room. Not Requested: no turndown service scheduled; Requested: guest or property standard requires turndown; Completed: turndown service has been performed; Declined: guest declined turndown service.']

  Note: 'Real-time operational status record for each physical room capturing the current housekeeping state (dirty, clean, inspected, pick-up), front-desk status (vacant, occupied, due-out, due-in), and maintenance flags. Tracks status transitions with timestamps, assigned housekeeper, last inspection time, and turndown service status. Integrates with Oracle OPERA PMS housekeeping module for live room status boards.'
}

Table inventory.availability_snapshot {
  availability_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the availability snapshot record. Primary key for this entity.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: Availability snapshots capture actual rooms sold/available; demand forecasts predict future demand. Linking actuals to forecasts enables forecast accuracy measurement (MAPE calculation), model perform']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.experiment. Business justification: A/B testing in hospitality involves inventory availability experiments (scarcity messaging tests, overbooking threshold optimization, dynamic availability display). Experiments must track which availa']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property for which this availability snapshot is recorded.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the room type (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite) for which availability is tracked.']
  closed_to_arrival_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new reservations are allowed to check in on this date. When true, no new arrivals are accepted, but existing multi-night stays can continue.']
  closed_to_departure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests are allowed to check out on this date. When true, reservations must extend beyond this date to capture higher-value multi-night stays.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this availability snapshot record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any discrepancies found during reconciliation, including root cause and resolution actions.']
  lra_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room type on this date. When true, the property commits to selling rooms at contracted rates even if only one room remains.']
  max_los_restriction varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for a reservation starting on this date. Used to prevent long stays during high-demand periods.']
  min_los_restriction varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for a reservation starting on this date. Used for demand management and revenue optimization.']
  net_available_rooms varchar [note: 'Net number of rooms available to sell after accounting for all deductions (OOO, OOS, house use, blocks). Calculated as total physical rooms minus all unavailable and blocked rooms.']
  occ_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Occupancy rate percentage for this room type on this date, calculated as (rooms sold + complimentary rooms) / net available rooms * 100. Key performance metric for revenue management.']
  overbooking_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms that can be overbooked for this room type on this date, based on historical no-show and cancellation patterns. Used for yield optimization.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the nightly reconciliation process for this snapshot. Indicates whether the snapshot has been validated against PMS folios and room status.']
  rooms_blocked_allotment varchar [note: 'Number of rooms allocated to contracted allotments (OTA, wholesaler, corporate contracts) that are held but not yet sold.']
  rooms_blocked_group varchar [note: 'Number of rooms blocked for group reservations (MICE, corporate blocks) that are not yet sold but are held off general inventory.']
  rooms_complimentary varchar [note: 'Number of rooms occupied by complimentary guests (e.g., loyalty rewards, service recovery, VIP comps). Counted in occupancy but not in revenue.']
  rooms_day_use varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold for day-use only (check-in and check-out on the same day), typically for meetings or short stays.']
  rooms_house_use varchar [note: 'Number of rooms occupied by hotel staff, contractors, or used for operational purposes (e.g., training, storage). Not available for sale.']
  rooms_out_of_order varchar [note: 'Number of rooms temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, repair, or refurbishment. These rooms are not available for sale.']
  rooms_out_of_service varchar [note: 'Number of rooms permanently or long-term unavailable due to major renovation, structural issues, or strategic decisions. Not available for sale.']
  rooms_overbooked varchar [note: 'Actual number of rooms overbooked (sold beyond physical capacity) for this room type on this date.']
  rooms_sold varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold (occupied by paying guests) for this room type on this date. Used in OCC (Occupancy Rate) calculation.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'The business date for which this availability snapshot is recorded. Represents the stay date, not the snapshot creation date.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this availability snapshot was captured. Typically recorded during the nightly audit process.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this availability snapshot was sourced. Typically Oracle OPERA PMS for operational data or IDeaS G3 RMS for forecasted availability.']
  stop_sell_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type is completely closed for sale on this date across all channels. When true, no new reservations are accepted.']
  total_physical_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of physical rooms of this room type at the property, representing the maximum inventory capacity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this availability snapshot record was last updated in the data warehouse.']

  Note: 'Date-level inventory availability record per room type per property capturing total physical rooms, rooms sold, rooms blocked (group and allotment), out-of-order rooms, out-of-service rooms, complimentary rooms, house-use rooms, day-use rooms, and net available rooms. The authoritative SSOT for real-time occupancy (OCC) calculation and available-to-sell inventory. Feeds IDeaS G3 RMS for yield optimization and supports LRA (Last Room Availability) flag tracking. Reconciled nightly during night audit against PMS folios and room status. Serves as the foundation for RevPAR, occupancy trending, and inventory reconciliation reporting.'
}

Table inventory.control {
  control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for control']
  inventory_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory control record. Primary key for the control entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Inventory control decisions (stop-sell, CTA/CTD, overbooking limits, LRA) are audited for brand standards compliance, revenue management policy adherence, and regulatory requirements. Links control to']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Inventory controls (stop-sell, CTA/CTD, LOS restrictions) are often campaign-driven (flash sale requires 2-night minimum, member-exclusive rates with specific restrictions). Required for promotional r']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, Direct) to which this control applies. Null indicates control applies to all channels.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory controls are managed by revenue management departments that map to specific cost centers for labor allocation, system cost allocation, and P&L responsibility. Required for departmental budge']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who manually overrode RMS-generated controls. Null if control was not manually overridden.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property to which this inventory control applies.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate plan to which this control applies. Null indicates control applies to all rate plans for the room type and date.']
  room_block_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the group block that this control is associated with. Null if control is not tied to a specific group reservation.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the room type (e.g., Deluxe King, Standard Queen) to which this control applies.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdles, stop-sell flags) are tier-differentiated in luxury hospitality. Properties set different availability rules for base vs platinum members. Revenue strategy req']
  advance_booking_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance that bookings can be made for this stay date. Null indicates no advance booking restriction.']
  channel_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total sell limit allocated to this specific channel. Used for channel-level inventory gating. Sum across all channels for a date should not exceed 100%.']
  competitive_set_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate of the competitive set (STR comp set) for comparable room types on this stay date. Used for competitive benchmarking.']
  control_source varchar [note: 'Origin of this control record. RMS indicates generated by IDeaS G3 RMS; manual indicates user override; API indicates external system integration.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this inventory control record. Active controls are enforced by PMS and CRS; inactive controls are ignored.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory control record was first created in the system.']
  cta_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new arrivals are prohibited on this date. True means guests cannot check in on this date, but existing reservations spanning this date are allowed.']
  ctd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are prohibited on this date. True means guests cannot check out on this date; used to enforce minimum stay through high-demand periods.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (hurdle_rate, forecast_adr, competitive_set_adr). Typically matches property base currency.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this control becomes effective. Controls are typically pushed to distribution channels in advance of the stay date.']
