This attribute should move to the association to support tracking of multiple applications with their effective dates and contexts.]']

  Note: 'This association product represents the application of a specific wash factor configuration to a group room block for forecasting and post-event performance analysis. It captures which wash factor was applied to which block, the predicted and actual wash percentages, variance analysis, and the business context (lead time bucket, block size tier) that determined factor selection. Each record links one room block to one wash factor with attributes that exist only in the context of this application event.. Existence Justification: In revenue management operations, a single group room block is evaluated against multiple wash factor configurations during its lifecycle—initial forecasting may apply one factor based on lead time and block size, revised forecasts may apply updated factors as the block matures, and post-event analysis compares actual performance against multiple factor models to refine future predictions. Conversely, a single wash factor configuration (e.g., \'Corporate groups, 60-90 day lead time, 25-50 rooms\') is applied to many different room blocks that match its criteria. Revenue managers actively track each block×wash_factor application as a distinct performance measurement record with predicted vs. actual wash variance.'
}

Table inventory.room_type_promotion {
  room_type_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this room type promotion configuration record. Primary key.']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the room type participating in this promotion']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion offering special terms for this room type']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this room type promotion configuration. Values: active (available for booking), paused (temporarily disabled by revenue management), sold_out (inventory cap reached), expired (booking window closed).']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges (YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD) when this room type is excluded from the promotion due to high demand periods, holidays, or inventory constraints. Example: 2024-12-20 to 2024-12-31, 2024-07-04 to 2024-07-04.']
  bonus_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Points earning multiplier specific to this room type and promotion combination. Example: 2.0 means double points, 3.0 means triple points. Overrides the promotion-level multiplier when room-type-specific earning rules apply.']
  booking_window_end_date date [note: 'Last date when members can book this room type under this promotion. May differ from promotion end_date to manage inventory availability or phase out specific room types from the offer.']
  booking_window_start_date date [note: 'First date when members can book this room type under this promotion. Used for early-access offers or phased promotional launches by room category.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this room type promotion configuration was created by the loyalty or revenue management team.']
  inventory_allocation_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms of this type allocated to this promotion. Used by revenue management to control promotional inventory and protect higher-rated business. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_los_override varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required to book this room type under this promotion. Overrides property-level or promotion-level minimum LOS when room-type-specific restrictions apply. Null if no override.']
  promotional_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Special promotional rate (in property currency) offered for this room type under this promotion. Overrides standard BAR or member rates. Null if promotion does not include rate discount.']
  rooms_booked_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of rooms of this type booked under this promotion to date. Updated in real-time by reservation system. Used to enforce inventory_allocation_cap.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the loyalty or revenue management team member who configured this room type for this promotion.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the promotional configuration between room types and loyalty promotions. It captures the specific promotional rates, bonus earning rules, and booking restrictions that apply when a member books a specific room type under a specific promotion. Each record links one room type to one promotion with attributes that define the promotional offer mechanics, rate overrides, and eligibility windows that exist only in the context of this specific room-promotion combination.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality loyalty programs, promotions (e.g., \'Book 3 Nights, Earn 5000 Bonus Points\' or \'Suite Upgrade Promotion\') apply to multiple room types with type-specific promotional rates, bonus earning multipliers, and booking restrictions. Conversely, each room type participates in multiple concurrent promotions throughout the year (seasonal offers, tier-specific promotions, flash sales). Revenue management and loyalty teams actively configure and manage these room-type-promotion combinations, setting promotional rates, blackout dates, inventory caps, and booking windows for each pairing.'
}

Table inventory.room_type_competitive_benchmark {
  room_type_competitive_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for room_type_competitive_benchmark']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the room type being benchmarked against the competitive set']
  revenue_competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the competitive set used for benchmarking this room type']
  advance_purchase_window_days varchar [note: 'The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons for this specific room type and competitive set pairing. Allows different lead time analysis for different room type segments (e.g., suites may use 30-day window, standard rooms 7-day window).']
  benchmark_room_type_category varchar [note: 'The STR room type category classification used for this specific room type when benchmarking against this competitive set. Maps property-specific room types to standardized STR categories for apples-to-apples comparison. Examples: STANDARD, DELUXE, SUITE, PREMIUM.']
  benchmark_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'The weighting percentage applied to this competitive set when calculating blended benchmark metrics for this room type. Used when a room type is benchmarked against multiple competitive sets with different importance levels. Sum of weights across all comp sets for a room type should equal 100.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this benchmark configuration record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this room type to competitive set benchmark configuration becomes active. Allows revenue managers to schedule future changes to benchmark mappings aligned with property renovations, rebranding, or competitive strategy shifts.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this benchmark configuration expires and is no longer used for rate shopping or STR reporting. Null indicates an active, ongoing configuration.']
  is_primary_comp_category boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary competitive set used for benchmarking this room type. A room type may be mapped to multiple competitive sets (primary luxury set, secondary airport set), but only one is designated as primary for executive reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the revenue manager who last modified this benchmark configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this benchmark configuration record was last modified.']
  los_benchmark_nights varchar [note: 'The standard length of stay in nights used for rate shopping and benchmarking comparisons for this room type and competitive set combination. Different room types may use different LOS windows (e.g., suites benchmarked at 3-night stays, standard rooms at 1-night stays).']
  rate_shop_frequency varchar [note: 'Override frequency for automated rate shopping for this specific room type and competitive set pairing. Allows revenue managers to configure more frequent rate checks for high-value room types or key competitive sets. Overrides the default rate_shop_frequency from the competitive_set master.']
  room_type_competitive_benchmark_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this benchmark configuration. ACTIVE configurations are used in live rate shopping and STR reporting. INACTIVE configurations are historical. SUSPENDED configurations are temporarily disabled but may be reactivated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the revenue manager who created this benchmark configuration mapping.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the configuration relationship between room types and competitive sets for STR benchmarking and rate shopping. It captures the specific mapping rules that revenue managers define to determine which room type categories are compared against which competitive set segments. Each record links one room type to one competitive set with relationship-specific attributes including benchmark category mapping, rate shopping frequency overrides, length-of-stay parameters, and effective date ranges that exist only in the context of this specific room-type-to-comp-set pairing.. Existence Justification: Revenue managers actively configure many-to-many mappings between room types and competitive sets for STR benchmarking and rate shopping. A single room type (e.g., Deluxe King) can be benchmarked against multiple competitive sets simultaneously (luxury downtown set, airport premium set, corporate traveler set), and each competitive set is used to benchmark multiple room types across the property. Each pairing requires relationship-specific configuration including STR category mapping, rate shopping frequency overrides, LOS parameters, and effective date ranges that cannot reside on either the room type or competitive set entity alone.'
}

Table revenue.revenue_rate_plan {
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate plan record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the revenue_rate_plan data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Rate plans are frequently created to support specific marketing campaigns (flash sales, seasonal promotions). Campaign managers track which rate plans were deployed per campaign to measure ROI and rev']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers create rate plans; employee attribution required for audit trail, approval workflow validation, and performance tracking of pricing strategy decisions in revenue management operations']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate plans are distributed through specific channels (OTA, GDS, direct booking). Revenue managers configure which channels can sell which rate plans - core distribution strategy. Replaces denormalized']
  included_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Rate plans include F&B benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, dining credits). Required for package fulfillment, benefit tracking, revenue allocation between rooms and F&B departments per USALI stand']
  included_menu_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Rate plans reference specific menus for included meals (breakfast menu version, dinner package menu). Required for package rate construction, menu version control, guest entitlement verification at ou']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Rate plans must post room revenue to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition and financial statement preparation. Essential for USALI compliance and audit trail.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate plans enforce tier-specific eligibility (loyalty_tier_required field exists). Business process: validating member tier qualification for rate plan access at booking time. Essential for tier-based']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Rate plans must satisfy regulatory obligations (ADA accessible rate availability, rate transparency requirements, consumer protection mandates). Rate loading process validates regulatory compliance be']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Rate plans must comply with pricing policies (fair pricing, non-discrimination, rate parity regulations). Rate approval workflows validate policy compliance before activation. Essential for regulatory']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Rate plans must validate against loyalty program rules (points eligibility, conversion rates, tier requirements). Business process: ensuring rate plan configuration aligns with active loyalty program ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or hotel at which this rate plan is applicable. Enables property-level rate plan management across a multi-property portfolio. A null value indicates a chain-wide or global rate plan applicable to all properties.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type or room category to which this rate plan applies. A null value indicates the rate plan applies to all room types at the associated property.']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Rate plans are designed around guest journey touchpoints (pre-arrival, check-in, in-stay offers) for targeted pricing strategies. Revenue managers create touchpoint-specific rates to optimize conversi']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Common values are 7, 14, 21, or 30 days for advance purchase promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this rate plan for distribution. Supports SOX financial controls and internal audit requirements for rate authorization governance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan was formally approved for activation and distribution. Distinct from created_timestamp; captures the authorization event in the rate plan lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The principal nightly room rate amount in the operating currency before any adjustments, taxes, or fees. Represents the rack rate or floor price for this rate plan. Used as the reference value for ADR (Average Daily Rate) and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) calculations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.']
  booking_window_close_date date [note: 'The last date on which new reservations can be made under this rate plan. After this date the rate plan is closed to new bookings even if the stay dates fall within the effective period.']
  booking_window_open_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which reservations can be made under this rate plan. Used to control promotional rate availability windows and prevent premature booking of seasonal or event-driven rates.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy associated with this rate plan (e.g., FLEX24H, NRR_NOREF, 48H_PENALTY). Determines guest refund eligibility and penalty charges upon cancellation. Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase rate plans.']
  commission_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the room rate payable as commission to the booking agent or OTA when is_commissionable is true. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Null when is_commissionable is false.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the base_rate_amount and all monetary fields on this rate plan are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.']
  discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the BAR (Best Available Rate) or rack rate to derive this rate plan\'s selling price. Used for corporate negotiated rates, loyalty member rates, and promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no discount from the reference rate.']
  drr_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is subject to DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) adjustments by the revenue management system. When true, IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS may automatically adjust the rate based on demand forecasts, occupancy levels, and competitive benchmarking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and bookable. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS to control rate availability windows. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended rate plan with no defined end date. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  gds_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport. Controls rate visibility to travel agents using GDS booking tools.']
  is_commissionable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a travel agent or OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission is payable on bookings made under this rate plan. Drives commission accrual and payment processing in SAP S/4HANA AP.']
  is_lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan carries LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, meaning the contracted rate must be honored even when only the last room in a category is available. Applicable to corporate negotiated rate contracts.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for a full refund upon cancellation within the policy window. False for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) plans; true for flexible BAR (Best Available Rate) plans.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan record was most recently updated in the source system. Used to detect changes for incremental ETL processing and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  loyalty_points_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan qualify for loyalty program points accrual. Wholesale, opaque, and certain promotional rates are typically ineligible for points earning.']
  loyalty_tier_required varchar [note: 'Minimum loyalty program membership tier required to access this rate plan. A value of \'none\' indicates the rate is open to all guests regardless of loyalty status. Used to gate exclusive member rates.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the market segment this rate plan targets (e.g., TRANSIENT, GROUP, CONTRACT, WHOLESALE). Aligns with STR STAR Report segmentation and USALI market segment definitions for revenue performance benchmarking.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan. Used to prevent extended stays at promotional or deeply discounted rates. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction.']
  meal_plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the F&B (Food and Beverage) inclusions bundled with this rate plan. Room only includes no meals; bed and breakfast includes morning meal; half board includes breakfast and dinner; full board includes all three meals; all-inclusive covers meals, beverages, and selected activities.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must book to qualify for this rate plan. A LOS (Length of Stay) restriction used in yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.']
  ota_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Booking.com, Expedia, or Agoda. Controls rate visibility on third-party booking platforms.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping rate plans by the market segment or distribution channel they serve. Public rates are available to all guests; negotiated rates are contracted with specific accounts; wholesale rates are sold through tour operators; group rates apply to block bookings; loyalty rates are exclusive to program members; employee rates are internal staff benefits.']
  rate_ceiling_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum rate above which this rate plan cannot be sold, used to cap pricing during peak demand periods for contracted or promotional rates. Null indicates no ceiling constraint.']
  rate_floor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum rate below which this rate plan cannot be sold, regardless of dynamic pricing adjustments. Protects rate integrity and brand positioning. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS as a hard constraint in yield optimization algorithms.']
  rate_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the organizational scope at which this rate plan is defined and managed. Property-level plans apply to a single hotel; brand-level plans apply across a brand portfolio; chain-level plans apply across the entire company; global plans may span international markets.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rate plan across systems (e.g., BAR, CORP001, PKG-SPA). Used as the business key in IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate plan lookup and distribution. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  rate_plan_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the rate plan\'s terms, inclusions, and conditions as displayed to guests and booking agents. Includes details on what is included in the rate, any special conditions, and marketing messaging.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and revenue managers (e.g., \'Best Available Rate\', \'AAA Member Rate\', \'Advance Purchase 21-Day\'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR (Best Available Rate) is the publicly available flexible rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees availability to contracted accounts; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) carries no cancellation flexibility; corporate rates are negotiated with companies; package rates bundle room with ancillary services; promotional rates are time-limited offers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate plan. Active plans are available for booking; inactive plans are temporarily disabled; draft plans are under configuration; suspended plans are paused due to yield or compliance review; archived plans are retired and no longer bookable. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this rate plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same rate plan exists in multiple systems.']
  source_system_rate_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this rate plan in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Oracle OPERA PMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.']
  stay_date_restriction varchar [note: 'Defines any day-of-week or date-specific restrictions on when stays under this rate plan are permitted. Weekday-only and weekend-only restrictions are common for corporate and leisure rates respectively; blackout dates exclude specific high-demand periods.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the base_rate_amount includes applicable taxes (VAT, occupancy tax, city tax). True for tax-inclusive markets; false for markets where taxes are added at checkout. Critical for accurate revenue recognition under USALI and IFRS/GAAP.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all rate plans available across properties, including BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), corporate negotiated rates, package rates, and promotional rates. Defines rate structure, cancellation policy, meal plan inclusions, booking conditions, and distribution eligibility. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. This is the SSOT for rate plan definitions.'
}

Table revenue.rate_restriction {
  rate_restriction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate restriction record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the rate_restriction data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Revenue managers apply cancellation policy restrictions as part of yield management strategy. Rate restrictions must enforce specific cancellation terms (non-refundable, free cancellation windows) to ']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Restrictions (stop-sell, min LOS, CTA/CTD) are applied per distribution channel. Revenue managers close channels selectively based on demand - essential inventory control operation. Enables channel-sp']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Revenue managers apply specific rate restrictions (min LOS, CTA/CTD, hurdle rates) when large event bookings create displacement risk. Essential for RMS restriction management around group blocks and ']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Rate restrictions impact revenue posting patterns and require GL account mapping for revenue management adjustments and variance analysis in financial reporting.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be scoped to market segments (e.g., restrict Leisure segment on peak dates). rate_restriction has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N rate_restrictio']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel/resort) to which this rate restriction applies. Enables property-level yield optimization and restriction management across the portfolio.']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the group block associated with this restriction, when the restriction is applied to protect inventory for a specific group booking or MICE event. Null for transient/individual restrictions.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan to which this restriction applies. Links to the rate plan master in the CRS/RMS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, DRR). A restriction may apply to one or multiple rate plans; this field captures the primary rate plan association.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type to which this restriction applies. When null, the restriction applies to all room types under the associated rate plan. Enables granular inventory control by room category.']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Supports NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase yield strategies. Null when restriction type is not ADVANCE_PURCHASE.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction was activated and distributed to connected channels (CRS, GDS, OTA). Represents the principal business event time — when the restriction became operationally effective in the distribution ecosystem. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that created this rate restriction record. Supports SOX financial controls audit trail and accountability for yield management decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the hurdle_rate_amount and any monetary thresholds defined in this restriction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI standards.']
  days_of_week_mask varchar [note: '7-character binary string indicating which days of the week the restriction applies, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., \'1111100\' = Mon–Fri only, \'0000011\' = Sat–Sun only). Enables day-of-week yield strategies such as weekend minimum stay requirements.']
  demand_forecast_level varchar [note: 'Demand forecast classification at the time this restriction was created or last updated, as determined by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Provides context for why the restriction was applied and supports post-hoc yield strategy analysis.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current distribution state of this restriction across connected channels via Sabre SynXis CRS. \'pending\' = queued for distribution; \'distributed\' = successfully pushed to all channels; \'failed\' = distribution error; \'partial\' = distributed to some but not all channels.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this rate restriction becomes active and enforceable. Corresponds to the stay date (not booking date) unless the restriction type is ADVANCE_PURCHASE. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The last calendar date on which this rate restriction is enforceable (inclusive). Null indicates an open-ended restriction with no defined end. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  hurdle_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable rate (in the property\'s base currency) below which a booking will be rejected for this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A key RMS yield control used to protect ADR during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not HURDLE_RATE.']
  is_blackout boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the effective date range is designated as a blackout period, during which the rate plan is completely closed to new bookings. Commonly applied during peak demand events, holidays, or sold-out periods.']
  is_cta boolean [note: 'Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check in on the restricted date, preventing short-stay bookings that fragment inventory during high-demand periods.']
  is_ctd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check out on the restricted date, used to extend stays through high-demand nights and maximize occupancy continuity.']
  is_loyalty_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of the hotel\'s loyalty program are exempt from this restriction (e.g., LRA — Last Room Availability guarantee for elite members). Supports loyalty program administration and member benefit fulfillment.']
  last_distributed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this restriction was last successfully distributed to external channels (OTA, GDS) via Sabre SynXis CRS. Used to monitor distribution latency and SLA compliance for rate parity management.']
  max_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days prior to arrival within which a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Defines the booking window ceiling for advance purchase restrictions, preventing bookings made too far in advance at promotional rates.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). Used to prevent long-stay bookings at discounted rates during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not MAX_LOS.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A core yield optimization lever in revenue management. Null when restriction type is not MIN_LOS.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes or context provided by the revenue manager regarding this restriction. May include references to specific events, competitive intelligence, or strategy rationale not captured in structured fields.']
  override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue manager or front desk supervisor is permitted to manually override this restriction for individual bookings. When False, the restriction is hard-enforced and cannot be bypassed in the PMS or CRS.']
  override_authorization_level varchar [note: 'Minimum staff authorization level required to override this restriction when override_allowed is True. Ensures appropriate governance over yield control exceptions. Null when override_allowed is False.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank used to resolve conflicts when multiple restrictions apply to the same rate plan, room type, and date. Lower values indicate higher priority. Ensures deterministic restriction enforcement when overlapping rules exist in the RMS.']
  restriction_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate restriction rule as defined in IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and distribution to OTA and GDS channels.']
  restriction_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the rate restriction rule (e.g., \'Weekend Min 2-Night Stay\', \'Holiday CTA Block\'). Used by revenue managers and front desk staff to identify restrictions in PMS and RMS interfaces.']
  restriction_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why this restriction was applied (e.g., \'Peak demand event\', \'Holiday blackout\', \'Group block protection\', \'RMS auto-recommendation\'). Supports revenue strategy documentation and post-stay performance analysis.']
  restriction_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the level at which this restriction is applied: \'property\' = entire property; \'room_type\' = specific room category; \'rate_plan\' = specific rate plan only; \'channel\' = specific distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct). Determines how broadly the restriction is enforced across inventory.']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate restriction record. \'active\' = currently enforced; \'inactive\' = disabled but retained for history; \'pending\' = scheduled but not yet effective; \'expired\' = past end date; \'overridden\' = manually superseded by a revenue manager.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the restriction mechanism applied. MIN_LOS = Minimum Length of Stay; MAX_LOS = Maximum Length of Stay; CTA = Closed to Arrival; CTD = Closed to Departure; ADVANCE_PURCHASE = minimum days before arrival required to book; BLACKOUT = date fully closed to booking; HURDLE_RATE = minimum acceptable rate threshold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MIN_LOS|MAX_LOS|CTA|CTD|ADVANCE_PURCHASE|BLACKOUT|HURDLE_RATE — promote to reference product]']
  rms_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction was system-recommended by the RMS (True) or manually created by a revenue manager (False). Enables analysis of RMS automation adoption rates and comparison of system vs. human yield decisions.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Seasonal classification code associated with this restriction (e.g., PEAK, SHOULDER, OFF_PEAK, HOLIDAY). Aligns restrictions with the property\'s seasonal demand calendar and supports STR STAR Report benchmarking analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this rate restriction record. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  source_system_restriction_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this restriction record in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, etc.). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that last modified this rate restriction record. Supports audit trail and governance of yield management changes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction record was last modified in the source system. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Operational records of rate restrictions applied to rate plans and room types, including minimum LOS (Length of Stay), maximum LOS, closed-to-arrival (CTA), closed-to-departure (CTD), advance purchase requirements, and blackout dates. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. Drives yield optimization by controlling which rates are bookable under specific conditions.'
}

Table revenue.dynamic_rate_rule {
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) record in the revenue management system. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules are often triggered by marketing campaigns (e.g., increase rates during high-awareness periods). Revenue managers need campaign context to evaluate rule performance and campaign-']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitive set (comp set) used as the benchmark for competitive-based Dynamic Rate Rules. References the STR STAR Report comp set configuration in Infor EzRMS for RGI, MPI, and ARI-driven pricing decisions.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers configure dynamic pricing rules; employee attribution required for approval workflow, rule performance evaluation, and audit trail of automated pricing strategy decisions in RMS opera']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand forecast record from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS that this forecast-based Dynamic Rate Rule references for its trigger evaluation.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific channels differently (e.g., OTA rates adjust based on commission costs, direct channel gets best rate). RMS systems apply channel-specific rate optimization strat']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the local demand event (e.g., MICE event, conference, festival, sporting event) that triggers this event-based Dynamic Rate Rule. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus or the events domain.']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules incorporate guest satisfaction scores to adjust rates for properties with declining NPS/GSS (reputation-based yield management). Properties below GSS thresholds may trigger rate ']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific guest segments for personalized rate adjustments. Revenue management systems apply segment-specific pricing logic (e.g., loyalty tier discounts, corporate negotia']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Dynamic pricing adjustments must post to specific GL accounts for audit trail, revenue variance tracking, and SOX compliance in automated pricing systems.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules adjust rates based on member tier for personalized revenue optimization. Business process: applying tier-specific rate adjustments in revenue management systems to reward high-va']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules can be scoped to specific market segments. dynamic_rate_rule has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N DRR records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant m']
  monitored_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules monitor outlet occupancy for total revenue optimization (restaurant full = adjust room+dining packages). Required for integrated revenue management systems that optimize property']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules must comply with consumer protection policies. Automated pricing systems require policy guardrails to prevent discriminatory or predatory pricing. Essential for algorithmic prici']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all eligible rate plans for the property.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe suite) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all room types at the property.']
  target_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Dynamic rules can target specific high-value members for personalized pricing strategies. Business process: VIP member-specific rate rules for retention and revenue optimization, common in luxury hosp']
  adjustment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the Dynamic Rate Rule increases or decreases the rate when triggered. Provides explicit directional intent separate from the sign of adjustment_value to prevent ambiguity in RMS configuration.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.']
  adjustment_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The magnitude of the rate adjustment applied when the Dynamic Rate Rule is triggered. Interpreted according to adjustment_type: a value of 0.10 with type \'percentage\' means a 10% rate increase; a value of 25.00 with type \'absolute_amount\' means a $25 rate increase; a value of -15.00 with type \'bar_offset\' means $15 below BAR.']
  advance_booking_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance of the arrival date within which a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Null indicates no upper advance booking restriction.']
  advance_booking_min_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance of the arrival date that a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Supports advance purchase pricing strategies.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state of the Dynamic Rate Rule indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by a revenue manager before activation. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this Dynamic Rate Rule for activation. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail on pricing decisions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was approved for activation by the authorized revenue manager. Part of the pricing governance audit trail required under SOX financial controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for pricing configuration governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the property in which rate floor, ceiling, and absolute adjustment values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  days_of_week_mask varchar [note: 'Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of \'1111100\' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.']
  last_triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.']
  los_max_nights varchar [note: 'Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.']
  los_min_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.']
  max_rate_ceiling decimal(10,2) [note: 'The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property\'s maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.']
  min_rate_floor decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property\'s minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.']
  pickup_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.']
  rms_rule_reference_code varchar [note: 'Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule\'s business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: occupancy_based|pickup_based|competitive_based|event_based|los_based|forecast_based — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this Dynamic Rate Rule was originated or last synchronized. Supports data lineage tracking across the revenue management technology stack.']
  trigger_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule has been triggered and applied a pricing adjustment since its activation date. Used to assess rule utilization and yield optimization effectiveness.']
  trigger_threshold_operator varchar [note: 'Comparison operator applied to the trigger threshold value to determine whether the rule condition is met. For example, \'greater_than\' with a threshold of 0.80 means the rule fires when OCC exceeds 80%.']
  trigger_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the trigger threshold value. Percentage applies to OCC-based rules; room_count applies to pickup-based rules; index_value applies to competitive benchmarking rules (RGI, MPI, ARI); currency_amount applies to ADR-based triggers; nights applies to LOS-based rules.']
  trigger_threshold_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric threshold at which this Dynamic Rate Rule fires. For occupancy-based rules, this is the OCC percentage (e.g., 0.80 = 80%). For pickup-based rules, this is the number of rooms picked up within the pickup window. For competitive-based rules, this is the RGI or ARI index value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer ETL pipeline.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version counter tracking the number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule configuration has been modified. Supports audit trail and change management for pricing strategy governance.']

  Note: 'DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) definitions governing automated pricing adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. Captures rule type (occupancy-based, pickup-based, competitive-based, event-based), trigger thresholds, adjustment type (percentage, absolute, BAR offset), applicable room types, applicable rate plans, effective date ranges, and rule priority. Core mechanism for yield optimization and dynamic pricing strategy.'
}

Table revenue.pricing_override {
  pricing_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each manual pricing override record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the pricing_override data product.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically a Director of Revenue Management or VP Revenue) who formally authorized the pricing override. May differ from revenue_manager_id when a second-level approval is required for large rate variances.']
  banquet_event_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Manual rate overrides for groups tied to F&B minimum spend commitments from banquet events. Required for group contract evaluation, displacement analysis that includes total property spend, not just r']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Manual rate overrides are sometimes applied to honor campaign promises or fix campaign-related pricing errors. Tracking the campaign context is essential for audit, reconciliation, and campaign cost a']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Manual rate overrides often apply to specific channels (e.g., override OTA rate due to parity issue, adjust GDS rate for corporate segment). Revenue managers need to track which channel triggered over']
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) that was in effect and being superseded by this manual override. Enables analysis of how frequently manual overrides conflict with automated DRR logic and supports RMS rule tuning.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific event, conference, or MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) event that triggered the pricing override when override_reason_code is \'event_demand\'. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Manual rate overrides require GL posting for revenue variance tracking, management override reporting, and SOX control documentation in financial audits.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Manual rate overrides require policy compliance validation. Override approval workflows check against pricing authority policies and rate floor/ceiling policies. Critical for pricing governance and au']
  primary_pricing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the revenue manager employee who applied or approved the pricing override. Used for accountability tracking and override frequency analysis per individual manager.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the pricing override was applied. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the group room block associated with this pricing override when override_reason_code is \'group_wash\'. Links to the group block record in Oracle OPERA PMS or Delphi by Amadeus for group displacement analysis.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, group) against which the pricing override is applied. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS rate plan catalog.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Suite, Standard Double) to which the pricing override applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing override required formal approval before activation, based on the rate variance threshold or property-level governance policy. True = approval workflow triggered; False = auto-approved within tolerance.']
  approval_threshold_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The rate variance percentage threshold (as a decimal, e.g., 0.2000 = 20%) that triggered the approval requirement for this override. Captures the governance rule in effect at the time of submission for audit purposes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the pricing override was formally approved by the authorizing revenue manager or director. Null if override is still pending or was reversed without approval.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies which distribution channels the overridden rate is published to. \'all_channels\' = no restriction; \'direct_only\' = hotel direct and brand website only; \'ota_excluded\' = all channels except Online Travel Agencies (OTA); \'gds_only\' = Global Distribution System (GDS) only; \'brand_website_only\' = brand.com only.']
  closed_to_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Arrival (CTA) restriction, preventing new reservations with an arrival date falling within the override effective window. True = CTA restriction active; False = arrivals permitted.']
  closed_to_departure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Departure (CTD) restriction, preventing check-outs on specific dates within the override window. True = CTD restriction active; False = departures permitted.']
  competitive_rate_reference decimal(10,2) [note: 'The competitor or market reference rate (in property local currency) that informed the override decision, particularly when override_reason_code is \'competitive_pressure\'. Sourced from STR STAR Report, OTA rate shopping, or RMS competitive benchmarking module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when the pricing override record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rates captured in this override record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures multi-currency properties report rates in the correct denomination.']
  day_of_week_mask varchar [note: 'Seven-character binary string (1=active, 0=inactive) representing the days of the week the override applies to, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., \'1111100\' = Mon–Fri only). Applicable when override_scope is \'day_of_week\'.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The first calendar date on which the pricing override rate becomes active and is applied to reservations. Defines the start of the override window in the property\'s local time zone.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The last calendar date on which the pricing override rate remains active. After this date, the override status transitions to \'expired\' and the RMS-recommended rate is reinstated. Nullable for open-ended overrides.']
  inventory_rooms_affected varchar [note: 'Number of room inventory units to which the pricing override applies. Null or zero indicates the override applies to all available inventory for the specified room type and date range.']
  is_bar_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this override specifically targets the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate for a given date. True = BAR is being overridden; False = override applies to a non-BAR rate plan.']
  los_restriction_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under the overridden rate. Null if no maximum LOS restriction is applied. Used in conjunction with los_restriction_min to define the eligible stay window.']
  los_restriction_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to qualify for the overridden rate. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applied alongside the rate override to manage demand patterns and prevent single-night bookings during high-demand periods.']
  override_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the revenue manager explaining the business rationale for the override, particularly when override_reason_code is \'other\' or when additional context is required for audit purposes.']
  override_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The manually set rate (in property local currency) applied by the revenue manager, superseding the RMS-recommended rate. This is the Best Available Rate (BAR) or specific rate plan rate that will be published to distribution channels.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code classifying why the revenue manager overrode the system-generated rate. \'competitive_pressure\' = OTA/competitor rate response; \'group_wash\' = group block displacement adjustment; \'event_demand\' = MICE or local event surge; \'error_correction\' = RMS data or configuration error fix; \'distressed_inventory\' = last-minute unsold inventory; \'other\' = non-standard reason documented in override_notes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: competitive_pressure|group_wash|event_demand|error_correction|distressed_inventory|other — promote to reference product]']
  override_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to the pricing override for audit trail, cross-system reconciliation, and revenue manager communication. Format: PO- followed by 6–20 alphanumeric characters.']
  override_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the temporal scope of the pricing override. \'single_date\' = applies to one specific stay date; \'date_range\' = applies across a contiguous range from effective_date to expiry_date; \'day_of_week\' = applies to specific days of the week within the effective window.']
  override_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing override. \'pending\' = submitted awaiting approval; \'approved\' = authorized and scheduled; \'active\' = currently in effect; \'expired\' = past effective end date; \'reversed\' = manually cancelled or rolled back.']
  rate_variance_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary difference between the override_rate and the rms_recommended_rate (override_rate minus rms_recommended_rate). Positive value indicates override is above RMS recommendation; negative indicates below. Used for override impact analysis.']
  rate_variance_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the override_rate from the rms_recommended_rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Calculated as (override_rate - rms_recommended_rate) / rms_recommended_rate. Supports threshold-based alerting and RMS calibration.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why the pricing override was reversed. Populated only when override_status is \'reversed\'. Supports post-mortem analysis of override quality and revenue impact of reversals.']
  reversed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the pricing override was reversed or cancelled, restoring the RMS-recommended rate. Null for overrides that have not been reversed.']
  rms_forecast_demand_level varchar [note: 'The demand level classification (low, medium, high, very_high) as forecasted by the RMS at the time the override was submitted. Provides context for evaluating whether the override was aligned with or contrary to demand signals.']
  rms_recommended_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The original rate (in property local currency) recommended by the automated Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) prior to the manual override. Baseline for override variance analysis and RMS tuning.']
  rms_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the Revenue Management System (RMS) that generated the original recommended rate being overridden. \'ideas_g3\' = IDeaS G3 RMS; \'infor_ezrms\' = Infor EzRMS; \'manual\' = no RMS recommendation existed (fully manual rate entry).']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the revenue manager submitted the pricing override request for approval. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to the pricing override record in the data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of manual pricing overrides applied by revenue managers to supersede system-generated rates from IDeaS G3 RMS. Captures original system-recommended rate, override rate, override reason code (competitive pressure, group wash, event demand, error correction), approving revenue manager, property, room type, rate plan, effective date range, and override status (pending, approved, expired, reversed). Provides audit trail for manual revenue management interventions and supports override frequency analysis for RMS tuning.'
}

Table revenue.rate_availability {
  rate_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each daily rate and availability snapshot record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the rate_availability data product.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Rate availability snapshots distributed to OTAs and GDS must include the specific cancellation policy for rate shopping compliance and guest transparency. Channel managers need this link to ensure acc']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate/availability snapshots are channel-specific - each channel sees different rates and inventory based on connectivity and contracts. Core distribution management requires tracking what each channel']
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied by IDeaS G3 RMS to derive the current BAR for this snapshot. DRRs define the algorithmic pricing logic (demand-based adjustments, day-of-week factors, lead-time curves) that drive dynamic pricing decisions.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Rate availability changes drive revenue recognition patterns and require GL account mapping for channel-specific revenue tracking and financial reporting.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this rate and availability snapshot applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) governing the pricing for this snapshot. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS rate plan catalog.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this rate and availability snapshot is captured. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers update rate availability controls; employee tracking required for operational audit trail, override authority validation, and accountability for daily inventory and pricing distributi']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date (e.g., 7-day advance purchase, 21-day advance purchase for NRR rates). A key rate restriction parameter managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed availability status for this property, room type, rate plan, and date combination as managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. \'open\' = bookable; \'closed\' = inventory blocked; \'stop_sell\' = yield-driven closure; \'on_request\' = requires manual confirmation; \'min_stay_restriction\' = LOS restriction active; \'closed_to_arrival\' = no new arrivals permitted on this date.']
  available_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms of this room type available for sale on the snapshot date at this property. Represents net sellable inventory after deducting out-of-order, out-of-service, and already-committed rooms. Core input for OCC (Occupancy Rate) and RevPAR calculations.']
  bar_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The Best Available Rate (BAR) for this room type, property, and stay date — the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate as determined by IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing engine. BAR is the anchor rate from which other rate plans are derived and is the primary rate distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and OTA channels.']
  closed_to_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new arrivals are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check in on this date but may continue an existing stay through it. A yield management restriction used to optimize multi-night booking patterns.']
  closed_to_departure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check out on this date, forcing stays to extend through it. Used in conjunction with CTA restrictions to shape booking patterns during peak periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate and availability snapshot record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ETL pipeline tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all rate amounts (BAR, min, max, rack) for this record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international channel distribution via Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  demand_forecast_level varchar [note: 'Demand forecast classification for the snapshot date as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Drives dynamic pricing decisions and inventory restriction strategies. Levels range from very_low (deep discounting warranted) to very_high (maximum rate and stop-sell controls active).']
  effective_from_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time from which this rate and availability record became active and was distributed to booking channels. Used for bi-temporal modeling of rate history and supports point-in-time rate reconstruction for revenue audits and dispute resolution.']
  effective_until_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time until which this rate and availability record remains active. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for bi-temporal modeling and historical rate reconstruction. When a new rate update is received, the prior record\'s effective_until_timestamp is set to the new record\'s effective_from_timestamp.']
  gds_rate_code varchar [note: 'The rate code as published on GDS (Global Distribution System) platforms (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport) via Sabre SynXis CRS. GDS rate codes follow standardized formats required for travel agent booking systems and corporate travel management programs.']
  group_block_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms on the snapshot date allocated to group blocks (MICE, corporate groups, tour operators) as managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus. Group block inventory is excluded from transient availability and tracked separately for yield optimization.']
  is_package_rate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan includes bundled package components (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, airport transfer) beyond the base room charge. Package rates require component-level revenue allocation across USALI departments in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate and availability record was last modified in the source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS). Represents the business event timestamp of the most recent pricing or availability change. Critical for real-time channel distribution synchronization and SLA monitoring.']
  los_maximum varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan for the snapshot date. Used to prevent long-stay bookings at promotional rates during high-demand periods. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS as an upper LOS (Length of Stay) restriction.']
  los_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date. A key yield optimization restriction managed in IDeaS G3 RMS. LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions are used to maximize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring multi-night commitments.']
  max_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The ceiling rate (maximum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Caps pricing during peak demand periods to maintain guest trust and brand integrity, and supports rate parity compliance across channels.']
  meal_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this rate plan (RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board; FB = Full Board; AI = All Inclusive). Affects rate composition, F&B revenue allocation, and package pricing in Oracle OPERA PMS and MICROS POS.']
  min_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The floor rate (minimum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the lowest price at which the room may be sold under any circumstance, enforcing revenue floor controls and preventing rate erosion.']
  occupancy_forecast_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted occupancy rate (OCC%) for the property on the snapshot date as projected by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this snapshot. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to validate pricing decisions and benchmark against STR STAR Report actuals.']
  ota_rate_code varchar [note: 'The rate identifier as assigned by the primary OTA (Online Travel Agency) partner (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) for this rate plan and property combination. Used for rate parity monitoring, OTA reconciliation, and channel performance reporting.']
  out_of_order_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms of this room type that are out of order (OOO) on the snapshot date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Out-of-order rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and impact OCC denominator calculations per USALI standards.']
  pickup_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms booked (picked up) for the snapshot date since the previous snapshot capture. Pickup velocity is a key revenue management signal used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS to detect demand acceleration and trigger dynamic rate adjustments.']
  pricing_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current rate for this snapshot was manually overridden by a revenue manager, superseding the automated recommendation from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Manual overrides are tracked for revenue strategy audit and RMS model calibration.']
  pricing_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the revenue manager when manually overriding the RMS-recommended rate. Populated only when pricing_override_flag is True. Used for revenue strategy review, RMS model improvement, and audit trail compliance.']
  rack_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The published full undiscounted rate (rack rate) for this room type and stay date. Serves as the reference rate for discount calculations, loyalty redemption valuations, and walk-in pricing. Typically the highest rate tier and the baseline for USALI revenue reporting.']
  rate_parity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate record is subject to rate parity obligations with OTA (Online Travel Agency) partners. When True, the rate must match or be lower than rates published on OTA channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and channel distribution management.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS (e.g., BAR, CORP01, PKG_BB, NRR_ADV21). Used for channel distribution, GDS mapping, and OTA rate loading.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan governing this availability record. Determines pricing logic, cancellation policy, and channel eligibility. BAR (Best Available Rate) = dynamic public rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) = guaranteed availability; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) = prepaid non-cancellable; corporate = negotiated account rate; package = bundled inclusions; group = block rate; loyalty = member rate; promotional = limited-time offer; wholesale = OTA/tour operator net rate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|group|loyalty|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]']
  revenue_management_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the Revenue Management System (RMS) that generated or last updated the pricing recommendation for this snapshot record. Supports data lineage tracking and system-of-record attribution for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'The stay date (arrival night) for which this rate and availability record applies. Represents the business event date — the night a guest would occupy the room at the stated rate. Core dimension for RevPAR and ADR reporting.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS) for this rate and availability record. Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation, reprocessing, and audit purposes.']
  stop_sell boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this room type and rate plan combination has been closed to all new bookings on the snapshot date regardless of available inventory. A hard yield management control applied in IDeaS G3 RMS to protect inventory for higher-value bookings or group blocks.']
  total_rooms varchar [note: 'Total physical room count for this room type at the property on the snapshot date. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations. Reflects the property\'s sellable capacity before any restrictions.']

  Note: 'Daily rate and availability snapshot per property, room type, and rate plan combination as managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. Captures open/closed status, available inventory count, current BAR, minimum and maximum rates, and last-updated timestamp. Represents the real-time pricing grid used for reservation fulfillment and channel distribution.'
}

Table revenue.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each demand forecast record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Primary key for the demand_forecast data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Demand forecasts incorporate expected lift from planned marketing campaigns. Forecasters link campaigns to forecast assumptions for post-campaign variance analysis and to refine future campaign lift m']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue analysts generate demand forecasts; employee attribution required for forecast accuracy tracking, performance evaluation, and accountability for override decisions in revenue management foreca']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Demand forecasts incorporate known definite event bookings as constrained demand drivers. Critical for pickup forecasting, unconstrained vs constrained demand modeling, and event impact analysis in re']
