  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Demand forecasts are reconciled against financial budgets for variance analysis, reforecasting cycles, and board reporting on revenue performance vs plan.']
  forecasted_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Room demand forecasts drive outlet-level staffing and inventory planning (high occupancy forecast = increase outlet labor/stock). Required for integrated labor scheduling, procurement planning, outlet']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Demand forecasting models incorporate property GSS scores as a demand driver (higher satisfaction correlates with higher unconstrained demand). Revenue management systems use satisfaction metrics as r']
  loyalty_member_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_segment. Business justification: Demand forecasting incorporates loyalty segment behavior patterns (booking lead time, LOS, spend). Business process: segment-specific demand modeling for accurate revenue forecasts, essential for hote']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate, Leisure, OTA) for which the demand forecast is generated. Null when the forecast is at the property or room-type level only.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property for which this demand forecast was generated. Aligns with the Oracle OPERA PMS property master.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which the demand forecast is generated. Null when the forecast is at the property or market-segment level only.']
  ari_forecast decimal(8,4) [note: 'Forecasted Average Rate Index (ARI), representing the property\'s projected ADR relative to the competitive set\'s projected ADR. An ARI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to achieve a rate premium over its competitive set.']
  booking_pace_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of current bookings on-hand for the forecast date compared to the same point in time in the prior year or baseline period. A value above 1.0 indicates ahead-of-pace demand; below 1.0 indicates behind-pace. Used by revenue managers to assess demand trajectory.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the unconstrained demand forecast. Represents the pessimistic demand scenario used for risk-adjusted pricing and inventory control decisions in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the unconstrained demand forecast. Represents the optimistic demand scenario used for overbooking strategy and group evaluation in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  confidence_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (e.g., 90.00, 95.00) associated with the forecast confidence interval. Indicates the probability that actual demand will fall within the lower and upper confidence bounds.']
  constrained_demand decimal(10,2) [note: 'The forecasted room demand (in room-nights) after applying available inventory capacity constraints. Represents the demand the property can actually accommodate. Used for occupancy projection and revenue optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand forecast record was first captured and written to the Silver Layer data product. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary forecast values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures multi-currency properties report revenue in the correct denomination.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of the week corresponding to the forecast date. Used for day-of-week demand pattern analysis, weekend vs. weekday segmentation, and pricing strategy differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  demand_segment_mix_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted percentage share of total demand attributed to the specific market segment in this record. For example, if the Transient segment is expected to represent 65% of total demand on the forecast date, this field holds 65.00. Populated only for market-segment-level forecasts.']
  forecast_accuracy_mape decimal(8,4) [note: 'Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecast model for this property-segment-horizon combination, based on historical back-testing. Lower values indicate higher model accuracy. Used to weight forecast confidence and flag low-accuracy forecasts for revenue manager review.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The stay date (arrival date) for which the demand forecast values apply. Represents the future date being predicted, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  forecast_granularity varchar [note: 'The dimensional level at which this forecast record is generated. \'property\' is the highest level; \'room_type_segment\' is the most granular, combining room type and market segment dimensions as supported by IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  forecast_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the forecast run date to the forecast date, representing how far into the future this forecast projects. For example, a forecast run on 2024-01-01 for a stay date of 2024-04-01 has a horizon of 90 days. Used to assess forecast accuracy by horizon bucket.']
  forecast_model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the algorithmic approach used to generate the forecast. \'time_series\' uses historical booking patterns; \'regression\' uses demand drivers; \'machine_learning\' uses ML-based models; \'hybrid\' combines multiple approaches; \'manual_override\' indicates a revenue manager manually adjusted the system forecast.']
  forecast_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand forecasting model or algorithm used to generate this forecast record (e.g., \'IDeaS-G3-v8.2\', \'EzRMS-v5.1\'). Enables model performance tracking, A/B comparison, and audit of forecast methodology changes.']
  forecast_run_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the Revenue Management System (RMS) to the specific forecast generation run that produced this record. Enables traceability back to the originating RMS batch or on-demand run.']
  forecast_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast record was generated by the Revenue Management System. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this forecast record. \'active\' indicates the current operative forecast; \'superseded\' indicates a newer run has replaced it; \'draft\' indicates a preliminary run not yet published to pricing decisions; \'published\' indicates the forecast has been released to the Revenue Management System for pricing action; \'archived\' indicates historical retention only.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast methodology or purpose. \'unconstrained\' reflects true market demand without inventory limits; \'constrained\' reflects demand limited by available inventory; \'group_evaluation\' is used for group block assessment in IDeaS G3 RMS; \'pickup\' reflects incremental booking pace; \'budget\' reflects the annual budget forecast baseline.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the forecast date falls on a public or national holiday in the property\'s market. Used to apply holiday demand adjustment factors in the forecasting model.']
  is_override boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a revenue manager manually overrode the system-generated forecast values for this record. When true, the forecast reflects human judgment rather than pure algorithmic output. Used to track override frequency and assess model trust.']
  is_special_event boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the forecast date coincides with a special event (e.g., conference, concert, sporting event, public holiday) that is expected to materially impact demand. When true, the forecast may incorporate event-specific demand uplift factors.']
  mpi_forecast decimal(8,4) [note: 'Forecasted Market Penetration Index (MPI), representing the property\'s projected occupancy share relative to the competitive set\'s projected occupancy. An MPI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to capture more than its fair share of market demand.']
  projected_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property\'s local currency for the forecast date. Represents the expected average revenue per occupied room. Core revenue management KPI used to drive pricing strategy.']
  projected_cancellations decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted number of room-nights expected to cancel from the current on-books position before the forecast date. Used to calculate net demand and inform overbooking strategy.']
  projected_no_shows decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted number of room-nights expected to result in no-show arrivals on the forecast date. Used alongside cancellation forecasts to calibrate overbooking levels and protect revenue.']
  projected_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted occupancy rate expressed as a percentage of available rooms expected to be sold on the forecast date. Calculated as constrained demand divided by total available rooms. Key input to RevPAR and yield optimization decisions.']
  projected_pickup decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted incremental room-nights expected to be booked between the forecast run date and the forecast date. Represents the additional demand the RMS expects to materialize beyond rooms already on books.']
  projected_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property\'s local currency for the forecast date. Derived as projected ADR multiplied by projected occupancy rate. Primary yield optimization metric used by Revenue Management.']
  projected_room_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Forecasted total room revenue in the property\'s local currency for the forecast date. Calculated as projected rooms sold multiplied by projected ADR. Used for budget variance analysis and revenue strategy.']
  projected_rooms_available varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of rooms available for sale on the forecast date after accounting for out-of-order, out-of-service, and maintenance blocks. Denominator for occupancy and RevPAR calculations.']
  projected_rooms_sold varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of rooms expected to be sold (occupied) on the forecast date. Used for housekeeping scheduling, staffing planning, and inventory control decisions.']
  rgi_forecast decimal(8,4) [note: 'Forecasted Revenue Generation Index (RGI), representing the property\'s projected RevPAR relative to the competitive set\'s projected RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to outperform its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data integrated into Infor EzRMS.']
  rooms_on_books varchar [note: 'Number of rooms already confirmed (on books) for the forecast date at the time the forecast was generated. Represents the definite demand component; the RMS adds pickup forecast to arrive at total projected demand.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that generated this demand forecast record. \'IDeaS_G3_RMS\' indicates the IDeaS G3 Revenue Management System; \'Infor_EzRMS\' indicates the Infor EzRMS system; \'Manual\' indicates a revenue manager-entered forecast override.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the special event influencing demand on the forecast date (e.g., \'Super Bowl LIX\', \'New Year Eve\', \'City Marathon\'). Populated only when is_special_event is true.']
  unconstrained_demand decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total forecasted room demand (in room-nights) without any inventory capacity constraints applied. Represents true market demand as estimated by the RMS model. Used to identify demand compression and inform inventory control decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand forecast record was last modified in the Silver Layer, such as when a revised forecast supersedes a prior run for the same forecast date and segment.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking demand forecast records generated by IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS at the property, room type, and market segment level. Captures forecast date, forecast horizon, unconstrained demand, constrained demand, projected occupancy, projected ADR, projected RevPAR, forecast confidence interval, and forecast model version. Used to drive pricing decisions, inventory controls, and group evaluation.'
}

Table revenue.inventory_control {
  inventory_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_control']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdles, stop-sells) vary by channel. Revenue managers allocate inventory differently across direct, OTA, GDS channels based on profitability and strategy. Fundamental']
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied to derive the current_bar for this room type and stay date. Links to the pricing strategy configuration governing automated rate adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdle rates, overbooking caps) are adjusted based on event booking displacement analysis. Essential for inventory optimization around events and group block allocatio']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Inventory control decisions (stop-sell, hurdle rates, overbooking) impact revenue posting and require GL mapping for revenue optimization tracking and financial analysis.']
  primary_inventory_property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) to which this inventory control record applies. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration and IDeaS G3 RMS property setup.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) associated with this inventory control record. Determines the pricing tier and restrictions applied to the available inventory.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this inventory control record is defined. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration and IDeaS G3 RMS room class setup.']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers set inventory controls; tracking updater essential for override authority validation, compliance audit trails, and accountability for sell limits, hurdle rates, and overbooking decisi']
  allotment_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to contracted allotments (OTA allotments, wholesale, tour operator contracts). Managed via Sabre SynXis CRS channel management and reduces the freely-sellable transient inventory.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Designates the level of authority required to approve the overbooking level for this inventory control record. Ensures governance and accountability for overbooking decisions per property SOPs and SOX internal control requirements.']
  available_room_count varchar [note: 'Real-time count of rooms available for sale for this property, room type, stay date, and rate plan combination at the time of last update. Reflects physical inventory minus confirmed reservations, group blocks, and out-of-order rooms. Distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS for reservation fulfillment.']
  control_record_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this inventory control record as referenced in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this inventory control record indicating whether the room type/rate plan combination is open for sale, closed, restricted, pending review, or subject to a manual override. This is the real-time availability state distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the category of inventory control applied to this record. Determines how the control_value is interpreted and enforced by the Revenue Management System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurdle_rate|sell_limit|overbooking_level|los_restriction|cta_restriction|ctd_restriction|allotment|group_block — promote to reference product]']
  control_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The operative value for this control record as applied by the Revenue Management System. Interpretation depends on control_type: for hurdle_rate it is the minimum acceptable rate in local currency; for sell_limit it is the maximum rooms to sell; for overbooking_level it is the number of rooms above physical capacity authorized to sell.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory control record was first created in the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary rate fields in this record (current_bar, min_rate, max_rate, hurdle_rate). Ensures multi-currency properties report rates in the correct denomination.']
  current_bar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The current Best Available Rate (BAR) in local currency for this room type and stay date as published via Sabre SynXis CRS and channel distribution. Represents the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate and is the primary pricing signal for transient demand.']
  demand_forecast_rooms decimal(10,2) [note: 'IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS demand forecast for unconstrained room demand for this room type on the stay date at the time this control record was generated. Used to contextualize the control_value against projected demand and support RevPAR optimization.']
  effective_from_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time from which this inventory control record\'s control_value and restrictions became operative in the Revenue Management System. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical rate/availability grid reconstruction.']
  effective_until_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this inventory control record\'s control_value and restrictions ceased to be operative. Null for currently active records. Enables point-in-time historical analysis of the rate/availability grid.']
  group_block_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to group blocks (MICE, tour groups, corporate groups). Reduces the transient sell limit and is managed via Delphi by Amadeus group block management and IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation.']
  hurdle_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable rate (in local currency) below which a reservation for this room type on the stay date should not be accepted, as determined by the Revenue Management System. Used in displacement analysis and group evaluation in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  is_closed_to_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether arrivals are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Arrival / CTA restriction). When True, guests may not check in on this date but may stay through it as part of a longer booking.']
  is_closed_to_departure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Departure / CTD restriction). When True, guests may not check out on this date, requiring stays to extend beyond it.']
  is_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applied control_value represents a manual override of the system_recommended_value. True when a revenue manager has manually intervened; False when the system recommendation was accepted as-is. Critical for RMS model performance auditing.']
  last_distributed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory control record\'s rate and availability state was last successfully distributed to Sabre SynXis CRS and downstream channels (OTA, GDS). Critical for SLA monitoring and channel distribution reconciliation.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest may stay when booking this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. Used to prevent long-stay displacement of higher-value transient demand during peak periods.']
  max_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The ceiling rate in local currency above which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold. Used to enforce rate integrity, prevent rate gouging, and comply with local pricing regulations during high-demand periods.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction used to optimize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring longer stays.']
  min_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The absolute floor rate in local currency below which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold, regardless of channel or negotiated agreement. Enforced as a hard constraint in the Revenue Management System and CRS.']
  occupancy_on_books decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current confirmed occupancy rate (OCC) percentage for this room type on the stay date at the time of last update, calculated as confirmed reservations divided by physical_room_count. Key input to dynamic pricing and sell limit decisions in the Revenue Management System.']
  out_of_order_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date designated as out of order (OOO) due to maintenance, renovation, or FF&E replacement. These rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and occupancy calculations per USALI standards.']
  overbooking_absolute_cap varchar [note: 'Hard ceiling on the absolute number of rooms that may be overbooked above physical capacity for this room type on the stay date, regardless of the overbooking_pct calculation. Prevents excessive walk exposure during high-demand periods.']
  overbooking_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum authorized overbooking level expressed as a percentage above physical room capacity for this room type on the stay date. Defined in the overbooking policy and used by IDeaS G3 RMS to set the sell limit above physical_room_count.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded justification provided by the revenue manager when is_override is True. Documents the business rationale for deviating from the system recommendation (e.g., local event, competitive action, group displacement). Required for audit and RMS calibration.']
  physical_room_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical rooms of this room type at the property, representing the hard capacity ceiling before overbooking allowances are applied. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculations.']
  rate_availability_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or process that set the current rate and availability state for this control record. Supports data lineage, audit, and reconciliation between IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  sell_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms authorized to be sold for this room type on the stay date, inclusive of overbooking allowance. Set by the Revenue Management System and may exceed physical_room_count when overbooking is authorized. Enforced at the CRS and PMS booking engine.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date (night of stay) for which this inventory control record applies. Represents the primary dimension of the rate/availability grid alongside property, room type, and rate plan.']
  system_recommended_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The value recommended by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) prior to any manual override. Enables comparison between algorithmic recommendation and the applied control_value for yield optimization analysis and RMS model performance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory control record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  walk_compensation_type varchar [note: 'Type of compensation offered to a walked guest when the walk_policy requires compensation. Governs the financial and loyalty implications of overbooking execution and is tracked for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) and CPOR reporting.']
  walk_policy varchar [note: 'Defines the operational procedure to follow when a guest with a confirmed reservation cannot be accommodated due to overbooking (a \'walk\'). Determines whether the guest is walked to a partner hotel, upgraded, or compensated per AHLA guest relocation standards.']

  Note: 'Inventory control records managing the complete room availability and pricing grid as applied in IDeaS G3 RMS. Encompasses hurdle rates, sell limits, overbooking levels, room type allotments, group blocks, and the resulting daily rate availability state per property, room type, and rate plan combination. Captures control type, room type, stay date, control value, system-recommended value, override indicator, overbooking policy parameters (max overbooking percentage, absolute cap, walk policy, compensation type, partner hotel agreements, approval authority), and real-time availability state (open/closed status, available inventory count, current BAR, minimum/maximum rates, last-updated timestamp). This is the SSOT for all inventory control decisions, overbooking policy configuration, and the rate/availability grid distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS for reservation fulfillment and channel distribution.'
}

Table revenue.performance_actuals {
  performance_actuals_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_actuals']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: RevPAR and room revenue actuals are tracked by cost center for departmental P&L, USALI reporting, and operational performance management.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Daily actuals track which room nights and revenue came from specific event bookings for segment reporting. Required for event contribution analysis, MICE segment performance reporting, and group vs tr']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Actuals are compared against financial budgets for variance reporting, board presentations, and management performance evaluation against approved financial plans.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fiscal calendar period (month/quarter/year) to which this performance date belongs, enabling USALI-aligned financial period reporting and variance analysis against budget.']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue performance reporting includes GSS scores as a KPI alongside financial metrics for balanced scorecards (SALT program requirement). Daily flash reports show RevPAR and GSS together—mandatory fo']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Actual revenue performance posts to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and external financial reporting compliance.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Performance reporting breaks down revenue contribution by member tier for loyalty program ROI analysis. Business process: tier-level revenue attribution reporting required for evaluating loyalty progr']
  primary_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Daily actuals track outlet-level F&B revenue for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for total property revenue reporting, GOP analysis, outlet performance attribution in consolidated fi']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Property performance actuals roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial reporting, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level performance analysis.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this daily revenue performance record is captured. Links to the property master entity in the Property domain.']
  reconciled_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Finance/revenue analysts reconcile daily actuals; employee attribution required for data quality accountability, reconciliation workflow tracking, and performance evaluation of financial reporting acc']
  adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate in the property\'s operating currency, calculated as total room revenue divided by rooms sold (excluding complimentary). Core KPI for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, ARI, and MPI calculations.']
  ancillary_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total ancillary revenue on the performance date from upsells, packages, resort fees, and add-on services not captured in departmental revenue lines. Supports total revenue and TRevPAR reporting.']
  ari decimal(6,4) [note: 'Average Rate Index representing the property\'s ADR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. ARI > 1.0 indicates the property is achieving a rate premium over its comp set.']
  budget_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Average Daily Rate for the performance date. Used for ADR variance analysis against actuals in the DOR, revenue management reporting, and STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  budget_occupancy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Budgeted occupancy rate for the performance date as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%). Used for occupancy variance analysis against actuals in the DOR and revenue management reporting.']
  budget_room_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Budgeted room revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for variance analysis against actual room revenue in the DOR and ownership reporting.']
  budget_total_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Budgeted total property revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for total revenue variance analysis in the DOR and ownership reporting.']
  channel_direct_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold through direct channels (brand website, call center, walk-in) on the performance date. Supports direct vs. indirect channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  channel_ota_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold through Online Travel Agency (OTA) channels (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) on the performance date. Supports channel distribution mix analysis and OTA commission cost management.']
  cpor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost Per Occupied Room on the performance date, calculated as total rooms department operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures operational efficiency of the rooms department per USALI.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance actual record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this performance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency property portfolio reporting and consolidation.']
  demand_forecast_rooms varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of rooms to be sold on the performance date as generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Used for variance analysis between forecast and actual rooms sold.']
  ebitda_contribution decimal(14,2) [note: 'Property-level EBITDA contribution on the performance date, representing operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Used for ownership reporting, asset valuation, and management contract performance measurement.']
  fb_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total Food and Beverage revenue generated on the performance date across all F&B outlets (restaurants, bars, banquets, in-room dining). Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA. Included in TRevPAR calculation per USALI.']
  gop decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross Operating Profit on the performance date, calculated as total operated department revenue minus total operated department expenses per USALI. Key profitability metric for ownership reporting and management contracts.']
  goppar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room in the property\'s operating currency, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency. Core KPI for ownership reporting and management fee calculations.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this daily performance actual record has been reconciled between Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. True = reconciled and approved for reporting; False = pending reconciliation. Supports financial close and SOX controls.']
  miscellaneous_income decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total miscellaneous and rental income on the performance date including rentals, commissions, and other non-departmental income per USALI non-operating income classification.']
  mpi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Market Penetration Index representing the property\'s occupancy performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. MPI > 1.0 indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of market occupancy.']
  occupancy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Daily occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%), calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Core KPI for the STR STAR Report, DOR, and ownership reporting. Stored as a raw ratio; display layer applies percentage formatting.']
  other_operated_dept_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total revenue from all other operated departments on the performance date (e.g., golf, retail, recreation, business center) excluding rooms, F&B, spa, and parking. Classified per USALI other operated departments schedule.']
  parking_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total parking revenue generated on the performance date from valet, self-park, and garage operations. Classified under other operated departments per USALI and included in TRevPAR.']
  performance_date date [note: 'The calendar date (business date) for which this revenue performance actual record applies. Represents the stay date or operating day used in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and all KPI calculations.']
  prior_year_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'RevPAR for the same calendar date in the prior year. Used for year-over-year (YOY) variance analysis in the DOR, STR STAR Report benchmarking, and ownership reporting.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this performance actual record indicating whether figures are preliminary (intra-day), final (end-of-day close), adjusted (post-close correction), or closed (period-locked). Supports audit and reconciliation workflows.']
  revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room in the property\'s operating currency, calculated as ADR multiplied by occupancy rate (or total room revenue divided by rooms available). Primary rooms revenue KPI for ownership reporting, STR STAR Report, and RGI benchmarking.']
  rgi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Revenue Generation Index representing the property\'s RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. RGI > 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its comp set. Core competitive benchmarking KPI.']
  rooms_available varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms available for sale on the performance date after deducting out-of-order (OOO) and out-of-service (OOS) rooms. Denominator for Occupancy Rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations per USALI and STR STAR Report standards.']
  rooms_complimentary varchar [note: 'Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary (no-charge) basis on the performance date. Tracked separately per USALI for accurate ADR and revenue reporting, as complimentary rooms are excluded from ADR calculation.']
  rooms_out_of_order varchar [note: 'Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the performance date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Excluded from rooms available per USALI inventory standards.']
  rooms_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the performance date, including complimentary rooms. Used to calculate Occupancy Rate (OCC) and Average Daily Rate (ADR) per USALI standards.']
  segment_contract_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold under negotiated contract rates (airline crew, long-stay corporate contracts) on the performance date. Supports contract segment revenue analysis and channel mix reporting.']
  segment_group_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold to group accounts (MICE, corporate groups, tour groups) on the performance date. Supports group vs. transient mix analysis and group revenue contribution reporting per USALI.']
  segment_transient_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms sold to transient (individual/FIT) guests on the performance date. Supports segment-level revenue analysis and channel mix reporting per USALI market segmentation standards.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this performance actual record was sourced or reconciled (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS for rooms data, SAP S/4HANA for financial totals). Supports data lineage and reconciliation audits.']
  spa_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total spa and wellness revenue generated on the performance date. Included in total property revenue and TRevPAR calculation per USALI other operated departments classification.']
  total_property_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue across all operated departments and income streams on the performance date (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous + ancillary). Denominator basis for TRevPAR. Reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL per USALI.']
  total_room_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross room revenue generated on the performance date across all rate plans and channels, before deductions. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL. Numerator for ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI.']
  trevpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total Revenue Per Available Room in the property\'s operating currency, calculated as total property revenue divided by rooms available. Measures total revenue productivity of the property beyond rooms. Core KPI for ownership and management reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance actual record was last modified, including post-close adjustments or reconciliation corrections. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Daily revenue performance actuals at the property level capturing all revenue streams aligned to USALI departmental reporting. Includes room revenue metrics (ADR, OCC, RevPAR, rooms sold, rooms available, total room revenue) and total property revenue metrics (F&B revenue, spa revenue, parking, other operated departments, miscellaneous income, ancillary revenue, TRevPAR, GOPPAR, CPOR, GOP, EBITDA contribution). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA, reconciled against USALI standards. This is the single authoritative record for all daily revenue performance — both rooms and total revenue — used for the Daily Operations Report (DOR), ownership reporting, TRevPAR/GOPPAR calculation, and variance analysis against budget.'
}

Table revenue.market_segment {
  market_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the market segment record. Primary key for the market_segment data product in the revenue domain. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE — this entity represents a classification resource used across revenue, reservation, and channel domains.']
  default_loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Market segments map to typical loyalty tiers for segmentation alignment and forecasting. Business process: aligning revenue management segments with loyalty tier definitions for consistent guest class']
  loyalty_member_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_segment. Business justification: Revenue market segments correlate with loyalty member segments for unified guest profiling. Business process: cross-domain segmentation mapping enables consistent guest targeting across revenue optimi']
  preferred_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Market segments have outlet preferences/restrictions (corporate guests → business center cafe, leisure → poolside bar). Required for outlet positioning strategy, menu design, targeted marketing, guest']
  ada_accommodation_typical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests in this market segment typically require ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible accommodations. Relevant for government, medical, and certain group segments. Informs room inventory allocation and accessibility compliance reporting.']
  avg_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days between booking date and arrival date for this market segment. Key input for demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. Segments with short lead times (e.g., OTA last-minute) require different yield strategies than long-lead corporate or group segments. Represents the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this resource.']
  avg_length_of_stay_nights decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of nights guests in this segment stay per booking (ALOS — Average Length of Stay). Used in revenue management for LOS (Length of Stay) restriction optimization, demand forecasting, and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  cancellation_propensity varchar [note: 'Qualitative classification of the historical cancellation rate tendency for this market segment. Informs overbooking strategy, no-show reserve calculations, and revenue protection policies in IDeaS G3 RMS. High-propensity segments (e.g., OTA flexible rate) require higher overbooking buffers.']
  channel_affinity varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel through which this market segment predominantly books. Drives channel strategy, OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission management, and GDS (Global Distribution System) rate loading decisions in Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|ota|gds|voice|group|wholesale|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings in this segment are subject to travel agent or OTA commission payments. Direct corporate and government segments are typically non-commissionable; OTA and GDS segments carry commission obligations. Drives accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate percentage applicable to bookings in this segment when commission_eligible is true. Expressed as a percentage of room revenue (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, OTA contract management, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations. Confidential as it reflects contracted commercial terms.']
  contribution_margin_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment\'s typical contribution margin profile relative to other segments. Reflects net revenue after channel commissions, OTA fees, GDS fees, and loyalty redemption costs. Premium tiers (e.g., direct corporate) yield higher GOP (Gross Operating Profit) than low-margin OTA or wholesale segments. Used in GOPPAR analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market segment record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record provenance tracking. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for MASTER_RESOURCE entities.']
  crs_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for central reservations and channel distribution. Used for rate plan distribution, availability controls, and channel-level segment reporting. Enables cross-system segment reconciliation.']
  demand_pattern varchar [note: 'Classification of the temporal demand pattern exhibited by this market segment. Corporate transient segments typically show weekday concentration; leisure segments show weekend and seasonal peaks; event-driven segments spike around MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) events. Used in Infor EzRMS demand forecasting.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this market segment definition becomes active and available for use in reservations, rate plans, and revenue reporting. Supports temporal validity management for segment taxonomy changes aligned with fiscal year or STR reporting period updates.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this market segment definition is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Supports segment lifecycle management and historical reporting continuity when segment taxonomies are revised.']
  gdpr_data_subject_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest data associated with bookings in this market segment is subject to GDPR data subject rights (right to erasure, portability, access). Relevant for segments involving EU/EEA guests. Drives data retention and anonymization policies in the data lakehouse.']
  gds_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. Controls rate loading and availability display in Sabre SynXis CRS for GDS-connected travel agencies.']
  group_block_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment supports group room block reservations. Group-eligible segments (e.g., MICE, sports groups, tour groups) require group block management in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus for BEO (Banquet Event Order) coordination.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this segment within the market segment hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level categories (e.g., Transient, Group); Level 2 represents sub-categories (e.g., Corporate, Leisure); Level 3 represents granular segments (e.g., Corporate Negotiated, Leisure OTA). Used for hierarchical roll-up in revenue reporting.']
  is_internal_use_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment is for internal reporting and analytics purposes only and should not be exposed to guests, OTA partners, or external distribution channels. Internal segments (e.g., complimentary, house use, employee rate) are excluded from external rate distribution in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  loyalty_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings under this market segment are eligible to earn loyalty program points and benefits. Wholesale, crew, and certain contracted segments may be excluded from loyalty accrual per program rules. Drives loyalty point calculation logic in Salesforce CRM loyalty management.']
  market_segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its defining characteristics, typical guest profile, booking behavior, and applicable rate strategies. Used for training, documentation, and business glossary alignment across revenue management, reservations, and sales teams.']
  market_segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the market segment record. Active segments are available for rate plan assignment, reservation coding, and revenue reporting. Inactive or deprecated segments are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new bookings. Aligns with MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  min_rate_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue managers are permitted to override the minimum rate floor for this segment. Restricting overrides for high-value segments (e.g., LRA corporate) protects rate integrity. Configurable in IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls and Oracle OPERA PMS rate management.']
  negotiated_rate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings in this segment require a pre-negotiated rate agreement (e.g., corporate account rate, government per diem, airline crew contract). When true, a valid rate agreement must exist before reservations can be accepted at segment rates in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  no_show_propensity varchar [note: 'Qualitative classification of the historical no-show rate tendency for this market segment. Distinct from cancellation propensity — no-shows occur without prior cancellation and have different revenue recovery implications. Used in overbooking model calibration within IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  ota_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Expedia, Booking.com, and Hotels.com. Controls channel availability in Sabre SynXis CRS and impacts OTA commission cost tracking.']
  pci_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment card data associated with bookings in this segment falls within PCI DSS scope. Segments with direct card-present or card-not-present transactions require PCI-compliant data handling. Drives data masking and tokenization requirements in the lakehouse.']
  pms_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reservation coding, folio routing, and rate plan assignment at the property level. Enables reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational PMS source system.']
  price_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment\'s sensitivity to rate changes. Inelastic segments (e.g., airline crew, government contracted) book regardless of rate fluctuations. Highly elastic segments (e.g., leisure OTA) respond strongly to price changes. Drives DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) configuration in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  rate_plan_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan type typically associated with this segment. BAR (Best Available Rate) for transient leisure, negotiated for corporate accounts, LRA (Last Room Availability) for top-tier corporate, NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) for advance purchase segments. Drives rate loading in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|negotiated|lra|nrr|wholesale|package|complimentary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revenue_bucket varchar [note: 'Primary revenue category associated with this market segment for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) and USALI departmental reporting. Rooms-focused segments contribute primarily to rooms revenue; MICE segments contribute across rooms, F&B (Food and Beverage), and events revenue buckets.']
  rms_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Used for demand forecasting, pricing optimization, and inventory control at the segment level. May differ from PMS segment code due to system-specific taxonomy requirements.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping the segment into a primary demand category. Drives pricing strategy, channel allocation, and revenue reporting roll-ups. Aligns with STR segment category definitions used in STAR Report benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|contract|wholesale|ota|corporate|government|leisure|crew|airline — promote to reference product]']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the market segment, used as the externally-known business identifier across Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS. Examples: CORP, TRANS, LEIS, OTA, GRP, GOVT, CREW. Aligns with STR segment code taxonomy for benchmarking and STAR Report mapping.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the market segment as displayed in reporting, dashboards, and revenue management systems. Examples: Corporate Negotiated, Leisure Transient, Online Travel Agency, Government/Military, Group Meetings. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this resource entity.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Secondary classification distinguishing whether the segment represents individual (FIT — Free Independent Traveler) bookings, group blocks, negotiated corporate accounts, wholesale/tour operator contracts, or consortia arrangements. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS for demand segmentation and pricing strategy.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of market segments in reports, dashboards, and system configuration screens. Ensures consistent ordering in Oracle OPERA PMS segment lists, IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration, and BI reporting tools.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this market segment definition originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS segment taxonomies in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  str_segment_code varchar [note: 'The official STR (Smith Travel Research) segment code used for competitive benchmarking and STAR Report alignment. Enables mapping of internal segment definitions to the industry-standard STR taxonomy for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market segment record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes to segment definitions, status updates, and taxonomy revisions. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines feeding IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.']
  usali_segment_code varchar [note: 'The USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) standardized segment code used for financial reporting and departmental P&L allocation. Ensures consistency in GOP (Gross Operating Profit), NOI (Net Operating Income), and EBITDA reporting across properties and for external financial audits under GAAP/IFRS.']

  Note: 'Master classification of revenue market segments used for demand analysis, pricing strategy, and performance reporting. Defines segment code, segment name, segment category (transient, group, contract, wholesale, OTA, corporate, government, leisure, crew, airline), contribution margin profile, booking behavior characteristics, average lead time, and cancellation propensity. SSOT for market segment taxonomy aligned to STR segment definitions and used across revenue, reservation, and channel domains.'
}

Table revenue.competitive_set {
  competitive_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competitive_set']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers define competitive sets; employee tracking required for strategic decision audit trail, comp set performance evaluation, and accountability for benchmarking configuration in revenue s']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Competitive benchmarking (RGI, MPI, ARI targets) informs budget planning and strategic financial targets for annual budget approval and strategic planning.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Competitive intelligence gathering must comply with antitrust and data privacy policies. Comp set approval validates legal compliance before rate shopping activation. Essential for competitive intelli']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subject property (the \'home\' property) that owns this competitive set definition. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  advance_purchase_window_days varchar [note: 'The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons within this competitive set (e.g., 0 = same-day, 7 = 7-day advance, 30 = 30-day advance). Ensures rate intelligence is captured at consistent booking horizons for fair ADR comparison.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the competitive set definition was formally approved by the revenue management authority. Supports audit trail requirements for comp set governance and STR STAR Report submission integrity.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the revenue management leader or Director of Revenue who approved this competitive set definition. Required for governance and audit trail of comp set changes that affect STR STAR Report submissions.']
  ari_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Average Rate Index (ARI) target threshold for this competitive set. ARI = Property ADR / Comp Set ADR. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets a higher ADR than the competitive set average. Drives BAR pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  benchmarking_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for normalizing ADR, RevPAR, and TRevPAR benchmarking metrics across all properties in the competitive set (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Ensures consistent monetary comparison in STR STAR Report analysis.']
  competitive_set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the competitive set (e.g., \'Downtown Luxury Primary Set\', \'Airport Corridor Secondary Set\'). Used in Infor EzRMS and STR STAR Report submissions to identify the grouping.']
  competitive_set_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competitive set. \'active\' sets are used in live RMS benchmarking and STR reporting; \'inactive\' sets are suspended; \'pending_approval\' awaits revenue management sign-off; \'archived\' are historical sets retained for trend analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitive set record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  demand_segment_focus varchar [note: 'The primary guest demand segment this competitive set is designed to benchmark. \'transient\' focuses on FIT (Free Independent Traveler) and leisure; \'group\' focuses on MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) and group blocks; \'contract\' focuses on negotiated corporate rates; \'all_segments\' covers the full mix.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this competitive set definition becomes active and is used for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, MPI, and ARI calculations. Aligns with STR reporting period start.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this competitive set definition expires and is no longer used for active benchmarking. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active set. Supports historical trend analysis when comp set membership changes.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or submarket in which the competitive set operates (e.g., \'Manhattan Midtown\', \'Chicago O\'Hare Corridor\', \'Miami Beach\'). Aligns with STR market definitions used for Market Penetration Index (MPI) calculations.']
  ideass_comp_set_code varchar [note: 'The competitive set reference code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS for pricing strategy alignment. Enables synchronization of comp set definitions between Infor EzRMS benchmarking and IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing rules (DRR) and BAR optimization.']
  is_str_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competitive set has been formally submitted to STR (Smith Travel Research) for STAR Report benchmarking. True means the set is registered with STR and receives STAR Report data; False means it is an internal-only comp set.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the competitive set membership and parameters were reviewed by the revenue management team. Best practice requires annual or semi-annual review to ensure comp set relevance as market conditions evolve.']
  los_benchmark_nights varchar [note: 'The standard Length of Stay (LOS) in nights used as the basis for rate comparison within this competitive set. Typically set to 1 night for transient benchmarking. Used to normalize ADR comparisons across properties with different LOS mix.']
  market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary geographic market for this competitive set (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Used for multi-national portfolio benchmarking and currency normalization in revenue performance reporting.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Hotel chain scale segment classification of the competitive set as defined by STR. Ensures that the comp set is composed of properties within the same or adjacent competitive tier. Used to validate comp set composition integrity for RGI and MPI calculations.']
  max_room_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guestrooms a competitor property may have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Used alongside min_room_count to define the acceptable size range for comp set membership.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of competitor properties currently included in this competitive set. STR STAR Report typically requires a minimum of 3 and maximum of 10 competitor properties for statistically valid benchmarking. Derived from the comp set membership roster but stored here for quick reference.']
  min_room_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of guestrooms a competitor property must have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Ensures size comparability for RevPAR and occupancy benchmarking. Typically aligned with the subject property\'s room count range.']
  mpi_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Market Penetration Index (MPI) target threshold for this competitive set. MPI = Property Occupancy Rate / Comp Set Occupancy Rate. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets higher occupancy than the competitive set average. Used in revenue strategy planning.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this competitive set definition. Ensures comp set membership remains current and aligned with market dynamics. Triggers a review workflow in the Revenue Management System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for revenue management team annotations regarding this competitive set, such as rationale for competitor inclusion/exclusion, market context, or pending changes. Not used in automated benchmarking calculations.']
  primary_benchmarking_metric varchar [note: 'The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to evaluate competitive performance for this comp set. Options include RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room), ADR (Average Daily Rate), Occupancy Rate (OCC), TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room), or GOPPAR (Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room).']
  property_type_filter varchar [note: 'Filter defining the property service type composition of this competitive set. Ensures benchmarking is conducted against properties of comparable service level and amenity offering. Aligns with AHLA property classification standards.']
  rate_shop_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which competitor rates are retrieved from OTA and GDS channels for properties in this competitive set. Drives the cadence of dynamic pricing adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS. Only applicable when rate_shopping_enabled is True.']
  rate_shopping_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated rate shopping (competitive rate intelligence) is active for this competitive set. When True, the RMS retrieves competitor BAR rates from OTA channels and GDS for dynamic pricing decisions. When False, only STR STAR Report data is used.']
  rgi_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target threshold set by the revenue management team for this competitive set. RGI = Property RevPAR / Comp Set RevPAR. A target above 1.00 indicates the property aims to outperform the comp set. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS for performance goal-setting.']
  rms_comp_set_reference varchar [note: 'The native competitive set identifier as stored in the Revenue Management System (Infor EzRMS or IDeaS G3 RMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational RMS.']
  set_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitive set indicating its strategic purpose. \'primary\' is the main STR STAR Report comp set; \'secondary\' is an alternate benchmarking group; \'custom\' is an ad-hoc internal set; \'str_defined\' is a set defined by STR directly; \'internal\' is used for internal rate intelligence only.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this competitive set definition was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. \'infor_ezrms\' = Infor EzRMS; \'ideass_g3\' = IDeaS G3 RMS; \'manual\' = manually entered; \'str_direct\' = received directly from STR.']
  str_comp_set_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric code assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to identify this competitive set in the STAR Report system. Required for submitting and retrieving benchmarking data from STR. Maps directly to the STR comp set identifier in Infor EzRMS.']
  str_market_code varchar [note: 'The STR-assigned market code identifying the geographic market in which this competitive set is benchmarked. Used to retrieve market-level aggregates for MPI calculation and to align with STR STAR Report market definitions.']
  str_report_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which STR STAR Report data is received and processed for this competitive set. Determines the cadence of RGI, MPI, and ARI metric refreshes in the Revenue Management System.']
  str_submarket_code varchar [note: 'The STR-assigned submarket code for more granular geographic benchmarking within a broader STR market. Used when the competitive set operates in a distinct submarket (e.g., airport vs. downtown within the same city market).']
  str_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this competitive set was submitted to STR for STAR Report registration. Relevant only when is_str_submitted is True. Used for audit and compliance tracking of STR data agreements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitive set record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes in comp set definitions, track membership updates, and support incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master definition of competitive hotel sets used for STR STAR Report benchmarking and competitive rate intelligence. Captures comp set name, property membership, comp set type (primary, secondary, custom), effective date range, and STR property codes. Managed in Infor EzRMS and used to calculate RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index).'
}

Table revenue.str_benchmark {
  str_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for str_benchmark']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'The identifier for the competitive set (comp set) as defined in the STR STAR Report submission. A comp set is the group of competitor properties against which the subject property is benchmarked for ADR, occupancy, and RevPAR performance.']
  imported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue analysts import and validate STR benchmark data; employee attribution required for data quality accountability, submission workflow tracking, and performance evaluation of competitive intellig']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: STR benchmark data can be segmented by market segment for competitive analysis. str_benchmark has market_segment (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N str_benchmark records → 1 market_segment. ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property for which this STR benchmark or rate shop record applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  ari decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Average Rate Index (ARI) measures the subject property\'s ADR (Average Daily Rate) relative to its STR competitive set. An ARI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is achieving a higher average rate than its comp set peers.']
  benchmark_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all STR STAR Report monetary benchmark values (ADR, RevPAR) are reported for this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  benchmark_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this benchmark or rate shop record. \'active\' = current valid record; \'superseded\' = replaced by a revised STR submission; \'pending_review\' = awaiting data quality validation; \'rejected\' = failed data quality checks.']
  comp_set_adr decimal(12,2) [note: 'The aggregated Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the ARI and assess the property\'s rate positioning relative to the market.']
  comp_set_occupancy_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The aggregated occupancy rate (OCC) of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the MPI and assess the property\'s demand capture relative to the market.']
  comp_set_revpar decimal(12,2) [note: 'The aggregated RevPAR of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the RGI and assess the property\'s overall revenue performance relative to the market.']
  comp_set_rooms_available varchar [note: 'The total number of rooms available (supply) across all properties in the STR competitive set during the benchmark report period. Used as the denominator for comp set RevPAR and occupancy calculations.']
  competitor_property_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or code assigned to the competitor property within the rate shopping tool or STR system (e.g., STR property code, OTA Insight property ID). Used for consistent competitor tracking across rate shop cycles.']
  competitor_property_name varchar [note: 'The name of the competitor hotel or resort property for which a rate shop observation was captured. Populated only for records with record_type = \'competitor_rate_shop\'. Sourced from OTA Insight or RateGain rate shopping tools.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The originating data source or feed from which this benchmark or rate shop record was ingested. Identifies whether the record came from the STR STAR Report data feed, OTA Insight, RateGain rate shopping tool, Infor EzRMS, or manual entry.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the competitor rate was observed during rate shopping. Key channels include OTA (Online Travel Agency), direct (hotel website), GDS (Global Distribution System), brand website, and voice. Enables channel-level competitive rate analysis.']
  ingested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this STR benchmark or rate shop record was ingested into the silver layer lakehouse from the source data feed (STR, OTA Insight, RateGain, or Infor EzRMS). Used for data lineage, SLA monitoring, and ETL audit.']
  is_rate_parity_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopped competitor rate is in compliance with rate parity agreements across distribution channels. True = rate parity maintained; False = rate disparity detected. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between the shop date and the stay date at the time the competitor rate was captured. A key dimension for rate shop analysis — rates typically vary by lead time (e.g., 0-day, 7-day, 30-day, 90-day windows).']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'The Length of Stay (LOS) in nights for which the competitor rate was shopped. Rate shopping tools capture rates for specific LOS windows (e.g., 1-night, 2-night, 7-night) as rates may vary by LOS due to minimum stay restrictions.']
  market_location_type varchar [note: 'The STR-defined market location type for the subject property or competitor property (e.g., urban, suburban, airport, resort, small metro, interstate). Used to contextualize benchmark performance within the appropriate market geography.']
  mpi decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Market Penetration Index (MPI) measures the subject property\'s occupancy rate relative to its STR competitive set. An MPI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of available demand.']
  ota_platform_name varchar [note: 'The specific OTA platform name from which the competitor rate was shopped (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com, Hotels.com, Agoda). Populated when distribution_channel = \'OTA\'. Enables OTA-level competitive rate parity analysis.']
  property_adr decimal(12,2) [note: 'The subject property\'s Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the benchmark report period, expressed in the reporting currency. ADR is calculated as total room revenue divided by total rooms sold, per USALI standards.']
  property_occupancy_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The subject property\'s occupancy rate (OCC) for the benchmark report period, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%). Calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available per USALI standards.']
  property_revpar decimal(12,2) [note: 'The subject property\'s Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the benchmark report period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. A primary KPI for hotel revenue performance per USALI and STR standards.']
  property_rooms_available varchar [note: 'The total number of rooms available (supply) at the subject property during the benchmark report period. Used as the denominator for RevPAR and occupancy calculations per USALI standards.']
  rate_availability_status varchar [note: 'The availability status of the competitor rate at the time of the rate shop observation. Indicates whether the rate was available for booking, sold out, restricted (e.g., minimum stay), on request, or not shopped for this combination.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'The type of rate plan under which the competitor rate was shopped or the benchmark rate applies. Includes BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), package, corporate, group, and promotional rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|LRA|NRR|package|corporate|group|promotional|other — promote to reference product]']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an STR STAR Report benchmark index record or a granular competitor rate shopping observation. Enables partitioned querying across both data types within this unified product.']
  report_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the STR STAR Report benchmark period for which the benchmark indices and performance metrics are reported.']
  report_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the STR STAR Report benchmark period (e.g., start of week, month, or year-to-date window) for which the benchmark indices and performance metrics are reported.']
  report_period_type varchar [note: 'The temporal granularity of the STR STAR Report benchmark period. Determines whether the record represents a daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, or year-to-date benchmark snapshot.']
  rgi decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) measures the subject property\'s RevPAR performance relative to its STR competitive set. An RGI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is outperforming its comp set on RevPAR. Sourced from STR STAR Report.']
  room_type_category varchar [note: 'The standardized room type category for which the competitor rate was shopped (e.g., standard, deluxe, suite). Enables like-for-like rate comparison across competitor properties. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|deluxe|suite|junior_suite|executive|connecting|accessible|villa|other — promote to reference product]']
  shop_date date [note: 'The date on which the rate shopping observation was captured from the competitor channel. The difference between shop_date and stay_date defines the booking lead time for competitive rate analysis.']
  shopped_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The publicly available rate amount captured for the competitor property during the rate shopping observation, expressed in the shopped rate currency. Represents the per-night room rate for the specified stay date, room type, and rate plan.']
  shopped_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the shopped competitor rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the benchmark currency if the competitor property operates in a different market.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific arrival or stay date for which the competitor rate was shopped. Represents the future date of intended guest stay, enabling forward-looking competitive rate analysis and demand forecasting.']
  str_property_code varchar [note: 'The unique property identifier assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to the subject property. Used to match and reconcile STR STAR Report submissions and benchmark data feeds.']
  str_report_version varchar [note: 'The version number of the STR STAR Report data file or submission from which this benchmark record was sourced. STR may issue revised reports for the same period; this field tracks which version of the report was ingested.']
  str_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the property submitted its performance data to STR for inclusion in the STAR Report benchmark cycle. Used for data reconciliation and audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this record was last modified in the silver layer, such as when a revised STR STAR Report submission supersedes a prior record or when a data quality correction is applied.']

  Note: 'Competitive intelligence records combining STR STAR Report benchmark indices and granular competitor rate shopping data. Stores RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), ARI (Average Rate Index), property vs comp set ADR/occupancy/RevPAR, report period, and STR submission date from STR data feed. Also captures all shopped competitor rates by competitor property, room type category, rate plan type, shopped rate amount, currency, stay date, shop date, lead time, channel (OTA, direct, GDS), and data source. Sourced from STR (Smith Travel Research) and rate shopping tools (OTA Insight, RateGain) into Infor EzRMS. This is the single authoritative product for all competitive rate intelligence and market benchmarking.'
}

Table revenue.competitor_rate {
  competitor_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each competitor rate intelligence record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.competitive_set. Business justification: Competitor rate shopping is organized by competitive sets. competitor_rate currently has competitive_set_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N competitor_rate records belong to 1 competiti']
