  competitor_property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitor property record against which this rate was shopped. Links to the competitor property master.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Rate shopping activities must comply with terms-of-service and data privacy policies. Rate shopping program requires legal approval to ensure compliant data collection methods. Essential for competiti']
  shopped_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue analysts conduct competitor rate shopping; employee attribution required for competitive intelligence workflow tracking, data quality accountability, and performance evaluation of rate shoppin']
  ari decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average Rate Index (ARI) calculated as Travel Hospitality\'s ADR divided by the competitive set\'s ADR for the same period. ARI > 1.0 indicates rate premium over the competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report or computed from shopped rates.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Availability status of the competitor room type and rate plan at the time of the shop. Indicates whether the rate was bookable (available), the room type was sold out, or the rate was closed to arrival. Used in demand and compression analysis.']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Cancellation policy associated with the shopped competitor rate. Distinguishes refundable (flexible) rates from NRR (Non-Refundable Rates), which is a key dimension in rate plan competitive analysis.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Specific name of the booking channel or OTA platform where the rate was shopped (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com, Hotels.com, Marriott.com, Amadeus GDS). Provides granular channel-level competitive intelligence beyond the channel category.']
  channel_shopped varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the competitor rate was observed. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com), GDS = Global Distribution System, direct = competitor brand website. Enables channel-level rate parity and competitive positioning analysis.']
  competitor_property_code varchar [note: 'Externally recognized alphanumeric code identifying the competitor property as defined in the competitive set configuration within Infor EzRMS or STR STAR Report. Used for benchmarking alignment.']
  competitor_property_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the competitor hotel or resort property as it appears in the competitive set or OTA listing. Supports reporting and analyst review.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the competitor property is located (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA). Supports geographic segmentation and multi-market competitive analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competitor rate record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring for competitive intelligence data freshness.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the shopped rate is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency normalization in global competitive benchmarking.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method by which the competitor rate data was obtained. Infor EzRMS automated shop, STR STAR Report feed, OTA scrape, GDS feed, manual entry, or third-party rate intelligence API. Affects data quality and freshness assessment.']
  data_source_reference varchar [note: 'Specific URL, feed identifier, or external reference from the data source where the competitor rate was retrieved (e.g., OTA listing URL, STR report batch ID, GDS transaction ID). Supports data lineage and auditability.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of the week corresponding to the stay date (MON through SUN). Pre-computed to support day-of-week rate pattern analysis and weekday vs. weekend competitive pricing strategies without requiring date function computation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI|SAT|SUN — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fees_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory fees (resort fees, destination fees, amenity fees) are included in the shopped rate (True) or excluded (False). Affects true cost comparison for rate parity and competitive positioning analysis.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay date falls on a weekend (Friday or Saturday night, True) or a weekday (False). Supports weekend vs. weekday rate positioning analysis and demand segmentation.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the shop date and the stay date (stay_date minus shop_date). Key metric in revenue management for understanding booking window pricing behavior and demand curve positioning.']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'The number of nights for which the shopped rate applies, representing the Length of Stay (LOS) restriction or assumption used during the rate shop. Affects rate comparability across competitors.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'STR-defined or internal market code representing the geographic market in which the competitor property operates (e.g., NYC, MIAMI, LONDON). Aligns with STR STAR Report market definitions for RGI and MPI benchmarking.']
  meal_plan_code varchar [note: 'Meal plan included in the shopped rate. RO = Room Only, BB = Bed and Breakfast, HB = Half Board, FB = Full Board, AI = All Inclusive. Affects rate comparability and value positioning analysis.']
  occupancy_assumption varchar [note: 'Number of guests (occupancy) assumed when the rate was shopped (typically 1 or 2 adults). Affects rate comparability as some competitors price differently by occupancy.']
  our_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Travel Hospitality\'s own Best Available Rate (BAR) for the equivalent room type, stay date, and channel at the time of the shop. Enables direct rate gap and rate index calculations without requiring a join to the rate plan table.']
  previous_shopped_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The competitor rate captured in the immediately preceding shop for the same property, stay date, room type, and channel. Enables rate change delta calculation and trend analysis without requiring a self-join.']
  property_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the competitor property (luxury, premium, select-service, extended stay, resort). Enables segment-level competitive benchmarking aligned with Travel Hospitality\'s own portfolio segmentation.']
  rate_change_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of rate movement compared to the previous shop for the same competitor, stay date, room type, and channel. Supports rate velocity tracking and competitive pricing alerts in Infor EzRMS.']
  rate_delta decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute difference between the current shopped rate and the previous shopped rate (shopped_rate minus previous_shopped_rate). Positive value indicates a rate increase; negative indicates a decrease. Supports competitive pricing alert thresholds in RMS.']
  rate_gap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between Travel Hospitality\'s own rate and the competitor\'s shopped rate (our_rate minus shopped_rate). Positive value indicates our rate is higher than the competitor; negative indicates we are priced below. Core metric for rate positioning decisions.']
  rate_in_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Competitor shopped rate converted to US Dollars (USD) using the exchange rate applicable at the time of the shop. Enables normalized cross-currency competitive benchmarking and global RevPAR index calculations.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the competitor rate plan as displayed on the booking channel (e.g., \'Advance Purchase 21 Days\', \'Flexible Rate\', \'Member Exclusive\'). Provides context for rate plan classification.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitor rate plan type shopped. BAR = Best Available Rate, NRR = Non-Refundable Rate. Enables comparison of like-for-like rate plan positioning across the competitive set.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this competitor rate record in the Silver Layer. Active = current valid record; superseded = replaced by a newer shop for the same key dimensions; invalid = data quality issue flagged; pending_review = awaiting analyst validation.']
  room_type_category varchar [note: 'Standardized room type category used for cross-competitor comparison (e.g., Standard, Deluxe, Suite, Junior Suite). Normalized from competitor-specific room type codes to enable apples-to-apples benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|deluxe|superior|suite|junior_suite|executive|villa — promote to reference product]']
  shop_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the competitor rate was shopped or retrieved. Used to calculate lead time and to track rate movement over time.']
  shop_run_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate shopping run or batch session that produced this record, enabling traceability back to the originating shop event.']
  shop_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the competitor rate was captured during the shopping session. Enables intra-day rate movement tracking.']
  shopped_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The publicly available or shopped room rate for the competitor property for the specified stay date, room type, and rate plan. Represents the gross rate as displayed on the booking channel before taxes and fees. Core input for ADR and RevPAR competitive benchmarking.']
  special_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay date coincides with a known special event, conference, or demand driver (True) that may explain elevated competitor rates. Supports demand-adjusted competitive rate analysis.']
  star_rating decimal(2,1) [note: 'Official star rating of the competitor property (1.0 to 5.0) as recognized by the relevant hospitality rating authority. Used to filter and segment competitive benchmarking analysis.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date for which the competitor rate applies — i.e., the check-in date or the date of the night being priced. Core dimension for rate positioning analysis.']
  taxes_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopped rate includes taxes and fees (True) or is a pre-tax rate (False). Critical for ensuring rate comparability across channels and geographies where tax display conventions differ.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this competitor rate record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Competitive rate intelligence records capturing publicly available or shopped rates from competitor properties. Stores competitor property identifier, room type category, rate plan type, shopped rate, currency, stay date, shop date, lead time, channel shopped (OTA, direct, GDS), and data source. Used in Infor EzRMS for competitive benchmarking and rate positioning decisions.'
}

Table revenue.group_evaluation {
  group_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each group business evaluation record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Primary key for the group_evaluation data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate account, travel agency, or group organizer account associated with this group inquiry, as maintained in Salesforce CRM and Oracle OPERA PMS AR module.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group RFPs often originate from marketing campaigns targeting corporate/SMIC segments. Linking the campaign helps measure campaign effectiveness in driving qualified group leads and conversion rates.']
  decision_maker_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Group bookings often involve loyalty members as decision-makers, influencing pricing decisions. Business process: tracking member-led group inquiries for loyalty credit attribution and personalized gr']
  guest_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Revenue managers evaluate guest group blocks for displacement analysis and rate decisions. The group_evaluation process directly assesses guest.guest_group_block entities to determine whether to accep']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Group booking decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL account mapping for group revenue tracking, displacement cost accounting, and profitability analysis.']
  primary_banquet_event_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Group evaluations assess F&B revenue contribution from banquet events to offset room displacement. Required for displacement analysis, group acceptance decisions, total property profitability evaluati']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the group evaluation is being conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Group evaluations are performed for specific rate plans. group_evaluation has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N group_evaluation records → 1 revenue_rate_plan. Removes redund']
  wash_factor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.wash_factor. Business justification: Group evaluations apply wash factors from the wash_factor master table to estimate attrition. N group_evaluation records → 1 wash_factor. Note: wash_factor_pct on group_evaluation is the APPLIED value']
  ancillary_revenue_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated ancillary revenue from the group including spa, parking, audio-visual, business center, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Used in total spend calculation for net revenue impact assessment.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Proposed first night of the group room block. Used as the start of the displacement analysis window in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  attrition_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected attrition percentage for the group block by group type, representing the proportion of contracted rooms the group is expected not to use. Distinct from wash factor in that attrition is the contractual exposure, while wash is the statistical pickup adjustment.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the group inquiry was received (e.g., direct sales, GDS, OTA, travel agency). Used for channel contribution analysis and commission cost modeling in the net revenue impact calculation.']
  counter_offer_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Alternative per-room per-night rate proposed by the revenue manager as a counter-offer to the group organizer when the proposed_group_rate is below the minimum_acceptable_rate. Populated only when revenue_manager_decision = \'counter_offer\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was first captured in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this evaluation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI and IFRS/GAAP standards.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'The date by which unbooked rooms in the group block are released back to general inventory. After this date, the property may sell unreserved block rooms to transient guests. Critical for inventory management and wash factor actualization.']
  decision_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the revenue manager when their decision overrides the RMS recommendation (e.g., strategic account relationship, future business potential, competitive displacement risk). Required when revenue_manager_decision differs from rms_recommendation.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue manager recorded their final accept, decline, or counter-offer decision. Used for SLA tracking and decision audit trail.']
  demand_forecast_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted transient Occupancy Rate (OCC) for the property during the group\'s proposed stay dates, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. High forecasted occupancy increases displacement cost and raises the minimum acceptable rate.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Proposed last night of the group room block (checkout date). Defines the end of the displacement analysis window.']
  displacement_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue lost from displaced transient business if the group block is accepted, calculated as transient_rooms_displaced multiplied by transient_adr_displaced across the stay period. Key component of the net revenue impact analysis.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initial inquiry receipt through revenue manager decision. \'pending\' = awaiting RMS analysis; \'under_review\' = revenue manager reviewing; \'recommended_accept\' = RMS recommends acceptance; \'recommended_decline\' = RMS recommends decline; \'accepted\' = revenue manager approved; \'declined\' = revenue manager rejected.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when the group evaluation was initiated and processed by IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  fb_revenue_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus event catering estimates and historical group F&B spend patterns.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Name of the group or event associated with the booking inquiry (e.g., \'Acme Corp Annual Sales Conference 2025\'). Used for identification and reporting across Delphi by Amadeus and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  group_room_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total projected room revenue from the group block at the proposed group rate, calculated as adjusted room block (post-wash) multiplied by proposed_group_rate across all nights. Gross revenue contribution from rooms only.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group by business segment. Drives wash factor parameters and attrition modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|government|leisure|sports|wedding|social|tour_operator|incentive — promote to reference product]']
  historical_wash_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average wash percentage for this group type and property, derived from past group performance data in IDeaS G3 RMS. Serves as the baseline input before adjustments for lead time and current demand conditions.']
  inquiry_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the group booking inquiry, as generated by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS). Used to cross-reference the evaluation with the originating inquiry across Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  is_definite_booking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group inquiry has converted to a definite (contracted) booking following a positive revenue manager decision. True = definite booking confirmed; False = still in tentative or evaluation stage.']
  lead_time_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorical bucket representing the number of days between the group inquiry date and the proposed arrival date. Used as a parameter in wash factor modeling, as groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher wash rates.']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of nights in the proposed group stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Represents the Length of Stay (LOS) for the group block used in displacement and revenue impact modeling.']
  minimum_acceptable_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum per-room per-night rate recommended by IDeaS G3 RMS below which accepting the group would result in negative net revenue impact after accounting for displacement costs. This is the RMS-generated floor rate for negotiation.']
  net_revenue_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net revenue impact of accepting the group, calculated as total group spend (room + F&B + ancillary) minus displacement cost. Positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative value indicates the group destroys revenue versus transient alternative. Primary decision metric in the evaluation.']
  peak_night_rooms varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms occupied on any single night within the group block. Used for capacity planning and displacement analysis at the peak demand point.']
  proposed_group_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The per-room per-night rate proposed by the group organizer or sales manager for the room block, expressed in the property\'s operating currency. This is the rate under evaluation against the displacement cost and minimum acceptable rate.']
  revenue_manager_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the revenue manager after reviewing the RMS recommendation and displacement analysis. May align with or override the RMS recommendation. \'pending\' indicates no decision has been recorded yet.']
  revpar_impact decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated change in Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the property during the group stay dates if the group is accepted, compared to the transient-only forecast. Negative RevPAR impact is a key indicator for decline recommendation.']
  rgi_benchmark decimal(5,2) [note: 'The property\'s Revenue Generation Index (RGI) from the STR STAR Report for the competitive set during the evaluation period. Used as a competitive context benchmark to assess whether accepting the group at the proposed rate is competitively rational.']
  rms_recommendation varchar [note: 'Automated accept, decline, or counter-offer recommendation generated by IDeaS G3 RMS based on displacement analysis, wash factor modeling, and net revenue impact. This is the system-generated recommendation prior to revenue manager override.']
  room_block_size varchar [note: 'Total number of guest rooms requested in the group block across all nights of the stay. This is the peak or contracted room block size as submitted in the group inquiry.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation ID or Delphi by Amadeus opportunity ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  transient_adr_displaced decimal(10,2) [note: 'The forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the transient business that would be displaced by the group block, as estimated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Used to quantify the revenue opportunity cost of accepting the group.']
  transient_rooms_displaced varchar [note: 'Estimated number of transient (individual) room nights that would be displaced if the group block is accepted, as calculated by IDeaS G3 RMS displacement analysis. Core input to the net revenue impact calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, including revenue manager decision updates or status changes.']
  wash_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The adjusted wash factor percentage applied to the contracted room block to estimate actual pickup. Derived from historical wash percentage, group type attrition patterns, and booking lead time bucket. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15% wash). Used to calculate the adjusted room block for revenue modeling.']

  Note: 'Group business evaluation records generated by IDeaS G3 RMS for group booking inquiries, encompassing the full displacement analysis and wash factor modeling. Captures group inquiry reference, proposed dates, room block size, proposed group rate, displacement cost analysis (transient rooms displaced, transient ADR displaced, net revenue impact, total spend including F&B and ancillary), wash factor parameters (expected attrition percentage by group type, booking lead time bucket, historical wash percentage, adjusted wash factor), recommended minimum rate, accept/decline recommendation, evaluation status, and revenue manager decision. This is the SSOT for all group revenue decision-making including displacement modeling and block attrition forecasting.'
}

Table revenue.pickup_report {
  pickup_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pickup report record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete revenue management snapshot event tracking booking pace against forecast for a given stay date and property.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pickup velocity and booking pace are tracked by cost center for departmental revenue forecasting and operational planning at the department level.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the revenue manager or analyst responsible for reviewing and acting on this pickup report. Links to the workforce/employee master. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE requirement — the accountable internal party for this revenue management snapshot.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports track room block pickup rates for specific event bookings against cutoff dates. Essential for group pace reporting, attrition risk assessment, and event booking performance mon']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Pickup reports track booking pace by market segment. pickup_report has segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N pickup_report records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant segment_cod']
  monitored_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Pickup reports track outlet reservation pace for outlets accepting reservations (fine dining, specialty restaurants). Required for outlet capacity planning, labor scheduling, inventory management alig']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this pickup report is generated. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  adr_on_books decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate (ADR) of all rooms currently on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. Calculated as total room revenue on books divided by rooms_on_books. Core revenue performance indicator alongside occupancy pace. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  adr_variance_to_forecast decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between adr_on_books and forecasted_adr (adr_on_books minus forecasted_adr). Positive value indicates rate is tracking above forecast; negative value indicates rate is below forecast. Used alongside rooms_variance_to_forecast to assess overall revenue pace.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the on-books reservations were sourced for this pickup report scope (e.g., DIRECT, OTA, GDS, VOICE, BRAND_WEB, WHOLESALE). When null, the report covers all channels. Enables channel-level pace analysis to assess OTA (Online Travel Agency) vs direct booking mix. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DIRECT|OTA|GDS|VOICE|BRAND_WEB|WHOLESALE|CORPORATE|GROUP — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup report record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this pickup report record (adr_on_books, forecasted_adr, revpar_on_books, forecasted_revpar, prior_year_adr, adr_variance_to_forecast). Typically the property\'s local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of the week corresponding to the stay_date (e.g., Monday, Friday, Saturday). Critical demand pattern dimension — weekday vs weekend demand profiles differ significantly in hospitality, driving different pricing strategies and pace expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  demand_level varchar [note: 'Revenue management system classification of expected demand intensity for the stay_date as of the report_date. Drives pricing strategy and inventory control decisions: \'distressed\' triggers promotional actions; \'peak\' triggers rate maximization and LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions.']
  forecasted_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for the expected ADR (Average Daily Rate) on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Baseline against which adr_on_books is compared to assess rate performance vs plan.']
  forecasted_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) on the stay_date. Baseline against which revpar_on_books is compared to assess overall revenue pace vs plan. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  forecasted_rooms varchar [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for total rooms expected to be occupied on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this report snapshot. Baseline against which rooms_on_books pace is measured to compute variance.']
  is_special_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay_date coincides with a designated special event (conference, concert, sporting event, holiday, local festival) that materially impacts demand. When true, pace expectations and pricing strategies are adjusted relative to normal demand patterns.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay_date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Boolean flag used to segment pickup reports into weekday vs weekend cohorts for pace analysis, as weekend demand patterns and pricing strategies differ materially from weekday.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the report_date and the stay_date (stay_date minus report_date). Represents the booking window or days-out at the time of this snapshot. Critical for pace analysis — revenue managers compare pickup velocity across standard lead-time intervals (90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1 days out).']
  los_restriction_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a LOS (Length of Stay) restriction (minimum stay requirement) is currently active for the stay_date. LOS restrictions are a key revenue management lever applied during high-demand periods to maximize total revenue across multiple nights.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to book the stay_date, when a LOS restriction is active. Null when los_restriction_active is false. Used to enforce stay-through requirements during high-demand periods to prevent short-stay displacement of longer, higher-value bookings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text annotations entered by the revenue manager to document observations, strategy decisions, or contextual factors affecting the pickup pace for this stay_date (e.g., \'Rate opened on OTA due to slow pace\', \'Group block released — expect transient pickup\'). Supports audit trail and strategy documentation.']
  pickup_velocity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate of new reservations being added per day for the stay_date, calculated as the incremental rooms picked up divided by the number of days elapsed in the measurement window. Expressed as rooms per day. Enables revenue managers to assess acceleration or deceleration of booking pace.']
  prior_year_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'ADR (Average Daily Rate) on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time. Enables year-over-year rate comparison to assess whether current pricing strategy is yielding rate improvement or erosion versus the prior period.']
  prior_year_rooms_on_books varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time (days out). Enables year-over-year (YOY) pace comparison — a standard revenue management benchmarking technique to contextualize current booking pace against historical performance.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Rate plan identifier for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, CORP, PKG). When null, the report covers all rate plans. Enables rate-plan-level pace analysis to assess BAR (Best Available Rate) and NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) booking velocity.']
  report_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this pickup snapshot was captured. Represents the \'as-of\' date for all rooms-on-books and ADR figures in this record. The difference between stay_date and report_date defines the lead time (days out). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP requirement (date-precision is standard for daily pickup reports).']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this pickup report record, typically formatted as PKP-{PropertyCode}-{StayDate}-{SequenceNumber}. Used by revenue managers to reference specific pickup snapshots in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this pickup report record. \'draft\' indicates the snapshot is being assembled; \'published\' indicates it is available for revenue manager consumption; \'superseded\' indicates a later snapshot for the same stay_date/report_date exists; \'cancelled\' indicates the report was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pickup report generation cadence: \'daily\' for standard automated daily snapshots, \'weekly\' for weekly pace summaries, \'monthly\' for month-end pace reviews, or \'adhoc\' for analyst-triggered on-demand reports. Determines the reporting frequency context for pace trend analysis.']
  revpar_on_books decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) derived from current on-books position, calculated as (adr_on_books multiplied by rooms_on_books) divided by total_available_rooms. Composite pace metric combining rate and occupancy performance. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Room type classification for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., STD, DLX, STE, KNG, DBL). When null, the report covers all room types for the property. Enables room-type-level pace analysis to support inventory controls and upsell strategy.']
  rooms_on_books varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms confirmed on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. This is the primary pace indicator — the current committed inventory position. Revenue managers compare this against forecast and prior-year actuals to assess demand trajectory.']
  rooms_on_books_14d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 14 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 14 days prior to stay_date). Critical short-window pace indicator used to trigger last-minute pricing tactics and inventory controls.']
  rooms_on_books_1d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 1 day (i.e., the snapshot captured 1 day prior to stay_date). Final pre-arrival pace indicator representing near-final committed occupancy before day-of walk-ins.']
  rooms_on_books_30d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 30 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 30 days prior to stay_date). Key pace benchmark for near-term demand assessment and rate strategy adjustments.']
  rooms_on_books_60d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 60 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 60 days prior to stay_date). Standard pace benchmark used by revenue managers to assess mid-range booking momentum.']
  rooms_on_books_7d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 7 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 7 days prior to stay_date). Near-arrival pace indicator used to assess last-week demand and trigger promotional or restriction actions.']
  rooms_on_books_90d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 90 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 90 days prior to stay_date). Used as a historical pace benchmark for comparison against current rooms_on_books to measure pickup velocity over the 90-day window.']
  rooms_variance_to_forecast varchar [note: 'Difference between rooms_on_books and forecasted_rooms (rooms_on_books minus forecasted_rooms). Positive value indicates ahead-of-pace (over-performing vs forecast); negative value indicates behind-pace (under-performing). Primary KPI for revenue manager intervention decisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this pickup report data was sourced (IDeaS_G3_RMS, Infor_EzRMS, OPERA_PMS, or Manual for analyst-entered overrides). Enables data lineage tracking and source-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the special event associated with the stay_date when is_special_event is true (e.g., \'Super Bowl Weekend\', \'Annual Tech Conference\', \'New Year\'s Eve\'). Provides context for anomalous pace patterns in revenue management reporting.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date for which booking pace and pickup velocity are being measured. This is the future arrival/occupancy date that revenue managers are monitoring. Central dimension of the pickup report — all rooms-on-books and ADR snapshots relate to this date.']
  total_available_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms available for sale for the stay_date at the property (net of out-of-order and out-of-service rooms). Denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculation and RevPAR computation. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup report record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement.']

  Note: 'Reservation pickup tracking records measuring the pace of bookings against forecast for a given stay date and property. Captures stay date, report date, rooms on books at various lead-time snapshots (90-day, 60-day, 30-day, 14-day, 7-day, 1-day), ADR on books, RevPAR on books, pickup velocity, and variance to forecast. Critical for revenue managers to assess booking pace and adjust pricing strategy.'
}

Table revenue.revenue_overbooking_policy {
  revenue_overbooking_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the overbooking policy record. Primary key for the revenue_overbooking_policy data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who formally approved this overbooking policy. Links to the workforce/employee master record. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Overbooking practices must comply with consumer protection policies. Overbooking policy approval requires compliance review to ensure fair walk procedures and compensation. Essential for consumer prot']
  primary_revenue_property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this overbooking policy applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  risk_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_register. Business justification: Overbooking carries operational and reputational risk requiring risk management oversight. Risk committee reviews overbooking policies and monitors walk incidents. Essential for enterprise risk manage']
  ada_room_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible rooms are excluded from the overbooking pool under this policy. True = ADA rooms are never overbooked. Required for ADA compliance.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational authority level required to approve and activate this overbooking policy. Defines the governance hierarchy for policy authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue_manager|director_of_revenue|gm|corporate_rm|vp_revenue|cro — promote to reference product]']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this overbooking policy was formally approved by the designated authority. Recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  auto_adjust_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS is authorized to automatically adjust the overbooking percentage within the defined min/max bounds without manual revenue manager intervention. True = dynamic auto-adjustment enabled.']
  cancellation_rate_assumption_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical or forecast cancellation rate percentage used as the actuarial basis for setting the overbooking level. Derived from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS demand forecasting models and reviewed periodically by revenue management.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Distribution channel scope to which this overbooking policy applies. \'all\' = all booking channels. \'direct\' = brand.com and direct reservations. \'ota\' = Online Travel Agency bookings. \'gds\' = Global Distribution System bookings. \'voice\' = call center. \'group\' = group block reservations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this overbooking policy record was first created in the system, recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  day_of_week_mask varchar [note: 'Seven-character binary string representing the days of the week this policy applies to, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., \'1111100\' = Mon–Fri only, \'1111111\' = all days). Enables granular day-of-week yield optimization.']
  demand_tier varchar [note: 'Demand classification tier from the Revenue Management System that this policy is calibrated for. Aligns with IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS demand forecasting segments to enable yield optimization by demand level.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this overbooking policy becomes active and enforceable in the Property Management System and Revenue Management System.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this overbooking policy ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no defined end date.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this overbooking policy was formally reviewed by the revenue management team. Used to track compliance with the defined review frequency.']
  los_restriction_min varchar [note: 'Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this overbooking policy to apply. Supports LOS-based yield optimization strategies in IDeaS G3 RMS. Null indicates no minimum LOS restriction.']
  loyalty_points_walk_bonus varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program bonus points awarded to walked guests as additional compensation under this policy. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management and the Loyalty domain for points fulfillment.']
  loyalty_tier_exempt varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty program tier codes (e.g., \'PLATINUM,DIAMOND\') whose members are exempt from being walked under this policy. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.']
  min_overbooking_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum overbooking level as a percentage below which the revenue management system should alert or auto-adjust. Supports dynamic overbooking optimization in IDeaS G3 RMS to prevent under-selling.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this overbooking policy by the revenue management team. Drives workflow alerts in the Revenue Management System.']
  no_show_rate_assumption_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical or forecast no-show rate percentage used as the actuarial basis for setting the overbooking level. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS historical data and IDeaS G3 RMS forecasting models.']
  overbooking_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum absolute number of rooms that may be overbooked above physical inventory, regardless of the overbooking percentage. Acts as a hard ceiling to limit walk exposure. Enforced in Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.']
  overbooking_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum authorized overbooking level expressed as a percentage above the physical room inventory for the applicable room type (e.g., 5.00 = 5% above capacity). Core yield optimization parameter managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and enforced in OPERA PMS inventory controls.']
  pms_rule_code varchar [note: 'Oracle OPERA PMS inventory control rule code corresponding to this overbooking policy. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational PMS configuration.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for revenue management team annotations, special conditions, or contextual notes about this overbooking policy (e.g., \'Applies during citywide convention periods only\', \'Reviewed post-Q3 STR STAR Report\').']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the overbooking policy. \'active\' means the policy is enforced in OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3 RMS. \'draft\' indicates pending approval. \'suspended\' means temporarily disabled. \'archived\' means retired from use.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the overbooking policy by reservation segment. \'standard\' applies to general transient bookings. \'dynamic\' indicates IDeaS G3 RMS auto-adjusts levels. \'group\' applies to group blocks. \'guaranteed\' applies to guaranteed reservation types only.']
  rate_plan_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate plan codes (BAR, NRR, LRA, corporate, etc.) to which this overbooking policy applies. Null indicates the policy applies to all rate plans. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS rate distribution configuration.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this overbooking policy is reviewed and potentially recalibrated by the revenue management team. Supports governance and continuous optimization of overbooking levels.']
  rms_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Native policy identifier or rule code as stored in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the source RMS configuration.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'OPERA PMS room type code to which this overbooking policy applies (e.g., \'KING\', \'DBLQ\', \'SUITE\'). A null or wildcard value indicates the policy applies to all room types at the property.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Revenue management season classification code (e.g., \'PEAK\', \'SHOULDER\', \'OFF\') that scopes this overbooking policy. Aligns with IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting season definitions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this overbooking policy record was most recently modified, recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  vip_reservation_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether VIP-flagged reservations (loyalty elite, corporate VIP, celebrity) are excluded from walk eligibility under this policy. True = VIP reservations are protected from walk. Integrates with Salesforce CRM guest profiles.']
  walk_alert_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of overbooked rooms at which the system triggers an alert to the front desk and revenue management team to initiate proactive walk management. Configured in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  walk_compensation_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation amount authorized for walked guests under this policy, expressed in the property\'s operating currency. Applicable when walk_policy_type is \'cash_compensation\' or \'voucher\'. Governed by AHLA walk policy standards.']
  walk_compensation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the walk compensation amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.']
  walk_policy_type varchar [note: 'Type of compensation offered to guests who are walked (relocated) due to overbooking. \'comp_room\' = complimentary room at partner hotel. \'cash_compensation\' = monetary payment. \'upgrade\' = room upgrade if available. \'partner_hotel\' = relocation to contracted partner property. \'voucher\' = future stay voucher. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comp_room|cash_compensation|upgrade|partner_hotel|voucher|loyalty_points|transport_voucher — promote to reference product]']
  walk_transport_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether complimentary transportation to the partner hotel is included in the walk compensation package under this policy. True = transportation is provided at property expense.']

  Note: 'Property-level overbooking policy configuration defining authorized overbooking levels by room type, day of week, and season. Captures maximum overbooking percentage, absolute overbooking cap, walk policy (compensation type, partner hotel agreements), and approval authority. Managed by revenue management and enforced in Oracle OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls.'
}

Table revenue.revenue_budget {
  revenue_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each revenue budget record. Primary key for the revenue_budget data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  budgeted_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Revenue budgets include outlet-level F&B targets for total property budget planning. Required for USALI budget preparation, outlet performance targets, variance analysis, GOP planning at department le']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Annual revenue budgets allocate expected revenue by campaign (e.g., Q2 leisure campaign expected to drive $500K room revenue). Finance needs this link for budget-vs-actual reporting and campaign ROI v']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the STR (Smith Travel Research) competitive set used as the benchmarking reference for RGI, MPI, and ARI targets in this budget. Links to the STR STAR Report competitive set definition.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue budgets are allocated to cost centers for departmental planning, manager accountability, and USALI-compliant departmental budget reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the revenue director or senior leader who formally approved this budget record. Supports governance, audit trail, and owner presentation accountability.']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Budget planning incorporates known definite event bookings as committed revenue for fiscal period forecasting. Required for budget vs actual variance analysis with event segmentation and MICE revenue ']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Revenue budgets are components of the overall financial budget and must reconcile for consolidated budget approval, owner reporting, and financial planning.']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Annual revenue budgets set GSS score targets alongside financial targets (SALT program requirements). Budget submissions must include target GSS by period—mandatory for brand compliance. Revenue and e']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the market segment (e.g., transient leisure, transient corporate, group, wholesale, OTA) for which this budget is defined. Enables segment-level revenue planning and variance analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue budgets roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial planning, ownership entity reporting, and portfolio-level budget consolidation.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this revenue budget applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Budget data feeds forward-looking regulatory disclosures for publicly traded parent companies. SEC filings and investor disclosures require budget attestations with clear audit trails. Essential for f']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue director formally approved this budget version. Marks the transition from \'draft\' to \'approved\' strategy status.']
  budgeted_available_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of room-nights planned to be available for sale during the planning period (capacity baseline). Used as the denominator for RevPAR, TRevPAR, and GOPPAR calculations and STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  budgeted_cpor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Cost Per Occupied Room (CPOR) in the property\'s operating currency, representing the planned direct operating cost per occupied room night. Used for profitability planning and variance analysis against actuals.']
  budgeted_events_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budgeted revenue from Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions (MICE) and event spaces for the planning period. Supports TRevPAR planning and event sales governance in Delphi by Amadeus.']
  budgeted_fb_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budgeted Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period. Supports TRevPAR planning and USALI departmental revenue reporting.']
  budgeted_gop decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted Gross Operating Profit (GOP) in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period, per USALI departmental and undistributed expense structure. Used for GOPPAR derivation and owner NOI reporting.']
  budgeted_occupied_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of room-nights planned to be occupied (sold) during the planning period. Derived from budgeted available rooms × target OCC, stored as a planning target for demand forecasting and group block management.']
  budgeted_other_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budgeted ancillary revenue (spa, parking, resort fees, retail, etc.) in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period. Completes the TRevPAR revenue stack beyond rooms, F&B, and events.']
  budgeted_room_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budgeted room revenue (in operating currency) for the planning period and market segment. The primary monetary commitment in the revenue budget, used for variance analysis against actuals in the DOR and management reporting. Classified confidential as a strategic financial target.']
  budgeted_total_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate budgeted total revenue across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + events + other) in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period. The top-line financial commitment used in owner presentations and EBITDA planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue budget record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.']
  demand_forecast_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or method used to generate the demand forecast underpinning this budget: \'IDeaS_G3\' (IDeaS G3 RMS automated forecast), \'Infor_EzRMS\' (Infor EzRMS forecast), \'manual\' (revenue manager manual input), or \'hybrid\' (combination). Supports data lineage and forecast accuracy tracking.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal month or period number (1–12) within the fiscal year for monthly budget granularity. Supports period-level variance analysis in the DOR and management reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this revenue budget belongs (e.g., 2025). Aligns with the property\'s USALI-compliant financial reporting calendar and SAP S/4HANA GL fiscal periods.']
  is_owner_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget has received formal approval from the property owner or ownership group (True = owner-approved; False = pending or not required). Relevant for managed properties where owner sign-off is contractually required.']
  key_tactical_actions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the near-term tactical revenue actions planned for this budget period (e.g., BAR adjustments, LRA controls, OTA channel mix shifts, promotional rate activations). Captured from the revenue strategy narrative in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or commentary associated with this revenue budget record (e.g., macro-economic assumptions, renovation impact adjustments, force majeure notes). Supports management reporting and audit documentation.']
  planning_horizon_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning horizon for this budget record: \'annual\' (full-year plan), \'quarterly\' (90-day rolling), \'monthly\' (period-level), or \'weekly\' (short-term tactical). Determines the reporting cadence for variance analysis.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning horizon covered by this revenue budget (e.g., 2025-12-31 for an annual plan). Defines the effective end of the budget period.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning horizon covered by this revenue budget (e.g., 2025-01-01 for an annual plan). Defines the effective start of the budget period for variance tracking against actuals.']
  property_segment varchar [note: 'Brand/service tier of the property for which this budget applies: \'luxury\', \'premium\', \'select_service\', or \'extended_stay\'. Enables cross-segment benchmarking and portfolio-level revenue governance.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this budget record, used in management reports, owner presentations, and cross-system references (e.g., SAP S/4HANA GL budget entries). Format: RB-{PropertyCode}-{Year}.']
  strategic_initiatives varchar [note: 'Free-text description of longer-horizon strategic revenue initiatives included in this budget (e.g., loyalty program rate strategy, new OTA partnership, dynamic pricing rule (DRR) rollout, group business development targets). Supports owner presentations and management reporting.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this revenue budget and strategy record. \'draft\' = under preparation; \'approved\' = signed off by revenue director; \'active\' = in-period execution; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version; \'archived\' = closed period record.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the revenue strategy scope covered by this budget: \'transient\' (FIT/BAR-driven room revenue), \'group\' (MICE and group block revenue), \'total_revenue\' (TRevPAR-level all-revenue planning), \'food_and_beverage\' (F&B revenue plan), \'events\' (event/conference revenue), or \'other\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|other — promote to reference product]']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget version was formally submitted for review and approval. Used for governance tracking and audit trail in owner presentations and management reporting.']
  target_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period. Represents the planned average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Core revenue KPI used in STR STAR Report, RGI, and MPI benchmarking.']
  target_alos decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budgeted Average Length of Stay (ALOS) in nights for the planning period and market segment. Supports LOS-based pricing strategy, minimum stay restrictions, and demand pattern planning in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  target_ari decimal(6,4) [note: 'Budgeted Average Rate Index (ARI) target, representing the property\'s planned ADR performance relative to its STR competitive set (property ADR / comp set ADR). Supports rate strategy and competitive positioning planning.']
  target_goppar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) in the property\'s operating currency. Represents the profitability target per available room after operating expenses, used in owner presentations and GOP governance.']
  target_mpi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Budgeted Market Penetration Index (MPI) target, representing the property\'s planned occupancy performance relative to its STR competitive set (property OCC / comp set OCC). Supports competitive benchmarking strategy.']
  target_occupancy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budgeted occupancy rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the planning period. Represents the planned proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core KPI input for RevPAR calculation and STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  target_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property\'s operating currency for the planning period. Calculated as ADR × OCC but stored as a strategic target for direct comparison against actuals and STR competitive set benchmarks (RGI).']
  target_rgi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Budgeted Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target, representing the property\'s planned RevPAR performance relative to its STR competitive set (property RevPAR / comp set RevPAR). RGI > 1.0 indicates planned market share gain.']
  target_trevpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Budgeted Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) in the property\'s operating currency, encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, events, spa, parking, etc.) per available room. Supports total revenue strategy planning beyond rooms-only RevPAR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue budget record was most recently modified. Tracks version changes, reforecast updates, and approval workflow transitions for audit and governance purposes.']
  version varchar [note: 'Indicates the iteration of this budget record: \'original\' (initial annual plan), \'revised\' (mid-year adjustment), or \'reforecast\' (rolling forecast update). Supports version-controlled variance analysis in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and management reporting.']

  Note: 'Revenue planning, strategy, and budget records combining strategic revenue targets with financial budget commitments at the property and market segment level, aligned to USALI standards. Captures planning period, property, strategy type (transient, group, total revenue), target and budgeted ADR/occupancy/RevPAR/TRevPAR, budgeted room revenue, key tactical actions and strategic initiatives, budget version (original, revised, reforecast), strategy status (draft, approved, active), planning horizon, and approving revenue director. This is the SSOT for all revenue planning governance — both strategic direction and financial targets — used for variance analysis against actuals in the DOR, management reporting, owner presentations, and strategic revenue governance.'
}

Table revenue.channel_contribution {
  channel_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each channel contribution record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional summary entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete revenue contribution measurement event per distribution channel, property, and reporting period.']
  analyzed_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Channel analysis includes F&B attachment rates by booking source (OTA guests vs direct = different outlet usage). Required for channel profitability analysis, package optimization, outlet marketing st']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Channel performance reports must attribute bookings/revenue to the marketing campaigns that drove traffic to each channel. This is core marketing ROI measurement and enables campaign-level profitabili']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel master record (e.g., OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, corporate, wholesale). Used to join channel-level attributes such as channel name, partner agreement, and commission tier.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Channel revenue and distribution costs are tracked by cost center for departmental P&L, channel profitability analysis, and USALI departmental reporting.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Channel revenue and distribution costs post to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, commission expense tracking, and revenue net of distribution reporting.']
  loyalty_member_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_segment. Business justification: Channel performance analysis by loyalty segment reveals segment-specific booking behavior and channel preferences. Business process: segment-level channel effectiveness reporting for optimizing distri']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the market segment associated with bookings through this channel (e.g., transient leisure, corporate, group, wholesale). Supports revenue management segmentation analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Channel performance rolls up to profit centers for consolidated reporting, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level channel strategy evaluation.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property for which channel contribution is being measured. Enables property-level channel mix analysis and OTA partnership management decisions.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Channel contribution analysis is segmented by rate plan. channel_contribution has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK to revenue_rate_plan. N channel_contribution records → 1 reve']
  adr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average Daily Rate achieved through this distribution channel during the reporting period, calculated as gross room revenue divided by rooms sold. Industry-standard KPI per STR STAR Report methodology. Enables channel-level ADR benchmarking.']
  advance_booking_days decimal(7,2) [note: 'Average number of days in advance that reservations were made through this channel (booking lead time). Measures booking pace by channel and informs dynamic pricing and rate restriction strategies in the Revenue Management System.']
  alos decimal(7,2) [note: 'Average Length of Stay (ALOS) in nights for bookings originating from this distribution channel during the reporting period. Calculated as total room nights divided by booking volume. Informs LOS (Length of Stay) restriction strategies in the RMS.']
  booking_volume varchar [note: 'Total number of confirmed reservations received through this distribution channel during the reporting period. Core metric for channel productivity analysis and OTA partnership evaluation.']
  business_date date [note: 'The principal operational business date on which this channel contribution record was generated or posted, as defined by the Property Management System (PMS) business date. Distinct from audit timestamps; used for daily operations reporting (DOR) and period-end reconciliation.']
  cancellation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reservations cancelled through this distribution channel during the reporting period. Used to calculate channel-level cancellation rate and assess OTA cancellation policy impact on revenue.']
  cancellation_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of total bookings through this channel that were cancelled during the reporting period (cancellation_count / booking_volume * 100). High cancellation rates from OTA channels impact revenue predictability and yield optimization.']
  channel_mix_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of total property booking volume or room nights attributable to this distribution channel during the reporting period. Measures channel contribution to overall distribution mix. Used for channel optimization strategy and OTA dependency analysis.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel through which bookings were made. OTA (Online Travel Agency) includes Expedia, Booking.com; GDS (Global Distribution System) includes Sabre, Amadeus; direct_web is brand.com; voice is call center; corporate is negotiated accounts; wholesale is tour operators and bed banks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|direct_web|voice|corporate|wholesale|metasearch|loyalty_redemption — promote to reference product]']
  commission_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total commission or distribution fee paid to the channel partner (OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings during the reporting period. Deducted from gross revenue to derive net revenue. Critical for channel cost analysis and OTA contract management.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Average commission rate (as a percentage of gross revenue) paid to the channel partner during the reporting period. Calculated as commission_amount / gross_revenue * 100. Used to benchmark OTA commission costs and negotiate channel agreements.']
  contribution_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the channel contribution record. \'draft\' indicates preliminary data; \'confirmed\' indicates validated and finalized; \'adjusted\' indicates post-close corrections applied; \'closed\' indicates period locked; \'cancelled\' indicates record voided.']
  cost_per_booking decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total distribution cost per confirmed booking through this channel, including commissions, GDS transaction fees, and incremental booking costs. Also known as Cost Per Acquisition (CPA). Key metric for channel ROI analysis and OTA partnership management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global properties and ensures financial reporting compliance.']
  data_extract_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when data was extracted from the source operational system (OPERA PMS, SynXis CRS, or IDeaS G3 RMS) for loading into the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for data freshness monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.']
  gds_transaction_fees decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total GDS (Global Distribution System) per-transaction fees incurred for bookings processed through GDS channels (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport) during the reporting period. Applicable only to GDS channel type; zero for non-GDS channels. Included in total distribution cost.']
  gross_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross room revenue generated through this distribution channel before deducting commissions, OTA fees, or GDS transaction fees. Represents the full rack or negotiated rate charged to guests. Used in ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI standards.']
  is_direct_channel boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a direct booking channel (brand.com, loyalty app, call center, walk-in). Direct channels carry zero or minimal commission cost. Used to track direct booking share as a KPI for reducing OTA dependency.']
  is_ota_channel boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this distribution channel is an Online Travel Agency (OTA) such as Expedia or Booking.com. Enables quick segmentation of OTA vs. non-OTA channels for commission cost analysis and direct booking strategy reporting.']
  loyalty_bookings_count varchar [note: 'Number of bookings through this channel made by loyalty program members during the reporting period. Tracks loyalty member channel preference and supports direct booking incentive strategies to reduce OTA dependency.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net room revenue retained by the property after deducting all channel commissions and distribution fees from gross revenue. Primary metric for evaluating true channel profitability and informing channel mix optimization decisions.']
  no_show_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reservations from this channel where the guest did not arrive and did not cancel. No-shows impact revenue recovery and are tracked per channel to assess booking quality and enforce no-show policies.']
  nrr_bookings_count varchar [note: 'Number of bookings made at Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) through this channel during the reporting period. NRR bookings provide revenue certainty and reduce cancellation risk. Used to assess channel-level rate plan mix.']
  occupancy_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Occupancy rate (OCC) attributable to this distribution channel, expressed as a percentage of available rooms occupied by channel-sourced bookings. Supports channel-level yield analysis and demand forecasting integration with IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel contribution record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per SOX and GDPR compliance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel contribution record was last modified. Tracks adjustments, restatements, or corrections to channel revenue data. Required for SOX financial controls.']
  reporting_granularity varchar [note: 'Time granularity at which this channel contribution record is aggregated. \'daily\' for day-level DOR reporting; \'weekly\' for pace analysis; \'monthly\' for USALI period reporting; \'quarterly\' for STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the reporting period (stay date range) for which channel contribution metrics are aggregated. Together with reporting_period_start_date defines the measurement window.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the reporting period (stay date range) for which channel contribution metrics are aggregated. Used to define the measurement window for ADR, RevPAR, and channel mix analysis.']
  revenue_net_of_distribution decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net revenue after deducting all distribution costs (commissions, GDS fees, OTA fees) from gross revenue. Represents the true retained revenue contribution of the channel. Supports channel profitability ranking and optimization decisions.']
  revpar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room contributed by this channel, calculated as channel gross revenue divided by total available rooms for the reporting period. Core yield optimization metric per STR STAR Report and USALI standards.']
  rgi decimal(7,4) [note: 'Revenue Generation Index (RGI) attributable to this channel, measuring the property\'s RevPAR performance relative to the competitive set for channel-sourced bookings. RGI > 1.0 indicates above-market performance. Sourced from STR STAR Report and Infor EzRMS.']
  room_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights generated by bookings through this channel during the reporting period. A room night equals one room occupied for one night. Fundamental input for occupancy rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this channel contribution data was sourced (e.g., OPERA_PMS, SYNXIS_CRS, IDEASG3_RMS). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between systems in the Databricks Silver Layer ETL pipeline.']
  total_distribution_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total all-in cost of distributing inventory through this channel, including commissions, GDS transaction fees, OTA merchant model costs, and any incremental booking fees. Used to calculate net channel profitability and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) by channel.']

  Note: 'Transactional records measuring revenue contribution by distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, corporate, wholesale) per property and stay period. Captures channel type, booking volume, room nights, gross revenue, net revenue after commission, average commission rate, cost per booking (CPA), and channel mix percentage. Supports channel optimization and OTA partnership management decisions.'
}

Table revenue.displacement_analysis {
  displacement_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each displacement analysis record. Primary key for the displacement_analysis data product in the revenue domain Silver layer.']
  banquet_event_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Displacement calculations include banquet F&B revenue to offset room revenue displacement from group blocks. Required for group profitability analysis, acceptance decisions, total property revenue opt']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: When evaluating group blocks during high-demand periods driven by marketing campaigns, revenue managers need campaign context to assess true displacement cost and whether to accept group business.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand forecast record used as the basis for transient displacement estimates in this analysis. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product.']
  guest_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Displacement analysis quantifies revenue impact of accepting specific guest group blocks. Revenue managers calculate transient displacement cost, net contribution, and RevPAR impact for each guest.gue']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Displacement decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL posting for group vs transient revenue tracking, profitability analysis, and revenue mix reporting.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the market segment associated with the group or contract business being evaluated (e.g., MICE, corporate group, leisure group). Drives segmentation in revenue reporting.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the displacement analysis is being conducted. Links to the property master record.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the proposed rate plan associated with the group or contract business being evaluated. Used to assess the proposed rate against transient BAR (Best Available Rate).']
  analysis_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the displacement analysis was performed by the Revenue Management System or revenue manager. Represents the principal business event date for this record.']
