  analysis_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the displacement analysis, typically generated by IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation module (e.g., \'DISP-2024-00123\'). Used for cross-system traceability and revenue manager communication.']
  analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the displacement analysis record. \'draft\' indicates in-progress; \'pending_review\' awaits revenue manager sign-off; \'approved\' or \'rejected\' reflects final decision; \'superseded\' indicates a newer analysis replaced this one.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of business being evaluated for displacement. Distinguishes between group room blocks, long-stay contracts, tour operator allotments, and wholesale agreements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: group_evaluation|contract_evaluation|tour_operator|wholesale|corporate_negotiated|government — promote to reference product]']
  ancillary_revenue_contribution decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated ancillary revenue contribution from the group if accepted, including spa, parking, audio-visual, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Supports total spend analysis for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) evaluation.']
  channel_source varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the group or contract inquiry originated (e.g., direct sales, GDS (Global Distribution System), OTA (Online Travel Agency), Delphi event sales). Informs channel cost considerations in the displacement calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the displacement analysis record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this displacement analysis record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explaining the revenue manager\'s reasoning behind the recommendation outcome, including demand conditions, competitive context, or strategic considerations not captured in quantitative fields.']
  displacement_risk_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk rating assigned to the displacement analysis indicating the severity of transient revenue displacement if the group is accepted. Derived from net_revenue_impact and forecast_occupancy_pct thresholds configured in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  estimated_transient_adr_displaced decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated average per-room per-night rate that transient guests would have paid for the same rooms and dates, as forecast by IDeaS G3 RMS. Core metric for measuring revenue opportunity cost of accepting group business.']
  estimated_transient_rooms_displaced varchar [note: 'Estimated number of transient room-nights that would be displaced (lost) if the group block is accepted. Derived from IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecast for the stay period.']
  fb_revenue_contribution decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group if accepted, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Part of the total spend analysis supporting TRevPAR-based evaluation.']
  forecast_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected occupancy percentage (OCC) for the property during the stay period at the time of analysis, as sourced from the demand forecast. Contextualizes the displacement risk — high OCC increases displacement cost.']
  group_room_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total projected room revenue from the group block if accepted, calculated as proposed_group_rate × room_block_size × los_nights. Represents the revenue inflow side of the displacement equation.']
  is_peak_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay dates fall within a designated peak demand period (e.g., major events, holidays, high-season). Peak periods carry higher displacement risk and typically require higher minimum acceptable rates.']
  is_special_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a special event (concert, convention, sporting event, etc.) is occurring in the market during the stay period, which may elevate transient demand and increase displacement cost.']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of nights in the proposed group stay period (LOS). Calculated as stay_date_to minus stay_date_from. Used in yield optimization and LOS restriction analysis.']
  min_acceptable_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum per-room per-night rate at which accepting the group block would be revenue-neutral or accretive, as calculated by the displacement model. Used by sales teams as a negotiation floor.']
  net_revenue_impact decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net revenue impact of accepting the group business, calculated as total_group_spend minus transient_room_revenue_displaced. A positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative indicates displacement loss exceeds group gain.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or commentary entered by the revenue manager regarding special circumstances, negotiation history, or strategic context not captured in structured fields.']
  proposed_group_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The proposed per-room per-night rate offered to the group or contract client. Compared against estimated transient ADR (Average Daily Rate) to determine revenue opportunity cost.']
  recommendation_outcome varchar [note: 'Revenue management recommendation resulting from the displacement analysis. \'accept\' indicates the group is revenue-positive; \'reject\' indicates displacement loss exceeds group value; \'accept_with_conditions\' requires rate or block size negotiation; \'defer\' awaits further demand data; \'counter_offer\' proposes alternative terms.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who reviewed and acted upon the displacement analysis recommendation. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue manager completed their review and recorded the final recommendation outcome. Distinct from created_timestamp and updated_timestamp; represents the business decision event time.']
  revpar_impact decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated change in RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) attributable to accepting the group block, expressed as a positive or negative dollar amount per available room. Key KPI for revenue management decision-making.']
  rms_evaluation_reference varchar [note: 'Native evaluation or job identifier assigned by IDeaS G3 RMS when the group evaluation was executed. Enables traceability back to the source system run for audit and re-evaluation purposes.']
  room_block_size varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms in the proposed group or contract room block being evaluated for displacement impact. Core input to the displacement calculation.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the room type or category requested in the group block (e.g., STD, DLX, STE). Used to assess displacement impact at the room-type level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that generated or originated the displacement analysis (e.g., IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the special event influencing demand during the stay period, when is_special_event is true. Provides context for the displacement risk assessment.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'First night of the proposed group or contract stay period being evaluated. Defines the start of the displacement window for transient demand comparison.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'Last night of the proposed group or contract stay period being evaluated. Defines the end of the displacement window for transient demand comparison.']
  total_group_spend decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue from the group across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + ancillary). Used as the numerator in the full displacement value comparison to support TRevPAR-based decision-making.']
  transient_room_revenue_displaced decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated transient room revenue that would be lost if the group block is accepted, calculated as estimated_transient_adr_displaced × estimated_transient_rooms_displaced × los_nights. Represents the revenue opportunity cost.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the displacement analysis record was last modified. Tracks revisions made by revenue managers or automated RMS re-evaluations.']

  Note: 'Displacement analysis records evaluating the revenue opportunity cost of accepting group or contract business versus holding inventory for higher-rated transient demand. Captures analysis date, stay dates, room block size, proposed rate, estimated transient displacement rooms, estimated transient ADR displaced, net revenue impact, total spend analysis (rooms + F&B + ancillary), and recommendation outcome. Generated by IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation module.'
}

Table revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate {
  revenue_negotiated_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the negotiated rate agreement record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_account. Business justification: Negotiated rates are contracted with specific event accounts (corporate, consortia, national accounts). Essential for contract rate management, event planner rate loading, and account-specific pricing']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates are sometimes created as part of B2B marketing campaigns. Tracking the originating campaign supports contract performance analysis and measures campaign success in driving c']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are loaded to specific channels (GDS with specific chain codes, OTA corporate programs, direct booking engines). Rate loading and distribution tracking requires knowing whic']
  included_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates include outlet benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, lounge access). Required for contract fulfillment, benefit tracking, outlet revenue attribution, guest entitlement ver']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates post to specific GL accounts for contract revenue tracking, corporate account profitability analysis, and revenue recognition compliance.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Negotiated rates are classified by market segment (e.g., Corporate, Wholesale). revenue_negotiated_rate has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N negotiated_rate records → 1']
  minimum_loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates often include tier-based eligibility or enhanced benefits. Business process: enforcing tier requirements for corporate rate access, common in negotiated contracts that offer']
  negotiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales/revenue managers negotiate corporate rates; contract owner tracking required for renewal workflow management, commission attribution, and performance evaluation of negotiated rate portfolio mana']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates must comply with anti-discrimination and contract policies. Contract approval process validates policy compliance before rate activation. Essential for corporate rate govern']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this negotiated rate agreement applies. A single negotiated rate may be scoped to one property or replicated per property for multi-property contracts.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent rate plan definition under which this negotiated rate is structured. Links to the rate plan master in Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type to which this negotiated rate applies. Null if the rate applies to all room types under the agreement.']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance a reservation must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates same-day booking is permitted.']
  amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted nightly room rate (Average Daily Rate) agreed upon for this account. Represents the fixed or base rate per room per night in the specified currency. Core pricing field for revenue management and contract compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this negotiated rate agreement. Rates must be approved by revenue management leadership before loading into CRS/GDS channels.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for distribution.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate agreement received final approval for loading and distribution.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a structured string; detailed blackout records are managed in the rate_restriction product.']
  breakfast_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether breakfast is included in the negotiated rate. Affects F&B (Food and Beverage) revenue allocation and rate value proposition reporting.']
  commission_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage payable to the TMC, travel agency, or consortia for reservations booked under this negotiated rate. Expressed as a percentage of the negotiated rate amount.']
  committed_room_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights the account has committed to book under this negotiated rate agreement for the contract period. Basis for volume discount qualification and contract performance tracking.']
  consortia_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) under which this negotiated rate is offered. Null for direct corporate accounts not affiliated with a consortia.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and the rate is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended agreements. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement reference number assigned by the account, TMC, or legal department. Used for contract management, audit, and dispute resolution.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes contractually binding and the rate is available for booking. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter GDS chain code used to identify the hotel brand/chain in Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, and Worldspan for rate loading and distribution of this negotiated rate.']
  iata_number varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned numeric identifier for the Travel Management Company (TMC) or travel agency associated with this negotiated rate. Used for commission tracking and GDS rate access validation.']
  is_lra boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the property to honor the contracted rate as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of demand or yield management controls.']
  is_non_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reservations booked under this negotiated rate are non-refundable (NRR). Affects cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive nights allowed per reservation under this negotiated rate. Null if no upper limit is imposed.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of consecutive nights required per reservation to qualify for this negotiated rate. Enforced at booking in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  min_night_commitment varchar [note: 'Minimum number of room nights the account is contractually committed to consume during the contract period. Used for volume tracking and contract compliance reporting.']
  negotiation_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or sales manager responsible for negotiating and managing this rate agreement. Used for accountability and performance tracking.']
  rate_bar_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount or premium of this negotiated rate relative to the Best Available Rate (BAR) at time of contract negotiation. Used by revenue management to assess rate competitiveness and yield impact.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric rate code used to identify and load this negotiated rate in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. This is the externally-known business identifier referenced in GDS/CRS rate loading and reservation transactions.']
  rate_includes_tax boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the negotiated rate amount is inclusive of applicable taxes. Critical for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and guest billing accuracy.']
  rate_loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the negotiated rate was successfully loaded into Sabre SynXis CRS and/or Oracle OPERA PMS for distribution. Null if loading has not yet occurred.']
  rate_loading_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rate loading process in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Indicates whether the negotiated rate has been successfully distributed to all applicable booking channels.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the negotiated rate agreement, typically referencing the account or program name (e.g., \'Acme Corp 2025 Corporate Rate\'). Used in reporting and rate management dashboards.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable and distributed through CRS/GDS channels.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing structure for this negotiated rate. Fixed rates are static for the contract period; dynamic rates may fluctuate within agreed bounds; tiered rates vary by volume; per diem rates are government-mandated ceiling rates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this negotiated rate record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Salesforce CRM.']
  source_system_rate_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this negotiated rate record in the originating operational system (Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Salesforce CRM). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental ETL processing.']

  Note: 'Corporate and wholesale negotiated rate agreements defining contracted pricing for specific accounts, travel management companies (TMCs), and consortia. Captures account identifier, rate code, negotiated rate amount, LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, blackout dates, minimum night commitment, contract period, and rate loading status in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Distinct from public rate plans — these are account-specific contracted rates.'
}

Table revenue.strategy {
  strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for strategy']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Revenue strategies often incorporate specific marketing campaigns as tactical levers (e.g., deploy summer family campaign to drive weekend occupancy). Strategy owners need this link for execution trac']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive set definition used for benchmarking this strategy. The competitor set defines the peer properties against which RGI, MPI, and ARI targets are measured via STR STAR Report.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies are implemented at cost center level for tactical execution, departmental accountability, and operational performance management.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand forecast record that informed and underpins this revenue strategy. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product to provide the analytical basis for strategy targets and tactical decisions.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Revenue strategies are aligned with financial budgets for strategic planning, ensuring revenue tactics support approved financial targets and ownership expectations.']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue strategies explicitly target GSS improvement as a demand driver (experience-driven revenue strategy). Strategic plans link rate positioning to satisfaction goals—properties pursuing rate growt']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this revenue strategy applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  superseded_by_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer revenue strategy record that replaced this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables lineage tracking of strategy evolution and supports audit trail for revenue governance.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the strategy status transitioned to \'active\' and began governing live pricing and inventory decisions. Distinct from approval timestamp — a strategy may be approved in advance and activated at a later point.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Director or authorized revenue management leader who formally approved this strategy. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions per SOX internal controls requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue strategy was formally approved by the authorizing Revenue Director. Establishes the official approval event in the strategy lifecycle for governance and audit trail purposes.']
  channel_focus varchar [note: 'The primary distribution channel(s) this strategy targets for demand generation (e.g., \'Direct Web\', \'GDS\', \'OTA\', \'Voice/CRS\', \'Corporate Direct\'). Informs channel mix optimization and OTA partnership management decisions. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS channel codes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue strategy record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the strategy lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary target values in this strategy record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.']
  horizon_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the temporal scope of the strategy planning horizon. \'weekly\' for short-term tactical adjustments, \'monthly\' for rolling monthly plans, \'quarterly\' for seasonal strategies, \'annual\' for full-year budget-aligned strategies, \'event_based\' for strategies tied to specific demand events.']
  inventory_control_approach varchar [note: 'The approved inventory management methodology for this strategy period. \'open\' allows all bookings, \'hurdle_rate\' applies minimum rate thresholds, \'length_of_stay\' (LOS) enforces minimum/maximum stay restrictions, \'nested\' uses tiered availability controls, \'closed\' restricts availability for specific segments or channels.']
  key_tactical_actions varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the specific tactical actions approved as part of this strategy (e.g., \'Open BAR for all channels on shoulder nights\', \'Apply 5-night minimum LOS restriction over peak weekend\', \'Close OTA channels when occupancy exceeds 85%\'). Provides governance documentation for revenue decisions.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this revenue strategy. Drives workflow triggers in the revenue management calendar and ensures timely strategy adjustments based on actual vs. target performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text commentary, rationale, or contextual notes provided by the revenue management team regarding this strategy. May include market intelligence, competitive observations, or risk considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full strategy window for yield optimization and performance tracking.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Defines the beginning of the stay-date window over which the strategy governs pricing and inventory decisions.']
  pricing_approach varchar [note: 'The approved pricing methodology governing rate-setting for this strategy period. \'bar_based\' uses Best Available Rate (BAR) as the anchor, \'dynamic\' applies algorithmic Dynamic Rate Rules (DRR), \'fixed\' uses static rate plans, \'hybrid\' combines approaches, \'competitive_parity\' aligns rates to competitive set benchmarks.']
  primary_market_segment_focus varchar [note: 'The primary guest market segment this strategy is designed to optimize (e.g., \'Corporate Transient\', \'Leisure FIT\', \'Group MICE\', \'OTA Leisure\', \'Government\'). Aligns with market segment codes used in Oracle OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3 RMS segmentation.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The approved cadence at which this strategy is scheduled for performance review and potential revision by the revenue management team. Governs the operational rhythm of the revenue management process.']
  rms_strategy_reference_code varchar [note: 'The native strategy or scenario identifier from the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables traceability back to the originating RMS record for reconciliation and system-of-record alignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this strategy record originated. \'IDeaS_G3_RMS\' for strategies generated by IDeaS G3, \'Infor_EzRMS\' for Infor-sourced strategies, \'Manual\' for strategies entered directly by revenue management staff.']
  special_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this strategy was created in response to a specific demand-driving special event (e.g., major conference, sporting event, concert, holiday period). When True, the strategy may include event-specific pricing premiums and inventory controls.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name of the demand-driving special event associated with this strategy when special_event_flag is True (e.g., \'Super Bowl LX\', \'Annual Medical Conference\', \'New Year Holiday Period\'). Provides context for strategy rationale and post-event performance analysis.']
  strategy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this revenue strategy record within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS strategy identifiers.']
  strategy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the revenue strategy (e.g., \'Q3 2025 Transient Yield Push\', \'Holiday Season Total Revenue Strategy\'). Used for identification in reporting and governance workflows.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue strategy record. \'draft\' indicates work-in-progress, \'pending_approval\' awaits revenue director sign-off, \'approved\' is authorized but not yet in effect, \'active\' is currently governing pricing decisions, \'superseded\' has been replaced by a newer strategy, \'archived\' is retained for historical audit.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue strategy by business segment. \'transient\' covers individual guest pricing, \'group\' covers group block strategies, \'total_revenue\' encompasses all revenue streams (TRevPAR focus), \'food_and_beverage\' targets F&B revenue optimization, \'events\' covers MICE and event revenue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|loyalty — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  target_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property\'s operating currency for the strategy period. ADR is the primary pricing KPI representing average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Used to benchmark actual ADR performance against strategic intent.']
  target_ari decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Average Rate Index (ARI) for the strategy period. ARI = Property ADR / Competitive Set ADR. Measures the property\'s rate positioning relative to its competitive set. Used alongside MPI and RGI for comprehensive competitive benchmarking.']
  target_goppar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) for the strategy period. GOPPAR incorporates operating costs and provides a profitability-adjusted view of revenue performance, aligned with USALI GOP reporting standards.']
  target_mpi decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Market Penetration Index (MPI) for the strategy period. MPI = Property Occupancy / Competitive Set Occupancy. Measures the property\'s occupancy share relative to its competitive set as defined in STR STAR Report.']
  target_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The approved target Occupancy Rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the strategy period. Represents the proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core input to RevPAR calculation and yield optimization decisions.']
  target_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property\'s operating currency for the strategy period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. The primary composite KPI for hotel revenue performance and competitive benchmarking via STR STAR Report.']
  target_rgi decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Revenue Generation Index (RGI) for the strategy period. RGI = Property RevPAR / Competitive Set RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data.']
  target_trevpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, spa, events, ancillary) per available room for the strategy period. Relevant for total revenue strategies beyond rooms-only yield optimization.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the last person to modify this strategy record. Supports change management and audit trail for revenue governance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue strategy record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change event for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this strategy record. Increments each time the strategy is revised and re-approved. Supports audit trail and change management for revenue governance workflows.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue management professional who created this strategy record. Supports governance, accountability, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Revenue strategy records documenting the approved pricing and inventory strategy for a property over a defined planning horizon. Captures strategy period, property, strategy type (transient, group, total revenue), target ADR, target occupancy, target RevPAR, key tactical actions, strategy status (draft, approved, active), and approving revenue director. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions.'
}

Table revenue.wash_factor {
  wash_factor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each wash factor record in the revenue domain Silver layer. Primary key for the wash_factor data product.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the market segment associated with this wash factor record (e.g., corporate group, association, SMERF, leisure group). Supports segment-level attrition analysis and Revenue Management System (RMS) demand segmentation.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this wash factor record applies. Enables property-level group demand forecasting and displacement analysis within IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the room type to which this wash factor applies. Allows wash factor differentiation by room category (e.g., standard, suite, accessible) for granular group block attrition modeling.']
  adjusted_wash_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Revenue manager-adjusted wash factor applied to group block forecasting after applying business judgment, seasonal adjustments, or RMS recommendations on top of the historical wash percentage. This is the operative value consumed by IDeaS G3 RMS for net group pickup forecasting and transient displacement calculations.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the revenue manager for any manual adjustment applied to the historical wash percentage to derive the adjusted wash percentage. Documents the business rationale for overrides (e.g., \'Adjusted upward due to new corporate account with unproven pickup history\').']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved the adjusted wash percentage for use in RMS forecasting. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wash factor record was formally approved by the revenue manager for activation in the RMS. Provides a precise audit trail for governance and SOX compliance.']
  block_size_max_rooms varchar [note: 'Maximum number of contracted group room nights that defines the upper bound of the block size tier. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., mega blocks with no ceiling).']
  block_size_min_rooms varchar [note: 'Minimum number of contracted group room nights that defines the lower bound of the block size tier for this wash factor record.']
  block_size_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical tier representing the size of the group room block (e.g., small: 1–10 rooms, medium: 11–50 rooms, large: 51–200 rooms, mega: 200+ rooms). Block size is a significant predictor of wash behavior; larger blocks tend to exhibit higher attrition rates.']
  booking_lead_time_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorical bucket representing the number of days between group block creation and the group arrival date. Lead time is a critical predictor of wash behavior; groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher attrition rates. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS to apply the correct wash factor based on booking horizon.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel code associated with this wash factor (e.g., GDS, direct, OTA, voice). Group blocks sourced through different channels may exhibit distinct attrition patterns, warranting channel-specific wash factors.']
  confidence_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level associated with the historical wash percentage estimate, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Derived from the sample size and variance of historical group block attrition data. Informs revenue managers of the reliability of the wash factor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wash factor record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the record lifecycle in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  day_of_week_pattern varchar [note: 'Day-of-week pattern for which this wash factor applies. Group attrition rates can differ between weekday and weekend arrivals. Used to apply day-of-week-sensitive wash adjustments in the RMS forecast.']
  displacement_impact_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of transient room revenue displaced by the group block after applying the wash factor. Quantifies the opportunity cost of accepting the group block versus holding inventory for transient demand. Used in group displacement analysis within IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this wash factor record becomes active and is applied by the Revenue Management System (RMS) for group demand forecasting. Supports temporal versioning of wash factor configurations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this wash factor record ceases to be active. A null value indicates the record is open-ended and remains in effect until superseded. Enables temporal management of wash factor versioning.']
  factor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wash factor record. Controls whether the record is actively consumed by the RMS for forecasting. \'Superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced this record; \'pending_review\' indicates the record requires revenue manager approval before activation.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group business type to which this wash factor applies. Group type is a primary driver of attrition behavior; corporate groups typically wash differently than SMERF (Social, Military, Educational, Religious, Fraternal) or association groups. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|smerf|leisure|government|sports|tour_operator — promote to reference product]']
  historical_observation_end_date date [note: 'End date of the historical data window used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Together with historical_observation_start_date, defines the precise lookback period for wash factor derivation.']
  historical_observation_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the historical data window used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Defines the lookback period for the statistical derivation of the wash factor.']
  historical_wash_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Observed historical attrition (wash) percentage derived from actual group block pickup data. Represents the proportion of contracted group room nights that were not consumed (i.e., rooms blocked but not picked up). Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.2500 = 25%). Source data from Oracle OPERA PMS group block history.']
  is_default_factor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wash factor record serves as the default fallback when no more specific wash factor matches a group block\'s attributes (group type, lead time, block size, season). True = default factor; False = specific factor.']
  is_rms_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wash factor record is currently being consumed by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for live group demand forecasting and transient displacement calculations. True = actively used in RMS; False = not currently applied.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this wash factor record was most recently reviewed and validated by the revenue management team. Used to identify stale wash factors that may require recalibration.']
  lead_time_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days before arrival that defines the upper bound of the booking lead time bucket. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., 365+ days). Together with lead_time_min_days, precisely defines the lead time window.']
  lead_time_min_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days before arrival that defines the lower bound of the booking lead time bucket. Together with lead_time_max_days, precisely defines the lead time window for this wash factor record.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and recalibration of this wash factor record. Derived from last_reviewed_date and review_frequency. Supports proactive revenue management governance workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional revenue manager commentary, special conditions, or contextual information relevant to this wash factor record. May include references to specific group accounts, market events, or RMS configuration notes.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Prescribed frequency at which this wash factor record should be reviewed and recalibrated by the revenue management team. Ensures wash factors remain statistically current and reflective of evolving group booking behavior.']
  rms_wash_factor_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this wash factor record as stored in the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver layer data product and the operational RMS source system.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of historical group block observations used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Indicates statistical reliability of the wash factor; low sample sizes may warrant wider confidence intervals or manual override.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Seasonal classification for which this wash factor is applicable. Wash behavior varies significantly by season; peak season groups may exhibit lower attrition due to higher demand, while off-peak groups may wash more aggressively.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this wash factor record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and auditability in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wash factor record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.']
  wash_factor_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this wash factor configuration within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used as the business key for integration with IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.']
  wash_factor_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for this wash factor configuration (e.g., \'Corporate Group 30-Day Lead Time — High Season\'). Used by revenue managers to identify and manage wash factor rules.']
  wash_pct_variance decimal(7,4) [note: 'Difference between the adjusted wash percentage and the historical wash percentage (adjusted_wash_pct minus historical_wash_pct). Quantifies the degree of manual override applied by the revenue manager relative to historical norms. Positive values indicate a more conservative (higher attrition) assumption.']

  Note: 'Group block wash factor records defining the expected attrition (wash) percentage applied to group room blocks when forecasting net group pickup. Captures group type, booking lead time bucket, historical wash percentage, adjusted wash factor, effective date, and property. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS to constrain group demand in the forecast and avoid over-displacement of transient business.'
}

Table revenue.total_revenue_actuals {
  total_revenue_actuals_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each daily total revenue actuals record at the property level. Primary key for this Silver Layer data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Total revenue actuals are tracked by cost center for USALI departmental reporting, manager accountability, and departmental P&L preparation.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Total revenue actuals are compared against financial budgets for variance analysis, board reporting, and management performance evaluation against approved plans.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fiscal calendar period (month/quarter/year) under which this stay date falls, enabling USALI-aligned financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Total revenue actuals post to GL accounts for financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and external financial reporting compliance.']
  primary_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Total revenue actuals aggregate outlet-level F&B for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for financial consolidation, total property performance reporting, outlet contribution analysis t']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Total revenue actuals roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial statements, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level performance analysis.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which total revenue actuals are recorded. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.']
  reconciled_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Finance analysts reconcile total property revenue; employee attribution required for data quality accountability, GL posting workflow tracking, and performance evaluation of financial reporting accura']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue actuals record contains a post-close adjustment or restatement to previously posted figures. When true, the adjustment_reason field provides context. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any post-close adjustment or restatement applied to this revenue actuals record. Populated only when adjustment_flag is true. Required for SOX audit trail and USALI financial controls.']
  adr decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average Daily Rate for the stay date, calculated as rooms revenue divided by rooms sold. Core KPI for revenue performance benchmarking against STR STAR Report and competitive set. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS daily actuals feed.']
  ari decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average Rate Index for the stay date, representing the property\'s ADR divided by the competitive set\'s ADR. Measures the property\'s pricing performance relative to its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report.']
  complimentary_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary basis (zero-rated) on the stay date. Tracked separately per USALI and STR reporting standards to distinguish from revenue-generating occupied rooms.']
  cpor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total operating cost per occupied room for the stay date, calculated as total departmental operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Key efficiency metric per USALI and AHLA benchmarking standards. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA cost accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue actuals record was first created in the Silver Layer data product, typically following the nightly ETL load from Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency property portfolios.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of the week corresponding to the stay date. Used for day-of-week revenue pattern analysis, demand segmentation, and yield optimization in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ebitda_contribution decimal(18,4) [note: 'EBITDA contribution for the stay date at the property level, representing GOP less management fees and other fixed charges but before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA. Required for SOX financial controls and IFRS/GAAP reporting.']
  fb_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net Food and Beverage revenue for the stay date, aggregated across all F&B outlets (restaurant, bar, banquet, in-room dining) sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and Delphi by Amadeus. Aligns with USALI Food and Beverage Department.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue figures were posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from stay date due to night audit timing or period-end adjustments. Required for USALI and SOX financial controls.']
  gop_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross Operating Profit for the stay date, representing total revenue minus total departmental and undistributed operating expenses, before fixed charges. Core USALI profitability metric sourced from SAP S/4HANA GL.']
  goppar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency per available room. Key executive KPI per USALI and AHLA standards.']
  house_use_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms occupied for internal hotel use (staff, management) on the stay date. Excluded from revenue-generating occupancy per USALI and STR standards.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay date falls on a public or bank holiday in the property\'s market. Used for holiday demand uplift analysis and dynamic pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  is_special_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay date coincides with a special event (e.g., convention, concert, sporting event) that materially impacts demand and pricing. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting and revenue strategy.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay date falls on a weekend (Friday or Saturday night per hospitality convention). Used for weekend vs. weekday revenue segmentation and pricing strategy analysis.']
  miscellaneous_income decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net miscellaneous and non-operating income for the stay date, including rental income, cancellation fees, and other non-departmental revenue lines per USALI Non-Operating Income and Expenses.']
  mpi decimal(8,4) [note: 'Market Penetration Index for the stay date, representing the property\'s occupancy rate divided by the competitive set\'s occupancy rate. Measures the property\'s fair share of market demand. Sourced from STR STAR Report.']
  occupancy_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Occupancy percentage for the stay date, calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). Core KPI for yield optimization and STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  other_operated_dept_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net revenue from all other operated departments (e.g., golf, retail, recreation, business center) for the stay date, excluding rooms, F&B, spa, and parking. Aligns with USALI Other Operated Departments aggregate line.']
  out_of_order_rooms varchar [note: 'Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the stay date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Deducted from available room count per STR and USALI standards.']
  parking_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net parking and valet revenue for the stay date, classified under USALI Other Operated Departments.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the revenue actuals record indicating whether figures are preliminary, posted to the general ledger, adjusted, period-closed, or voided. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA GL posting workflow.']
  reporting_segment varchar [note: 'Brand or service tier segment of the property for this revenue record (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service). Enables cross-segment revenue performance benchmarking and portfolio analytics aligned with AHLA and STR segmentation standards.']
  revpar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as rooms revenue divided by rooms available (or ADR multiplied by occupancy rate). Primary yield optimization metric tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS and benchmarked via STR STAR Report.']
  rgi decimal(8,4) [note: 'Revenue Generation Index for the stay date, representing the property\'s RevPAR divided by the competitive set\'s RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report and Infor EzRMS.']
  rooms_available varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms available for sale on the stay date after deducting out-of-order and out-of-service rooms. Denominator for RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory.']
  rooms_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net rooms revenue for the stay date at the property, sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Represents the primary revenue line under USALI Rooms Department. Used as the numerator in ADR and RevPAR calculations.']
  rooms_revenue_budget decimal(18,4) [note: 'Budgeted rooms revenue for the stay date as set in the annual operating plan. Enables variance analysis between actual and budgeted rooms revenue performance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budgeting module.']
  rooms_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the stay date, excluding complimentary and house-use rooms unless otherwise configured. Used to compute ADR and Occupancy Rate (OCC). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this revenue actuals record was sourced (e.g., OPERA_PMS for rooms revenue, SAP_S4HANA for financial close figures). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Silver Layer.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., OPERA PMS daily revenue summary ID or SAP S/4HANA document number). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  spa_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net spa and wellness revenue for the stay date, classified under USALI Other Operated Departments. Includes treatments, retail, and membership fees.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which revenue actuals are reported. Represents the business date (night) in Oracle OPERA PMS, used as the primary time dimension for ADR, RevPAR, TRevPAR, and GOPPAR calculations.']
  total_operating_expenses decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total departmental and undistributed operating expenses for the stay date, including labor, cost of goods sold, and other direct costs across all departments. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA cost accounting. Used to derive GOP.']
  total_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net revenue across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous income) for the stay date. Primary metric for TRevPAR calculation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA daily revenue close.']
  total_revenue_budget decimal(18,4) [note: 'Budgeted total revenue for the stay date across all departments as set in the annual operating plan. Enables variance analysis between actual and budgeted total revenue. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budgeting module.']
  trevpar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total Revenue Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as total revenue divided by rooms available. Captures full revenue contribution across all departments beyond rooms. Key metric for holistic revenue strategy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue actuals record was last modified in the Silver Layer, reflecting any adjustments, restatements, or corrections applied after initial load.']

  Note: 'Daily total revenue actuals aggregated across all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, spa, parking, ancillary) at the property level, aligned to USALI departmental reporting. Captures stay date, property, rooms revenue, F&B revenue, other operated department revenue, miscellaneous income, total revenue, CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room), GOP (Gross Operating Profit), and EBITDA contribution. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA. Supports TRevPAR and GOPPAR calculation.'
}

Table revenue.segment_program_eligibility {
  segment_program_eligibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the segment-program eligibility record. Primary key.']
  experience_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the experience program for which this market segment is eligible']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the market segment that is eligible for this experience program']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the experience program for which this market segment is eligible']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment-program eligibility configuration expires. Null indicates ongoing eligibility.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment-program eligibility configuration becomes active. Allows for time-bound eligibility rules.']
  enrollment_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests in this market segment are eligible to enroll in this experience program. False may indicate restricted or conditional eligibility.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the priority or preference level for this segment-program combination. Lower numbers indicate higher priority when multiple programs are available.']
  program_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Segment-specific discount percentage applied to this experience program for guests in this market segment. Null indicates standard program pricing applies.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the eligibility configuration between market segments and experience programs. It captures which market segments are eligible for which experience programs, along with segment-specific enrollment rules, discounts, and priority rankings. Each record links one market segment to one experience program with attributes that exist only in the context of this eligibility relationship.. Existence Justification: Experience programs are configured to target multiple market segments with segment-specific eligibility rules, discounts, and priority rankings. For example, a Romance Package program may be eligible for Leisure, Couples, and Transient segments, each with different discount percentages and priority levels. Conversely, a single market segment like Transient participates in multiple programs (Romance, Spa, Golf packages) with different eligibility criteria and effective dates for each program.'
}

Table loyalty.member {
  member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key.']
  enrollment_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Member acquisition costs are allocated to the enrolling property\'s cost center for departmental P&L. Marketing ROI analysis and CAC (customer acquisition cost) reporting by department require linking ']
  marketing_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Members are assigned to marketing segments for targeted campaign delivery, personalization, and offer optimization. Essential for marketing operations to identify which loyalty members belong to which']
  member_enrollment_hotel_property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property where the member enrolled in the loyalty program, if enrollment occurred at a physical location.']
  member_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'FK to guest.profile.profile_id — MUST-HAVE: Loyalty member must resolve to guest identity for personalization, stay history correlation, and profile merge operations. Without this FK, loyalty benefits cannot be applied at check-in.']
  member_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain. Links the loyalty member to their guest profile which owns PII and stay history.']
  member_property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the loyalty tier product. Represents the current tier assignment (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Elite).']
  account_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the membership account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for membership account closure. Used for churn analysis and program improvement.']
  communication_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive marketing communications and promotional offers from the loyalty program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the member record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_preference varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred currency for displaying points value and pricing. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code.']
  current_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current available points balance that can be redeemed by the member. Reflects earned points minus redeemed and expired points.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive email communications. Separate from general communication opt-in for channel-specific consent.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel effectiveness analysis.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the beginning of the member lifecycle.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred language for communications and service delivery. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.']
  last_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the member\'s most recent completed stay. Used for recency analysis and churn prediction.']
  lifetime_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights stayed by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculation.']
  lifetime_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points earned by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and lifetime value tracking.']
  lifetime_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the member across all stays, F&B (Food and Beverage), and ancillary services since enrollment. Key LTV (Lifetime Value) metric.']
  lifetime_stays varchar [note: 'Total number of completed stays by the member since enrollment. Key metric for member engagement and tier qualification.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique membership identifier assigned to the loyalty program member. Used for member recognition and program transactions.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership account. Active members can accrue and redeem points; suspended or closed accounts have restricted access.']
  next_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the member\'s next confirmed reservation. Used for engagement tracking and personalized offers.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) rating provided by the member. Scale of -100 to 100, measuring likelihood to recommend the brand.']
  partner_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to share their data with loyalty program partners (airlines, car rentals, etc.) for cross-program benefits.']
  points_expired varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points that have expired due to inactivity or program rules.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points redeemed by the member for rewards, upgrades, or benefits since enrollment.']
  preferred_room_type varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred room type for reservations. Used for personalized booking experiences and upgrade offers.']
  salt_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'SALT (Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking) composite score measuring member satisfaction and loyalty. Proprietary metric combining multiple engagement and satisfaction indicators.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive SMS/text message communications.']
  tier_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s current tier status expires. Used for tier renewal campaigns and retention efforts.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the member qualified for their current tier. Used for tier anniversary tracking and benefit eligibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the member record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is designated as a VIP for special recognition and service. May be based on tier, LTV (Lifetime Value), or manual assignment.']
  ytd_nights varchar [note: 'Number of room nights stayed by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.']
  ytd_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the member in the current calendar year. Used for tier qualification and annual performance tracking.']
  ytd_stays varchar [note: 'Number of completed stays by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Captures membership identity, enrollment date, current tier assignment (FK to tier), lifetime points balance, LTV metrics (total revenue, stays, nights), SALT scores, program opt-in preferences, communication consent, membership number, and account status (active, suspended, closed). This is the SSOT for loyalty membership identity and current member state — distinct from the guest profile in the guest domain, which owns PII and stay history. References guest_id as a foreign key to the guest domain. Also serves as the anchor for member segment assignments (segment memberships tracked temporally within member_segment).'
}

Table loyalty.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program tier. Primary key.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Tier definitions are part of program configuration - the tier structure is defined at the program level. Adding program_config_id links tier definitions to their governing program, enabling program-le']
  bonus_points_on_enrollment varchar [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier status.']
  breakfast_benefit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive complimentary breakfast during their stays.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used for visual representation of the tier in digital interfaces and marketing materials (e.g., #C0C0C0 for Silver, #FFD700 for Gold).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the system.']
  dedicated_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have access to a dedicated customer service line or concierge team.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying tiers in user interfaces and reports. Lower numbers appear first.']
  downgrade_grace_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of grace period provided before downgrading a member who no longer meets qualification thresholds.']
  early_checkin_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before standard check-in time that members at this tier are entitled to for early check-in (e.g., 2 for 1pm check-in when standard is 3pm).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this tier definition expires or was discontinued. Null for currently active tiers with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this tier definition became or will become active and available for member assignment.']
  guaranteed_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have guaranteed room availability when booking within specified advance notice periods.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL or path to the icon or badge image representing this tier in digital channels.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was last updated.']
  late_checkout_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours past standard checkout time that members at this tier are entitled to for late checkout (e.g., 2 for 2pm checkout when standard is 12pm).']
  lounge_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have complimentary access to executive lounges or club floors at participating properties.']
  points_earning_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earning for members at this tier (e.g., 1.5 means 50% bonus points, 2.0 means double points).']
  priority_reservation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive priority handling for reservations and waitlist requests.']
  qualification_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the qualification spend threshold (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  qualification_nights_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying nights required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  qualification_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which qualification activity is measured (e.g., 12 for annual qualification, 24 for rolling two-year window).']
  qualification_points_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of loyalty points required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  qualification_spend_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary spend amount required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period. Typically measured in the program\'s base currency.']
  qualification_stays_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying stays required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  room_upgrade_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier are eligible for complimentary room upgrades at check-in (subject to availability).']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, DIAMOND). Used for system integration and display.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tier, including benefits summary and value proposition, used for member communications and marketing.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the program hierarchy. Lower numbers indicate entry-level tiers, higher numbers indicate elite status. Used for tier comparison and upgrade logic.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Silver Elite, Gold Premier, Platinum Honors, Diamond Ambassador).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily suspended; discontinued tiers are retired but may have legacy members.']
  validity_months varchar [note: 'Number of months that tier status remains valid once achieved, before re-qualification is required (e.g., 12 for annual renewal, 24 for two-year validity).']
  welcome_amenity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive a welcome amenity (e.g., fruit basket, beverage credit) upon arrival.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the loyalty program tier hierarchy (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captures tier name, tier code, qualification thresholds (nights, stays, points, spend), benefit entitlements, upgrade/downgrade rules, tier validity period, and display order. Governs tier assignment logic and benefit eligibility across the program.'
}

Table loyalty.tier_history {
  tier_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this tier status change record. Primary key for the tier history entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the agent or administrator who manually triggered this tier change, if applicable. Null for automated system-triggered changes.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who experienced this tier status change.']
  new_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: tier_history has new_tier_code (STRING) but no FK to the tier entity. Adding new_tier_id FK formalizes the link to the tier entity for the new tier, enabling tier-level history analysis and ensuring r']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: tier_history has promotional_campaign_code (STRING) for tier changes resulting from promotional campaigns. Adding promotion_id FK formalizes this relationship, enabling promotion-driven tier change tr']
  bonus_points_awarded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to the member as a welcome benefit or incentive for achieving this new tier status.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this tier change event, used for external reference and audit tracking.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the business reason for this tier status change.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why this tier change occurred, including any special circumstances or manual intervention rationale.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this tier status change was processed and recorded in the system.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the directional nature of this tier change relative to the tier hierarchy.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the new tier status became active and benefits became available to the member.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring requalification or resulting in downgrade.']
  is_current_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier history record represents the members current active tier status (true) or a historical tier status (false).']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification (email, SMS, app push) was successfully sent to the member informing them of this tier change.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member.']
  override_approval_code varchar [note: 'Authorization or approval code for manual tier overrides, used for audit and compliance tracking of non-standard tier grants.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed business justification provided by the approving authority for manual tier status overrides.']
  previous_tier_code varchar [note: 'The tier status code the member held immediately before this change (e.g., SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).']
  qualification_basis varchar [note: 'The primary metric or criteria used to determine eligibility for this tier change (nights, stays, points, spend, or combination thereof). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualifying_nights|qualifying_stays|qualifying_points|qualifying_spend|combination|status_match|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualifying_nights_achieved varchar [note: 'Total number of qualifying nights the member accumulated during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the measurement period during which qualifying activity was accumulated for this tier change.']
  qualifying_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the measurement period during which qualifying activity was accumulated for this tier change.']
  qualifying_points_achieved decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of qualifying loyalty points the member earned during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary spend amount in the program currency that the member accumulated during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_spend_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the qualifying spend amount.']
  qualifying_stays_achieved varchar [note: 'Total number of qualifying stays the member completed during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  rollover_nights_granted varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying nights carried forward or granted as a tier benefit to assist with future requalification.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this tier history record originated (e.g., SFDC_CRM, OPERA_PMS).']
  status_match_source_program varchar [note: 'Name of the external loyalty program from which status was matched, if this tier change resulted from a competitive status match offer.']
  status_match_source_tier varchar [note: 'The tier level held by the member in the external program that was matched to grant this tier status.']
  threshold_nights_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying nights required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_points_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying points required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_spend_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum monetary spend amount required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_stays_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying stays required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  tier_extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extension of the tier expiry date was granted as part of this change (e.g., due to service recovery, pandemic policy, or loyalty gesture).']
  tier_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of months by which the tier expiry date was extended beyond the standard qualification period.']
  triggered_by_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system that initiated or triggered this tier change (e.g., Salesforce CRM, batch requalification job, manual override interface).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every tier status change for a loyalty member, including the qualification evidence that triggered the change.
