  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion. Values: draft (being designed), scheduled (approved, not yet live), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), cancelled (terminated early).']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotion mechanism. Values: bonus_points_multiplier (earn extra points per dollar), double_nights (accelerate tier qualification), targeted_offer (personalized to segment), welcome_back (re-engagement), birthday_bonus (member birthday reward), spend_challenge (gamified spending goal).']
  qualifying_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the requirements a member must meet to earn the promotion bonus. Examples: \'Complete 3 stays between June 1 and August 31\', \'Spend minimum $500 on F&B (Food and Beverage) during stay\', \'Book via mobile app using rate code MOBILE2024\'.']
  qualifying_property_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property IDs or property codes where stays qualify for this promotion. Null or \'ALL\' if promotion applies to all properties. Example: \'PROP001,PROP045,PROP102\' or \'ALL\'.']
  qualifying_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes (BAR - Best Available Rate, corporate codes, promotional codes) that qualify for this promotion. Null or \'ALL\' if all rate codes qualify. Example: \'BAR,CORP,PROMO2024\'.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must explicitly register or opt-in to participate in this promotion. True = registration required before qualifying activity counts; False = automatic enrollment for eligible members.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions. True = member can earn bonuses from multiple promotions simultaneously; False = this promotion cannot be stacked with others.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes active and members can begin earning toward the bonus. Qualifying activity before this date does not count.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the full legal terms and conditions document for this promotion. Displayed to members during enrollment and in promotional materials.']
  total_completions varchar [note: 'Count of members who have successfully completed all qualifying criteria and earned the promotion bonus. Used for conversion rate and campaign effectiveness analysis.']
  total_enrollments varchar [note: 'Count of unique members who have enrolled or been auto-enrolled in this promotion. Used for campaign reach and engagement metrics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the marketing or loyalty team member who created this promotion record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining loyalty program promotions and bonus offers, including member participation tracking. Captures promotion name, type (bonus points multiplier, double nights, targeted offer, welcome back, birthday bonus, spend challenge), target segment or tier, qualifying criteria (minimum nights, minimum spend, specific properties, specific rate codes), bonus awarded, registration requirement, start/end dates, and budget cap. For member participation: enrollment date, enrollment channel, member promotion status (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded), qualifying activity progress, bonus awarded flag, award date, and opt-out date. Drives personalized offer delivery, campaign economics, and promotion fulfillment tracking.'
}

Table loyalty.promotion_enrollment {
  promotion_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion enrollment record. Primary key.']
  campaign_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_execution. Business justification: Tracks which specific marketing campaign execution (email send, paid ad, social post) drove a member to enroll in a loyalty promotion. Critical for marketing attribution and campaign ROI measurement.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Promotion enrollments must link to distribution channel for campaign channel attribution analysis, measuring which channels drive highest promotion participation rates, and calculating channel-specifi']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who enrolled in the promotion.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific promotion the member enrolled in.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion bonus was awarded to the member\'s account.']
  award_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction ID for the bonus award, used for reconciliation and member inquiries.']
  award_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the bonus was posted to the member\'s loyalty account.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bonus awarded (e.g., account credit, voucher value).']
  bonus_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion bonus has been awarded to the member (True/False).']
  bonus_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bonus amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  bonus_points varchar [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to the member upon promotion completion.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was cancelled by the system or staff.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation of the enrollment (e.g., member ineligibility, promotion terminated, fraud detection).']
  communication_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrollment confirmation communication was sent to the member (True/False).']
  communication_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment confirmation communication was sent to the member.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the member completed the qualifying activity requirements for the promotion.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the promotion requirements were completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the data platform.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the member enrolled or was enrolled in the promotion.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the enrollment occurred (opt-in by member, auto-enrolled by system, staff-enrolled, imported from external source).']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for this promotion enrollment, used for member communication and tracking.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the member\'s participation in the promotion (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded, cancelled, opted-out). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|in_progress|completed|expired|awarded|cancelled|opted_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the enrollment was recorded in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment expires if the member has not completed the promotion requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the enrollment, used for special circumstances or staff annotations.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'Date when the member opted out of the promotion, if applicable.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the member or system for opting out of the promotion.']
  progress_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of qualifying activity completed toward the promotion threshold (0.00 to 100.00).']
  progress_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current progress value toward the qualifying threshold (e.g., 3 nights completed out of 5 required).']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Property code where the enrollment occurred, if applicable (e.g., front desk enrollment).']
  qualifying_activity_type varchar [note: 'Type of activity required to complete the promotion (nights stayed, number of stays, revenue spent, points earned, transactions, referrals).']
  qualifying_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target threshold value the member must achieve to complete the promotion (e.g., 5 nights, 1000 points, 500 USD revenue).']
  qualifying_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the qualifying threshold (nights, stays, points, currency, transactions, referrals).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created this enrollment record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, OPERA PMS, Mobile App).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this enrollment record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tier_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Loyalty tier of the member at the time of enrollment, used for tier-specific promotion eligibility and tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Transactional association record capturing a loyalty member registering or being enrolled in a specific promotion. Captures enrollment date, enrollment channel, promotion status for the member (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded), qualifying activity progress (nights completed, spend accumulated), bonus awarded flag, award date, and opt-out date if applicable. Enables promotion fulfillment tracking and member communication triggers.'
}

Table loyalty.member_preference {
  member_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the member preference record. Primary key.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who holds this preference. Links to the loyalty member master record.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property guest services teams honor member preferences (e.g., \'never book me at this property\', \'preferred property for business travel\') during reservation and check-in. Currently restricted_property']
  brand_restriction varchar [note: 'The brand code if this preference is restricted to a specific brand within the portfolio. Nullable if preference applies across all brands.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'The confidence or reliability level of this preference. Stated preferences have highest confidence; inferred preferences are scored based on frequency and recency of observed behavior.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was first created in the loyalty system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this preference becomes active and should be applied to member experiences.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this preference is no longer valid or should no longer be applied. Nullable for preferences with no expiration.']
  last_confirmed_date date [note: 'The date when the member explicitly confirmed or reaffirmed this preference. Distinct from last_updated_date which may include system-inferred updates.']
  last_observed_date date [note: 'The most recent date when this preference was observed in a stay or interaction. Used for recency scoring in confidence calculations.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'The date when this preference was last modified or confirmed by the member or system. Used to track recency for confidence scoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or additional context about this preference. May include special instructions, member comments, or operational guidance.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this preference has been observed in the member\'s stay history or interactions. Used to calculate confidence level for inferred preferences.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference can be overridden by operational constraints or property-level decisions. True if override is allowed; false if preference must be honored.']
  preference_category varchar [note: 'The category or domain of the preference (e.g., room type, pillow type, floor preference, amenity preferences, dietary restrictions, communication channel, language, newspaper, housekeeping schedule). Classifies the type of personalization being captured. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_type|pillow_type|floor_preference|amenity|dietary_restriction|communication_channel|language|newspaper|housekeeping_schedule — promote to reference product]']
  preference_source varchar [note: 'The origin or channel through which this preference was captured (e.g., stated at enrollment, updated via mobile app, updated via website, captured by call center, inferred from stay history, imported from PMS profile).']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this preference record. Active preferences are used for personalization; inactive or expired preferences are retained for historical analysis.']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific value or selection for this preference (e.g., \'King Bed\', \'Hypoallergenic Pillow\', \'High Floor\', \'Vegetarian\', \'Email\'). Free-text or structured value depending on category.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority or importance ranking of this preference relative to other preferences for the same member. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when preferences conflict.']
  property_restriction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this preference applies to all properties, a specific property, a brand, or a region. Supports property-specific personalization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system that originated or last updated this preference record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS, Mobile App).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this preference record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']

  Note: 'Master record of a loyalty member\'s stated and inferred preferences used to personalize the hospitality experience. Captures preference category (room type, pillow type, floor preference, amenity preferences, dietary restrictions, communication channel, language, newspaper, housekeeping schedule), preference source (stated at enrollment, updated via app, inferred from stay history), confidence level, and last updated date. Distinct from guest domain preferences as this is loyalty-program-managed personalization data.'
}

Table loyalty.program_config {
  program_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program configuration record. Primary key. This is the single source of truth configuration governing program-wide business rules and economics.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or administrator who last modified this configuration record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  api_integration_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external systems can integrate with this loyalty program via API. True = API access available for partners and properties; False = manual integration only.']
  breakage_assumption_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of earned points that will never be redeemed (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used for USALI-compliant revenue recognition and liability estimation. Based on historical redemption patterns.']
  cash_plus_points_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can combine cash and points for redemptions. True = hybrid payment allowed; False = points-only or cash-only redemptions.']
  ccpa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this program configuration meets CCPA requirements for California resident data. True = CCPA controls implemented; False = non-California program or pending compliance.']
  configuration_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this specific configuration version became effective. Used for time-based rule application and member communication of program changes.']
  configuration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this configuration version expires and is replaced by a new version. Null if currently active with no planned end date.']
  contact_center_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for member inquiries and loyalty program support. Used for member communication and published on program website.']
  contact_center_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for member services and loyalty program support. Used for member communication materials and website display.']
  conversion_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the points-to-currency conversion rate (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Defines the monetary unit for program economics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loyalty currency unit (e.g., \'Points\', \'Miles\', \'Credits\', \'Stars\'). This is the term displayed to members for their balance.']
  data_retention_policy_months varchar [note: 'Number of months member data is retained after account closure, per GDPR and CCPA requirements. After this period, PII is purged per right-to-erasure regulations.']
  family_pooling_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can pool points with family members into a shared account. True = pooling allowed; False = pooling disabled.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this program configuration meets GDPR requirements for EU member data. True = GDPR controls implemented; False = non-EU program or pending compliance.']
  gifting_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can gift points to other members. True = gifting allowed; False = gifting disabled. Subject to anti-fraud controls.']
  inactivity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of account inactivity (no earn or redeem transactions) before points expire, if points_expiry_policy is \'inactivity_based\'. Null otherwise.']
  liability_estimation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method for estimating loyalty program liability. incremental_cost = cost to fulfill; fair_value = market value of rewards; redemption_value = points * conversion rate. Must align with USALI and ASC 606.']
  lifetime_status_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program offers lifetime elite status after achieving cumulative thresholds. True = lifetime tiers available; False = annual re-qualification required.']
  maximum_earn_per_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of points a member can earn from a single stay, regardless of spend. Null if no cap applies. Used to manage program liability.']
  minimum_redemption_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of points required to initiate any redemption transaction. Prevents micro-redemptions and reduces operational costs.']
  mobile_app_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dedicated mobile application is available for this loyalty program. True = mobile app available; False = web-only access.']
  partner_transfer_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can transfer points to partner programs (airlines, car rentals, etc.). True = transfers allowed; False = transfers disabled.']
  points_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Rule governing when points expire. no_expiry = points never expire; inactivity_based = expire after N months of no activity; fixed_period = expire after N months from earn date; rolling_expiry = expire on a rolling window.']
  points_purchase_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can purchase additional points with cash. True = purchase allowed; False = earn-only program. Subject to regulatory limits in some jurisdictions.']
  points_to_currency_conversion_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Exchange rate for converting loyalty points to monetary value (e.g., 0.0100 means 100 points = 1 USD). Used for redemption valuation and breakage accounting per USALI standards.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this loyalty program in operational systems (e.g., \'HNRS\', \'ELITE01\'). Used for system integration and reporting.']
  program_launch_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty program was originally launched. Used for program anniversary communications and historical analysis.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official marketing name of the loyalty program (e.g., \'Honors Rewards\', \'Elite Guest Club\'). This is the customer-facing brand name.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the loyalty program. Active programs accept enrollments and transactions; suspended programs are temporarily paused; sunset programs are being phased out.']
  program_terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the legal terms and conditions document governing this program configuration. Links to the published T&C document for member consent and compliance.']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the program configuration (e.g., \'2.1\', \'3.0.1\'). Incremented when program rules or economics change materially.']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the loyalty program where members can view terms, check balances, and redeem rewards. Used for marketing and member self-service.']
  program_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when the program year begins if tier_year_definition is \'program_year\'. Null for calendar or rolling definitions. 1=January, 12=December.']
  rolling_expiry_window_months varchar [note: 'Number of months from earn date after which points expire, if points_expiry_policy is \'rolling_expiry\'. Null otherwise. Example: 24 means points earned on Jan 1, 2023 expire Jan 1, 2025.']
  status_match_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program offers status matching from competitor loyalty programs. True = status match campaigns allowed; False = earn-only tier qualification.']
  terms_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current program terms and conditions became effective. Used for member communication and regulatory compliance.']
  tier_rollover_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether qualifying nights/stays can roll over to the next qualification period if a member exceeds their tier threshold. True = rollover allowed; False = no rollover.']
  tier_year_definition varchar [note: 'Method for calculating the qualification period for tier status. Calendar = Jan 1 - Dec 31; rolling_12_month = trailing 12 months from any date; program_year = custom fiscal period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Reference master storing the global configuration parameters of the loyalty program. Captures program name, program version, currency name (points, miles, credits), points-to-currency conversion rate, tier year definition (calendar vs. rolling 12-month), minimum redemption threshold, maximum earn per stay, points expiry policy (inactivity period, rolling expiry window), program terms version, GDPR/CCPA data retention policy, breakage assumption rate, and active status. Single configuration record governing program-wide business rules and program economics assumptions.'
}

Table loyalty.member_segment {
  member_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty member segment definition. Primary key.']
  marketing_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Loyalty member segments often map to broader marketing guest segments for unified targeting across loyalty and marketing campaigns. Enables coordinated segmentation strategy and prevents conflicting a']
  primary_member_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created this segment definition. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  allow_overlap_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can belong to this segment simultaneously with other segments (true) or if this segment is mutually exclusive with others (false).']
  brand_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of brand codes (luxury, premium, select-service) that limit segment membership. Empty if no brand restriction applies.']
  campaign_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this segment are eligible for targeted marketing campaigns (true) or if this is an analytical-only segment (false).']
  confidence_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum confidence score (0.0000-1.0000) required for model-driven segment assignment. Null if not model-driven. Used to control precision vs. recall trade-off.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment definition record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  current_member_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of members currently assigned to this segment as of the last refresh or calculation run. Updated periodically based on refresh frequency.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition becomes active and available for use in campaigns and targeting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  engagement_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum engagement score (0-100 scale) required for segment membership. Null if engagement score is not a criterion. Used for behavioral segmentation.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining conditions that disqualify a member from this segment even if inclusion criteria are met (e.g., account_closure_date IS NOT NULL, communication_opt_in=false).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition is no longer valid or active. Null for segments with no planned end date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3) or region codes that limit segment membership. Empty if no geographic restriction applies.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed business rules and conditions that define member eligibility for this segment (e.g., tier=Platinum AND ytd_nights>=50 AND last_stay_date<=90 days). Stored as human-readable text or structured expression.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment definition record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when member assignments to this segment were last recalculated and updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  maximum_ltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum lifetime value threshold for segment membership. Null if no upper LTV bound applies. Used to define LTV bands or exclude ultra-high-value guests.']
  maximum_nights varchar [note: 'Maximum number of lifetime or qualifying-period nights for segment membership. Null if no upper nights bound applies.']
  minimum_ltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum lifetime value threshold required for segment membership. Null if LTV is not a criterion for this segment. Used for high-value guest targeting.']
  minimum_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum number of lifetime or qualifying-period nights required for segment membership. Null if nights are not a criterion.']
  minimum_stays varchar [note: 'Minimum number of lifetime or qualifying-period stays required for segment membership. Null if stays are not a criterion.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the predictive model used for this segment. Null if not model-driven. Enables model lineage and reproducibility.']
  next_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next recalculation of member assignments to this segment. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, business context, or operational notes about this segment definition. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  nps_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum NPS score required for segment membership. Null if NPS is not a criterion. Used to segment promoters, passives, and detractors.']
  personalization_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personalized offers and content should be generated for members in this segment (true) or if standard communications apply (false).']
  predictive_model_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the machine learning or predictive model used to assign members to this segment (e.g., Churn_Risk_Model_v2, Upsell_Propensity_RF). Null if not model-driven.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to resolve conflicts when a member qualifies for multiple segments. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for campaign targeting hierarchy.']
  property_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes that limit segment membership to guests of specific hotels or resorts. Empty if no property restriction applies.']
  recency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since last stay used as a criterion for segment membership (e.g., lapsed guests with no stay in last 90 days). Null if recency is not a criterion.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How often member assignments to this segment are recalculated and updated (e.g., real-time, daily batch, weekly, monthly, quarterly, on-demand, event-driven upon specific triggers). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: real_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|on_demand|event_driven — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  salt_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum SALT score required for segment membership. Null if SALT score is not a criterion. Used for satisfaction-based segmentation.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment for operational use and system integration (e.g., HVG_PLAT, LAPSED_90D, RFM_TOP10).']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the segment purpose, target audience characteristics, and intended use cases for marketing and retention campaigns.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the loyalty member segment used in marketing campaigns and reporting (e.g., High-Value Gold Members, Lapsed 90-Day Guests).']
  segment_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit, team, or individual responsible for managing this segment (e.g., Loyalty Marketing, CRM Operations, Regional Marketing EMEA).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition: active (in use), inactive (not currently used), draft (under development), archived (historical reference), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment by its business purpose: strategic (long-term program design), tactical (short-term campaigns), campaign-specific, operational (day-to-day targeting), analytical (reporting/insights), or regulatory (compliance-driven).']
  segmentation_basis varchar [note: 'The primary methodology or dimension used to define this segment (e.g., tier-based, LTV band, RFM analysis, geographic region, behavioral patterns, engagement score, spend level, recency, frequency, predictive model, custom criteria). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier|ltv_band|rfm|geographic|behavioral|engagement|spend|recency|frequency|predictive|custom — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_population_size varchar [note: 'Expected or target number of members that should fall into this segment based on historical data or business planning. Used for campaign sizing and resource allocation.']
  tier_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty tier codes that are eligible for this segment. Empty if no tier restriction applies. Used to limit segment membership by tier level.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining loyalty member segments used for targeted marketing, personalized offers, and retention campaigns. Captures segment name, segment code, segmentation basis (tier, LTV band, recency-frequency-monetary, geographic, behavioral), inclusion criteria, segment owner (marketing or loyalty operations), effective date, active status, and temporal member assignment records (member_id, assignment_date, assignment_method, confidence_score, effective/expiry dates). This product owns both segment definitions AND the versioned member-to-segment mappings, enabling campaign targeting with historical segment attribution. Distinct from guest domain segmentation — this is loyalty-program-specific segmentation for program economics and CRM targeting.'
}

Table loyalty.certificate {
  certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty certificate or voucher. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that voided the certificate. Null if not voided.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who owns or was issued this certificate.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner program if certificate was issued through or redeemable with a partner (e.g., airline miles certificate). Null if hotel-only certificate.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Certificates are often issued as part of loyalty promotions. Currently certificate has campaign_code (STRING) but no formal FK. Adding promotion_id creates a formal link to the promotion that triggere']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation where this certificate was redeemed. Null if not yet redeemed or if redeemed outside reservation context (e.g., F&B voucher used at Point of Sale).']
  spa_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Loyalty certificates (experience awards, complimentary treatment vouchers) are redeemed for specific spa appointments. Required for certificate validation at booking, redemption tracking, and certific']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that redemption must be booked. Null if no advance booking requirement.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges when certificate cannot be redeemed (e.g., peak holiday periods). Format: YYYY-MM-DD:YYYY-MM-DD. Null if no blackout dates apply.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique certificate or voucher number used for redemption and tracking. Alphanumeric code printed on certificate or provided digitally.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certificate: issued (active and available), reserved (held for pending booking), redeemed (used), expired (past validity date), voided (invalidated by system), or cancelled (invalidated by member or admin).']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Category of certificate or voucher issued. Defines the benefit type: free night award, room upgrade certificate, Food and Beverage (F&B) credit, spa voucher, airline miles certificate, or experience voucher.']
  combinable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate can be combined with other promotions, discounts, or certificates (true) or must be used standalone (false).']
  complimentary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate was issued complimentary (true) or required points redemption (false). Complimentary certificates are typically issued for service recovery, promotions, or elite status benefits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certificate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the face value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if certificate has no monetary value.']
  eligible_property_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes where certificate can be redeemed. Null if no specific property restriction or if restriction is defined by brand/region/tier.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certificate expires and can no longer be redeemed. Null indicates no expiration.']
  face_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the certificate in the specified currency. Represents the benefit amount (e.g., dollar value of F&B credit, estimated value of free night). Zero for non-monetary certificates.']
  issue_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the certificate was issued: web (loyalty website), mobile app, call center, property (front desk or guest services), email (automated fulfillment), partner portal, or admin console (manual issuance by staff). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|property|email|partner_portal|admin_console — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certificate was issued to the member. Marks the start of the certificate validity period.']
  issue_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for issuing the certificate: points redemption (member purchased with points), elite benefit (tier entitlement), promotion (marketing campaign), service recovery (guest complaint resolution), compensation (operational failure), partner transfer (from partner program), or campaign (targeted offer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: points_redemption|elite_benefit|promotion|service_recovery|compensation|partner_transfer|campaign — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the certificate was issued to the member, including time zone. Used for audit and operational tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certificate record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for redemption (Length of Stay - LOS restriction). Null if no maximum stay limit.']
  minimum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for redemption (Length of Stay - LOS restriction). Null if no minimum stay requirement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the certificate, including special handling instructions, member requests, or operational remarks.']
  points_cost varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points deducted from member account to issue this certificate. Zero if certificate was issued complimentary or as compensation.']
  property_restriction varchar [note: 'Scope of properties where certificate can be redeemed: all properties (no restriction), specific property (single property code), brand level (specific brand only), region level (geographic region), or tier level (property tier/category restriction).']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the certificate was redeemed. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  redemption_date date [note: 'Date the certificate was redeemed by the member. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the certificate was redeemed, including time zone. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this certificate record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS, loyalty platform).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this certificate in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full text or reference to the terms and conditions governing the certificate usage, restrictions, and redemption rules.']
  tier_at_issue varchar [note: 'Loyalty tier code of the member at the time the certificate was issued. Used for historical tracking and tier-specific benefit analysis.']
  tier_at_redemption varchar [note: 'Loyalty tier code of the member at the time the certificate was redeemed. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate can be transferred to another member (true) or is non-transferable (false).']
  voided_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for voiding the certificate (e.g., fraud detection, duplicate issuance, system error, member request). Null if not voided.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was voided by system or administrator. Null if not voided.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a loyalty certificate or voucher issued to a member as a reward, compensation, or promotional benefit. Captures certificate type (free night award, room upgrade certificate, F&B credit, spa voucher, airline miles certificate), face value, points cost or complimentary basis, issue date, expiry date, redemption status (issued, redeemed, expired, voided), associated reservation on redemption, and issuing channel. Distinct from the redemption transaction — this is the instrument itself.'
}

Table loyalty.fraud_alert {
  fraud_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fraud alert record. Primary key.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member account affected by the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner program if the fraud alert involves partner points transfer or partner-originated activity. Null if fraud is internal to hotel loyalty program.']
  points_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the points ledger transaction that reversed fraudulent points. Links to points_ledger for audit trail. Null if no reversal occurred.']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Loyalty fraud investigations that uncover data breaches (unauthorized account access, PII exposure, credential stuffing) must trigger privacy incident records for GDPR/CCPA notification obligations. R']
  related_fraud_alert_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fraud_alert (related_fraud_alert_id)']
  triggering_points_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.points_ledger. Business justification: fraud_alert currently has triggering_transaction_id (STRING) and triggering_transaction_type (STRING). When the fraud is detected on a points transaction, adding triggering_points_ledger_id FK creates']
  triggering_points_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.points_transfer. Business justification: When fraud is detected on a points transfer transaction, this FK links to the specific transfer record. Nullable and mutually exclusive with other transaction FKs. Partner transfers are a common fraud']
  triggering_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When fraud is detected on a redemption transaction, this FK links to the specific redemption record. Nullable and mutually exclusive with triggering_points_ledger_id. Enables direct navigation to the ']
  account_frozen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member account was frozen as a result of this fraud alert. True if account is currently frozen; false otherwise.']
  account_frozen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member account was frozen due to fraud alert. Null if account was not frozen.']
  alert_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the fraud alert, formatted as FA- followed by 10 digits.']
  alert_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the fraud alert based on potential financial impact, member risk, and program integrity threat. Critical indicates immediate action required with significant financial or reputational risk; high indicates substantial risk requiring urgent investigation; medium indicates moderate risk requiring timely review; low indicates minor risk for routine investigation.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fraud alert in the investigation workflow. Open indicates alert created and awaiting assignment; investigating indicates active investigation in progress; confirmed_fraud indicates fraud validated and resolution actions taken; false_positive indicates alert determined to be legitimate activity; closed indicates investigation completed and case archived.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity type. Account takeover indicates unauthorized access to member account; unauthorized redemption indicates points redeemed without member consent; points theft indicates fraudulent points accrual or transfer; manufactured spending indicates artificial transaction generation to earn points; velocity abuse indicates abnormal transaction frequency or volume; credential stuffing indicates automated login attempts using stolen credentials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud alert record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value involved in the fraud alert.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the fraudulent activity was detected. Rule-based indicates detection by predefined business rules; ML model indicates detection by machine learning algorithm; manual report indicates detection by fraud analyst review; partner notification indicates alert received from partner program; member report indicates member-initiated fraud report.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraudulent activity was first detected by the system or reported. Represents the business event time of fraud discovery.']
  false_positive_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the fraud alert was determined to be a false positive. Documents legitimate business activity that triggered the alert. Null if alert was not classified as false positive.']
  geolocation_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from source IP address geolocation. Used to identify suspicious cross-border activity.']
  investigation_assigned_to varchar [note: 'User identifier of the fraud analyst or investigator assigned to review and resolve the alert. Null if alert is unassigned.']
  investigation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the investigation process, findings, evidence reviewed, and analyst observations. Confidential operational information.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when investigation of the fraud alert was initiated by an analyst. Null if investigation has not yet started.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud alert record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or reference number provided by law enforcement agency if fraud was reported. Null if not reported to authorities.']
  law_enforcement_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether law enforcement authorities were notified of the confirmed fraud. True if notification occurred; false otherwise.']
  member_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify the member of the fraud alert. Email indicates electronic mail; SMS indicates text message; phone indicates voice call; mail indicates postal mail; in_app indicates mobile application notification.']
  member_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loyalty member was notified of the fraud alert. Null if member was not notified.']
  member_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loyalty member was notified of the fraud alert and investigation. True if member was contacted; false otherwise.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the fraud detection model or rule set that generated the alert. Enables tracking of model performance and alert quality over time.']
  monetary_value_involved decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the fraudulent activity in the program\'s base currency. Calculated based on points value, cash redemptions, or other financial impact.']
  points_amount_involved varchar [note: 'Total number of loyalty points involved in the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity. Null if fraud does not involve points movement.']
  points_reversed_amount varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points reversed or clawed back from the member account as part of fraud resolution. Null if no points reversal occurred.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when regulatory report was submitted to authorities. Null if no regulatory reporting was required or report not yet submitted.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fraud alert meets thresholds requiring regulatory reporting to authorities or industry bodies. True if reporting is required; false otherwise.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the fraud alert. Account freeze indicates temporary suspension of account activity; points reversal indicates fraudulent points removed from account; password reset indicates forced credential change; account closure indicates permanent account termination; no action indicates alert closed without intervention; member notification indicates member contacted regarding suspicious activity.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the resolution decision, actions taken, and final outcome. Confidential operational information.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud alert was resolved and final action taken. Null if alert remains open or under investigation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned by fraud detection system, typically ranging from 0.00 to 100.00, with higher scores indicating greater fraud likelihood. Generated by rule-based engine or ML model.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Business channel through which the triggering transaction or activity occurred. Web indicates website; mobile_app indicates mobile application; call_center indicates phone reservation or service; property indicates on-property transaction; partner indicates partner program; API indicates system-to-system integration.']
  source_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for the triggering transaction or activity. Desktop indicates web browser on computer; mobile indicates smartphone; tablet indicates tablet device; API indicates programmatic access; unknown indicates device type could not be determined.']
  source_ip_address varchar [note: 'Internet Protocol address associated with the triggering transaction or suspicious activity. Used for fraud pattern analysis and geolocation. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  triggering_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of loyalty program transaction or activity that triggered the fraud alert. Identifies the specific action that raised suspicion.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity on a loyalty member account. Captures alert type (account takeover, unauthorized redemption, points theft, manufactured spending, velocity abuse, credential stuffing), detection method (rule-based, ML model, manual report, partner notification), alert severity, member account affected, triggering transaction references, investigation status (open, investigating, confirmed_fraud, false_positive, closed), resolution action taken (account freeze, points reversal, password reset, account closure), investigating agent, and resolution date. Enables fraud operations team workflow and supports regulatory reporting on loyalty program abuse.'
}

Table loyalty.package_purchase {
  package_purchase_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this package purchase transaction. Primary key.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty member who purchased or redeemed the spa package']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa package catalog item that was purchased']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property where this package was purchased or is valid for use. May differ from member\'s home property.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation code provided to the member for this package purchase. Used for lookup and verification at spa reception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this package purchase record was created. Used for audit and operational tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the purchase price. Supports multi-currency operations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this package purchase expires and can no longer be used. Calculated based on package validity rules and purchase date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this package purchase record was last updated. Tracks status changes and session usage updates.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this package purchase. Tracks whether the entitlement is active, in use, exhausted, or no longer valid.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the member to purchase or partially pay for this package. Zero if purchased with cash only.']
  purchase_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member purchased this spa package. Used for channel attribution and member behavior analysis.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the member purchased or redeemed this spa package. Marks the beginning of the entitlement period.']
  purchase_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual price paid by the member for this package purchase. May differ from rack rate due to promotions, member discounts, or dynamic pricing.']
  sessions_included varchar [note: 'Total number of spa sessions or treatments included in this package purchase. Defines the entitlement quantity.']
  sessions_used varchar [note: 'Number of sessions or treatments from this package that the member has already consumed. Used to track remaining entitlement.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the transactional event of a loyalty member purchasing or redeeming a spa package. It captures purchase-specific data including purchase date, expiration, sessions tracking, redemption method, and pricing that exists only in the context of this specific purchase transaction. Each record links one member to one spa package purchase instance with temporal and financial attributes.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality loyalty programs, members purchase or redeem spa packages multiple times over their membership lifecycle, and each spa package catalog item is purchased by many different members. The business actively manages these purchase transactions as distinct operational records, tracking purchase-specific data such as purchase date, expiration, sessions remaining, redemption method (points vs. cash), and purchase price. This is not a reference relationship but a transactional event that creates an entitlement record.'
}

Table loyalty.promotion_distribution {
  promotion_distribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this promotion-channel distribution record. Primary key.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution channel carrying this promotion']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion being distributed through this channel']
  actual_bookings varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of bookings generated by this promotion through this channel. Updated as bookings are attributed to the promotion-channel combination.']
  budget_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the total promotion budget allocated specifically to this distribution channel. Sum of all channel allocations should not exceed promotion.budget_cap_amount. Identified in detection phase.']
  budget_consumed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of the channel-specific budget consumed by bonuses awarded through this channel. Tracks against budget_allocation_amount.']
  channel_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank controlling the display priority of this promotion within this channel when multiple promotions are active. Lower numbers = higher priority. Identified in detection phase.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion-channel distribution record was created in the system.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this promotion distribution in this channel. Values: scheduled (configured but not yet live), active (currently available), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended normally), cancelled (terminated early).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this promotion becomes available for booking through this specific channel. May differ from promotion.start_date to support phased rollout strategies. Identified in detection phase.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this promotion is no longer available through this channel. May differ from promotion.end_date to support channel-specific campaign windows. Identified in detection phase.']
  performance_target_bookings varchar [note: 'Target number of qualifying bookings expected from this promotion through this channel. Used for channel performance tracking and ROI analysis. Identified in detection phase.']
  promotion_rate_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Channel-specific adjustment multiplier applied to the base promotion bonus for this distribution channel. Allows different bonus rates by channel (e.g., 2.0x for direct web, 1.5x for OTA). Identified in detection phase.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the marketing team member who configured this promotion for distribution through this channel.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the distribution contract between loyalty promotions and distribution channels. It captures channel-specific promotion configuration, budget allocation, performance targets, and rate adjustments that exist only when a specific promotion is made available through a specific channel. Each record links one promotion to one distribution channel with attributes governing how that promotion is presented, priced, and tracked in that channel.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality operations, loyalty promotions are strategically distributed across multiple channels with channel-specific configurations. A single promotion (e.g., \'Double Points Summer\') is simultaneously available through direct web, mobile app, OTA partners, and GDS with different rate multipliers, budget allocations, and display priorities per channel. Conversely, each distribution channel carries multiple concurrent promotions targeting different segments and travel periods. Marketing teams actively manage these promotion-channel distributions as operational records with performance tracking, budget monitoring, and channel-specific start/end dates.'
}

Table loyalty.promotion_treatment_rule {
  promotion_treatment_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this promotion-treatment rule. Primary key.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion offering the bonus or discount']
  treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa treatment eligible under this promotion']
  bonus_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base loyalty points earned when this treatment is purchased under this promotion. Example: 2.0 means double points. This is promotion-treatment specific and may differ from the promotion\'s global multiplier.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion-treatment rule was created in the system.']
  current_redemptions varchar [note: 'Running count of how many times this promotion-treatment combination has been redeemed to date. Updated at point-of-sale.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the treatment price when purchased under this promotion. Example: 15.00 means 15% off. This is specific to the promotion-treatment combination.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this specific promotion-treatment rule expires. May differ from the promotion\'s overall end date if treatments are removed early.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this specific promotion-treatment rule becomes active. May differ from the promotion\'s overall start date if treatments are added mid-campaign.']
  maximum_redemptions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this promotion can be applied to this treatment across all members. Used to cap exposure on high-value treatments within a broader promotion.']
  minimum_spend decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum spend amount required on this specific treatment to qualify for the promotion bonus or discount. Treatment-specific threshold within the promotion.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this promotion-treatment rule. Values: active (currently in effect), paused (temporarily disabled), expired (end date passed), removed (manually deactivated).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the loyalty marketing team member who configured this promotion-treatment rule.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between promotion and treatment. It captures the specific terms under which a loyalty promotion applies to a spa treatment, including bonus multipliers, discounts, spend thresholds, and redemption limits. Each record links one promotion to one treatment with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship. This enables the business to configure promotion-specific treatment rules that are neither promotion-global nor treatment-intrinsic.. Existence Justification: In the travel hospitality loyalty business, promotions are frequently designed to incentivize specific service categories, including spa treatments. A single promotion (e.g., \'Summer Wellness Bonus\') applies to multiple spa treatments (massage, facial, body wrap), and a single treatment can be included in multiple concurrent or overlapping promotions (e.g., \'Double Points on Spa\', \'Birthday Month 20% Off\', \'Elite Member Spa Discount\'). The business actively manages promotion-treatment rules with treatment-specific terms such as bonus multipliers, discount percentages, spend thresholds, and redemption caps that are neither global to the promotion nor intrinsic to the treatment.'
}

Table channel.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for channel']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distribution channels incur operating costs (connectivity fees, commissions, marketing) that must be allocated to cost centers for USALI-compliant departmental P&L reporting and budget variance analys']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Channel managers are employees who own distribution channel relationships, manage OTA partnerships, and handle channel performance. Standard hospitality practice tracks which employee owns each channe']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Many distribution channels are operated by OTA partners (e.g., Expedia operates multiple channels/brands). This FK establishes the ownership relationship. Removes redundant platform_name which can be ']
  primary_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Distribution channels often have primary marketing campaigns driving bookings. This FK enables attribution analysis and campaign performance tracking by channel. Essential for marketing ROI measuremen']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this distribution channel was first activated and began accepting live bookings. Distinct from contract_start_date which reflects the commercial agreement date. Used for channel onboarding tracking and performance benchmarking from go-live.']
  bar_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is eligible to display and sell the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate at the time of booking. True = BAR rates are distributed to this channel; False = channel receives only specific contracted or negotiated rates. Drives rate distribution strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  booking_fee_usd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Per-transaction booking fee charged by the channel in addition to or instead of a commission percentage, expressed in USD. Common in GDS channels (e.g., GDS segment fee per booking). Used in cost-of-acquisition and channel profitability analysis.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel (e.g., \'24H_FREE\', \'48H_PENALTY\', \'NON_REFUNDABLE\'). Cancellation policies may differ by channel based on contractual agreements. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the channel by booking relationship: DIRECT = guest books directly with the property (web, mobile, voice, corporate direct); INDIRECT = third-party intermediary involved (OTA, GDS, wholesaler, metasearch); OPAQUE = rate and property identity concealed until after booking (e.g., Hotwire, Priceline opaque). Drives cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel mix strategy.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the distribution channel across enterprise systems including Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and IDeaS G3 RMS. Used for channel mapping and cross-system reconciliation.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the distribution channel, including its market positioning, target guest segment, special contractual terms, or operational notes. Used for channel management documentation and onboarding reference.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the distribution channel (e.g., \'Expedia Partner Central\', \'Booking.com Extranet\', \'Sabre GDS\', \'Direct Web\', \'Voice Reservations\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the distribution channel. active = channel is live and accepting bookings; inactive = channel is configured but not currently selling; suspended = channel temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity violation, connectivity issue); pending_activation = channel onboarded but not yet live; decommissioned = channel permanently retired.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the distribution channel by its market segment and technology interface. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo); DIRECT_WEB = brand.com web booking; DIRECT_MOBILE = brand mobile app; VOICE = call center reservations; CORPORATE_DIRECT = negotiated corporate accounts; WHOLESALER = tour operators and bed banks; METASEARCH = Google Hotel Ads, Trivago. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|DIRECT_WEB|DIRECT_MOBILE|VOICE|CORPORATE_DIRECT|WHOLESALER|METASEARCH — promote to reference product]']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'The commercial model under which commission is calculated for this channel. NET_RATE = channel marks up a net rate provided by the property; GROSS_RATE = commission deducted from the gross rate charged to the guest; MARKUP = channel applies a markup over the property\'s net rate; FLAT_FEE = fixed fee per booking regardless of rate. Critical for accurate revenue and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage charged by this distribution channel on each booking, expressed as a percentage of the room revenue (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Drives cost-of-acquisition calculations and net revenue reporting per USALI standards. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  connectivity_fee_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual or periodic connectivity/subscription fee charged by the channel or switch provider for maintaining the technical integration, expressed in USD. Included in total cost-of-acquisition analysis alongside commission. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  connectivity_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect the property management system or CRS to this distribution channel. API = RESTful or SOAP API integration; XML = XML-based messaging (e.g., OTA XML); SFTP = file-based batch transfer; DIRECT_CONNECT = proprietary direct connection; SWITCH = via a channel manager switch (e.g., SiteMinder, Channex); MANUAL = manually managed extranet updates. Impacts SLA and rate update latency.']
  content_distribution_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities, policies) is actively distributed to this channel via a content API or content management system. True = content is pushed/synced to this channel; False = content is managed manually or not distributed. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal management and channel lifecycle planning. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission audit trails, and regulatory compliance. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution channel record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.']
  crs_channel_code varchar [note: 'Channel identifier as configured in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the CRS source of record. Enables traceability of bookings back to the originating CRS channel configuration.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this distribution channel was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active channels. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting, and audit trails. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  gdpr_data_processor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel acts as a GDPR data processor for guest personal data collected during the booking process. True = a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with this channel; False = channel does not process personal data on behalf of the property. Required for GDPR compliance and data governance.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter GDS chain code assigned to the hotel brand or property group within Global Distribution Systems (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Required for GDS-type channels to route availability and rate requests correctly. Null for non-GDS channels.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of this distribution channel. GLOBAL = channel operates worldwide; REGIONAL = channel serves a specific geographic region (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe); DOMESTIC = channel serves a single country; LOCAL = channel serves a specific city or metro area. Used for channel mix strategy and market segmentation analysis.']
  inventory_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which room inventory is allocated to this distribution channel. POOLED = channel draws from the shared central inventory pool (real-time); ALLOTMENT = a fixed block of rooms is pre-allocated to the channel; FREE_SALE = channel can sell without prior allocation up to a defined limit; ON_REQUEST = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel has Last Room Availability (LRA) rights — meaning the property must make the last available room accessible through this channel at the contracted rate. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. True = LRA applies; False = LRA does not apply.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution channel record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  loyalty_bookings_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and redemption. True = loyalty points are awarded and redeemable; False = bookings through this channel do not qualify for loyalty benefits (common for opaque OTA channels). Managed in Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.']
  mice_bookings_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel supports group and MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) bookings in addition to transient reservations. True = MICE/group bookings can be sourced through this channel; False = transient bookings only. Relevant for event sales and Delphi by Amadeus integration.']
  nrr_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) products are distributed through this channel. True = NRR fences are available on this channel; False = only flexible/refundable rates are distributed. Impacts revenue management yield strategy and cancellation policy enforcement.']
  payment_model varchar [note: 'Defines who collects payment from the guest for bookings made through this channel. HOTEL_COLLECT = property collects payment directly at check-in/check-out; CHANNEL_COLLECT = the OTA or intermediary collects payment and remits net to the property; SPLIT = partial payment collected by each party. Impacts accounts receivable and revenue recognition per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.']
  pci_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel is certified as compliant with PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) for handling cardholder data. True = channel holds current PCI DSS certification; False = channel is not PCI compliant or certification has lapsed. Required for risk management and payment security governance.']
  primary_market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market served by this distribution channel (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Used for geographic channel mix analysis, market penetration reporting, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU-based channels).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the strategic priority of this channel in the distribution mix, where lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority, typically direct web). Used by revenue management to determine inventory release sequencing and rate strategy. Configured in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  rate_parity_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel contractually requires rate parity — i.e., the property must offer the same or lower rates on this channel as on any other channel. True = rate parity clause is active in the channel agreement; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management compliance and rate strategy.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation for this channel. BROAD = property must match rates across all channels including direct; NARROW = property must match rates on other OTAs but may offer lower rates on direct channels (common post-EU regulatory changes); NONE = no rate parity obligation. Drives revenue management and direct booking strategy.']
  remittance_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the channel remits collected payments to the property for CHANNEL_COLLECT payment model bookings. Applicable only when payment_model is CHANNEL_COLLECT or SPLIT. Drives accounts receivable forecasting and cash flow management in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable response time in seconds for availability and rate queries from this channel, as defined in the channel\'s Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used to monitor channel connectivity performance and enforce SLA compliance. Typical values: 3 seconds for OTA/GDS, 5 seconds for XML channels.']
  sla_uptime_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed uptime percentage for this channel\'s connectivity, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.90 for 99.9% uptime). Used to monitor channel reliability and enforce SLA penalties. Managed against actual uptime metrics in channel performance reporting.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes supported by this distribution channel for booking and payment (e.g., \'USD,EUR,GBP,JPY\'). Relevant for multi-currency rate distribution and foreign exchange management. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  supported_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 two-letter language codes supported by this channel for content display and guest communication (e.g., \'en,fr,de,zh,ja\'). Relevant for content localization strategy and international market reach.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all distribution channels through which Travel Hospitality properties sell inventory, including OTA platforms (Expedia, Booking.com), GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo), direct web/mobile, voice/call center, corporate direct, wholesalers, and metasearch engines. Captures channel type, connectivity method, commission tier, rate parity obligations, and channel-level SLA parameters. This is the SSOT for channel identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table channel.ota_partner {
  ota_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the OTA partner record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the ota_partner master data product.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: OTA account managers are employees managing partner relationships, negotiating contracts, and resolving disputes. Essential for accountability in partnership management, performance tracking, and esca']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: OTA partnerships are brand-specific in multi-brand hospitality companies; Marriott Bonvoy partners differ from Ritz-Carlton partners. Brand-level partnership management, commission structures, and con']
  third_party_due_diligence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_due_diligence. Business justification: OTA partners are third-party processors of guest PII and payment data, requiring due diligence screening for GDPR compliance, sanctions screening, cybersecurity assessment, and financial stability. Re']
  affiliate_network boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner operates an affiliate or sub-distribution network through which third-party websites or agents can book on behalf of the OTA. True = affiliate network active; False = direct OTA platform only. Relevant for booking source attribution accuracy.']
  base_commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage charged by the OTA partner on each confirmed booking, expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Foundational input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel profitability analysis.']
