  cancellation_policy_type varchar [note: 'Default cancellation policy type applied to bookings made through this OTA channel. Flexible = free cancellation up to 24-48 hours; Moderate = partial penalty; Strict = high penalty; Non_Refundable = NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) — no refund on cancellation. Impacts revenue management and guest experience.']
  channel_manager_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the channel manager or switch platform used to manage the technical connectivity to this OTA partner (e.g., Sabre SynXis, Siteminder, Amadeus iHotelier, RateGain). Relevant when connectivity is not direct API-to-API.']
  commission_model varchar [note: 'Commercial model governing how the OTA partner earns revenue. Net Rate = hotel sells at net, OTA marks up; Merchant = OTA collects full payment and remits net; Agency = OTA collects commission on hotel-collected payment; Retail = standard published rate with commission; Opaque = rate hidden from consumer until post-booking (e.g., Hotwire).']
  connectivity_fee_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the OTA partner or channel manager. Annual = yearly flat fee; Monthly = recurring monthly; Per_Booking = transactional fee per confirmed booking; One_Time = setup/onboarding fee only.']
  connectivity_fee_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the OTA partner or channel manager for maintaining the technical integration (API/XML/GDS switch). Expressed in USD. Used for total cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation and channel profitability reporting per USALI.']
  connectivity_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for two-way inventory, rate, and availability (IRA) connectivity between the hotel\'s CRS/PMS and the OTA partner. XML = legacy XML push/pull; REST_API = modern RESTful API; GDS = Global Distribution System switch; SFTP = file-based batch; OTA_XML = OpenTravel Alliance XML standard; HTNG = HTNG web services.']
  content_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the completeness and quality of the hotel\'s content (photos, descriptions, amenities) on the OTA partner\'s platform. Higher scores correlate with improved search ranking and conversion rates. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner becomes legally binding and operational. Governs the start of commission obligations and rate parity clauses.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended contract. Used for contract renewal management and risk monitoring.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number of the master distribution agreement or contract executed with the OTA partner. Links to the contract management repository in SAP S/4HANA for financial and legal audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA partner record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_sharing_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the OTA partner governing the exchange of guest booking data, anonymised analytics, and performance reporting. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance when guest PII is transmitted.']
  gdpr_data_processor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner is formally designated as a GDPR Data Processor under Article 28, processing guest personal data on behalf of the hotel as Data Controller. True = Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed; False = not designated as processor.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter chain code assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) to identify the hotel brand/chain within GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Applicable only when connectivity_protocol is GDS. Null for non-GDS OTA partners.']
  hq_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the OTA partner\'s legal headquarters is registered. Used for tax treaty application, withholding tax determination, and regulatory jurisdiction identification.']
  inventory_allocation_model varchar [note: 'Model governing how room inventory is made available to the OTA partner. Free_Sell = real-time availability from CRS with no pre-allocated block; Allotment = pre-contracted room block allocated to the OTA; On_Request = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) clause, requiring the hotel to make its last available room accessible to this channel. LRA clauses impact revenue management inventory controls and are tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the OTA partner organisation as it appears on the master distribution agreement (e.g., Expedia, Inc.; Booking Holdings B.V.). Used for contract management, AP reconciliation, and regulatory filings.']
  market_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the primary geographic markets or regions where the OTA partner has significant consumer reach and booking volume (e.g., \'North America, Western Europe\'; \'Asia-Pacific\'; \'Global\'). Used for channel mix optimisation and market-specific distribution strategy.']
  mobile_app_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner operates a dedicated mobile application channel through which bookings are made. True = mobile app channel is active and tracked separately for attribution; False = desktop/web only. Supports channel mix and mobile booking trend analysis.']
  ota_account_manager_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager. Used for commercial communications, promotional campaign coordination, and escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  ota_account_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager or market manager assigned to the hotel account. Primary point of contact for commercial negotiations, promotional campaigns, and issue resolution.']
  ota_account_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager. Used for urgent escalations and commercial negotiations. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the OTA partner within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Used for channel mapping, rate distribution, and booking source attribution in Sabre SynXis and Oracle OPERA.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTA partner relationship. Active = live and distributing inventory; Pending = contract under negotiation or connectivity setup in progress; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity breach); Terminated = contract ended.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution partner by channel category. OTA = Online Travel Agency (Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (Sabre, Amadeus); METASEARCH = price comparison engine (Google Hotel Ads, Trivago); DIRECT = brand.com or call centre; WHOLESALE = tour operator/bed bank; AFFILIATE = sub-affiliate network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|METASEARCH|DIRECT|WHOLESALE|AFFILIATE — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  payment_collection_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party collects payment from the guest. Hotel = property collects at check-in/check-out (agency model); OTA = OTA collects full payment and remits net to hotel (merchant model); Split = partial payment collected by each party.']
  preferred_commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated commission rate applicable when the property holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with the OTA. Typically lower than the base rate in exchange for enhanced visibility or placement. Null if preferred status is not applicable.']
  preferred_partner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hotel or brand holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with this OTA, typically in exchange for a higher commission rate or marketing investment. Preferred partners receive enhanced search ranking and visibility on the OTA platform.']
  primary_market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the OTA partner\'s primary consumer market or country of dominant booking volume (e.g., USA, GBR, SGP). Used for geographic channel attribution and market penetration analysis.']
  rate_parity_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a rate parity clause requiring the hotel to offer rates no lower on other channels than those offered on this OTA. True = rate parity clause is active; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management and legal compliance monitoring.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation. Broad = hotel cannot offer lower rates on ANY channel including its own website; Narrow = hotel cannot offer lower rates on OTHER OTAs but may offer lower rates on its own direct channels; None = no parity obligation. Relevant for EU regulatory compliance following rate parity investigations.']
  remittance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the guest check-out date within which the OTA partner remits commission payments or net booking proceeds to the hotel. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., 30 = net-30 terms).']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of guest reviews published on the OTA partner\'s platform for the hotel. Used alongside review_score to assess statistical significance and platform engagement. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.']
  review_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average guest review score for the hotel as published on the OTA partner\'s platform (e.g., Booking.com score out of 10, Expedia score out of 5). Used for reputation management, competitive benchmarking, and NPS/GSS correlation analysis via Medallia.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT, GST, or equivalent tax registration number of the OTA partner\'s legal entity. Required for tax invoice processing, withholding tax compliance, and AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing brand name under which the OTA partner operates (e.g., Hotels.com, Agoda). May differ from the legal entity name when the OTA operates multiple consumer brands under one legal umbrella.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA partner record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master data for Online Travel Agency partners including Expedia, Booking.com, Agoda, Hotels.com, Airbnb, and regional OTAs. Captures partner legal entity, contract reference, commission rate tiers, connectivity protocol (XML/API/GDS), rate parity clause status, preferred partner designation, market coverage, and account management contacts. Distinct from the channel record — an OTA partner may operate multiple channels (desktop, mobile app, affiliate).'
}

Table channel.gds_connection {
  gds_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each GDS connection configuration record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master configuration entity.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: GDS connections are associated with specific booking source codes for attribution when reservations come through GDS channels. Many GDS connections per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution channel record associated with this GDS connection, enabling channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GDS connectivity fees (segment fees, monthly charges) are departmental operating expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate distribution cost tracking and budget management per USALI stand']
  gds_account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GDS account managers are employees managing GDS connectivity, troubleshooting technical issues, and optimizing GDS performance. Critical for operational accountability, issue escalation, and performan']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this GDS connection is configured. Links the GDS connectivity record to the specific hotel or resort property.']
  third_party_due_diligence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_due_diligence. Business justification: GDS providers are third-party data processors handling guest PII and payment data, requiring due diligence for data security, sanctions compliance, and financial stability. Real business process: tech']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this GDS connection was first activated and made live for bookings. Used for contract term tracking, connectivity health history, and channel distribution reporting.']
  availability_sync_method varchar [note: 'Method by which room availability is synchronized between the property management system and the GDS. Real-time provides instant updates; batch updates on a scheduled interval; on_request queries availability at booking time; seamless provides live two-way availability.']
  chain_code varchar [note: 'Two- to four-character hotel chain code registered with the GDS network, identifying the hotel brand or management company within the GDS. Assigned by the GDS provider and used for inventory and rate loading.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission payable to travel agents or GDS intermediaries on bookings made through this GDS connection. Used for commission accrual, accounts payable processing, and channel cost analysis per USALI standards.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the GDS connection. Active indicates live booking capability; suspended indicates temporary halt; pending_activation indicates setup in progress; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.']
  connectivity_fee_monthly_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly fee charged by the GDS provider or switch vendor for maintaining the technical connectivity of this GDS connection. Distinct from per-segment fees; used for OpEx budgeting and channel cost allocation.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Technical connectivity model used for this GDS connection. Direct connect provides real-time two-way communication; switch connect routes through a switch provider; seamless provides full availability and rate transparency; standard uses batch updates.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the GDS connectivity contract. Null for evergreen contracts. Used for renewal alerts, contract renegotiation planning, and channel distribution continuity management.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the GDS connectivity contract governing this connection. Used for contract lifecycle management, renewal planning, and financial obligation tracking.']
  corporate_rate_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether negotiated corporate rates are loaded and accessible through this GDS connection. Corporate rates are a primary revenue stream for GDS channels and require specific rate plan configuration in the CRS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.']
  crs_interface_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the CRS-to-GDS interface configuration within Sabre SynXis CRS. Links the GDS connection to the specific CRS interface instance managing rate distribution and availability synchronization.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are loaded and displayed to travel agents in this GDS connection. Ensures rate accuracy and currency consistency for international GDS distribution.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this GDS connection was or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for currently active connections. Used for contract lifecycle management and channel distribution planning.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking that determines the property\'s display order in GDS search results for travel agents. Lower values indicate higher priority. Influenced by GDS tier, participation level, and performance metrics.']
  gds_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the GDS provider (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan, Travelport). Used for reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.']
  gds_network_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard two-character GDS network identifier assigned by IATA/ARC. 1S=Sabre, 1A=Amadeus, 1G=Galileo/Travelport, 1P=Worldspan. Used for routing and segment fee attribution.']
  gds_tier varchar [note: 'Participation tier level for this GDS connection, reflecting the level of GDS merchandising, display priority, and content features contracted. Preferred tier provides enhanced display and content capabilities; standard is base participation; basic is minimal listing.']
  health_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent automated health check for this GDS connection. Healthy indicates full two-way connectivity; degraded indicates partial functionality; failed indicates connectivity loss; unknown indicates health check has not been performed.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated connectivity health check performed for this GDS connection. Used for SLA monitoring, connectivity uptime reporting, and proactive issue detection.']
  lra_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this GDS connection, requiring the property to make the last available room accessible to GDS bookers. LRA is a contractual obligation with some GDS providers and corporate accounts.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this GDS connection in the PMS (e.g., TRAN for transient, CORP for corporate, LEIS for leisure). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation and STR STAR Report benchmarking.']
  max_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance that reservations can be booked through this GDS connection. Controls the booking window loaded to the GDS and aligns with the Revenue Management System (RMS) inventory control horizon.']
  min_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that reservations must be booked through this GDS connection. Supports Last Room Availability (LRA) and advance purchase rate controls managed by the Revenue Management System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes regarding this GDS connection, including special configuration requirements, known issues, contractual exceptions, or migration history. Used by channel distribution and revenue management teams.']
  nrr_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) can be loaded and booked through this GDS connection. NRR support is a key revenue management capability enabling advance purchase rate strategies via GDS.']
  property_code_gds varchar [note: 'The unique property identifier as registered within the specific GDS network. May differ from the internal PMS property code. Used for inventory display and booking routing within the GDS.']
  pseudo_city_code varchar [note: 'GDS-assigned Pseudo City Code (PCC) that identifies the travel agency or booking office within the GDS network. Used for commission tracking, segment attribution, and agency relationship management.']
  rate_loading_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical protocol or standard used to load and update rate plans into the GDS. HTNG and OTA are XML-based standards; DHISCO is a switch-based protocol; manual indicates rates are loaded via GDS terminal; API indicates direct programmatic loading.']
  rate_parity_enforced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity is contractually enforced for this GDS connection, requiring that rates loaded to the GDS match the Best Available Rate (BAR) offered on direct channels. Critical for OTA and GDS contract compliance monitoring.']
  rate_plan_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct rate plans currently loaded and active for this GDS connection. Used to monitor rate plan coverage, identify gaps in GDS rate distribution, and support rate parity audits.']
  rate_update_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which rate updates are pushed from the Revenue Management System (RMS) or CRS to this GDS connection. Real-time supports dynamic pricing; scheduled frequencies reflect batch update configurations.']
  segment_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the GDS segment fee. Enables multi-currency cost-of-acquisition reporting for properties operating in non-USD markets.']
  segment_fee_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Per-booking segment fee charged by the GDS provider in US dollars for each reservation booked through this GDS connection. A GDS segment is one hotel booking transaction. Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the GDS provider\'s technical support team for this connection. Used for connectivity issue escalation, health check alerts, and SLA breach notifications.']
  switch_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party switch provider (e.g., DHISCO, TravelClick, Pegasus) used to route availability and rate data between the CRS and the GDS, when connectivity_type is switch_connect. Null for direct connect configurations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance in the silver layer lakehouse.']
  uptime_sla_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually agreed minimum uptime percentage for this GDS connection as specified in the GDS connectivity SLA. Used for vendor performance monitoring and contract compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for each Global Distribution System connection (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Captures GDS network identifier, property chain code, pseudo city code (PCC), connectivity status, rate loading protocol, availability sync method, segment fee structure, and GDS-specific rate plan mappings. Supports GDS segment fee tracking and connectivity health monitoring.'
}

Table channel.crs_channel_mapping {
  crs_channel_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CRS-to-channel mapping configuration record in the Sabre SynXis CRS. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a configuration resource defining how a property\'s rates, room types, and inventory are translated and published to a specific distribution channel.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define which booking source codes are used for reservations from each channel. Many mappings per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source_code which can be retrieved via F']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this mapping applies. Identifies the connected channel partner in the channel master.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define how rate plans map between Sabre SynXis CRS and distribution channels. The crs_channel_mapping table has crs_rate_plan_code and channel_rate_plan_code (STRING). Adding chan']
  gds_connection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: CRS channel mappings can be specific to GDS connections when the channel is a GDS. Adding nullable gds_connection_id FK allows linking CRS mappings to specific GDS connection configurations (Sabre, Am']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: CRS channel mappings must link channel room type codes to physical inventory room types for rate loading, availability synchronization, and booking fulfillment. Essential for channel manager operation']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property for which this CRS channel mapping is configured. Links the mapping to the specific property in the property master.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel mapping (e.g., \'FLEX24H\' for 24-hour free cancellation, \'NRR\' for non-refundable). Determines the terms communicated to guests at the point of booking on the channel.']
  channel_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the commercial contract governing the distribution relationship with this channel (e.g., OTA master agreement number, GDS connectivity contract ID). Used for contract management, commission dispute resolution, and audit compliance.']
  channel_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier code as recognized by the distribution channel (e.g., Booking.com hotel ID, Expedia property ID, GDS chain/property code). Used to match the CRS property to the channel\'s own property record during rate and availability pushes.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the distribution channel type. Determines the connectivity protocol, commission structure, and rate parity rules applicable to this mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|mobile|wholesaler|corporate|metasearch — promote to reference product]']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission percentage charged by the distribution channel on bookings made through this mapping (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, channel profitability reporting, and USALI-compliant distribution cost accounting. Classified as confidential due to contractual sensitivity.']
  connectivity_fee_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed or per-booking connectivity fee charged by the channel or GDS switch for maintaining the distribution connection, expressed in USD. Used in total cost-of-acquisition calculations and channel ROI analysis per USALI standards.']
  connectivity_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical messaging protocol used for rate and availability distribution between Sabre SynXis CRS and the channel (e.g., OTA XML, HTNG, OpenTravel Alliance ARI). Determines the message format and integration method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA_XML|HTNG|OpenTravel|ARI|TravelPort|Amadeus|direct_API — promote to reference product]']
  content_sync_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities) is synchronized to the channel through this mapping in addition to rates and availability. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels that require content distribution alongside rate/inventory feeds.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with GDPR data processing records requirements.']
  crs_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier code as registered in Sabre SynXis CRS. This is the CRS-side property reference used in all rate and availability distribution messages to channels. Distinct from the internal property_id.']
  crs_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The rate plan code as defined in Sabre SynXis CRS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate rate codes). This is the source-side rate plan identifier that is translated and mapped to the channel-specific rate plan code for distribution.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are distributed to this channel (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency of rate publication and commission calculation for this channel mapping.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this CRS channel mapping configuration becomes active and rates/availability begin distributing to the channel. Used to schedule future mapping activations and track the start of channel distribution periods.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this CRS channel mapping configuration expires and distribution to the channel ceases. Null indicates the mapping is open-ended with no scheduled end date. Used for contract-aligned channel distribution management.']
  guarantee_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payment guarantee policy required for bookings through this channel mapping (e.g., \'CC_REQUIRED\' for credit card guarantee, \'DEPOSIT\' for advance deposit). Ensures PCI DSS-compliant payment handling requirements are communicated to the channel.']
  inventory_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Defines how room inventory is allocated to this channel. \'pooled\' means the channel draws from the shared inventory pool; \'allotment\' means a fixed block is reserved for the channel; \'free_sell\' means unlimited sell without allocation; \'on_request\' means availability must be confirmed manually.']
  is_mobile_optimized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured to distribute mobile-specific rates or content optimized for mobile booking platforms. Supports mobile channel performance analytics and targeted mobile rate strategy.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful rate and availability synchronization push from Sabre SynXis CRS to the distribution channel for this mapping. Used to monitor channel connectivity health, detect stale distributions, and support SLA compliance reporting.']
  lra_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured with Last Room Availability (LRA), meaning the channel has access to the last available room in the property\'s inventory. LRA is typically granted to GDS corporate accounts and preferred OTA partners under contractual obligation.']
  mapping_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this CRS-to-channel mapping configuration within Sabre SynXis. Used as the business identifier for cross-system reference and audit. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  mapping_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for this CRS channel mapping configuration (e.g., \'Booking.com Standard Rate Mapping - NYC Midtown\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of this CRS channel mapping. \'active\' means the mapping is live and distributing rates/availability; \'suspended\' means temporarily halted; \'testing\' means in QA validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  mapping_type varchar [note: 'Classification of what this mapping configuration governs — whether it maps rate plans, room types, inventory controls, packages, or promotional offers to the channel. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'The market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this channel mapping (e.g., \'LEISURE\', \'CORPORATE\', \'WHOLESALE\', \'OTA\'). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation, STR benchmarking, and RevPAR analysis by channel.']
  max_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance that a booking can be made through this channel mapping (booking window). Controls how far into the future availability is published to the channel. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for a booking through this channel mapping. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction. Used to control long-stay bookings on specific channels as part of yield optimization strategy.']
  min_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that a booking must be made through this channel mapping. Used to enforce advance purchase restrictions (e.g., NRR non-refundable rates requiring 7-day advance booking) as distributed via the CRS.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for a booking to be accepted through this channel mapping. A value of 1 means no minimum stay restriction. Used to enforce LOS (Length of Stay) controls as part of revenue management strategy distributed via the CRS.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate type distributed through this channel mapping. Indicates whether the channel receives Best Available Rate (BAR), Last Room Availability (LRA) rates, Non-Refundable Rates (NRR), corporate negotiated rates, packages, promotional rates, or wholesale rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]']
  rate_offset_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether a rate adjustment (offset) is applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. \'none\' means the CRS rate is published as-is; \'flat_amount\' means a fixed currency amount is added or subtracted; \'percentage\' means a percentage markup or markdown is applied.']
  rate_offset_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The numeric value of the rate offset applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. Positive values indicate a markup; negative values indicate a markdown. Interpreted as a currency amount or percentage depending on rate_offset_type. Null when rate_offset_type is \'none\'.']
  rate_parity_enforced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity rules are enforced for this channel mapping, requiring that the rates distributed to this channel match the Best Available Rate (BAR) published on other channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and rate integrity monitoring.']
  sell_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms that can be sold through this channel per night under the current allocation configuration. A null or zero value indicates no sell limit is applied (free sell). Used to control channel-level inventory exposure and prevent overbooking.']
  sync_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Configured frequency in minutes at which rate and availability updates are pushed from the CRS to this channel. Lower values indicate near-real-time distribution (e.g., 5 minutes for OTA partners); higher values indicate batch distribution schedules.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Critical for detecting configuration drift in channel distribution settings.']

  Note: 'Mapping configuration between Sabre SynXis CRS and each distribution channel, defining how rate plans, room types, and inventory controls are translated and published per channel. Captures CRS property code, channel-specific rate plan codes, room type mapping, inventory allocation rules, sell limits, and last-room-availability (LRA) flags. Critical for ensuring accurate rate and availability distribution across all connected channels.'
}

Table channel.channel_rate_plan {
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each channel-specific rate plan configuration record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional rate plans operationalize marketing offers; a "Summer Sale" campaign offer manifests as specific rate plans loaded into distribution channels. Linking rate plans to offers enables offer pe']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Each channel-specific rate plan configuration belongs to one distribution channel. Many rate plans per channel. Removes redundant channel_code, channel_name, and channel_type which can be retrieved vi']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property for which this channel rate plan configuration applies. Enables property-level rate plan and channel management across a multi-property portfolio.']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance of arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on this channel. For example, a value of 7 means the guest must book at least 7 days before check-in. Supports advance purchase rate strategies and demand management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the revenue management or distribution team member who approved this channel rate plan configuration for publication. Supports governance, approval workflow tracking, and audit compliance for rate distribution decisions.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The base room rate amount (in property currency) from which the channel-specific rate is derived. For BAR-derived plans, this is the current BAR value. For flat rates, this is the fixed rate. Expressed per room per night. Supports rate parity monitoring and channel pricing strategy analysis.']
  booking_window_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the closing of the booking window. Nullable if the rate plan remains bookable indefinitely until the stay date.']
  booking_window_start_date date [note: 'The earliest calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the opening of the booking window for this rate plan on this channel. Used for promotional and advance purchase rate plan management.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy linked to this channel rate plan. Examples: FLEX24H (free cancellation up to 24 hours), NRR-NOCANCEL (non-refundable, no cancellation), SEMI-FLEX48H (partial refund if cancelled 48+ hours prior). Critical for NRR and advance purchase rate plans.']
  channel_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The final channel-specific rate amount (in property currency) published to this distribution channel after applying the rate derivation method and adjustment. This is the rate visible to consumers on the channel. Per room per night. Core metric for rate parity compliance monitoring.']
  channel_rate_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel rate plan configuration. Active = currently published and bookable on the channel; Inactive = deactivated and not available; Suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., rate parity violation); Pending = awaiting activation or approval; Expired = past effective end date; Draft = configured but not yet submitted for distribution.']
  close_out_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before arrival after which this rate plan is closed out and no longer bookable on this channel. Complements advance_purchase_days to define the booking window. A value of 0 means the rate is bookable up to the day of arrival.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The commission percentage payable to the distribution channel (e.g., OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings made under this rate plan. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Critical for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.']
  connectivity_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount (in property currency) charged by the channel or connectivity provider per booking or per period for distributing this rate plan on the channel. Distinct from commission (which is percentage-based). Supports total cost of acquisition calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  crs_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The rate plan identifier as assigned in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRS. Enables traceability of rate plan configurations back to the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rate amounts on this channel rate plan. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, AED. Supports multi-currency channel distribution and rate parity analysis across international markets.']
  days_of_week_applicable varchar [note: 'A 7-character binary string indicating which days of the week (Mon-Sun) this rate plan is applicable on the channel. Each position represents a day: position 1=Monday through position 7=Sunday. \'1\' = applicable, \'0\' = not applicable. Example: \'1111100\' = Monday through Friday only. Supports weekday/weekend rate differentiation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this channel rate plan configuration becomes active and the rate plan is published to the channel. Defines the start of the rate loading schedule for this channel-rate plan combination.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this channel rate plan configuration expires and the rate plan is no longer published to the channel. Nullable for open-ended configurations. Supports rate loading schedule management and seasonal rate plan publishing.']
  is_package_rate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel rate plan is a package rate that bundles room accommodation with additional inclusions (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, F&B voucher). True = package rate with inclusions; False = room-only rate. Supports package revenue attribution and channel mix analysis.']
  is_rate_parity_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity rules apply to this channel rate plan configuration. True = this rate plan is subject to rate parity monitoring and must match rates across applicable channels; False = exempt from parity (e.g., opaque rates, member-only rates, negotiated corporate rates). Supports rate parity compliance management.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings made under this channel rate plan are eligible for a refund upon cancellation. True = refundable (flexible rate); False = non-refundable (NRR). Key attribute for consumer transparency and rate plan classification.']
  last_loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate plan was most recently successfully loaded or updated on the distribution channel. Supports rate loading schedule management, troubleshooting, and audit of channel rate distribution operations.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under this rate plan on this channel. Nullable if no maximum stay restriction applies. Used in conjunction with min_los to define the eligible stay window for this channel rate plan.']
  meal_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this channel rate plan. RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board (breakfast + dinner); FB = Full Board (all meals); AI = All Inclusive; EP = European Plan (no meals); CP = Continental Plan (continental breakfast). Relevant for package rate plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RO|BB|HB|FB|AI|EP|CP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this rate plan on this channel. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions are a key Revenue Management System (RMS) tool for yield optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this channel rate plan configuration. Examples: rate loading exceptions, channel-specific negotiation notes, promotional campaign references, or revenue strategy rationale.']
  pms_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The rate plan code as configured in Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for reconciliation between CRS channel distribution and PMS rate plan setup. Ensures rate plan consistency between the CRS and PMS for accurate reservation processing.']
  rate_adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the channel-specific pricing adjustment is a markup (rate higher than base, e.g., to cover OTA commission) or a markdown (rate lower than base, e.g., promotional discount). NONE indicates no adjustment is applied and the base rate is published as-is.']
  rate_adjustment_unit varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the rate_adjustment_value is expressed as a PERCENTAGE of the base rate or as a fixed AMOUNT in the property currency. Determines how the channel-specific rate is computed from the base rate and adjustment value.']
  rate_adjustment_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric value of the rate adjustment applied to derive the channel-specific rate from the base rate. Interpretation depends on rate_adjustment_unit: if PERCENTAGE, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%); if AMOUNT, this is the absolute currency amount. Used in conjunction with rate_adjustment_type to compute the final channel rate.']
  rate_derivation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate the channel-specific rate from the base rate. FLAT = fixed absolute rate amount; PERCENTAGE = percentage markup or markdown applied to a base rate; BAR_DERIVED = derived as a percentage or offset from the Best Available Rate (BAR); YIELD_MANAGED = dynamically set by the Revenue Management System (RMS); NEGOTIATED_FIXED = fixed contracted rate for corporate or wholesale agreements.']
  rate_loading_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the rate plan loading process to the channel. Loaded = successfully transmitted and active on the channel; Pending Load = queued for transmission to the channel; Load Failed = transmission error requiring remediation; Not Loaded = not yet submitted; Deactivated = previously loaded but now removed from the channel.']
  rate_parity_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the rate parity group to which this channel rate plan belongs. Rate plans within the same parity group are monitored for price consistency across channels. Enables grouping of comparable rate plans for parity compliance reporting.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as configured in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Examples: BAR, NRR, LRA, CORP001, PKG-BB. This is the business identifier used across Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate loading and distribution.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the rate plan as displayed in the CRS and channel interfaces. Examples: \'Best Available Rate\', \'Non-Refundable Rate\', \'Last Room Availability Corporate\', \'Bed and Breakfast Package\'.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR = Best Available Rate (dynamic public rate); NRR = Non-Refundable Rate (discounted, no cancellation); LRA = Last Room Availability (guaranteed availability for corporate); CORPORATE = negotiated corporate rate; PACKAGE = bundled rate with inclusions; PROMOTIONAL = limited-time promotional rate; WHOLESALE = net rate for tour operators; NEGOTIATED = contracted rate; OPAQUE = channel-opaque pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|NRR|LRA|CORPORATE|PACKAGE|PROMOTIONAL|WHOLESALE|NEGOTIATED|OPAQUE — promote to reference product]']
  room_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of room type codes to which this channel rate plan applies. If null or \'ALL\', the rate plan applies to all room types at the property. Examples: \'STD,DLX,STE\' or \'ALL\'. Supports room-type-specific channel pricing strategies.']
  stay_date_end date [note: 'The latest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings must have an arrival date on or before this date. Nullable for open-ended rate plans. Supports seasonal and promotional rate plan management.']
  stay_date_start date [note: 'The earliest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings made under this rate plan must have an arrival date on or after this date. Supports seasonal rate plan publishing and blackout date management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Channel-specific rate plan configurations defining which rate plans (BAR, NRR, LRA, corporate, package) are published to which channels, with channel-specific pricing adjustments, markup/markdown rules, and rate loading schedules. Captures rate plan code, channel applicability, rate derivation method (flat, percentage, derived from BAR), advance purchase restrictions, and cancellation policy linkage. Supports rate parity compliance and channel-specific pricing strategy.'
}

Table channel.booking_source {
  booking_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each booking source attribution record in the channel distribution master. Primary key for the booking_source reference master. [ROLE: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity is a reference master / code list for booking source attribution codes; per canonical-attrs-enforced rule, REFERENCE_LOOKUP role is exempt from per-role minimums. _canonical_skip_reason: booking_source is a reference master / code table classifying reservation origin at sub-channel granularity; it does not represent a party, agreement, resource, transaction, or event log.]']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Booking source is a granular attribution code that rolls up to a parent distribution channel. Many booking sources belong to one channel. Removes redundant parent_channel_code and parent_channel_name ']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Booking sources may be associated with specific OTA partners for attribution and commission tracking. Many booking sources can belong to one OTA partner. Removes redundant ota_partner_code which can b']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this booking source was first activated and made live for reservation acceptance in the CRS and PMS. Used for channel performance benchmarking (measuring ramp-up period) and historical channel mix analysis.']
  api_connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Technical connectivity method used to exchange availability, rates, and inventory (ARI) data between the hotel\'s CRS/PMS and this booking source. Determines integration complexity, latency, and update frequency for rate and inventory distribution.']
  attribution_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the booking source attribution hierarchy used for revenue reporting roll-ups. Level 1 = top-level channel (e.g., OTA), Level 2 = sub-channel (e.g., OTA Desktop), Level 3 = specific source (e.g., Expedia Desktop US). Enables hierarchical channel mix analysis from property to brand level.']
  booking_source_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the booking source record. \'active\' = currently receiving reservations; \'inactive\' = temporarily disabled; \'pending\' = configured but not yet live; \'suspended\' = paused due to contract or compliance issue; \'retired\' = permanently decommissioned. Used for channel activation management in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  booking_window_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS. Defines the furthest point in advance that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for long-range demand forecasting and group block management.']
  booking_window_min_days varchar [note: 'Minimum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS and RMS. Defines the earliest point before arrival that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for demand forecasting and inventory control in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to reservations booked through this source, as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Determines refundability rules (e.g., Non-Refundable Rate (NRR), flexible cancellation). Impacts revenue recognition and overbooking strategy.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the distribution channel type indicating whether the booking originates from a direct brand channel, an indirect intermediary (OTA/GDS/Wholesale), a voice channel (call center/reservation agent), or a group/MICE channel. Used for strategic channel mix reporting and direct booking ratio KPI tracking.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the basis on which commission is calculated for this booking source: net_rate (commission on net room rate), gross_rate (commission on gross rate including taxes), flat_fee (fixed amount per booking), or per_segment (GDS segment fee per booking). Drives commission calculation logic in accounts payable processing.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage payable to the booking source intermediary (OTA, travel agency, GDS) per confirmed reservation. Expressed as a percentage of the net room revenue. Used for cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation, channel profitability analysis, and accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA. Null for direct/zero-commission channels.']
  connectivity_fee_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee charged per booking or per GDS segment for this booking source, expressed in the property\'s base currency (USD). Applicable when connectivity_fee_applicable is True. Used alongside commission_rate to compute total cost of acquisition (COA) per channel.']
  connectivity_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a GDS or channel connectivity fee is charged per booking for this source, in addition to or instead of a commission. True for GDS sources with per-segment fees (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for total cost-of-acquisition calculation and channel profitability reporting.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Null for open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal alerts, commission rate expiry management, and channel deactivation planning.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission rate validity tracking, and channel activation scheduling in SAP S/4HANA contract management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this booking source record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and reference data change management. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  crs_source_code varchar [note: 'Source code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for this booking source. This is the operational identifier used during reservation creation and rate distribution. May differ from the internal source_code when CRS uses a legacy or partner-assigned code.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which commissions and connectivity fees for this booking source are settled. Used for multi-currency commission processing in SAP S/4HANA accounts payable and foreign exchange reconciliation.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this booking source was deactivated or retired from the CRS and PMS. Null for currently active sources. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting continuity, and audit trail maintenance.']
  gds_code varchar [note: 'Standard GDS identifier code for the booking source when the channel is a Global Distribution System participant. Applicable values include Amadeus (1A), Sabre (1S), Travelport Galileo (1G), Travelport Worldspan (1P). Null for non-GDS sources. Used for GDS connectivity reconciliation and GDS segment fee billing.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic reach of this booking source: global (worldwide distribution), regional (specific world region), domestic (single country), or property_specific (single property only). Used for channel distribution strategy and regional channel mix analysis.']
  iata_code varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned numeric agency code for travel agency or OTA booking sources participating in IATA-governed distribution. Used for agency commission processing, GDS segment attribution, and travel agency performance reporting. Null for non-agency sources.']
  inventory_update_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which room inventory and rate availability are pushed or synchronized to this booking source channel. Real-time channels receive instant updates via API; others receive batch updates. Impacts overbooking risk management and rate parity compliance.']
  is_direct_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking source is a direct brand channel (True) or an indirect intermediary channel (False). Direct channels include brand website, brand app, call center, and walk-in. Used for direct booking ratio KPI tracking and cost-of-acquisition benchmarking against indirect channels.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking source has Last Room Availability (LRA) contractual rights, meaning the channel must be offered inventory even when only the last room is available. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. Impacts inventory control rules in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  marketing_attribution_model varchar [note: 'Defines the marketing attribution model applied to bookings from this source for campaign effectiveness measurement and marketing ROI (Return on Investment) reporting. Used by Salesforce CRM and marketing analytics to attribute revenue to marketing campaigns. \'none\' for channels not subject to marketing attribution (e.g., GDS corporate).']
  payment_model varchar [note: 'Defines the payment collection model for this booking source: merchant (OTA collects payment from guest and remits net to hotel), agency (hotel collects and pays commission to channel), net_rate (channel marks up a net rate), or direct_bill (corporate direct billing). Impacts revenue recognition, accounts receivable, and cash flow management.']
  pms_source_code varchar [note: 'Source code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS for this booking source. Used for front desk reservation attribution, daily operations reporting (DOR), and property-level channel mix analysis. May differ from CRS source code due to property-level configuration.']
  preferred_partner_tier varchar [note: 'OTA or channel partner tier classification indicating the level of preferred partnership status. Preferred partners typically receive enhanced placement, lower commission rates, or co-marketing benefits. Used for OTA partnership management and channel strategy prioritization.']
  rate_parity_monitored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking source is subject to rate parity monitoring as part of the OTA contract or channel distribution agreement. When True, the Revenue Management System (RMS) and channel manager enforce rate parity rules to prevent undercutting on this channel. Critical for OTA contract compliance.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the rate parity obligation for this booking source: full_parity (rates must match all channels), narrow_parity (rates must match other OTAs but may differ from direct), or no_parity (no rate parity obligation). Drives rate distribution rules in Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying booking source records in PMS, CRS, and reporting tool dropdown lists and channel mix reports. Lower values appear first. Used for UI configuration in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS administration screens.']
  source_category varchar [note: 'Standardized sub-channel category classifying the booking source by interface type and traveler segment. Drives cost-of-acquisition attribution, commission tier assignment, and channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota_desktop|ota_mobile|gds_corporate|direct_web|direct_app|voice|group|wholesale — promote to reference product]']
  source_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the booking source within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). This is the operational key used in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS for source attribution on reservations. Examples: \'EXPEDIA_WEB\', \'GDS_AMADEUS\', \'DIRECT_APP\'.']
  source_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the booking source, including its operational context, target traveler segment, and how it integrates with the distribution ecosystem. Used for onboarding documentation and channel strategy reference.']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the booking source as used in reporting dashboards, revenue management analysis, and channel mix reports. Examples: \'Expedia Desktop\', \'Amadeus GDS Corporate\', \'Brand Direct App\'.']
  supports_best_available_rate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking source is eligible to receive and display the Best Available Rate (BAR) as distributed through the CRS. Channels with full rate parity obligations typically receive BAR. Used in rate distribution configuration in Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  supports_loyalty_accrual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reservations booked through this source are eligible for loyalty program points accrual. Some OTA and wholesale channels contractually exclude loyalty point earning. Used by Salesforce CRM loyalty management module to determine points posting eligibility at check-in.']
  traveler_segment varchar [note: 'Primary traveler segment targeted by this booking source. Classifies whether the channel primarily serves leisure travelers, corporate travelers, group bookings, MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions), wholesale/tour operators, or Free Independent Travelers (FIT). Used for demand segmentation and revenue mix analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this booking source record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']

  Note: 'Reference master for booking source attribution codes classifying the origin of each reservation at sub-channel granularity. Captures source code, source name, parent channel association, source category (OTA desktop, OTA mobile, GDS corporate, direct web, direct app, voice, group, wholesale), and attribution hierarchy for revenue reporting. SSOT for booking source classification used across reservation and revenue domains for channel mix analysis, cost-of-acquisition attribution, and marketing campaign effectiveness measurement.'
}

Table channel.channel_inventory_allocation {
  channel_inventory_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory allocation record in the channel distribution system. Primary key for the inventory_allocation data product.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan associated with this inventory allocation. Determines which pricing rules apply to the allocated inventory on this channel.']
  primary_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this inventory allocation applies. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration.']
  primary_channel_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe double, suite) for which inventory is being allocated to the channel.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which inventory is being allocated. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'The type of inventory control action that generated this record. CREATE indicates a new allocation was established; UPDATE modifies an existing allocation; CLOSE applies a stop-sell or close-out; REOPEN lifts a restriction; RELEASE returns unsold inventory to the pool; OVERRIDE applies a manual revenue management override.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this channel inventory allocation. Active allocations are enforced by the channel management system.']
  allocated_units varchar [note: 'The total number of room nights (units) assigned to this channel for the specified stay date range and room type. Represents the allotment block size or sell limit granted to the channel.']
  allocation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) to uniquely identify this inventory allocation action. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inventory allocation record. ACTIVE indicates the allocation is live and controlling channel availability; INACTIVE means it has been superseded; EXPIRED means the stay date range has passed; CANCELLED means it was manually revoked; PENDING means it is awaiting activation.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Method by which inventory is allocated to this channel for this room type. POOLED = shared inventory pool, ALLOCATED = fixed allocation, FREESALE = unlimited access, BLOCKED = no access.']
  allotment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory allotment method applied to this channel. FREE_SELL allows the channel to sell without a fixed block; ALLOCATION assigns a fixed block of rooms; STOP_SELL halts sales on the channel; CLOSE_OUT removes all availability; ON_REQUEST requires manual confirmation before booking is confirmed.']
  applied_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system user who applied this inventory control action. Used for audit trail, accountability, and change management reporting.']
  available_units varchar [note: 'The number of room nights currently available for sale on this channel, calculated as allocated_units minus consumed_units. Represents the real-time sellable inventory for the channel on the specified stay dates.']
  cancellation_count varchar [note: 'The number of bookings cancelled on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Used to track net pickup and assess channel-level cancellation patterns for demand forecasting.']
  channel_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total room type inventory allocated to this channel. Used for allocation-based inventory management strategies.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The category of distribution channel for this allocation. OTA (Online Travel Agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), DIRECT_WEB (brand.com), VOICE (call center), GSO (Global Sales Office), WHOLESALE (tour operators/wholesalers), METASEARCH (Google, Trivago, etc.). Supports channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|gso|wholesale|metasearch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.15 for 15%) payable to the distribution channel for bookings made under this allocation. Used for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.']
  connectivity_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fixed or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the GDS or CRS for distributing inventory through this channel. Expressed in the property\'s local currency. Used for total cost of distribution analysis.']
  consumed_units varchar [note: 'The number of allocated room nights that have been booked or consumed by the channel as of the last system update. Used to calculate remaining availability and monitor channel pickup performance.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial fields (connectivity_fee) in this allocation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.']
  days_out varchar [note: 'The number of days between the allocation action date and the stay_date_from (booking window). Used in revenue management analysis to understand how far in advance inventory controls are being applied relative to the arrival date.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration expires. Null for currently active allocations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory allocation action became effective and was applied to the channel. Represents the business event timestamp when the allocation, stop-sell, or restriction was activated in the CRS.']
  is_closed_to_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the first date of the stay period for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check in on the stay_date_from date, preventing new bookings that start on that date.']
  is_closed_to_departure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are blocked on the stay_date_to date for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check out on that date, preventing bookings that end on that date.']
  is_rms_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory allocation action was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) as opposed to being manually entered by a revenue manager. Supports analysis of automation adoption and RMS recommendation acceptance rates.']
  is_stop_sell boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a stop-sell control has been applied to this channel for the specified stay dates and room type. When TRUE, the channel is prevented from accepting new bookings regardless of remaining available units.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was last updated.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is contractually required for this channel. When TRUE, the channel must be offered the last available room at the property, typically a contractual obligation with certain OTA or corporate accounts.']
  lift_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory allocation action was lifted, reversed, or superseded. Null if the allocation is still active. Used to track the full lifecycle of each inventory control action.']
  lra_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room-type-channel combination, overriding property-level LRA settings.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'The maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MAX_LOS. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction is applied.']
  maximum_los_override varchar [note: 'Channel-specific maximum length of stay restriction for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'The minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MIN_LOS. A value of 1 means no effective minimum restriction.']
  minimum_los_override varchar [note: 'Channel-specific minimum length of stay requirement for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.']
  overbooking_allowance varchar [note: 'The number of additional room nights above the physical inventory that this channel is permitted to sell as part of the property\'s overbooking strategy. A value of 0 means no overbooking is permitted on this channel.']
  overbooking_limit_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum overbooking percentage allowed for this room type on this channel. Used for revenue management optimization.']
  pickup_count varchar [note: 'The number of new bookings received on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Supports pickup reporting and demand forecasting in the Revenue Management System (RMS).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this channel when allocating inventory for this room type. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rate_parity_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity rules are actively enforced for this room type on this channel.']
  rate_parity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity compliance is enforced for this channel allocation. When TRUE, the allocated inventory must be offered at rates consistent with other channels per rate parity agreements. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the inventory control action (e.g., HIGH_DEMAND, GROUP_BLOCK, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, MAINTENANCE). Used for revenue management analysis and audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIGH_DEMAND|GROUP_BLOCK|OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION|RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION|CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE|MAINTENANCE|FORECAST_ADJUSTMENT|COMPETITIVE_RESPONSE — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the inventory control action beyond the standardized reason_code. Captures specific business justification entered by the revenue manager or system.']
  release_date date [note: 'The date on which any unsold allocated inventory is released back to the general property inventory pool. After this date, the channel no longer holds exclusive rights to the allocated units, enabling the property to sell them through other channels.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'The type of booking restriction applied to this inventory allocation. MIN_LOS enforces a minimum Length of Stay (LOS); MAX_LOS enforces a maximum LOS; CLOSED_TO_ARRIVAL (CTA) prevents new arrivals on the specified date; CLOSED_TO_DEPARTURE (CTD) prevents departures; FULL_PATTERN_LENGTH requires exact stay duration; NONE means no restriction is applied.']
  sell_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of room nights the channel is permitted to sell regardless of total property availability. Acts as a cap to prevent overselling on a specific channel and supports channel mix optimization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this inventory allocation action. Identifies whether the action was initiated by Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, or entered manually by a user.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'The first date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the start of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'The last date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the end of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory allocation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the allocation for audit trail and change management purposes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version counter for this inventory allocation record. Incremented each time the allocation is modified, enabling optimistic concurrency control and historical version tracking in the Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of inventory (room nights) allocated to specific channels per property, room type, and date range, including all inventory control actions (allocations, stop-sells, close-outs, and restrictions). Captures allocation quantity, sell limit, allotment type (free-sell, allocation, stop-sell), release date, consumed vs. available units, restriction type (minimum LOS, closed to arrival, closed to departure), reason code, applied-by user, effective and lift timestamps. Supports channel-level inventory controls, allotment management, revenue management restriction actions, and prevents overselling across distribution channels.'
}

Table channel.rate_parity_audit {
  rate_parity_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate parity audit record captured during channel monitoring. Primary key for this transactional audit entity.']
  channel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA or distribution channel contract under which parity obligations are defined. Links to the channel contract master record for commission rates, parity clauses, and dispute resolution terms.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, etc.) on which the audited rate was observed. Links to the channel master record.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) under which the audited rate was published. Links to the rate plan master in the Revenue Management domain.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which the rate parity audit was conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) for which the rate was observed during the parity audit.']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'The number of days in advance of the stay date that the rate requires booking (advance purchase restriction). Used to ensure parity comparisons account for advance purchase rate conditions that may legitimately differ across channels.']
  audit_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally referenceable business identifier for this audit record, used in OTA contract compliance disputes and parity violation correspondence. Format: RPA-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXXXXXX.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate parity audit record. \'open\' indicates a new observation; \'violation_confirmed\' indicates a confirmed parity breach; \'dispute_raised\' indicates a formal dispute filed with the OTA/channel; \'resolved\' indicates the channel corrected the rate; \'closed\' indicates no action required; \'waived\' indicates the violation was contractually waived.']
