Critical for ensuring inventory control actions are successfully propagated.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the restriction was successfully transmitted to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. Null if transmission is pending or failed. Supports SLA monitoring for inventory control propagation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this restriction record, such as a status change, date range extension, or reason code update.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of stop-sell, close-out, and restriction controls applied to specific channels, room types, and date ranges. Captures channel, property, room type, stay date range, restriction type (stop-sell, minimum LOS, closed to arrival, closed to departure), reason code, applied-by user, effective timestamp, and lift timestamp. Supports revenue management inventory control actions executed through the CRS and channel manager to optimize channel mix and protect direct booking rates.'
}

Table channel.metasearch_listing {
  metasearch_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each metasearch listing record in the silver layer. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed digital listing asset on a metasearch platform.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Metasearch listings (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor, Trivago) drive traffic that converts to bookings. Each metasearch platform should have an associated booking_source code for attribution tracking. T']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Metasearch advertising (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor) is campaign-driven; each listing/bid strategy belongs to a specific marketing campaign for budget tracking, performance attribution, and ROI meas']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel record representing the metasearch platform (e.g., Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor, Trivago, Kayak, Skyscanner) through which this listing is managed.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Metasearch advertising spend (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor) is a marketing expense requiring cost center allocation for budget tracking, ROI analysis, and departmental P&L reporting.']
  last_bid_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Metasearch bid updates by employees require tracking for operational accountability and performance analysis. Essential for understanding who manages metasearch campaigns and optimizing bid strategies']
  platform_account_id varchar [note: 'The account or hotel ID assigned by the metasearch platform (e.g., Google Hotel Center property ID, TripAdvisor hotel ID) to identify this property on the platform. Used for API authentication, platform-side reporting reconciliation, and connectivity management.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property associated with this metasearch listing. Links the listing to the property master record for performance attribution and bid management.']
  bid_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The monetary bid amount configured for this listing under the active bid strategy. For CPC, represents the maximum cost-per-click in the listing currency. For CPA, represents the target cost-per-acquisition. For commission-based, this field is null (see commission_rate_pct). Serves as the principal quantitative fact (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  bid_multiplier decimal(5,4) [note: 'A multiplier applied to the base bid amount for specific market or device segments (e.g., 1.20 = 20% bid increase for mobile). Enables granular bid adjustment strategies without changing the base bid. Used in conjunction with bid_amount for effective bid calculation.']
  bid_strategy_type varchar [note: 'The bidding model applied to this metasearch listing. CPC (Cost Per Click) charges per user click; CPA (Cost Per Acquisition) charges per completed booking; commission-based charges a percentage of booking revenue; target_ROAS optimizes for return on ad spend; manual allows direct bid amount control. Drives cost-of-acquisition modeling and channel investment decisions.']
  booking_count varchar [note: 'Total number of direct bookings attributed to this metasearch listing within the reporting period. Used to calculate conversion rate, cost-per-acquisition (CPA), and metasearch contribution to direct booking revenue. Attribution follows the platform\'s last-click or assisted-conversion model.']
  booking_revenue decimal(16,2) [note: 'Total gross booking revenue (in listing currency) attributed to direct bookings generated by this metasearch listing within the reporting period. Used to calculate return on ad spend (ROAS), channel contribution to revenue, and cost-of-acquisition metrics. Aligns with USALI revenue reporting.']
  click_count varchar [note: 'Total number of user clicks on this property listing that directed traffic to the hotel\'s direct booking engine or landing page within the reporting period. Used to calculate click-through rate (CTR) and cost-per-click (CPC) actuals.']
  click_through_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of clicks to impressions for this listing within the reporting period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 = 3.50% CTR). Sourced directly from platform reporting APIs to ensure consistency with platform-side optimization signals. Used to assess listing creative and bid competitiveness.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission percentage charged by the metasearch platform per completed booking when the bid strategy type is commission-based. Expressed as a percentage of gross booking value (e.g., 12.00 = 12%). Null for CPC and CPA bid strategies. Critical for cost-of-acquisition and channel contribution analysis.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'The technical integration method used to feed rate and availability data to this metasearch listing. \'direct_api\' = direct platform API integration; \'gds_feed\' = rates sourced via GDS (Global Distribution System); \'crs_feed\' = rates sourced via Sabre SynXis CRS; \'third_party_aggregator\' = via a connectivity partner. Impacts data freshness and rate accuracy.']
  conversion_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of bookings to clicks for this listing within the reporting period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50% conversion rate). Sourced from platform reporting. Measures the effectiveness of the direct booking engine in converting metasearch traffic. Key input for bid optimization and landing page performance analysis.']
  cpa_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual average cost per acquisition (CPA) realized for this listing within the reporting period, in listing currency. Calculated as total_spend divided by booking_count. Stored as a raw field sourced from platform reporting. Null for non-CPA bid strategies. Key metric for channel cost-of-acquisition benchmarking.']
  cpc_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual average cost per click (CPC) realized for this listing within the reporting period, in listing currency. Calculated as total_spend divided by click_count. Stored as a raw field sourced from platform reporting to avoid recalculation discrepancies. Null for non-CPC bid strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this metasearch listing record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and tracking the onboarding of new metasearch platform listings.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this listing record (bid_amount, daily_budget_cap, monthly_budget_cap, cpc_actual, cpa_actual). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.']
  daily_budget_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum daily spend limit configured for this metasearch listing in the listing currency. Controls total metasearch investment exposure per day. Null if no daily cap is set. Used for budget pacing and channel investment governance.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The device category for which this bid configuration applies. Metasearch platforms support device-level bid adjustments. \'all\' indicates a unified bid across all devices. Enables device-level performance analysis and bid optimization for direct booking conversion.']
  direct_booking_url varchar [note: 'The landing page URL to which users are directed when clicking on this metasearch listing. Points to the hotel\'s direct booking engine (typically Sabre SynXis CRS-powered). Used for link validation, UTM parameter tracking, and ensuring correct booking attribution.']
  display_rank varchar [note: 'The average position at which this property listing is displayed on the metasearch platform results page (e.g., rank 1 = top position). Lower rank numbers indicate higher visibility. Used to assess bid competitiveness and correlate rank with click-through and conversion performance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this listing configuration (bid strategy, bid amount, budget caps, device targeting) became active on the metasearch platform. Supports historical bid strategy analysis and change management auditing. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  effective_to date [note: 'The date on which this listing configuration ceases to be active. Null for currently active configurations. Enables slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking of bid strategy and configuration changes over time. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  impression_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this property listing was displayed to users on the metasearch platform within the reporting period. Foundational metric for calculating click-through rate (CTR) and measuring listing visibility. Sourced from platform reporting APIs.']
  is_direct_booking_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing is configured to drive direct bookings through the hotel\'s own booking engine (True) versus redirecting to an OTA or third-party booking path (False). Key flag for measuring direct booking channel contribution and loyalty program integration.']
  is_rate_parity_monitored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated rate parity monitoring is enabled for this metasearch listing. When True, the system actively checks for rate disparities between this listing and the direct booking channel. Supports compliance with AHLA rate parity guidelines and OTA contract obligations.']
  last_bid_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the bid amount, bid strategy, or budget configuration for this listing was last modified. Enables audit trail of bid management changes and supports analysis of bid adjustment impact on performance metrics. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  listing_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier assigned to this property listing on the metasearch platform. Used for reconciliation with platform-side reporting APIs and bid management systems. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the metasearch listing. Controls whether the property is actively bidding and displaying on the platform. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. \'active\' = live and bidding; \'paused\' = temporarily stopped; \'suspended\' = platform-enforced stop; \'pending_review\' = awaiting platform approval; \'inactive\' = deactivated; \'archived\' = historical record.']
  listing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the metasearch listing placement type. \'standard\' = organic/paid standard placement; \'sponsored\' = premium paid placement; \'featured\' = highlighted featured listing; \'free_booking_links\' = Google\'s commission-free direct booking link program. Determines cost structure and visibility tier.']
  monthly_budget_cap decimal(14,2) [note: 'Maximum monthly spend limit configured for this metasearch listing in the listing currency. Governs total monthly metasearch investment for the property-platform combination. Null if no monthly cap is set. Supports channel mix optimization and CapEx/OpEx planning.']
  platform_name varchar [note: 'Name of the metasearch platform on which this listing is active. Identifies the specific metasearch engine for bid strategy configuration, performance benchmarking, and cost-of-acquisition analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Google Hotel Ads|TripAdvisor|Trivago|Kayak|Skyscanner|Wego|HotelsCombined — promote to reference product]']
  rate_parity_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rates displayed on this metasearch listing are in parity with the Best Available Rate (BAR) across all channels. \'parity\' = rates match; \'disparity_detected\' = rates differ from direct channel; \'under_review\' = parity check in progress; \'exempt\' = contractual exemption applies. Critical for OTA contract compliance and direct booking channel protection.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the performance reporting period for which metrics on this record apply. Used in conjunction with reporting_period_start to define the measurement window for all performance KPIs on this listing record.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the performance reporting period for which impression, click, booking, spend, and rate metrics on this record apply. Enables time-series analysis of metasearch performance and aligns with STR benchmarking reporting cycles.']
  return_on_ad_spend decimal(10,4) [note: 'The ratio of booking revenue to total spend for this listing within the reporting period (e.g., 8.50 = $8.50 revenue per $1.00 spent). Sourced from platform reporting or CRS attribution data. Primary KPI for metasearch investment optimization and bid strategy evaluation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or platform API from which this metasearch listing record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the Sabre SynXis CRS channel management module and individual platform reporting APIs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Sabre SynXis|Google Hotel Ads API|TripAdvisor API|Trivago API|Kayak API|Skyscanner API — promote to reference product]']
  target_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or audience segment this listing bid configuration targets (e.g., US, UK, DE, APAC). Enables market-level bid differentiation and performance analysis. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS market segmentation and IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting by market.']
  total_spend decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount spent on this metasearch listing (in listing currency) within the reporting period, including all click charges, acquisition fees, or commission payments depending on bid strategy. Core input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel ROI calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this metasearch listing record was last updated in the data platform. Supports incremental ETL processing, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail requirements for channel distribution data governance.']
  utm_campaign_code varchar [note: 'The UTM campaign parameter appended to the direct booking URL for this listing, enabling attribution tracking in web analytics and CRM systems. Used to measure metasearch-sourced traffic and bookings in Salesforce CRM and digital analytics platforms.']

  Note: 'Master records for property listings on metasearch platforms (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor, Trivago, Kayak, Skyscanner). Captures listing status, bid strategy (CPC, CPA, commission-based), bid amounts by market and device, property display rank, click-through and conversion rates, cost metrics, and direct booking attribution. Supports metasearch investment optimization, bid management, and measurement of metasearch contribution to direct booking revenue.'
}

Table channel.wholesale_allotment {
  wholesale_allotment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the wholesale allotment record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Wholesale allotments are contracted with wholesaler partners who are represented as booking sources in the attribution model. Adding booking_source_id FK links the allotment to the specific wholesaler']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Wholesale allotments are sold through targeted marketing campaigns to tour operators and travel agencies; tracking campaign attribution for wholesale block sales enables campaign ROI measurement, whol']
  channel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master wholesale contract agreement under which this allotment is governed, including commercial terms and SLA obligations.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Wholesale allotments may be distributed through specific channels (e.g., a tour operator allotment sold via a specific distribution channel). Many allotments per channel. Removes redundant channel_cod']
  gds_connection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: Wholesale allotments may be distributed via GDS networks (e.g., tour operator allotments loaded into Sabre for travel agent booking). Many allotments per GDS connection. Removes redundant gds_code whi']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wholesale partner (tour operator, bed bank, or travel consortium) with whom this allotment is contracted.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property to which this allotment block applies, as managed in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., Deluxe King, Standard Double) included in this wholesale allotment block.']
  allotment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this allotment block, used in communications with the wholesale partner and in the Central Reservation System (CRS) via Sabre SynXis.']
  allotment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wholesale allotment block. \'active\' indicates rooms are available for the wholesaler to sell; \'released\' indicates the release period has passed and unsold rooms have been returned to general inventory; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily blocked by revenue management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|released|expired|cancelled|pending — promote to reference product]']
  allotment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allotment commitment model. \'hard_block\' means rooms are guaranteed and held regardless of demand; \'soft_block\' means rooms are held but can be reclaimed by the property; \'free_sale\' means the wholesaler can sell without prior confirmation; \'on_request\' means each booking requires property confirmation.']
  booking_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date by which the wholesale partner must confirm bookings against this allotment block. After this date, unconfirmed allotment rooms may be released or renegotiated.']
  cancellation_count varchar [note: 'Number of reservations cancelled against this allotment block by the wholesale partner. Used to monitor wholesaler booking behaviour and assess wash factor for future allotment sizing.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage payable to the wholesale partner or intermediary on consumed allotment bookings, where applicable. Expressed as a percentage of the contracted net rate. Some wholesale models are net-rate only with no commission.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Technical connectivity method used by the wholesale partner to access and consume this allotment. Determines how allotment updates and booking confirmations are exchanged.']
  consumed_units varchar [note: 'Number of room-nights from the allotment block that have been picked up (booked) by the wholesale partner to date. Updated in near-real-time from the CRS and PMS.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the wholesale allotment agreement becomes commercially binding. Aligns with the master contract effective date in SAP S/4HANA.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the wholesale allotment agreement expires and the wholesaler\'s right to sell rooms under this block terminates. Nullable for evergreen agreements.']
  contracted_allotment_size varchar [note: 'Total number of room-nights contracted with the wholesale partner for the defined stay period and room type. This is the original block size as agreed in the wholesale contract.']
  contracted_net_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The pre-agreed net rate (exclusive of markup) payable by the wholesale partner per room per night under this allotment contract. This is the wholesale net rate model — the wholesaler adds their own markup before selling to the end consumer. Expressed in the contract currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wholesale allotment record was first created in the source system or lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the contracted net rate and any financial terms in this allotment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the contract currency in SAP S/4HANA.']
  daily_allotment_size varchar [note: 'Number of rooms allocated per night within the stay period. Used when the allotment is expressed as a per-night room count rather than a total room-night block.']
  is_closed_to_arrival boolean [note: 'Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the stay_date_from under this allotment. A Closed to Arrival (CTA) restriction prevents new check-ins on specific dates while allowing through-stays.']
  is_stop_sell boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allotment has been closed to new bookings by the revenue management team, overriding remaining availability. When True, the wholesaler cannot make new reservations against this block regardless of remaining units.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wholesale allotment is subject to Last Room Availability (LRA) obligations, meaning the wholesaler must be offered the last available room at the contracted rate. A key commercial term in premium wholesale contracts.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this wholesale allotment record, whether from a source system update or a manual override by the revenue management team.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Revenue management market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this wholesale allotment (e.g., WHOLE, TOUR, CONSOR). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation and STR benchmarking.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights permitted for a single booking under this wholesale allotment. Used to prevent excessively long stays at contracted net rates during peak demand periods.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay for a booking to be accepted under this wholesale allotment. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applied at the allotment level to protect revenue during high-demand periods.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by the revenue management or channel distribution team regarding special conditions, exceptions, or commercial context for this allotment block.']
  pickup_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual reservations booked against this allotment block by the wholesale partner. Distinct from consumed_units (room-nights) — a single multi-night booking increments pickup_count by 1 but consumed_units by the number of nights.']
  rack_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Published Best Available Rate (BAR) or rack rate for the room type at the property, used as the reference rate for calculating the wholesale discount depth and monitoring rate parity obligations.']
  rate_parity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allotment\'s contracted net rate (after wholesaler markup) is expected to comply with rate parity obligations. True means parity monitoring is active for this allotment. Used in channel distribution compliance reporting.']
  release_date date [note: 'Calculated date on which unsold allotment rooms are automatically released back to general inventory, derived from stay_date_from minus release_period_days. Stored for operational scheduling and revenue management triggers.']
  release_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the arrival date by which unsold allotment rooms must be released back to the property\'s general inventory. A key commercial term in wholesale contracts — e.g., a 21-day release means rooms not sold 21 days before check-in revert to the hotel.']
  remaining_units varchar [note: 'Number of room-nights still available for the wholesale partner to sell within this allotment block. Calculated as contracted_allotment_size minus consumed_units, maintained as a stored operational field for real-time availability checks.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this allotment record was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'First date of the stay period covered by this allotment block. Defines the start of the contracted inventory window during which the wholesaler may sell rooms.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'Last date of the stay period covered by this allotment block. Defines the end of the contracted inventory window. Inclusive of this date.']
  wash_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical cancellation and no-show rate expressed as a percentage of contracted allotment, used to adjust effective allotment sizing in revenue forecasting. Derived from historical performance of this wholesaler-property-room type combination.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional records for room allotments contracted with wholesale partners (tour operators, bed banks, travel consortia). Captures wholesaler identity, property, room type, contracted allotment block size, release period, contracted net rate, allotment consumption, and remaining availability. Distinct from OTA channel records — wholesale allotments operate on pre-contracted net rate models with release-back provisions.'
}

Table channel.channel_negotiated_rate {
  channel_negotiated_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for negotiated_rate']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates (LNR) are loaded for specific booking sources such as TMCs (Travel Management Companies) and consortia. The channel_negotiated_rate table has iata_number (STRING) which shou']
  channel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are governed by channel contracts with TMCs, consortia, and corporate accounts. Adding nullable channel_contract_id FK links negotiated rates to their governing contract. Re']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (GDS, TMC, direct, OTA) through which this negotiated rate is loaded and distributed.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account entity that holds this negotiated rate agreement. Links to the CRM or PMS corporate account master.']
  loaded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Corporate rate loading by employees requires tracking for operational accountability and audit trails. Essential for understanding who loaded negotiated rates into systems and ensuring data quality in']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which this negotiated rate is applicable. A single corporate agreement may have multiple records — one per property.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan master record associated with this negotiated rate agreement.']
  tmc_corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Travel Management Company (TMC) associated with this negotiated rate agreement. TMCs manage corporate travel programs and book through GDS channels.']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that a booking must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates no advance purchase requirement.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable through distribution channels.']
  amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The agreed nightly room rate (ADR equivalent) for this corporate negotiated rate agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. This is the principal monetary value of the rate record.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for loading and distribution.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a descriptive string; detailed blackout records managed in rate restriction tables.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the cancellation policy applicable to bookings made under this negotiated rate (e.g., 24H, 48H, NON-REF). Governs penalty and refund rules.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission payable to the TMC or travel agency on bookings made under this negotiated rate agreement. Expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value.']
  consortia_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) through which this negotiated rate is distributed. Consortia rates are loaded separately from direct LNR agreements.']
  consortia_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the travel consortia organization associated with this rate agreement (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature Travel Network).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  crs_rate_code varchar [note: 'Native rate identifier as assigned by the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the CRS source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes active and bookable through distribution channels. Aligns with the contract start date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and is no longer bookable. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter GDS chain code identifying the hotel chain under which this rate is loaded in the GDS (e.g., HH for Hilton, MC for Marriott). Required for GDS rate distribution.']
  gds_loading_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the negotiated rate\'s loading into the GDS (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Tracks whether the rate is live and bookable through GDS channels.']
  is_last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries Last Room Availability (LRA) status, meaning the rate must be offered as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of inventory controls.']
  is_rate_parity_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity obligations apply to this negotiated rate across all distribution channels. Relevant for OTA and GDS channel compliance monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this negotiated rate record, including rate amount changes, status updates, or GDS loading status changes.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Market segment classification code assigned to bookings made under this negotiated rate (e.g., CORP, GOVT, CONS). Used for revenue management segmentation and USALI reporting.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights permitted under this negotiated rate. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for a booking to qualify for this negotiated rate. LOS = Length of Stay restriction applied at the channel level.']
  negotiation_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which this negotiated rate agreement was contracted. Corporate LNR agreements are typically renegotiated annually; this field supports year-over-year production analysis.']
  pms_rate_code varchar [note: 'Rate code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS for this negotiated rate. Used for cross-system reconciliation between CRS-distributed rates and PMS-posted rates.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Hotel segment category under which this negotiated rate is classified, aligning with the property\'s service tier (luxury, premium, select-service, standard).']
  rate_loading_date date [note: 'Date on which this negotiated rate was loaded into the GDS and/or CRS for distribution. Tracks operational readiness of the rate for booking.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric rate plan code as loaded in the CRS and GDS systems (e.g., CORP01, LNR2025). Used for rate identification across distribution channels and PMS.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the negotiated rate plan as displayed in the CRS, GDS, and TMC booking tools (e.g., \'Acme Corp 2025 LNR\').']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the negotiated rate type. LNR = Local Negotiated Rate, LRA = Last Room Availability, BAR = Best Available Rate, NRR = Non-Refundable Rate, consortia = travel consortia rate, government = government/per-diem rate.']
  room_type_inclusion varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or description of room type codes included under this negotiated rate agreement (e.g., \'KING,DBLQ,SUITE\'). Defines which room categories the corporate rate applies to.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this negotiated rate record originated (e.g., OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tmc_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Travel Management Company (TMC) managing the corporate travel program for this negotiated rate (e.g., American Express Global Business Travel, BCD Travel, CWT).']
  volume_commitment_room_nights varchar [note: 'Contractually committed number of room nights the corporate account has agreed to consume during the agreement period. Used for production tracking and rate renegotiation.']

  Note: 'Master records for corporate negotiated rate agreements (LNR — Local Negotiated Rates) loaded and managed through distribution channels. Captures corporate account identifier, negotiated rate amount, rate plan code, applicable properties, room type inclusions, blackout dates, volume commitment, TMC (Travel Management Company) association, consortia membership, and GDS loading status per channel. Lives in the channel domain because these rates are operationally managed through the channel distribution workflow — loaded to GDS, distributed via TMC channels, and tracked for production/consumption at the channel level.'
}

Table channel.inventory_allocation {
  inventory_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_allocation']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed inventory_allocation to channel']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inventory allocation contract between room types and distribution channels. It captures channel-specific inventory controls, sell limits, rate parity enforcement, and allocation rules that exist only in the context of a specific room-type-channel pairing. Each record links one room type to one distribution channel with operational controls that govern how that room type is sold through that specific channel.. Existence Justification: In hotel revenue management operations, each room type is distributed across multiple channels (OTA, GDS, direct, wholesale) with channel-specific inventory controls, and each channel sells multiple room types with distinct allocation rules. Revenue management teams actively manage these allocations by setting sell limits, LRA flags, rate parity enforcement, and allocation percentages for each room-type-channel pair. This is a core operational relationship with substantial relationship-specific data.'
}

Table channel.package_rate {
  package_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this channel-package rate configuration. Primary key.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: Package rates distributed through channels may be based on or derived from channel rate plans. Adding nullable channel_rate_plan_id FK allows linking package pricing to the underlying rate plan when a']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution channel through which the spa package is offered']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa package being distributed through the channel']
  cancellation_policy_override varchar [note: 'Channel-specific cancellation policy code that overrides the packages default cancellation policy. Used when channel agreements require different cancellation terms (e.g., OTAs may require more flexible cancellation than direct bookings).']
  channel_package_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Channel-specific price for this spa package. May differ from rack rate due to channel agreements, commissions, or promotional strategies. This is the price displayed and charged through this specific channel.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage charged by this channel for bookings of this specific package. May override the channels default commission rate for strategic packages or promotional periods.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel-package rate configuration was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this channel-package rate configuration is active and bookings can be accepted through this channel.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'End date after which this channel-package rate configuration expires and the package is no longer bookable through this channel. Null indicates indefinite availability.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel-package combination is currently active and accepting bookings. Revenue management uses this to control package availability by channel without deleting historical rate configurations.']
  last_rate_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent rate update pushed to this channel for this package. Used for rate synchronization monitoring and troubleshooting.']
  minimum_advance_booking varchar [note: 'Channel-specific minimum advance booking requirement in hours. May override the packages default minimum advance booking to accommodate channel-specific operational constraints or SLAs.']
  modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this channel-package rate configuration.']
  rate_loading_status varchar [note: 'Current status of rate loading to this channel for this package. LOADED = rates successfully pushed to channel, PENDING = awaiting transmission, FAILED = transmission error, SUSPENDED = temporarily disabled, INACTIVE = not currently distributed through this channel.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the distribution agreement between a distribution channel and a spa package. It captures channel-specific pricing, commission structures, booking rules, and availability controls that exist only in the context of offering a specific package through a specific channel. Each record links one distribution channel to one spa package with rate loading, promotional pricing, and channel-specific restrictions that revenue management actively maintains.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality revenue management, spa packages are actively distributed through multiple channels (OTA platforms, GDS, direct web, corporate) with channel-specific pricing, commission structures, and booking rules. Each spa package can be offered through many distribution channels simultaneously, and each channel offers many spa packages. Revenue management teams actively maintain channel-specific rates, promotional pricing, and availability controls for each channel-package combination.'
}

Table channel.ota_campaign_participation {
  ota_campaign_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this OTA partner\'s participation in a specific campaign. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign in which the OTA partner participates']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the OTA partner participating in this campaign']
  actual_booking_volume varchar [note: 'Actual number of bookings generated by this OTA partner during the campaign period. Updated as campaign progresses.']
  actual_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual revenue generated from bookings attributed to this OTA partner during the campaign period.']
  agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the co-marketing agreement or insertion order governing this OTA partner\'s participation in this campaign.']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'The date when this OTA partner\'s participation in the campaign concludes. May differ from the overall campaign end date if partners have different participation windows.']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'The date when this OTA partner\'s participation in the campaign begins. May differ from the overall campaign start date if partners join at different times.']
  content_requirements varchar [note: 'Campaign-specific content requirements negotiated with this OTA partner, including creative specifications, messaging guidelines, brand usage rules, and promotional placement details.']
  coop_marketing_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total co-operative marketing budget allocated for this OTA partner\'s participation in this specific campaign. Represents the combined hotel and partner investment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this OTA campaign participation record was created in the system.']
  hotel_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount contributed by the hotel or brand toward this OTA partner\'s participation in the campaign.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of who last modified this participation record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participation record was last updated.']
  ota_coop_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budget allocated specifically for OTA cooperative marketing programs within this campaign. [Moved from campaign: This attribute currently exists at the campaign level as a total OTA co-op budget, but in reality, co-op budgets are negotiated and tracked per OTA partner per campaign. The attribute should be decomposed into partner-specific participation records with coop_marketing_budget and partner_contribution_amount tracked for each OTA partner\'s participation.]']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the OTA partner\'s participation in this campaign indicating operational state of the co-marketing agreement.']
  partner_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount contributed by the OTA partner toward this campaign as part of the co-marketing agreement. May be cash, media credits, or promotional placement value.']
  performance_bonus_earned decimal(15,2) [note: 'Performance-based bonus amount earned by the OTA partner for exceeding agreed campaign targets (bookings, revenue, conversion rate). Tracked per participation agreement.']
  promotional_placement_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional placement provided by the OTA partner for this campaign (e.g., homepage banner, email feature, search boost, mobile app placement).']
  target_booking_volume varchar [note: 'Target number of bookings expected from this OTA partner during the campaign period. Used for performance tracking and bonus calculation.']
  target_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target revenue expected from this OTA partner\'s campaign participation. Used for ROI measurement and performance bonus calculation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of who created this participation record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation contract between OTA partners and marketing campaigns. It captures co-operative marketing agreements where OTA partners participate in hotel-led campaigns with negotiated budget contributions, performance incentives, and content requirements. Each record links one OTA partner to one campaign with financial terms, performance tracking, and partnership-specific campaign execution details that exist only in the context of this co-marketing relationship.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality, OTA co-marketing campaigns are a standard operational practice where hotels negotiate participation agreements with multiple OTA partners (Expedia, Booking.com, Agoda) for seasonal promotions, new property launches, and loyalty tie-ins. Each OTA partner participates in multiple campaigns throughout the year, and each campaign involves multiple OTA partners with partner-specific budget contributions, performance targets, content requirements, and promotional placements. Marketing teams actively manage these participation agreements as distinct business entities with negotiated terms, financial tracking, and performance measurement.'
}

Table event.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event account. Primary key for the B2B client entity that books MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) events.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales manager or account executive assigned to manage the relationship with this event account. Responsible for proposals, negotiations, and account growth.']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Corporate event accounts are classified into segments (high-value, regional, industry vertical) for targeted marketing. Enables account-based marketing, personalized campaign targeting, and segment-sp']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Corporate event accounts with credit terms require dedicated AR sub-ledger tracking for aging, credit limit monitoring, and bad debt reserves. Controllers assign specific GL accounts to high-volume ev']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent event account if this account is part of a corporate hierarchy or national account structure. Enables roll-up reporting and consolidated billing.']
  primary_contact_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Corporate event accounts designate a loyalty member as primary contact who manages bookings and earns points on group spend. Essential for loyalty point accrual on group business and tier recognition ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary or preferred property where the event account typically books events. Used for account assignment and relationship management.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or operating name of the corporate, association, or group client organization that books MICE events.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the event account. Used in contracts, proposals, and invoicing. Typically assigned by the CRS (Central Reservation System) or Delphi system.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event account. Active accounts can book new events; suspended accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification based on historical event spend, booking frequency, and relationship value. Determines service levels, pricing discounts, and account management resources.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event account based on the nature of the client organization. Determines pricing strategies, contract terms, and service offerings.']
  average_event_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average revenue per event for this account. Calculated as lifetime event spend divided by total events booked. Used for forecasting and opportunity sizing.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address for the event account, including street number and name.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address for additional address details such as suite, floor, or building number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the billing address of the event account.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the event account was closed or deactivated. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle reporting and reactivation analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event account record was first created in the system. Used for account age analysis and audit trails.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the event account. Used to control exposure and manage accounts receivable risk.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit approval status for the event account. Determines whether the account can book events on credit terms or requires prepayment.']
  external_account_reference varchar [note: 'Account identifier from the source system (e.g., Delphi Account ID, Salesforce Account ID). Used for system integration and data lineage tracking.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry sector or vertical market of the client organization (e.g., Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Education, Manufacturing). Used for market segmentation and targeted sales strategies.']
  is_national_account boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a national or enterprise-wide account with negotiated rates and terms across multiple properties.']
  is_vip_account boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a VIP or strategic account requiring special handling, executive attention, or premium service levels.']
  last_event_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent event held by this account. Used for recency analysis and account reactivation campaigns.']
  lifetime_event_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cumulative revenue generated from all events booked by this account since inception. Key metric for account tier classification and relationship value assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special requirements, preferences, historical context, or relationship management information for the event account.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Defines the credit period for the event account.']
  preferred_event_type varchar [note: 'Most frequently booked event type by this account (e.g., Conference, Training, Board Meeting, Product Launch). Used for targeted marketing and service customization.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the main contact person for event inquiries, proposals, and communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the client organization responsible for event planning and booking decisions.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the main contact person for event coordination and urgent communications.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or market-based sales territory code to which this event account is assigned for sales planning and performance tracking.']
  secondary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the secondary contact person for backup communications and escalations.']
  secondary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the secondary or backup contact person at the client organization for event planning continuity.']
  secondary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the secondary contact person for backup communications.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record where this event account was originally created (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Salesforce CRM).']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number (EIN) of the client organization for tax reporting and compliance.']
  total_events_booked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of events booked by this account since inception. Used for relationship depth analysis and loyalty assessment.']
  typical_attendee_count varchar [note: 'Average number of attendees for events booked by this account. Used for capacity planning and function space allocation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event account record was last modified. Used for data freshness tracking and change audit.']

  Note: 'Master record for the corporate, association, or group client account that books MICE events. Captures organization name, industry vertical, account tier, primary and secondary contacts, credit terms, preferred properties, historical event spend, and assigned account/sales manager. Serves as the B2B client identity anchor for the event domain — distinct from the guest domain\'s individual traveler profile and from the finance domain\'s AR customer record. All inquiries, proposals, bookings, and revenue link to this entity for relationship management, account-level performance reporting, and sales territory assignment.'
}

  inquiry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MICE inquiry or RFP. Primary key for the inquiry record.']

  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Group inquiries arrive via specific distribution channels (direct website inquiry forms, OTA group inquiry platforms, GDS shopping requests). Critical for lead source tracking, channel effectiveness a']
  inquiring_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Event inquiries frequently originate from loyalty members leveraging their status for group bookings. Sales teams reference member tier for pricing and concessions; loyalty members expect tier recogni']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or hotel where the inquiry was received and is being managed.']
  sanction_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanction_screening. Business justification: High-value inquiries from corporate clients or international organizations may trigger sanctions screening during lead qualification. Sales teams screen before investing resources in proposals. Risk m']
  alternate_dates_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client is flexible and willing to consider alternate dates for the event.']
  av_equipment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether audio-visual equipment is required for the event.']
  av_equipment_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of audio-visual equipment needs including projectors, screens, microphones, sound systems, and technical support.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  budget_range_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum budget amount indicated by the prospective client for the entire event including rooms, function space, and catering.']
  budget_range_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum budget amount indicated by the prospective client for the entire event including rooms, function space, and catering.']
  catering_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether catering services (F&B) are required as part of the event.']
  catering_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of catering needs including meal types, service styles, dietary restrictions, and beverage requirements.']
  client_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person from the client organization.']

  client_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person from the client organization.']

  estimated_attendance varchar [note: 'Estimated number of attendees or participants for the event as indicated in the inquiry.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Name or title of the proposed event as provided by the prospective client.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of MICE event requested (meeting, conference, incentive, exhibition, wedding, social event, or other). Aligns with MICE classification standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meeting|conference|incentive|exhibition|wedding|social|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  function_space_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of function space requirements including room types, capacities, setup styles, and special configurations needed for the event.']
  group_room_block_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of guest rooms required for the group block to accommodate event attendees.']
  inquiry_date date [note: 'Date when the MICE inquiry or RFP was received by the property or sales team.']
  inquiry_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique inquiry reference number generated by Delphi by Amadeus for tracking and communication with prospective clients.']
  inquiry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MICE inquiry in the sales pipeline. Tracks progression from initial lead through conversion or closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|qualified|proposal_sent|negotiation|converted|lost|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the sales manager or event coordinator assigned as the owner of this inquiry.']
  lead_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sales manager or event coordinator assigned as the owner of this inquiry.']
  lead_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score assigned to the inquiry based on qualification criteria such as budget fit, date flexibility, and conversion probability. Used for prioritization.']
  lost_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the inquiry was lost or not converted (e.g., budget constraints, dates unavailable, competitor selected). Populated when inquiry_status is \'lost\'.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for the inquiry (corporate, association, government, SMERF, incentive, other). Used for revenue management and reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inquiry record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional details, special requests, or context provided by the prospective client or recorded by the sales team.']
  preferred_end_date date [note: 'Preferred or requested end date for the event as specified by the prospective client.']
  preferred_start_date date [note: 'Preferred or requested start date for the event as specified by the prospective client.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inquiry has been qualified as a viable sales opportunity based on budget, dates, and requirements alignment.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Name or description of the referral source if the inquiry came through a referral (e.g., past client, partner organization, industry association).']
  room_nights_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated total room nights (rooms × nights) for the group block.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the MICE inquiry was received (e.g., direct contact, OTA, GDS, referral, website form, email, phone call, walk-in). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|ota|gds|referral|website|email|phone|walk_in — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special requirements or requests from the client including accessibility needs, branding, security, or unique setup configurations.']

  Note: 'Initial MICE lead or request for proposal (RFP) received from a prospective event client. Captures the inquiry source channel (OTA, direct, GDS, referral), event type (meeting, conference, incentive, exhibition), estimated attendance, preferred dates, function space requirements, catering needs, group room block estimate, budget range, decision timeline, and lead owner. Represents the top-of-funnel stage in the Delphi by Amadeus event sales pipeline.'
}

Table event.proposal {
  proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MICE event proposal record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account or client organization receiving this proposal.']
  banquet_menu_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_menu_package. Business justification: Proposals reference specific banquet packages when quoting F&B pricing to prospective group clients. Essential for accurate proposal costing, competitive positioning, client presentation materials, an']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Proposals often reference campaign-specific offers or are generated in response to campaign-driven inquiries. Needed to track which campaigns drive proposal activity and measure campaign influence on ']
  decision_maker_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Proposals are addressed to specific loyalty member decision-makers. Sales teams reference member tier when crafting proposals and pricing; loyalty status influences concessions and upgrade offers in g']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Proposals often originate from channel-specific inquiry mechanisms (OTA RFP platforms, direct website forms, GDS shopping tools). Required for lead source attribution, conversion rate analysis by chan']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: When a proposal is accepted, an event_booking is created. The proposal should directly reference which booking it generated. Currently, proposal links to event_contract, and event_contract links to ev']
  event_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal event contract generated upon proposal acceptance.']
  inquiry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating event inquiry or RFP (Request for Proposal) that triggered this proposal.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Proposals must account for compliance obligations affecting event feasibility (capacity restrictions, health protocols, noise ordinances). Sales managers verify obligations before quoting. Ensures pro']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.spa_package. Business justification: Event proposals include spa packages as value-adds for wellness conferences, incentive trips, executive retreats. Sales process requires linking proposed spa packages to calculate total event value an']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property where the proposed event will be hosted.']
  proposal_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or director who approved the proposal terms and pricing.']
  proposal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales manager or event sales executive responsible for this proposal.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval status indicating whether the proposal terms have been reviewed and authorized by revenue management or senior leadership.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proposal was internally approved for submission to the client.']
  attrition_clause varchar [note: 'Terms and conditions regarding penalties or fees if the client fails to meet minimum room block or F&B commitments.']
  av_package_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost for audio-visual equipment and services proposed.']
  av_package_description varchar [note: 'Description of proposed audio-visual equipment, technology packages, and production services included in the proposal.']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Terms outlining cancellation deadlines, penalties, and refund conditions for the proposed event.']
  client_feedback varchar [note: 'Client comments, objections, or feedback received during proposal review and negotiation.']
  client_response_date date [note: 'Date when the client formally responded to the proposal with acceptance, decline, or request for revision.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission rate offered to third-party planners, travel agents, or intermediaries if applicable.']
  competitive_positioning_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes capturing competitive intelligence, alternative venues being considered by the client, and strategic positioning rationale.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proposal record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the proposal.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date by which the client is expected to make a decision on accepting or declining the proposal.']
  deposit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Initial deposit or advance payment required to secure the proposed event dates and services.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the initial deposit must be received to hold the proposed event space and services.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'Proposed end date of the event or meeting.']
  event_start_date date [note: 'Proposed start date of the event or meeting.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the proposed event within MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meeting|incentive|conference|exhibition|wedding|social|corporate|association — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expected_attendance varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of attendees or participants for the proposed event.']
  fb_minimum_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed spend commitment for catering and food and beverage services as part of the proposal terms.']
  function_space_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of meeting rooms, ballrooms, and function spaces proposed for the event, including setup styles and capacities.']
  issued_date date [note: 'Date when the proposal was formally issued and sent to the prospective client.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proposal record was last updated or revised.']
  proposal_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the proposal, typically reflecting the event name and client organization.']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the proposal, used in client communications and contract references.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proposal in the negotiation and approval workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|revised|accepted|declined|expired|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  room_block_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of guest rooms proposed to be blocked for event attendees.']
  room_block_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Proposed negotiated nightly rate for the group room block, typically below BAR (Best Available Rate).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the proposal inquiry originated.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Client-specific requests, accessibility needs, dietary restrictions, or unique event requirements captured in the proposal.']
  total_estimated_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total estimated event value including rooms, F&B, AV, and ancillary services, representing the full revenue opportunity.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the proposal terms, rates, and availability are no longer guaranteed.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number tracking revisions and iterations of the proposal during negotiation lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Formal event proposal issued to a prospective MICE client in response to an inquiry or RFP. Captures proposed function spaces, room block rates, F&B minimums, AV packages, total estimated event value, validity period, proposal version, competitive positioning notes, and approval status. Tracks the negotiation lifecycle from initial proposal through revision and acceptance. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus proposal management module.'
}
