  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.spa_package. Business justification: Corporate events and weddings bundle banquet catering with spa packages (bridal party treatments, executive wellness sessions, conference attendee massages). Event planners book integrated F&B+spa exp']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.pos_check. Business justification: Banquet events often generate POS checks for actual consumption billing (especially for bar service, upgrades, or consumption-based beverage packages). This FK links the BEO to the actual POS transact']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property where the banquet event will take place.']
  revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Banquet events must be assigned to F&B revenue centers for USALI departmental reporting and cost center allocation. Currently BEO links to function_space and outlet but not to revenue_center. This FK ']
  actual_covers varchar [note: 'Actual number of guests who attended the banquet event. Recorded post-event for reconciliation and future forecasting.']
  beo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the BEO document. Used for communication with clients and operations teams.']
  beverage_package_type varchar [note: 'Type of beverage service selected for the event. Determines billing method and beverage offerings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hosted_bar|cash_bar|consumption_bar|beer_wine_only|soft_drinks_only|full_premium|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  beverage_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total beverage revenue for the event. May be package-based or consumption-based depending on beverage package type.']
  billing_instructions varchar [note: 'Special billing instructions including payment method, billing contact, invoice delivery preferences, and split billing arrangements.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the banquet event was completed and the BEO was closed.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the BEO was confirmed by the client and moved to confirmed status.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary client contact for BEO distribution and event correspondence.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary client contact for this banquet event. Person responsible for event decisions and communication.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary client contact for day-of-event communication and coordination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BEO record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this BEO.']
  dietary_requirements varchar [note: 'Special dietary needs and restrictions for event attendees including allergies, religious requirements, and preferences.']
  event_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the banquet event will take place. Primary business event timestamp for the BEO.']
  event_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end time for the banquet event including date and time components.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Name or title of the banquet event as provided by the client.']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for the banquet event including date and time components.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the banquet event order. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the banquet event type. Determines service style, menu options, and operational requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wedding|corporate_dinner|cocktail_reception|gala|meeting_break|conference_lunch|breakfast|awards_ceremony|charity_event|holiday_party — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  food_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total food revenue for the event calculated from guaranteed covers and per-person food price.']
  guaranteed_covers varchar [note: 'Number of guests guaranteed by the client for billing purposes. Minimum number the client will be charged for regardless of actual attendance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BEO record was last modified. Tracks changes to event details, pricing, or status.']
  per_person_beverage_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Beverage charge per guest for the selected beverage package. Applicable for hosted bar and package pricing models.']
  per_person_food_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Food charge per guest for the selected menu package. Excludes beverage, service charge, and tax.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Service charge applied to the event. Typically a percentage of food and beverage revenue to cover labor and service costs.']
  service_charge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Service charge rate applied to the event as a percentage of food and beverage revenue.']
  setup_style varchar [note: 'Room setup configuration for the event. Determines furniture arrangement, capacity, and service flow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: banquet_rounds|theater|classroom|u_shape|boardroom|hollow_square|reception|cocktail|buffet — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes and special requests for event execution including AV requirements, decor, timing, and client preferences.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax amount charged for the event including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate applied to the event as a percentage of taxable revenue.']
  total_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total revenue for the banquet event including food, beverage, service charge, and tax. Grand total for billing.']

  Note: 'Banquet Event Order (BEO) master record for catered F&B functions, capturing event name, date, type (wedding, corporate dinner, cocktail reception, gala, meeting break), function room, guaranteed and actual cover counts, menu package, per-person price, beverage package, setup style, dietary requirements, F&B revenue total, service charge, tax, billing instructions, and event status. Serves as the F&B execution document for group events originated in the event domain.'
}

Table fnb.banquet_menu_package {
  banquet_menu_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the banquet menu package. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Banquet package pricing and terms require director-level approval for contract authority and revenue protection. Links approval authority to workforce records for delegation of authority reporting and']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Banquet packages are often campaign-specific ("2024 Spring Wedding Collection", "Holiday Corporate Party Package"). Sales teams need to track package-campaign relationships for promotional period mana']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_category. Business justification: Banquet packages are assigned procurement categories for cost analysis, pricing strategy, profitability tracking, and sourcing decisions. Standard catering operations and event management practice.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the F&B (Food and Beverage) outlet or catering department responsible for delivering this package. Links to property outlet master data.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property offering this banquet menu package. Links to property master data.']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days advance notice required to book this package. Ensures adequate time for procurement, preparation, and staffing.']
  allergen_information varchar [note: 'Detailed allergen disclosure for menu items in this package (e.g., \'Contains: Dairy, Eggs, Wheat, Tree Nuts\'). Required for ISO 22000 food safety compliance and guest safety.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this banquet menu package was approved for sale. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  beverage_cost_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target beverage cost as a percentage of the per-person price (if beverages are included). Used for F&B (Food and Beverage) profitability analysis.']
  beverage_duration_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of hours of beverage service included in the package price (e.g., 2.0 for a two-hour cocktail reception). Null if beverage_inclusion is \'none\'.']
  beverage_inclusion varchar [note: 'Type of beverage service included in the per-person package price. \'None\' indicates beverages are charged separately. Impacts F&B (Food and Beverage) revenue allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|soft drinks|coffee and tea|beer and wine|full bar|premium bar|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Cancellation terms and penalties specific to this package (e.g., \'72-hour notice required, 50% penalty within 48 hours\'). Used in event contracts and BEO (Banquet Event Order) terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this banquet menu package record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the per-person price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dietary_accommodations varchar [note: 'Description of dietary options and accommodations available within this package (e.g., \'Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-Free, Kosher options available upon request\'). Supports ISO 22000 food safety compliance.']
  food_cost_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target food cost as a percentage of the per-person price. Used for F&B (Food and Beverage) profitability analysis and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) contribution tracking.']
  included_courses varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all food courses included in the package (e.g., \'Appetizer, Salad, Entrée with Two Sides, Dessert, Bread Service\'). Used in BEO (Banquet Event Order) documentation.']
  labor_hours_per_guest decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated service labor hours required per guest for this package. Used for workforce scheduling and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this banquet menu package record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests that can be served under this package configuration. Constrained by kitchen capacity, service staff, and function space limitations.']
  menu_category varchar [note: 'High-level menu category for reporting and analytics. Aligns with USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) F&B (Food and Beverage) departmental reporting.']
  minimum_guarantee varchar [note: 'Minimum number of guests required to book this package. Event will be charged for this minimum even if actual attendance is lower. Critical for group event revenue management.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the banquet menu package used in Delphi by Amadeus and BEO (Banquet Event Order) generation. Typically alphanumeric code used in event sales quoting.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the banquet menu package (e.g., \'Executive Breakfast Buffet\', \'Gala Dinner Package\', \'Cocktail Reception Deluxe\').']
  package_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and special instructions for event sales and catering staff regarding this package. May include preparation tips, substitution rules, or operational considerations.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the banquet menu package. Only \'active\' and \'seasonal\' packages are available for booking in Delphi by Amadeus.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the banquet menu package by meal period and service style. Determines pricing structure and operational requirements.']
  per_person_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base price per person for the banquet menu package in the property\'s local currency. Used for event sales quoting and BEO (Banquet Event Order) pricing calculations.']
  season_description varchar [note: 'Description of the season or time period when this package is available (e.g., \'Holiday Season\', \'Summer Months\', \'Wedding Season\'). Null if not seasonal.']
  seasonal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this package is offered only during specific seasons or year-round. True if seasonal, False if available year-round.']
  service_charge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage service charge (gratuity) applied to the package price. Typically ranges from 18% to 24% in the hospitality industry. Used in total event cost calculations.']
  service_style varchar [note: 'Method of food service delivery for the package (buffet, plated service, family style, food stations, reception style, cocktail service). Impacts labor requirements and guest experience.']
  setup_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of room setup, table configuration, linen, centerpieces, and equipment required for this package (e.g., \'Rounds of 10, Floor-Length Linens, Centerpieces, Stage, AV Equipment\'). Used for event operations planning.']
  tax_inclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the per-person price includes applicable taxes. True if tax-inclusive, False if taxes are added separately. Critical for accurate event quoting.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the validity period for this package. Package can be booked for events on or after this date.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date of the validity period for this package. Package can be booked for events on or before this date. Null indicates no expiration.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of banquet and catering menu packages offered for group events, capturing package name, package type (breakfast package, lunch buffet, dinner gala, coffee break, cocktail reception, wedding package), per-person price, minimum guarantee, included courses, beverage inclusions, setup requirements, and validity period. Supports event sales quoting in Delphi by Amadeus and BEO generation. Distinct from à la carte menus as packages are priced per-person for group consumption.'
}

Table fnb.inventory_item {
  inventory_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_item']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_category. Business justification: Inventory items are classified into procurement categories for ABC analysis, vendor selection, spend management, and sourcing strategy. Fundamental inventory management and procurement planning practi']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: F&B inventory items map to procurement material master for standardized procurement, vendor management, cost tracking, and cross-property reporting. Core ERP integration pattern in multi-unit hospital']
  alcohol_by_volume_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of alcohol content by volume for alcoholic beverages. Required for regulatory reporting and responsible service of alcohol programs.']
  alcohol_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains alcohol. Used for regulatory compliance, age verification at POS, and beverage cost tracking.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains or may contain common allergens (dairy, nuts, gluten, shellfish, etc.). Required for guest safety and regulatory compliance.']
  allergen_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific allergens present in the item (e.g., dairy, tree nuts, gluten, soy, eggs, fish, shellfish, peanuts). Used for menu labeling and guest communication.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand name of the item (e.g., Coca-Cola, Grey Goose, Sysco). Used for brand-specific reporting and guest preference tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory item record was first created in the system.']
  current_on_hand_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Real-time quantity of the item currently available in inventory across all storage locations. Updated by receipts, issues, and physical counts.']
  gluten_free_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified gluten-free or naturally contains no gluten. Critical for guests with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified Halal. Required for properties serving Muslim guests and for menu compliance.']
  iso_food_safety_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on ISO 22000 food safety standards. High-risk items (e.g., raw meat, dairy) require stricter handling and monitoring.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the inventory item distinguishing food, beverage, and supply types. Critical for F&B cost accounting and USALI departmental reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food|beverage|dry_goods|perishable|frozen|alcohol|non_alcohol|dairy|meat|seafood|produce|bakery|condiment|disposable — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  item_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the inventory item used across Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and central F&B stores. Typically alphanumeric SKU or internal catalog number.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the inventory item as it appears in menus, recipes, and POS systems.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory item. Inactive or discontinued items are excluded from ordering and recipe planning.']
  item_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular segmentation within the primary category (e.g., red wine, white wine, spirits under beverage-alcohol).']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified Kosher. Required for properties serving Jewish guests and for menu compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory item record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count for this item. Used for inventory accuracy monitoring and audit compliance.']
  last_physical_count_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count. Used for variance analysis against system on-hand quantity.']
  last_purchase_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit cost from the most recent purchase order. Used for cost variance analysis and standard cost updates.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order receipt for this item. Used for supplier performance tracking and inventory aging analysis.']
  local_sourced_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is sourced from local suppliers within a defined geographic radius. Supports farm-to-table programs and sustainability initiatives.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional item information, handling instructions, or special considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  organic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is certified organic. Used for menu labeling and sustainability reporting.']
  package_size varchar [note: 'Size or quantity per package unit (e.g., 750ml bottle, 12-count case, 5kg bag). Used for ordering and yield calculations.']
  par_level decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target inventory quantity that should be maintained to meet operational demand without overstocking. Used for automated reorder triggers.']
  reorder_point decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum inventory quantity threshold that triggers a purchase requisition or reorder workflow. Calculated based on lead time and consumption rate.']
  required_storage_temperature_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum safe storage temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled items. Exceeding this threshold triggers food safety alerts.']
  required_storage_temperature_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum safe storage temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled items. Used for cold chain monitoring and food safety audits.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the item remains safe and usable from receipt date. Critical for perishable items and ISO 22000 food safety compliance.']
  standard_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average unit cost of the item used for inventory valuation and F&B cost calculations. Updated periodically based on purchase history and supplier pricing.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the item is stored (e.g., central F&B store, outlet storeroom, walk-in cooler, dry storage, bar). Supports multi-location inventory tracking.']
  supplier_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the primary supplier for this item. Links to procurement vendor master for purchase order generation.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to determine applicable tax rates and exemptions (e.g., food, beverage, alcohol). Varies by jurisdiction.']
  taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item is subject to sales tax when sold through F&B outlets. Drives POS tax calculation logic.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item requires refrigeration or freezing to maintain safety and quality. Drives storage location assignment and handling procedures.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the item is stocked, ordered, and issued. Used for inventory valuation and recipe costing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|bottle|liter|kilogram|pound|ounce|gallon|dozen|box|bag|can|jar — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vegan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item contains no animal products or by-products. Used for menu labeling and dietary preference tracking.']
  vendor_item_code varchar [note: 'Suppliers unique identifier for this item. Used for purchase order line item matching and invoice reconciliation.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of usable product after preparation and waste (e.g., 75% yield for whole fish after filleting). Used for accurate recipe costing.']

  Note: 'F&B-specific inventory item master tracking food and beverage stock items held in outlet storerooms and central F&B stores. Captures item name, item category (food, beverage, dry goods, perishable, frozen, alcohol), unit of measure, par level, reorder point, standard cost, current on-hand quantity, storage location, supplier reference, shelf life, and ISO 22000 food safety classification (allergen, temperature-controlled). Distinct from the procurement domain\'s general inventory as this is F&B-specific with food safety attributes.'
}

Table fnb.stock_transaction {
  stock_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stock_transaction']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory transactions (purchases, waste, transfers) must allocate to cost centers for departmental expense tracking and variance analysis. Stock_transaction has cost_center_code (denormalized); FK en']
  destination_location_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location (outlet or storeroom) to which the item was moved. Null for waste/spoilage transactions.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the F&B outlet or storeroom involved in the transaction.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Stock transactions for receipts link to GR documents for inventory reconciliation, quality tracking, financial posting, and three-way match validation. Core ERP receiving and inventory control process']
  inventory_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inventory item involved in the transaction.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Inventory movements post to GL accounts: purchases to inventory asset, issues to COGS, waste to expense. Direct ledger link enables automated GL posting, inventory valuation, and financial statement a']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Stock movements reference material master for standardized item identification, costing, cross-property reporting, and consolidated procurement analytics. Essential for multi-unit hospitality operatio']
  original_transaction_stock_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original transaction ID if this is a reversal or correction transaction. Null for original transactions.']
  physical_count_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical inventory count session that generated this adjustment transaction. Null for non-adjustment transactions.']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POS transaction ID for end-of-day consumption or issue-to-outlet transactions linked to sales. Null for non-POS-linked transactions.']
  primary_stock_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who authorized or approved the transaction, particularly for waste, adjustments, and transfers.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the stock transaction occurred.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock receipts from vendors reference originating PO for three-way matching, variance analysis, audit trails, and invoice reconciliation. Standard procurement control and financial compliance requirem']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.recipe. Business justification: Stock transactions for recipe production/consumption need to reference the recipe being prepared. When kitchen prepares a recipe, ingredients are consumed (stock transaction type = \'recipe_consumption']
  source_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source location (outlet or storeroom) from which the item was moved. Null for receipts from vendors.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor for receipt or return-to-vendor transactions. Null for internal transactions.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the item for traceability and food safety compliance.']
  corrective_action_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing corrective actions taken or planned in response to waste or spoilage events. Null for non-waste transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the transaction cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the item batch, used for stock rotation and waste prevention.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number for receipt transactions. Null for non-receipt transactions.']
  meal_period varchar [note: 'Meal period during which the transaction occurred (breakfast, lunch, dinner, all-day, banquet, room service). Primarily used for waste tracking and consumption analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the transaction for operational reference.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item involved in the transaction, expressed in the item\'s unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data platform.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last updated in the data platform.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for reversing or cancelling the transaction. Null for active transactions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the transaction (e.g., Oracle Hospitality MICROS, SAP MM, manual entry).']
  source_system_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier in the source system for traceability and reconciliation.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of the transaction (quantity × unit cost), in property base currency.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Business date of the stock transaction, used for daily reporting and period-end reconciliation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number or reference code for the stock movement.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the transaction: pending (awaiting approval), completed (finalized), cancelled (voided before completion), or reversed (corrected after completion).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transaction occurred in the operational system.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of stock transaction: receipt (goods received from vendor), issue-to-outlet (storeroom to outlet), inter-outlet transfer (outlet to outlet), spoilage, over-production waste, plate waste, breakage, expired stock, physical count adjustment, end-of-day consumption, or return-to-vendor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|issue_to_outlet|inter_outlet_transfer|spoilage|over_production_waste|plate_waste|breakage|expired_stock|physical_count_adjustment|end_of_day_consumption|return_to_vendor — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the item at the time of the transaction, in property base currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., kg, liter, case, bottle, portion, each).']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost variance amount for physical count adjustment transactions (difference between book and actual). Null for non-adjustment transactions.']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'Category of waste for waste transactions: spoilage, over-production, plate waste (customer leftovers), breakage, expired stock, or other. Null for non-waste transactions.']
  waste_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason or description for the waste event. Null for non-waste transactions.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all F&B inventory movements and waste events across outlets and storerooms. Captures transaction type (receipt, issue-to-outlet, inter-outlet transfer, spoilage, over-production waste, plate waste, breakage, expired stock, physical count adjustment, end-of-day consumption, return-to-vendor), item reference, quantity, unit cost, total cost, source location, destination location, transaction timestamp, and authorizing manager. For waste/spoilage transactions: waste category, waste reason, meal period, recording staff, and corrective action notes. Supports food cost variance analysis, par level management, sustainability reporting, waste reduction KPIs, and ISO 22000 stock traceability. Integrates waste tracking previously in separate waste logs to provide unified inventory and waste analytics.'
}

Table fnb.discount {
  discount_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for discount']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Discount authorization requires manager-level approval for revenue protection and fraud prevention. Essential for audit trails, authorization limit enforcement, and tracking who approved promotional p']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this discount to a specific marketing campaign in the CRM system. Supports promotional campaign tracking and ROI analysis. Null if not campaign-driven.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: F&B discounts are often implementations of marketing offers ("Loyalty Platinum 20% dining discount"). POS systems need to link discount codes to offer definitions for eligibility validation, redemptio']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Discounts post to specific GL accounts for revenue adjustment tracking and promotional expense analysis. Discount has gl_account_code (denormalized); FK enables automated posting, supports marketing R']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Tier-based F&B discounts: platinum members receive 20% dining discount, gold 15%, etc. discount.loyalty_tier_required exists as text; FK enables POS validation, automated discount application, tier be']
  amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount deducted from the check or item (e.g., 5.00 for $5 off). Applicable when discount_type is fixed_amount. Null for non-fixed-amount discount types.']
  applicable_menu_item_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which menu items are eligible for this discount: all_items (any item), specific_items (named items only), menu_category (e.g., appetizers, beverages), revenue_class (e.g., food, beverage, alcohol).']
  applicable_outlet_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which F&B outlets can apply this discount: all_outlets (property-wide), specific_outlets (named outlets only), outlet_type (e.g., all restaurants, all bars).']
  applies_to_service_charge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is applied before or after service charge calculation. True if discount reduces service charge base, False if applied post-service-charge. Supports gratuity calculation rules.']
  applies_to_tax boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is applied before or after tax calculation. True if discount reduces taxable amount, False if applied post-tax. Supports tax compliance and revenue recognition.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount configuration was approved for activation. Null if no approval required or pending approval. Supports authorization workflow and audit trail.']
  authorization_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum staff authorization level required to apply this discount: none (no approval), server (server discretion), supervisor (shift supervisor), manager (outlet manager), director (F&B director), gm (general manager). Supports loss prevention and discount control.']
  combinable_with_other_discounts boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discounts on the same check. True if combinable, False if mutually exclusive. Supports discount stacking rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and configuration history.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Applicable when discount_amount is populated.']
  discount_category varchar [note: 'Business classification grouping discounts by purpose: promotional (marketing campaign), loyalty (program benefit), employee (staff meal), vip (guest recognition), service_recovery (guest satisfaction), group (event/MICE), seasonal (time-based offer), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotional|loyalty|employee|vip|service_recovery|group|seasonal|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the discount in the POS system. Used for transaction posting and reporting. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS discount configuration.']
  discount_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the discount terms, conditions, and business purpose. Used for staff training and operational reference.']
  discount_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the discount or promotion displayed to staff and on guest receipts.']
  discount_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the discount configuration: active (available for use), inactive (disabled), suspended (temporarily unavailable), expired (past validity period), pending_approval (awaiting authorization).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanism: percentage (percent off), fixed_amount (dollar/currency off), complimentary (full comp), loyalty_redemption (points-based), employee_meal (staff benefit), manager_comp (manager discretionary comp).']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes for F&B management regarding discount usage, approval history, or special handling instructions. Not visible to guests.']
  maximum_discount_amount_per_check decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total discount amount that can be applied to a single check, regardless of check total. Null for no cap. Supports loss prevention and discount control.']
  maximum_usage_per_check varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this discount can be applied to a single guest check. Null for unlimited usage. Supports discount control and loss prevention.']
  maximum_usage_per_guest varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single guest (identified by loyalty account or profile) can use this discount during the validity period. Null for unlimited. Supports promotional campaign tracking.']
  minimum_check_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum pre-discount check total required to qualify for this discount. Null if no minimum. Supports promotional campaign rules (e.g., $50 minimum for 20% off).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the staff member who last modified this discount configuration. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount configuration record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the check or item (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off). Applicable when discount_type is percentage. Null for non-percentage discount types.']
  promo_code_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a promotional code must be entered by the guest or staff to activate this discount. True if promo code required, False if automatically available. Supports campaign tracking.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Revenue classification for reporting purposes: food, beverage, alcohol, tobacco, other. Aligns with USALI F&B departmental reporting and supports revenue analysis.']
  valid_days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days when discount is applicable (e.g., Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday or All). Supports day-of-week restrictions for promotional campaigns.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the discount validity period. Discount becomes available for use on this date.']
  valid_time_from varchar [note: 'Start time of day when discount is applicable (HH:MM format, 24-hour). Supports daypart restrictions (e.g., happy hour, breakfast specials). Null if no time restriction.']
  valid_time_to varchar [note: 'End time of day when discount is applicable (HH:MM format, 24-hour). Supports daypart restrictions. Null if no time restriction.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date of the discount validity period. Discount expires after this date. Null for open-ended discounts.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the staff member who created this discount configuration. Supports accountability and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Master reference for F&B discount and promotion types applicable at POS, capturing discount name, discount type (percentage, fixed amount, complimentary, loyalty redemption, employee meal, manager comp, promotional offer), applicable outlet scope, applicable menu item scope, discount percentage or amount, authorization level required, validity period, and maximum usage limits. Supports discount control, loss prevention, and promotional campaign tracking. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS discount configuration.'
}

Table fnb.wine_cellar {
  wine_cellar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wine cellar inventory record. Primary key for vintage-level wine and premium beverage tracking.']
  fnb_inventory_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.inventory_item. Business justification: Wine cellar is specialized inventory tracking for premium beverages requiring vintage/bin management. Linking to inventory_item master enables integration with standard inventory processes (receiving,']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific F&B outlet (restaurant, bar, banquet) that manages this wine inventory.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property where this wine cellar inventory is maintained.']
  alcohol_by_volume_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of alcohol content by volume. Required for regulatory compliance and guest information.']
  appellation varchar [note: 'Geographic region or appellation of origin (e.g., Napa Valley, Bordeaux, Tuscany). Indicates terroir and quality designation.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location identifier within the wine cellar (e.g., rack number, bin code). Enables efficient retrieval and inventory management.']
  bottle_size_ml varchar [note: 'Volume of the bottle in milliliters (e.g., 750, 1500, 375). Standard bottle is 750ml.']
  by_the_glass_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wine is available for individual glass pours. True if offered by the glass, False if bottle-only.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the wine\'s origin (e.g., USA, FRA, ITA, AUS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wine cellar inventory record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  food_pairing_recommendations varchar [note: 'Suggested food pairings and menu items that complement the wine. Supports upselling and guest experience enhancement.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wine in the cellar inventory. Determines availability for ordering and wine list inclusion.']
  last_inventory_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count for this wine. Supports audit trail and shrinkage analysis.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order receipt for this wine. Supports aging and turnover analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wine cellar inventory record was most recently modified.']
  optimal_drinking_window_end varchar [note: 'Year when the wine is expected to pass its peak drinking quality. Guides inventory rotation and promotional timing.']
  optimal_drinking_window_start varchar [note: 'Year when the wine is expected to reach optimal maturity for consumption. Supports cellar aging strategy.']
  par_level varchar [note: 'Minimum inventory level that should be maintained. Triggers reorder when quantity on hand falls below this threshold.']
  producer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the winery, vineyard, or producer who created the wine.']
  purchase_cost_per_bottle decimal(15,2) [note: 'Unit cost paid to supplier per bottle. Used for inventory valuation and food cost calculations.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current number of bottles available in inventory. Updated by receipts, sales, and adjustments.']
  quantity_reserved varchar [note: 'Number of bottles reserved for upcoming events, banquets, or special orders. Not available for general sale.']
  rating_source varchar [note: 'Name of the publication or critic who provided the wine rating (e.g., Wine Spectator, Wine Advocate, Decanter).']
  reorder_quantity varchar [note: 'Standard quantity to order when inventory reaches par level. Optimized for supplier minimums and storage capacity.']
  selling_price_bottle decimal(15,2) [note: 'Retail price charged to guests for a full bottle. Supports revenue management and menu pricing.']
  selling_price_glass decimal(15,2) [note: 'Retail price charged to guests for a single glass pour. Applicable for by-the-glass program wines.']
  sommelier_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes from the sommelier regarding optimal serving temperature, decanting requirements, aging potential, or special handling instructions.']
  storage_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature in Celsius for optimal preservation. Supports cellar climate control compliance.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the distributor or supplier from whom the wine is purchased.']
  tasting_notes varchar [note: 'Sommelier-curated description of the wine\'s flavor profile, aroma, body, and finish. Used for wine list descriptions and staff training.']
  varietal varchar [note: 'Grape variety or blend composition (e.g., Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Bordeaux Blend).']
  vintage_year varchar [note: 'Year the grapes were harvested. Critical for vintage-level tracking and valuation. Non-vintage wines may use a standard code (e.g., 9999).']
  wine_list_category varchar [note: 'Classification for wine list organization and guest navigation. Supports menu design and pricing strategy.']
  wine_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the wine as it appears on the label and wine list.']
  wine_rating_score varchar [note: 'Professional critic rating or score (e.g., Wine Spectator, Robert Parker). Typically on a 100-point scale. Used for marketing and wine list positioning.']
  wine_type varchar [note: 'Classification of wine by style and production method.']

  Note: 'Master inventory record for property wine cellars and premium beverage collections requiring vintage-level tracking and sommelier-driven lifecycle management. Captures wine name, producer, vintage year, varietal, appellation/region, bin location, quantity on hand, quantity reserved, purchase cost, selling price (bottle and glass), par level, reorder quantity, tasting notes, food pairing recommendations, and sommelier notes. Supports cellar management, wine list curation, by-the-glass program management, and premium beverage cost control. Distinct from general F&B inventory due to vintage tracking, bin management, and sommelier-driven curation lifecycle that general inventory workflows cannot support.'
}

Table fnb.food_safety_inspection {
  food_safety_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the food safety inspection record. Primary key.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action. Business justification: Food safety inspections requiring corrective actions must link to compliance.corrective_action for CAPA tracking, verification of completion, and regulatory follow-up. Natural 1:N where inspection ref']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific F&B outlet or kitchen facility inspected (restaurant, bar, banquet kitchen, room service kitchen, etc.).']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the inspection took place.']
  responsible_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Health department inspections require documented manager accountability for corrective actions and regulatory compliance. Essential for OSHA/health authority reporting, performance reviews, and legal ']
  areas_inspected varchar [note: 'List or description of specific areas, zones, or stations within the outlet or kitchen that were inspected (e.g., prep area, cold storage, dishwashing station, dining room).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the outlet or facility following the inspection (compliant, non-compliant, conditional compliance, pending review).']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which all required corrective actions were completed and verified.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all corrective actions must be completed to achieve compliance.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of corrective action implementation (not started, in progress, completed, verified, overdue).']
  corrective_actions_required varchar [note: 'Detailed description of corrective actions that must be taken to address identified violations and achieve compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the system.']
  critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical violations identified during the inspection. Critical violations pose immediate health risks (e.g., improper food temperatures, cross-contamination, pest infestation).']
  haccp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet or facility is in compliance with HACCP principles based on this inspection (True/False).']
  inspection_checklist_used varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the standardized inspection checklist or audit protocol used during the inspection.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the food safety inspection was conducted.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the inspection in minutes, from start to completion.']
  inspection_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade or categorical rating assigned based on the inspection score (e.g., A, B, C, Pass, Fail, Satisfactory, Unsatisfactory).']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments recorded by the inspector during the inspection.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number assigned to this inspection for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  inspection_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full inspection report document stored in the document management system.']
  inspection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score or rating assigned to the inspection, typically on a scale defined by the inspecting authority (e.g., 0-100).']
  inspection_score_scale varchar [note: 'Description of the scoring scale used for this inspection (e.g., \'0-100 scale\', \'A-F letter grade\', \'Pass/Fail\').']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection (scheduled, in progress, completed, failed, passed, pending corrective action).']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'The category or type of inspection conducted (internal audit, health department inspection, ISO 22000 audit, HACCP review, pest control inspection, fire safety inspection, third-party audit). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_audit|health_department|iso_22000_audit|haccp_review|pest_control|fire_safety|third_party_audit — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspector_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or license number of the inspector, if applicable.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or lead inspector who conducted the inspection.']
  inspector_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency the inspector represents (e.g., local health department, ISO certification body, internal audit team, pest control vendor).']
  iso_22000_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outlet or facility is in compliance with ISO 22000 Food Safety Management System standards based on this inspection (True/False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  non_critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of non-critical violations identified during the inspection. Non-critical violations do not pose immediate health risks but require correction (e.g., minor equipment maintenance, documentation gaps).']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspection results and corrective action requirements were formally communicated to the responsible manager or property management.']
  permit_number_verified varchar [note: 'Health permit or food service license number that was verified or reviewed during the inspection.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body under whose jurisdiction this inspection was conducted (e.g., County Health Department, State Food Safety Division, ISO Certification Body).']
  reinspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up reinspection is required to verify corrective actions (True/False).']
  reinspection_scheduled_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the follow-up reinspection, if required.']
  violations_summary varchar [note: 'Textual summary of all violations identified during the inspection, including descriptions and locations.']

  Note: 'Food safety inspection and audit records for F&B outlets and kitchen facilities, capturing inspection date, outlet or kitchen area inspected, inspection type (internal audit, health department inspection, ISO 22000 audit, HACCP review, pest control inspection), inspector name, inspection score, critical violations count, non-critical violations count, corrective actions required, corrective action due date, corrective action completion date, and overall compliance status. Supports ISO 22000 compliance, regulatory reporting, and food safety management.'
}

Table fnb.waste_log {
  waste_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for waste_log']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Waste must be allocated to responsible cost centers for variance analysis and cost control. Waste_log links to outlet/revenue_center but needs cost_center for expense allocation, enabling managers to ']
  environmental_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_compliance. Business justification: Waste disposal tracking must link to environmental compliance permits for regulatory reporting, waste diversion metrics, and sustainability audits. Natural 1:N where waste log references the environme']
  fnb_inventory_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.inventory_item. Business justification: Waste log currently has waste_item_name and waste_item_code as strings but no FK to inventory_item master. Normalizing to inventory_item enables accurate waste cost tracking, inventory variance analys']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the F&B outlet or kitchen where the waste occurred.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Waste posts to specific expense GL accounts for P&L impact tracking. Direct ledger link enables automated waste expense posting, supports food cost variance analysis, and ensures accurate financial re']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.menu_item. Business justification: Waste can occur at finished menu item level (prepared dish not served, returned by guest, expired buffet item). This FK enables tracking waste by menu item for menu engineering analysis and portion co']
  primary_waste_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who recorded the waste event.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the waste event occurred.']
  revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue center associated with the waste event for USALI reporting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the wasted item, used for traceability and recall management.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action taken in response to the waste event to prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the waste log record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the waste cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method used to dispose of the waste (landfill, compost, recycling, donation, waste-to-energy, incineration) for sustainability reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the wasted item, if applicable, in format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  food_category varchar [note: 'The food category of the wasted item (protein, dairy, produce, bakery, dry goods, beverage, condiment, prepared dish) for detailed cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|dairy|produce|bakery|dry_goods|beverage|condiment|prepared_dish — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  health_safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waste event was associated with a health or safety incident requiring regulatory reporting.']
  iso_22000_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waste event was handled in compliance with ISO 22000 food safety standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the waste log record was last modified, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  meal_period varchar [note: 'The meal period or service type during which the waste occurred (breakfast, lunch, dinner, banquet, room service, bar).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the waste event, providing supplementary context.']
  quantity_wasted decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of the item wasted, expressed in the unit of measure for the item.']
  recording_staff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who recorded the waste event.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The storage location (e.g., walk-in cooler, dry storage, bar) where the wasted item was stored prior to waste.']
  sustainability_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this waste event has been flagged for sustainability impact reporting or waste reduction initiatives.']
  total_waste_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total cost of the waste event, calculated as quantity wasted multiplied by unit cost.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of the wasted item, used to calculate total waste cost.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the wasted quantity (kg, lb, oz, g, liter, gallon, each, portion, case, bottle). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|oz|g|liter|gallon|each|portion|case|bottle — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'The category of waste event: food spoilage, over-production, plate waste (customer leftovers), breakage, expired stock, or preparation error.']
  waste_date date [note: 'The date on which the waste event was recorded, in format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  waste_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or reason for the waste event, providing context for operational improvement and root cause analysis.']
  waste_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the waste log entry in the review and approval workflow (recorded, reviewed, approved, disputed, closed).']
  waste_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the waste event occurred, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  waste_type varchar [note: 'The type of waste: food, beverage, supplies, or packaging.']

  Note: 'Food and beverage waste and spoilage log capturing daily waste events by outlet and kitchen, recording waste item, waste category (food spoilage, over-production, plate waste, breakage, expired stock), quantity wasted, unit cost, total waste cost, waste reason, meal period, outlet, and recording staff. Supports food cost variance analysis, sustainability reporting, waste reduction initiatives, and ISO 22000 food safety compliance. Distinct from stock transactions as it captures the reason and category of waste for operational improvement.'
}

Table fnb.fnb_supply_agreement {
  fnb_supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fnb_supply_agreement']
  fnb_inventory_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the F&B inventory item being supplied under this agreement']
  supply_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this supply agreement record. Primary key.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor supplying the item under this agreement']
  agreement_end_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement expires or was terminated. Null for ongoing agreements. Used for contract renewal planning and vendor relationship lifecycle management.']
  agreement_start_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement became effective. Used for contract management and historical tracking of vendor-item relationships.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this supply agreement. Active agreements are available for procurement. Inactive or suspended agreements are not used for new orders but retained for historical reference.']
  last_purchase_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual unit cost from the most recent purchase order with this vendor for this item. Used for cost variance analysis and price trend tracking. Historical data point for this specific supply relationship.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor for this item. Used for supplier performance tracking and relationship management. Specific to this vendor-item pair.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from purchase order submission to delivery for this vendor-item combination. Critical for inventory planning and reorder point calculations. Varies by vendor.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this vendor for this item. Vendor-specific constraint that affects procurement batch sizing and inventory holding costs.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred/primary supplier for this item among multiple sourcing options. Used to prioritize vendors in automated procurement workflows while maintaining multi-sourcing capability.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Negotiated unit cost for this item from this specific vendor. Used for cost comparison across vendors and procurement decision-making. Varies by vendor for the same item.']
  vendor_item_code varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s unique identifier for this item. Used for purchase order line item matching and invoice reconciliation. This code is specific to the vendor-item combination.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual supply relationship between F&B inventory items and vendors. It captures vendor-specific sourcing terms, pricing, lead times, and purchase history for each item-vendor combination. Each record links one inventory item to one vendor with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific supply relationship, enabling multi-sourcing strategies, cost optimization, and supply continuity management.. Existence Justification: In hotel F&B operations, inventory items are routinely sourced from multiple vendors to ensure supply continuity, optimize costs, and manage risk. A single item (e.g., olive oil) may have a primary supplier, a backup supplier, and spot market vendors, each with different pricing, lead times, and minimum order quantities. Conversely, each vendor supplies hundreds of different F&B items. This is an operational multi-sourcing strategy actively managed by procurement teams.'
}

Table fnb.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for storage_location']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Storage locations belong to specific F&B outlets. The storage_location table has outlet_id (BIGINT) which is clearly intended to be a FK to fnb_outlet but uses non-standard naming. Standardizing to fn']
  parent_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on storage_location (parent_storage_location_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property where this storage location is physically located.']
  responsible_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically F&B manager or chef) responsible for managing inventory in this storage location.']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Security classification defining who has authorized access to this storage location for inventory control and loss prevention.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this storage location became operational and available for inventory storage.']
  active_until_date date [note: 'Date until which this storage location is scheduled to remain operational. Null for indefinite operation.']
  allergen_segregation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location requires allergen segregation protocols to prevent cross-contamination per food safety standards.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode identifier used for scanning and tracking inventory movements to and from this storage location.']
  building_name varchar [note: 'Name of the building or structure where the storage location is situated within the property.']
  capacity_cubic_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity of the location measured in cubic meters for space planning and inventory management.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which inventory stored in this location is allocated for USALI departmental reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when this storage location was permanently decommissioned and removed from active inventory use.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for decommissioning this storage location (e.g., renovation, consolidation, safety concerns).']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Floor or level designation where the storage location is located (e.g., Basement, Ground, 1st Floor).']
  hazmat_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is approved for storing hazardous materials such as cleaning chemicals per safety regulations.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required frequency in days between mandatory inspections for this storage location per food safety compliance requirements.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent food safety or health inspection conducted for this storage location.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the storage location used in inventory and procurement systems.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the storage location (e.g., Main Kitchen Dry Storage, Bar Cooler 1, Banquet Freezer).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location based on storage conditions and purpose.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory food safety or health inspection of this storage location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or remarks about this storage location.']
  primary_use_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the primary intended use and contents of this storage location for operational reference.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier for automated inventory tracking and access control at this storage location.']
  room_number varchar [note: 'Specific room or area number designation for the storage location within the building.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location in the inventory management system.']
  storage_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of items stored in this location for inventory segregation and food safety compliance.']
  target_temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum target temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled storage locations to maintain food safety standards.']
  target_temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum target temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled storage locations to maintain food safety standards.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location requires temperature control for food safety compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for storage_location. Referenced by source_location_id.'
}

Table fnb.physical_count {
  physical_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for physical_count']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who provided final approval of the physical count, typically a department head or controller.']
  counter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who performed the physical count, ensuring accountability and traceability.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Physical counts are performed at specific F&B outlets. The physical_count table has outlet_id (BIGINT) which is clearly intended to be a FK to fnb_outlet but uses non-standard naming. Standardizing to']
  parent_count_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original physical count if this record represents a recount or follow-up count, establishing the audit trail.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel, resort, or vacation property where the physical count is conducted.']
  recount_physical_count_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on physical_count (recount_physical_count_id)']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved the physical count results.']
  adjustment_posted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory adjustments resulting from this physical count have been posted to the general ledger and inventory system (True if posted).']
  adjustment_posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the inventory adjustments were posted to the financial and inventory systems.']
  adjustment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory adjustments are required to reconcile the physical count with the system book quantities (True if variances exist and adjustments are needed).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical count was officially approved and accepted into the inventory system.']
  compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the physical count procedures and results have been verified for compliance with ISO 22000 food safety standards and USALI reporting requirements (True if verified).']
  count_date date [note: 'The business date (day-level) on which the physical count was performed, used for period-end reporting and financial close alignment.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the physical count activity was completed, marking the end of the inventory freeze period.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the physical count: manual (visual count and paper recording), barcode_scan (handheld scanner), rfid (radio frequency identification), weight_scale (automated weighing), hybrid (combination of methods).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the physical count event, typically formatted as PC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for traceability and audit purposes.']
  count_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason or trigger for this physical count event (e.g., Month-end close, Annual audit, Suspected theft, System migration, New outlet opening).']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the physical count activity began, marking the start of the inventory freeze period.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the physical count event: scheduled (planned but not started), in_progress (counting underway), completed (count finished, pending review), approved (verified and accepted), rejected (discrepancies require recount), cancelled (count aborted).']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical count: full (complete inventory), partial (selected items), cycle (scheduled rotation), spot (random check), variance_recount (follow-up after discrepancy), opening (new outlet), closing (outlet shutdown).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this physical count record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this physical count (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal year and month (YYYY-MM format) to which this physical count is assigned for accounting and variance analysis purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this physical count record was most recently updated, tracking all changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding the physical count, including explanations for variances, operational issues encountered, or special circumstances.']
  pos_system_integration varchar [note: 'The POS system from which inventory consumption data is sourced for variance analysis: oracle_micros (Oracle Hospitality MICROS), simphony (Oracle MICROS Simphony), opera (Oracle OPERA Property Management), manual (no system integration), other (third-party system).']
  recount_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a recount was required, such as variance exceeds threshold, counting errors detected, or supervisor challenge.']
  recount_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount is required due to significant variances, procedural errors, or supervisor review findings (True if recount needed).']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical count was originally scheduled to occur, used for planning and compliance tracking.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The specific storage area, storeroom, or inventory location where the physical count was performed (e.g., Main Kitchen Dry Store, Bar Cellar, Banquet Freezer).']
  total_items_counted varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct inventory items (SKUs) included in this physical count event.']
  total_variance_items varchar [note: 'The number of inventory items where the physical count differed from the system book quantity, requiring investigation.']
  total_variance_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The aggregate monetary value of all inventory variances (positive and negative) identified during the physical count, used for shrinkage and loss analysis.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The variance value expressed as a percentage of the total book inventory value, used as a key performance indicator for inventory accuracy.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for physical_count. Referenced by physical_count_id.'
}

Business justification: Guest complaints about room cleanliness generate service cases requiring reference to specific housekeeping assignment. Real business process: Service recovery tracking, attendant accountability, and ']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the attendant completed work on this assignment, captured from mobile device or PMS entry.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the attendant began work on this assignment, captured from mobile device or PMS entry.']
  allergy_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of guest allergy or sensitivity flags that require special cleaning products or procedures (e.g., \'fragrance-free\', \'latex-free\', \'pet dander\').']
  amenity_replenishment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest amenities (toiletries, coffee, water, etc.) were replenished during this assignment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The business date on which the housekeeping assignment is scheduled to be performed.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the housekeeping assignment, typically formatted as property-date-sequence.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'The type of housekeeping service to be performed: stayover (occupied room refresh), departure (checkout cleaning), deep clean (thorough cleaning), turndown (evening service), VIP prep (special preparation), or inspection (quality check).']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the assignment was cancelled, if applicable (e.g., \'guest extended stay\', \'room out of order\', \'early checkout\').']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the assignment: assigned (not yet started), in_progress (attendant working), completed (attendant finished), inspected (supervisor approved), rejected (failed inspection), or cancelled (assignment voided).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the system.']
  dnd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has activated the Do Not Disturb indicator at the time of assignment. True if DND is active; attendant should skip or defer service.']
  estimated_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned timestamp when the attendant is expected to complete work on this assignment.']
  estimated_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned timestamp when the attendant is expected to begin work on this assignment.']
