Enables property-level P&L reporting, RevPAR contribution analysis, and GOPPAR tracking. Supports consolidated financial statements across the portfolio. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Profit Center Accounting.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry transaction.']
  allocation_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost allocation or expense distribution run that generated this journal entry, if applicable. Null for non-allocation entries. Used to track automated allocation postings.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to cost centers for departmental accounting. The cost_center STRING attribute should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cost ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fixed asset or FF&E item to which this journal entry is related, if applicable. Used for asset acquisition, depreciation, and disposal tracking.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Journal entries post to GL accounts in the chart of accounts. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master data for financi']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.payroll_run. Business justification: Payroll journal entries must reference the source payroll run for audit trail, GL-to-payroll reconciliation, and SOX compliance - enables auditors to trace GL postings back to source payroll calculati']
  primary_journal_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the person who created this journal entry in the system. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entries are allocated to profit centers for revenue-generating business unit tracking. The profit_center STRING attribute should be replaced with a proper FK to profit_center.profit_center_id ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property or resort to which this journal entry is attributed. Enables property-level P&L and financial reporting.']
  quaternary_journal_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the person who last modified this journal entry record. Part of the change tracking audit trail.']
  recurring_entry_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recurring journal entry template from which this entry was generated, if applicable. Null for non-recurring entries. Used to track automated periodic postings.']
  revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Manual journal entries often adjust revenue center-specific accounts (accruals, corrections, allocations). JE already links to cost_center and profit_center; adding revenue_center enables direct F&B d']
  tertiary_journal_posted_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the person who executed the final posting of this journal entry to the general ledger. Null if not yet posted. Part of SOX segregation of duties controls.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this journal entry was approved for posting. Null if not yet approved. Part of the approval workflow audit trail.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Additional audit trail notes or comments added by approvers, auditors, or system administrators for compliance and review purposes.']
  capex_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry relates to capital expenditure (CapEx) or furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) investments. True for CapEx/FF&E entries, False for operating expenditure (OpEx).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code within the enterprise structure to which this journal entry belongs. Used for consolidated financial reporting and intercompany elimination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this journal entry record was first created in the general ledger system. Part of the complete audit trail for SOX compliance.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total credit amount for this journal entry in the functional currency. Must balance with debit amount per double-entry accounting principles.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total debit amount for this journal entry in the functional currency. Must balance with credit amount per double-entry accounting principles.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry for reference and audit trail purposes. Typically follows format JE followed by numeric sequence.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry by its business purpose: standard (regular postings), allocation (cost distribution), intercompany (cross-entity transactions), recurring (template-based periodic entries), accrual (period-end adjustments), reversal (correction entries).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to functional currency at the time of posting. Null if transaction and functional currencies are the same.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (typically month 1-12 or period 1-13 for year-end adjustments) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry is posted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this journal entry is posted, derived from the posting date and the organization\'s fiscal calendar.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency in which this journal entry is recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction between legal entities within the enterprise. True for intercompany entries requiring elimination in consolidated reporting.']
  intercompany_partner_code varchar [note: 'The company code of the intercompany partner entity for intercompany transactions. Used for intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries in consolidated financial statements.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the business purpose and nature of this journal entry. Provides context for auditors and financial analysts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this journal entry record was last modified. Tracks all changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Null if not yet posted. This is the system processing time, distinct from the posting_date which is the accounting date.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date when this journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the business event date that determines which fiscal period the entry impacts, distinct from the creation or approval timestamps.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the journal entry in the approval and posting workflow. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval to final posting in the general ledger.']
  reference_text varchar [note: 'Additional reference information or notes related to this journal entry, such as supporting documentation references, approval notes, or special instructions.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal entry, False otherwise.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the originating source transaction document (e.g., invoice number, payment document, POS check number) that triggered this journal entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or module from which this journal entry was created or interfaced (e.g., SAP FI for manual entries, OPERA AR for accounts receivable postings, MICROS POS for point-of-sale revenue).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code or tax jurisdiction code applicable to this journal entry, if the entry involves tax-related postings (sales tax, VAT, occupancy tax, etc.).']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction currency if different from functional currency. Used for foreign exchange tracking.']

  Note: 'General Ledger journal entry transactional record capturing all financial postings including accruals, adjustments, intercompany transfers, expense allocations, and period-end closing entries. Each entry carries debit/credit totals, posting date, fiscal period, document type (standard, allocation, intercompany, recurring), reference to source transaction, and full audit trail (created by, approved by, posting timestamp). Supports SOX audit trail requirements, intercompany elimination tracking, and GAAP/IFRS compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA GL.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular financial transaction analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each journal entry line is allocated to a specific cost center for departmental accounting. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable join']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent journal entry header. Links this line to the overall journal entry batch and posting context.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Each journal entry line posts to a specific GL account. The gl_account_code and gl_account_name STRING attributes should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to the autho']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each journal entry line is allocated to a specific profit center for revenue-generating business unit tracking. The profit_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to profit_center.profi']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: GL line items for vendor expenses (utilities, maintenance, services) need direct vendor attribution for spend analytics, vendor performance tracking, and USALI departmental cost reporting by vendor.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Line item amount converted to the corporate group reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial statements and EBITDA reporting.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Line item amount converted to the property or entity local currency. Used for property-level P&L and GOP calculation.']
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Line item amount in the original transaction currency. Supports multi-currency operations for international properties.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Fixed asset number for CapEx and FF&E line items. Links GL entry to asset master for depreciation and asset management.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'Assignment field for additional sorting and grouping. Used for payment clearing, reconciliation, and sub-ledger matching.']
  baseline_payment_date date [note: 'Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Used for AP aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area classification for segment reporting. Used for brand, region, or business unit financial consolidation.']
  capex_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item represents capital expenditure (CapEx or FF&E). Used for asset capitalization and depreciation tracking.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when this open item was cleared. Critical for cash application and AR/AP aging analysis.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number that cleared this open item. Used for payment reconciliation and AR/AP management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this journal entry line record was created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  customer_code varchar [note: 'Customer or guest account code for AR line items. Links GL entry to customer master for receivables management.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) entry. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and GL balancing.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to local or group currency. Critical for multi-currency financial accuracy.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for cost-of-sales and functional expense reporting per USALI standards.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the line item. Provides business context for the transaction for audit and analysis purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the journal entry. Determines the order of line items for display and audit purposes.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency amount. Typically the property operating currency.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this journal entry line record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and SOX compliance.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms code for AP/AR line items. Determines due date calculation and discount eligibility.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key that controls the account type and debit/credit behavior. Standard SAP GL posting control mechanism.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Project or work breakdown structure (WBS) element for capital projects, renovations, or PIP (Property Improvement Plan) tracking.']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Property identifier for multi-property financial consolidation. Enables property-level P&L, GOP, and NOI reporting.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number (invoice, receipt, PO). Links GL entry to source operational transaction.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a reversal entry. Used for error correction and period-end adjustments.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Maintains audit trail for corrections.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Segment code for IFRS segment reporting. Supports brand, geography, or service-line financial disclosure.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is subject to SOX financial controls. Required for publicly traded hospitality companies.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item. Supports tax reporting and compliance requirements.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to this line item. Determines tax treatment for VAT, sales tax, occupancy tax, or other jurisdiction-specific taxes.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'Trading partner or intercompany entity code. Enables intercompany elimination and consolidation for multi-entity groups.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction amount. Enables multi-currency financial reporting.']
  usali_department_code varchar [note: 'USALI-compliant department code for hospitality-specific P&L reporting (rooms, F&B, GOP, undistributed expenses).']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a GL journal entry, capturing the specific account, cost center, profit center, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction and local currency, tax code, and business area. Enables granular financial analysis at the account-line level. Required for USALI departmental reporting and SOX controls. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA GL line items.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP invoices are allocated to cost centers for departmental accounting per USALI standards. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joini']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise fee invoices (royalty, marketing, reservation fees) reference specific franchise agreements for rate validation and compliance tracking. Real AP processing verifies fees against agreement te']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Three-way match validation requires direct link from invoice to goods receipt for quantity/quality verification, critical for F&B perishables and FF&E capital purchases before payment processing.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: AP invoices post to GL accounts in the chart of accounts. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master data (account_name, ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property to which this invoice is assigned. Links to the property master.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order number that authorized this procurement. May be null for non-PO invoices.']
  revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Outlet-specific AP invoices (supplies, equipment, services) must allocate to revenue centers for departmental P&L accuracy. Enables USALI-compliant expense allocation, supports outlet-level profitabil']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master in procurement domain.']
  ap_invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the goods or services covered by this invoice. Provides business context for the procurement.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the invoice in the multi-level approval workflow. Supports SOX financial controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved the invoice. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was approved for payment. Key milestone in the AP workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount or negotiated discount amount applied to the invoice.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was flagged as disputed. Null if no dispute exists.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for disputing the invoice (e.g., pricing discrepancy, quantity mismatch, quality issue). Populated when invoice_status is disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is due to the vendor per the payment terms.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the expense type. OpEx (Operating Expenditure) for recurring costs, CapEx (Capital Expenditure) for long-term assets, FF&E (Furniture Fixtures and Equipment) for property improvements.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this invoice is assigned.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this invoice is assigned for financial reporting purposes.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments. Base amount for goods or services procured.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. Principal business event timestamp for the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the vendor. Business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the AP workflow. Tracks approval and payment state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|rejected|paid|cancelled|on_hold|disputed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice type. Standard invoices represent normal purchases; credit memos represent vendor credits or returns.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a recurring invoice (e.g., monthly utilities, annual subscriptions). True if recurring, False otherwise.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final payable amount after applying taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount due to the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the invoice. Used for special instructions, exceptions, or internal communication.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as net_amount minus paid_amount.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount paid against this invoice to date. May be partial or full payment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date the payment was issued to the vendor. Null if invoice is unpaid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used to pay the invoice (check, ACH, wire transfer, credit card, electronic funds transfer).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number or transaction ID for the payment made against this invoice (e.g., check number, ACH trace number).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms negotiated with the vendor (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines discount and due date calculation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the general ledger. May differ from invoice date for period-end accruals.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was received by the property or corporate AP department.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice (sales tax, VAT, GST, etc.).']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match validation (PO, goods receipt, invoice). Ensures invoice accuracy and prevents duplicate payments.']
  vendor_tax_number varchar [note: 'The tax identification number (TIN, EIN, VAT number) of the vendor. Required for 1099 reporting and tax compliance.']

  Note: 'Accounts Payable invoice master representing vendor invoices received for goods and services procured by the property (FF&E, OTA commissions, utilities, F&B supplies). Tracks invoice number, vendor, PO reference, invoice date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, and approval status. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AP module. Supports CPOR analysis and OpEx management.'
}

Table finance.ap_payment {
  ap_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable payment transaction. Primary key.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AP invoice being paid by this payment transaction.']
  original_payment_ap_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment transaction if this is a reversal or correction payment.']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch payment run that generated this payment, used for grouping payments processed together.']
  primary_ap_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which this payment was disbursed.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property from which this payment originates.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor receiving this payment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status indicating whether the payment has been authorized for processing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this payment for processing, supporting SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was approved, supporting audit trail and SOX compliance requirements.']
  bank_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction fees charged by the bank or payment processor for executing this payment.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount converted to the company base currency using the exchange rate for consolidated reporting.']
  base_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the company base currency used for financial reporting and consolidation.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Physical check number when payment method is check, used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment cleared the bank and was confirmed as successfully processed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount or cash discount amount taken on this payment, reducing the total amount paid.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied when payment currency differs from the company base currency or invoice currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when this payment transaction is recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this payment transaction is recorded for financial reporting purposes.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting period date when this payment transaction was posted to the general ledger.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this payment record, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was last updated or modified.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount disbursed to the vendor after all discounts, withholdings, and fees are applied.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount disbursed to the vendor in the payment currency before any adjustments or fees.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was issued or executed. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or memo providing additional context about the payment purpose or details.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used to disburse funds to the vendor (e.g., ACH, wire transfer, check, EFT).']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique payment document number assigned by the payment system or payment run.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction indicating its processing state.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms applied to this transaction (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30) indicating discount and due date conditions.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction indicating its purpose or nature within the AP lifecycle.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or transaction ID provided by the bank or payment processor for tracking and reconciliation.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment has been reversed or cancelled after initial processing.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the payment was reversed, supporting audit and reconciliation processes.']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment amount is debited from the company bank account and becomes effective for cash flow purposes.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount withheld from the payment as required by tax regulations, reducing the net amount paid to vendor.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this payment record, supporting audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Accounts Payable payment transaction recording disbursements made to vendors against AP invoices. Captures payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check), bank account, cleared amount, exchange rate for foreign currency payments, and payment run reference. Supports cash flow management and vendor reconciliation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AP payment runs.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record. Primary key.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account for direct-bill invoices. Null for non-corporate invoices.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile associated with this invoice. Null for non-guest invoices.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: AR invoices post to GL accounts for revenue recognition. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master data for financial re']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where charges were incurred and invoice was generated.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary reservation associated with this invoice. Null for non-room invoices.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total post-billing adjustments including service recovery credits, billing corrections, and dispute resolutions. Can be positive or negative.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorization of invoice age for accounts receivable aging reports and collection workflow assignment.']
  ancillary_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total ancillary service charges including spa, parking, resort fees, minibar, laundry, and other guest services.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address for the entity being invoiced.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (suite, apartment, building).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for invoice delivery and billing correspondence.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for billing inquiries and collection contact.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity being billed (guest, company, agency, or OTA partner).']
  billing_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity being billed. Used for segmentation and credit policy application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporate|travel_agency|ota|group|government|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of collection efforts for overdue invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice.']
  days_outstanding varchar [note: 'Number of days since invoice date. Used for aging analysis and collection prioritization.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discounts applied to this invoice including promotional discounts, loyalty rewards, and negotiated rate adjustments.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is currently under dispute by the billing entity.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for invoice dispute if dispute_flag is true.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is expected based on payment terms. Used for aging and collection prioritization.']
  event_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total event and meeting charges including space rental, audio-visual, and catering for Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions (MICE).']
  fnb_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total food and beverage charges on this invoice from restaurants, bars, room service, and banquets.']
  folio_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique folio number assigned to this invoice in the Property Management System (PMS). Used for guest and accounting reference.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was issued. Used for revenue recognition and aging calculations.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The official invoice number used for accounting and legal purposes. May differ from folio number for consolidated billing.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|sent|partially_paid|paid|overdue|disputed|written_off|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on the billing entity type. Determines billing workflow and collection procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guest|corporate|group|travel_agency|ota|event|direct_bill — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this invoice record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments related to this invoice for internal reference and customer communication.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this invoice (total_amount minus paid_amount). Used for aging and collection prioritization.']
  paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid against this invoice to date. Updated as payments are received and applied.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument type used or expected for this invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|bank_transfer|check|cash|direct_bill|virtual_card|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for this invoice. Determines due date calculation and collection procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: due_on_receipt|net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date revenue was recognized for this invoice per GAAP/IFRS revenue recognition standards (ASC 606 / IFRS 15).']
  room_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room and accommodation charges on this invoice. Key component of Average Daily Rate (ADR) and Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) calculations.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total service charges and gratuities applied to this invoice, typically for F&B and event services.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'The last date of service covered by this invoice (e.g., check-out date for room charges).']
  service_start_date date [note: 'The first date of service covered by this invoice (e.g., check-in date for room charges).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all revenue line items before taxes and fees. Used for tax calculation base.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total taxes applied to this invoice including occupancy tax, sales tax, VAT, and local jurisdiction taxes.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final invoice total including all charges, taxes, fees, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount due from the billing entity.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was written off if write_off_flag is true.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice has been written off as uncollectible bad debt.']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for writing off this invoice.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this invoice record.']

  Note: 'Accounts Receivable invoice representing charges billed to guests, corporate accounts, travel agencies, and OTA partners for hotel stays, F&B, events, and ancillary services. Tracks folio number, billing entity, invoice date, due date, total charges, taxes, outstanding balance, and collection status. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS AR module and SAP S/4HANA AR. Supports ADR and TRevPAR revenue recognition.'
}

Table finance.ar_payment {
  ar_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable payment transaction. Primary key.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AR invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to the originating invoice for reconciliation and revenue recognition.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account making the payment. Applicable for direct bill corporate payments.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR payments are allocated to cost centers for departmental cash receipt tracking. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cos']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cashier or staff member who processed the payment. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile who made the payment. Applicable for individual guest payments.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: AR payments post to GL accounts for cash receipt and accounts receivable tracking. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account ma']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA partner remitting payment. Applicable for OTA settlement payments.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the payment was received. Supports property-level financial reporting and reconciliation.']
  reversed_payment_ar_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment that this payment reverses. Creates audit trail for payment corrections and chargebacks.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the payment amount that has been applied to specific AR invoices. May differ from payment amount if payment is partially applied or unapplied.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code provided by payment processor or card issuer confirming approval of the payment transaction. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the bank or financial institution for check, wire transfer, or ACH payments. Used for reconciliation and tracking.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment amount converted to the property base currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  base_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the property base currency. Standard currency for property-level financial reporting.']
  card_last_four varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the payment card number. Used for payment identification while maintaining PCI DSS compliance.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'The credit or debit card brand used for the payment. Applicable only for card-based payment methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|diners|unionpay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment card. Used for verification and reconciliation purposes.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The check number for check-based payments. Used for tracking and reconciliation with bank deposits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was deposited to the property bank account. Used for cash flow tracking and bank reconciliation.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the payment currency to the property base currency. Used for multi-currency payment processing.']
  is_advance_deposit boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment is an advance deposit received prior to guest arrival or service delivery. Used for revenue recognition timing.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment represents a refund to the guest or customer. True if refund, False if collection.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking per SOX requirements.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount received after deducting transaction fees. Represents actual cash collected by the property.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount received in the payment transaction, in the payment currency. Represents the total collected before any adjustments or fees.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or touchpoint through which the payment was received. Includes front desk, online portal, mobile app, call center, self-service kiosk, or mail.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount. Indicates the currency in which the payment was received.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The business date on which the payment was received or posted. Used for revenue recognition and cash flow reporting per USALI standards.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used to make the payment. Includes credit card, debit card, cash, check, wire transfer, ACH, or direct bill settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|check|wire_transfer|ach|direct_bill — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the payment transaction. Used to capture special instructions, exceptions, or additional context.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique payment reference number used for tracking and reconciliation. Generated by PMS or financial system.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks progression from authorization through settlement or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|settled|failed|reversed|refunded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment transaction was recorded in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number provided by the payer or payment processor. Used for cross-referencing with external systems and statements.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for payment reversal if the payment status is reversed. Includes chargeback, error correction, guest dispute, etc.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment processor settled funds to the property account. Used for reconciliation with processor settlement reports.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the cashier shift during which the payment was processed. Used for shift reconciliation and cash drawer balancing.']
  transaction_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fee charged by payment processor or financial institution for the transaction. Includes credit card merchant fees, wire transfer fees, etc.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor or gateway. Used for tracking and reconciliation with processor settlement reports.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the payment amount that has not yet been applied to specific AR invoices. Represents credit on account or overpayment.']

  Note: 'Accounts Receivable payment transaction recording collections received from guests, corporate accounts, and OTA partners against AR invoices. Captures payment date, payment method (credit card, direct bill, OTA settlement), amount received, currency, exchange rate, and application to specific invoices. Supports revenue recognition and cash management. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering and SAP S/4HANA AR.'
}

Table finance.finance_budget {
  finance_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the finance budget record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial budgets are allocated to cost centers for departmental budget planning. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cos']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Financial budgets are planned for specific GL accounts in the chart of accounts. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account mast']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Financial budgets are allocated to profit centers for revenue-generating business unit budget planning. The profit_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to profit_center.profit_center']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this budget applies. Links to property master data.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned or forecasted monetary amount for this budget line item. Represents the target financial value for the specified GL account, cost center, and fiscal period combination.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or committee who approved this budget. Supports SOX financial controls and audit trail.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget was formally approved. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the budget line: revenue (rooms, F&B, events), operating expense (utilities, supplies), labor expense (wages, benefits), capital expenditure (CapEx), or furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) reserve.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the budget (e.g., \'FY2024 Annual Operating Budget\', \'Q2 2024 Reforecast\').']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this budget document. Used for reference in management reports and approvals.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting approval), under review (being evaluated), approved (finalized), active (in use), closed (period ended), or superseded (replaced by newer version). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|active|closed|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget by planning cycle: annual operating budget, quarterly forecast, monthly budget, rolling forecast, reforecast, or capital expenditure budget.']
  budgeted_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Average Daily Rate for the budget period. Calculated as rooms revenue divided by rooms sold. Key rooms division KPI.']
  budgeted_available_rooms varchar [note: 'Forecasted total number of room nights available for sale during the budget period. Accounts for planned renovations and out-of-order rooms.']
  budgeted_covers varchar [note: 'Forecasted total number of F&B covers (guests served) for the budget period. Key volume metric for food and beverage operations.']
  budgeted_cpor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Cost Per Occupied Room for the budget period. Calculated as total departmental expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures cost efficiency.']
  budgeted_ebitda decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted EBITDA for the budget period. Represents operating profitability before financing and non-cash charges.']
  budgeted_events_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total events and meetings revenue for the budget period. Includes MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) revenue.']
  budgeted_fnb_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total food and beverage revenue for the budget period. Includes restaurant, bar, banquet, and room service revenue.']
  budgeted_gop decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted Gross Operating Profit for the budget period. Calculated as total revenue minus departmental expenses and undistributed operating expenses. Key profitability metric per USALI.']
  budgeted_goppar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room for the budget period. Calculated as GOP divided by available rooms. Measures profitability efficiency.']
  budgeted_labor_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total labor costs including wages, salaries, benefits, and payroll taxes for the budget period.']
  budgeted_noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted Net Operating Income for the budget period. Represents property-level profitability after all operating expenses but before debt service and taxes.']
  budgeted_occupancy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted occupancy rate as a percentage for the budget period. Calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Key demand metric.']
  budgeted_operating_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total operating expenses excluding labor for the budget period. Includes utilities, supplies, maintenance, and other departmental expenses.']
  budgeted_other_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from other sources (spa, parking, telecommunications, etc.) for the budget period.']
  budgeted_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Revenue Per Available Room for the budget period. Calculated as rooms revenue divided by available rooms, or ADR multiplied by occupancy rate. Primary revenue performance metric.']
  budgeted_room_nights varchar [note: 'Forecasted total number of room nights sold for the budget period. Key volume metric for rooms division.']
  budgeted_rooms_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total rooms revenue for the budget period. Key metric for rooms division performance planning.']
  budgeted_total_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total revenue across all departments for the budget period. Sum of rooms, F&B, events, and other revenue.']
  budgeted_trevpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted Total Revenue Per Available Room for the budget period. Calculated as total property revenue divided by available rooms. Measures total revenue productivity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended budgets.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this budget becomes effective and active for planning and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period within the year (e.g., \'P01\', \'P02\', \'Q1\', \'FY\'). Supports monthly, quarterly, and annual budget granularity.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget applies (e.g., 2024).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and commentary regarding budget assumptions, variances from prior periods, strategic initiatives, or special considerations.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget (e.g., \'V1.0\', \'V2.1 Reforecast\'). Supports tracking of budget revisions and reforecasts.']

  Note: 'Annual and periodic financial budget master representing planned and forecasted financial targets at both header and line-item granularity. Each budget contains line items by GL account, cost center, profit center, and fiscal period combination. Supports approved annual budgets, rolling forecasts, and reforecasts (distinguished by budget type/version). Captures budgeted revenue (rooms, F&B, events), budgeted expenses (labor, CPOR, FF&E reserve contributions), and budgeted KPIs (ADR, OCC, RevPAR, GOP, EBITDA, NOI). Enables granular budget-vs-actual and forecast-vs-actual variance analysis at the account, department, and property level. Supports USALI departmental budget reporting and management review. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Planning and Budgeting module.'
}

Table finance.budget_line {
  budget_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget line item. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_category. Business justification: Budget planning allocates spend by procurement category (F&B, housekeeping, maintenance, FF&E) for CPOR tracking, category-level variance analysis, and strategic sourcing decisions in hospitality oper']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to cost centers for departmental budget planning. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cost cen']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent budget header to which this line item belongs.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Budget lines are planned for specific GL accounts in the chart of accounts. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master da']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Budget lines for departmental operating expenses must tie to org units for operational budget ownership, manager accountability, and departmental variance reporting - enables department managers to tr']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Labor budget lines must tie to specific positions for headcount planning, position control, and budget-to-actual variance analysis - enables finance to track budgeted vs actual headcount by position a']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to profit centers for revenue-generating business unit budget planning. The profit_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to profit_center.profit_center_id t']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this budget line is planned. Enables property-level budget tracking and consolidation.']
  allocation_driver varchar [note: 'The driver or basis used for allocation if allocation_method is driver-based. Examples include room nights, square footage, headcount, or revenue percentage.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to allocate this budget line to the cost center or profit center. Supports transparency in budget allocation logic.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval status of this budget line. Supports budget workflow and SOX financial controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual who approved this budget line. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget line was approved. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the budget line. Distinguishes between revenue, operating expenses, fixed expenses, and capital expenditures per USALI standards.']
  budget_owner varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual or role responsible for this budget line. Supports accountability and budget governance.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'The type of budget this line belongs to. Supports multiple budget scenarios for planning and variance analysis.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier for this budget line. Supports multiple budget scenarios such as original budget, revised budget, forecast, or rolling forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the planned amount. Supports multi-currency budget planning and consolidation.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The department code to which this budget line is allocated. Aligns with USALI departmental structure such as Rooms, Food and Beverage, Sales and Marketing, Property Operations and Maintenance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date until which this budget line remains effective. Null indicates the budget line is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this budget line becomes effective. Supports temporal budget management and mid-year budget changes.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for which this budget line is planned. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for 13-period calendars.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this budget line is planned. Four-digit year representation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget line record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent budget. Used for ordering and referencing individual budget lines.']
  locked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line is locked from further edits. True if locked, False if editable. Supports budget freeze and SOX controls.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context or justification for this budget line. Used for management review and audit trail.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted monetary amount for this GL account, cost center, profit center, and fiscal period combination. Represents the planned expense or revenue target.']
  prior_year_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount from the prior fiscal year for the same GL account, cost center, and fiscal period. Used for year-over-year budget comparison and trend analysis.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The planned quantity associated with this budget line, if applicable. Used for volume-based budgeting such as room nights, covers, or headcount.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this budget line was loaded. Typically SAP S/4HANA or other ERP system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this budget line in the source system. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the budget quantity field. Examples include ROOM_NIGHTS, COVERS, FTE (Full-Time Equivalent), UNITS.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The planned unit price or rate used to calculate the planned amount. Derived from planned_amount divided by quantity.']
  variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance threshold percentage for this budget line. Used to flag significant budget-vs-actual variances for management review.']

  Note: 'Individual budget line item within a financial budget, capturing the planned amount for a specific GL account, cost center, profit center, and fiscal period combination. Enables granular budget-vs-actual variance analysis at the account and department level. Supports USALI departmental budget reporting and management review. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budget line items.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for the fixed asset master data product.']
  capex_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original CapEx request or approval that authorized the acquisition of this fixed asset.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixed assets are allocated to cost centers for departmental asset tracking and depreciation allocation. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Asset capitalization date and initial valuation depend on goods receipt posting for FF&E and capital improvements; required for PIP project tracking and depreciation start date determination.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Fixed assets post to GL accounts for asset capitalization and depreciation tracking. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account ']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Fixed assets (HVAC, kitchen equipment, pool systems) are physically located in specific facilities. Real asset management requires location tracking for maintenance scheduling, depreciation allocation']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or business unit where this fixed asset is located and capitalized.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the acquisition of this fixed asset.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom the fixed asset was purchased.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date since the asset was placed in service.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized cost of acquiring the fixed asset, including purchase price, installation, and directly attributable costs.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the fixed asset was acquired or placed into service for accounting purposes.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification or category within the asset class for granular reporting and analysis.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the fixed asset type. FF&E refers to Furniture, Fixtures, and Equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building|ffe|technology_system|leasehold_improvement|land|vehicle|equipment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the fixed asset.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique asset tag or identification number assigned to the fixed asset for tracking and inventory purposes.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset indicating its operational state and accounting treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_service|under_construction|retired|disposed|impaired|held_for_sale — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capex_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the CapEx request associated with this fixed asset acquisition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fixed asset record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost and financial amounts.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to calculate depreciation expense for this fixed asset.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculation begins for this fixed asset, typically the in-service date.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the fixed asset was disposed of, sold, retired, or otherwise removed from service.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the fixed asset was disposed of or removed from the asset register.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount received from the sale or disposal of the fixed asset.']
  ffe_reserve_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is eligible for FF&E reserve funding, typically used for planned replacement programs.']
  gain_loss_on_disposal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated gain or loss on disposal, computed as disposal proceeds minus net book value at disposal date.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the asset has been tested for impairment or shows indicators of impairment.']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of impairment loss recognized when the recoverable amount of the asset is less than its carrying value.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering this fixed asset, if separately insured.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service performed on the fixed asset.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Physical location or room number where the fixed asset is installed or stored within the property.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or brand of the fixed asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model number or product code of the fixed asset as designated by the manufacturer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fixed asset record was last modified or updated.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the fixed asset calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the fixed asset, including special handling, history, or other relevant information.']
  pip_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Property Improvement Plan under which this asset was acquired or renovated, if applicable.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life, not subject to depreciation.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number or unique identifier for the physical asset, if applicable.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the fixed asset in years for depreciation calculation purposes.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty for this fixed asset expires.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master representing the full lifecycle of capitalized property assets from CapEx request through acquisition, depreciation, and disposal. Covers buildings, FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures and Equipment), technology systems, and leasehold improvements. Tracks asset class, PIP (Property Improvement Plan) reference, CapEx approval status, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method and schedule, accumulated depreciation, net book value, and disposal status. Supports CapEx planning, depreciation posting to GL, EBITDA calculation (D&A add-back), FF&E reserve tracking, and IFRS/GAAP asset reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA).'
}

Table finance.tax_posting {
  tax_posting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax posting transaction record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax postings are allocated to cost centers for departmental tax expense tracking. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cos']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Tax postings post to GL accounts for tax liability and expense tracking. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master data ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile for occupancy tax and transient occupancy tax postings.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax postings reference revenue centers for tax allocation. The revenue_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK. Note: The target is profit_center because the existing FK profit_center.r']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the tax transaction originated.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation for room-related tax postings.']
  reversed_posting_tax_posting_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax posting ID that this record reverses, if applicable.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor for withholding tax postings on vendor payments.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any adjustment, correction, or reversal of the tax posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the tax amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number for this tax posting, used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  due_date date [note: 'The regulatory due date by which this tax must be filed and paid to avoid penalties.']
  exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The certificate or authorization number supporting the tax exemption claim, if applicable.']
  exemption_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this transaction qualifies for tax exemption.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date when the tax return or report was filed with the tax authority.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the tax filing and payment lifecycle: pending (not yet filed), filed (submitted to authority), paid (remitted), overdue (past due date), disputed (under review), or adjusted (corrected after filing).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this tax posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this tax posting is recorded for financial reporting purposes.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code representing the tax jurisdiction for system processing and reporting.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that last modified this tax posting record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to this tax posting for audit and compliance purposes.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date when the tax amount was remitted to the tax authority.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the tax transaction was posted to the general ledger.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The tax reporting period identifier (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually) for regulatory filing purposes.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tax posting is a reversal or correction of a previous posting.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the source document (e.g., folio number, invoice number, check number) that originated this tax transaction.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source document that generated this tax posting: guest folio (room charges), F&B check, event invoice, vendor invoice, adjustment, or other.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount posted to the general ledger for this transaction.']
  tax_base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The gross taxable amount upon which the tax is calculated, before tax is applied.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code identifier that defines the tax type, rate, and calculation method (e.g., VAT, GST, TOT, sales tax).']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The governmental jurisdiction or authority to which this tax is owed (e.g., federal, state, county, city, municipality).']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The tax rate percentage applied to the tax base amount to calculate the tax amount.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax being posted: VAT (Value Added Tax), GST (Goods and Services Tax), occupancy tax, TOT (Transient Occupancy Tax), sales tax on F&B and events, withholding tax on vendor payments, service tax, resort fee tax, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VAT|GST|occupancy_tax|TOT|sales_tax|withholding_tax|service_tax|resort_fee_tax|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The actual business date when the taxable transaction occurred (e.g., guest check-out date, F&B sale date).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that created this tax posting record.']

  Note: 'Tax transaction record capturing all tax-related financial postings including VAT/GST, occupancy tax, transient occupancy tax (TOT), sales tax on F&B and events, and withholding tax on vendor payments. Tracks tax code, tax base amount, tax amount, tax jurisdiction, reporting period, and filing status. Supports regulatory tax compliance and multi-jurisdiction reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Tax module.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Bank accounts are linked to GL accounts for bank reconciliation and cash management. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account ']
  master_account_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master bank account for zero balance account (ZBA) cash pooling arrangements. Null if this is not a ZBA or if this is the master account.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or corporate entity that owns this bank account. Links to property master data.']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'The date the bank account was closed, if applicable. Null for active accounts.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The legal name or title of the bank account as registered with the financial institution.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The full bank account number. Stored in masked or encrypted format for security and PCI DSS compliance.']
  account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Partially masked account number for display purposes (e.g., ****1234). Used in reports and user interfaces to protect sensitive data.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'The date the bank account was originally opened with the financial institution.']
  account_purpose varchar [note: 'Description of the primary business purpose or use case for this bank account (e.g., AP disbursements, AR collections, payroll funding, property operating account).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Active accounts are available for transactions; inactive or closed accounts are not.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account by its primary purpose (checking, savings, money market, payroll, escrow, investment).']
  ach_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ACH transactions (direct deposits, electronic payments) are enabled for this account.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The available balance for disbursements, net of holds, pending transactions, and minimum balance requirements.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific bank branch where the account is held.']
  bank_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or code for the bank, often used in internal systems or for integration with treasury management systems.']
  bank_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary bank contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contact person at the bank for account inquiries and issue resolution.']
  bank_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary bank contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the financial institution holding the account.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code or equivalent organizational unit to which this bank account is assigned. Used for financial consolidation and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency cash pooling and treasury management.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current balance of the bank account as of the last update or reconciliation. Reflects all posted transactions.']
  electronic_banking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electronic banking services (ACH, wire transfers, electronic statements) are enabled for this account.']
  iban varchar [note: 'The International Bank Account Number for accounts in jurisdictions that use IBAN for international transactions.']
  interest_bearing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account earns interest on deposited funds.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The annual interest rate applied to the account balance, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%). Null for non-interest-bearing accounts.']
  is_primary_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary operating bank account for the property or company code. Used for default account selection in AP and AR processes.']
  is_zero_balance_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a zero balance account that automatically sweeps funds to or from a master account for cash concentration.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent bank reconciliation for this account. Critical for SOX compliance and financial controls.']
  last_statement_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The ending balance reported on the most recent bank statement. Used as the baseline for reconciliation.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent bank statement received for this account. Used for reconciliation and cash flow tracking.']
  minimum_balance_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum balance that must be maintained in the account per bank agreement or internal treasury policy.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bank account record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments about the bank account.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The opening balance of the bank account at the start of the current fiscal period or reconciliation cycle.']
