  positive_pay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positive pay fraud prevention service is enabled. Positive pay matches issued checks against a list to prevent fraudulent checks from clearing.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the bank reconciliation process. Tracks whether the account has been reconciled, has discrepancies, or is pending review.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'The ABA routing number (US) or equivalent bank routing code used for electronic funds transfers and wire transactions.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'The SWIFT/BIC code for international wire transfers. Used for cross-border payments and multi-currency transactions.']
  wire_transfer_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether domestic and international wire transfers are enabled for this account.']

  Note: 'Bank account master representing property and corporate bank accounts used for cash management, AP disbursements, AR collections, and payroll funding. Tracks bank name, account number (masked), account type, currency, bank routing details, account status, assigned company code, last reconciliation date, reconciliation status, opening/closing balances, and reconciliation history. Supports treasury management, cash flow visibility, automated bank statement reconciliation (SOX control evidence), multi-currency cash pooling, and daily cash position reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Bank Master Data and Electronic Bank Statement module.'
}

Table finance.management_fee {
  management_fee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the management fee calculation record. Primary key. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Management fees are allocated to cost centers for departmental expense tracking. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id to enable joining to cost']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the management fee calculation.']
  hma_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Hotel Management Agreement contract that governs the terms and conditions of this management fee.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Management fees post to GL accounts for expense tracking and P&L reporting. The gl_account_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to ledger.ledger_id to enable joining to GL account master da']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property owner entity that is being charged the management fee under the Hotel Management Agreement (HMA).']
  prior_period_management_fee_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on management_fee (prior_period_management_fee_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Management fees are allocated to profit centers for revenue-generating business unit expense tracking. The profit_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to profit_center.profit_center_']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property for which the management fee is calculated.']
  reversed_fee_management_fee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original management fee record that this entry reverses, if applicable.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments applied to the calculated management fee (e.g., prior period corrections, contractual adjustments, dispute resolutions).']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for any adjustment applied to the management fee amount.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the management fee calculation, indicating whether it has been reviewed and approved by authorized personnel.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the management fee calculation was approved.']
  basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual financial amount (gross revenue, GOP, NOI, etc.) used as the calculation basis for this management fee, in the property\'s functional currency.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The financial metric used as the basis for calculating the management fee amount, as defined in the Hotel Management Agreement (HMA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gross_revenue|total_revenue|rooms_revenue|gop|noi|ebitda|adjusted_gop|custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date when the management fee was calculated, representing the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this management fee record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the management fee transaction.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated management fee amount charged to the property owner, in the property\'s functional currency.']
  fee_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or document number for this management fee calculation, used for owner reporting and audit trail.']
  fee_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to the calculation basis to determine the management fee amount, as specified in the Hotel Management Agreement (HMA).']
  fee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the management fee calculation and payment process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|calculated|approved|invoiced|paid|disputed|reversed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the management fee type per the Hotel Management Agreement (HMA). Base fee is typically a percentage of gross revenue; incentive fee is typically a percentage of GOP (Gross Operating Profit) or NOI (Net Operating Income).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for which the management fee applies.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the management fee applies, aligned with property financial reporting periods.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this management fee represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this management fee record was last modified or updated.']
  net_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net management fee amount after all adjustments, representing the amount due to the management company.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or explanations related to the management fee calculation, payment, or any special circumstances.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date when the management fee was paid by the property owner.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the management fee payment is due from the property owner per the Hotel Management Agreement (HMA) payment terms.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction identifier for the payment received from the property owner.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the management fee, tracking whether the owner has remitted payment.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance period covered by this management fee calculation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance period covered by this management fee calculation.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this management fee record represents a reversal or correction of a previously recorded fee.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for reversing the management fee calculation.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document or transaction in the originating system that generated this management fee record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the management fee record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, owner accounting system).']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this management fee transaction is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements for publicly traded entities.']

  Note: 'Management fee calculation record representing periodic fees charged by the hotel management company to property owners under Hotel Management Agreements (HMA). Tracks fee type (base fee as % of gross revenue, incentive fee as % of GOP/NOI), calculation basis, fee amount, payment schedule, owner entity, and fiscal period. Critical for owner reporting, P&L accuracy, and intercompany elimination in consolidated statements. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA and owner accounting systems.'
}

Table finance.owner_distribution {
  owner_distribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the owner distribution record. Primary key for this entity representing a single periodic distribution calculation and payment event under a Hotel Management Agreement (HMA).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this owner distribution for payment. Typically a senior finance officer or property general manager with appropriate authorization.']
  hma_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.hma_contract. Business justification: owner_distribution needs FK to hma_contract to link distributions to the governing management agreement. Currently has hma_contract_code (BIGINT code field). Adding hma_contract_id as proper FK enable']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal ownership entity receiving this distribution payment. May represent individual owners, investment funds, or ownership consortiums.']
  prior_period_owner_distribution_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on owner_distribution (prior_period_owner_distribution_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property for which this owner distribution is calculated. Links to the property master record.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this owner distribution was approved for payment. Critical for audit trail and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance.']
  base_management_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base management fee charged by the hotel operator, typically calculated as a percentage of gross revenue per the Hotel Management Agreement. First deduction in the waterfall.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner distribution waterfall calculation was performed. Typically occurs after period close and financial statement finalization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this owner distribution record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this distribution record. Typically matches the property\'s functional currency.']
  debt_service_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal and interest payments on property-level debt obligations deducted from the distribution waterfall. Includes mortgage payments, construction loans, and other secured financing.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner formally disputed this distribution. Starts the contractual dispute resolution timeline per the Hotel Management Agreement.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner has disputed this distribution calculation or payment. Triggers review and resolution workflow.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the owner\'s dispute regarding this distribution. Documents the nature of the disagreement for resolution tracking.']
  distribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount distributed to the owner entity after all waterfall deductions. This is the actual payment remitted to the ownership entity.']
  distribution_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this owner distribution payment. Typically follows a sequential or period-based numbering scheme for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the owner distribution. Tracks progression from initial calculation through approval, payment execution, and final settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|calculated|approved|pending_payment|paid|cancelled|disputed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ffe_reserve_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative balance in the Furniture Fixtures and Equipment reserve fund after this period\'s contribution. Tracks funds available for capital expenditure (CapEx) projects.']
  ffe_reserve_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Mandatory contribution to the Furniture Fixtures and Equipment reserve fund, typically calculated as a percentage of gross revenue. Funds capital expenditure (CapEx) for property maintenance and renovation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (typically month) within the fiscal year for which this distribution is calculated. Standard range is 1-12 for monthly distributions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which this owner distribution is calculated. Aligns with the property\'s financial reporting calendar.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the owner distribution transaction was posted to the general ledger. May differ from payment date due to accounting period cutoffs.']
  gop_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross Operating Profit calculated as gross revenue minus operating expenses and management fees. Key performance metric in hotel financial reporting and distribution calculations.']
  gross_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated by the property during the distribution period. First line item in the distribution waterfall calculation, includes rooms, food and beverage (F&B), events, and ancillary revenue streams.']
  incentive_management_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Performance-based incentive management fee earned by the operator when property achieves specified Gross Operating Profit (GOP) or other performance thresholds defined in the Hotel Management Agreement.']
  insurance_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Property insurance premiums paid during the period, including general liability, property damage, business interruption, and other required coverage per the Hotel Management Agreement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this owner distribution record was last modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes to distribution calculations or payment details.']
  noi_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Operating Income calculated after all operating expenses, management fees, reserves, debt service, and other deductions. Represents the distributable income available to owners.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments regarding this owner distribution. May include explanations of unusual items, adjustments, or special circumstances affecting the calculation.']
  other_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional contractual deductions from the distribution waterfall not captured in standard categories. May include special assessments, brand fees, or owner-approved expenditures.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the owner distribution payment was executed and funds transferred to the owner entity.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Contractual due date by which the owner distribution payment must be remitted per the Hotel Management Agreement terms.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to remit the owner distribution payment. Defines the financial instrument and transfer mechanism.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank or payment system reference number for the distribution payment transaction. Used for reconciliation and audit trail purposes.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the operating period covered by this distribution calculation. Defines the close of the revenue and expense accumulation window.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the operating period covered by this distribution calculation. Defines the beginning of the revenue and expense accumulation window.']
  property_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Real estate property taxes and assessments paid during the period. Deducted in the distribution waterfall before calculating net distributable income.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution record is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley financial controls and enhanced audit requirements. Applies to publicly traded entities.']
  total_operating_expenses_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total operating expenses incurred during the period, including departmental costs, undistributed operating expenses, and property operations and maintenance. Deducted after management fees in the waterfall.']
  working_capital_reserve_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative balance in the working capital reserve fund maintained per Hotel Management Agreement requirements. Provides liquidity buffer for operational needs.']

  Note: 'Owner distribution and P&L waterfall record representing the periodic calculation and payment of net operating income to property owners under Hotel Management Agreements (HMA). Tracks waterfall steps (gross revenue → management fees → operating expenses → FF&E reserve → debt service → owner distribution), distribution amount, payment date, owner entity, property, fiscal period, and reserve fund balances. Critical for managed hotel financial reporting, owner relations, and compliance with HMA contractual terms.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source bank account from which funds will be disbursed for this payment run.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this payment run. Required for audit trails and segregation of duties per SOX controls.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this payment run for execution. Required for SOX audit trails and segregation of duties.']
  gl_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL batch in SAP S/4HANA where payment run transactions were posted for financial consolidation.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity executing the payment run. Critical for multi-entity consolidated financial reporting per GAAP/IFRS.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property or resort for which this payment run is being executed. Supports property-level financial segregation per USALI standards.']
  reversal_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversal_payment_run_id)']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment run requires managerial or executive approval before execution, typically based on amount thresholds or payment type per SOX controls.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run was approved by an authorized user.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run completed processing all payments, whether successful or failed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all payments in this run (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All payments within a single run must use the same currency.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the purpose, scope, or special circumstances of this payment run.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'System-generated error message if the payment run failed, providing technical details for troubleshooting and resolution.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment run was actually executed and payments were initiated.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference number from the source system or bank for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails.']
  failed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount from payments that failed processing and were not disbursed.']
  failed_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that failed during processing due to validation errors, insufficient funds, or other issues.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date when the payment run transactions are posted to the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was last modified.']
  net_disbursement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount disbursed after deducting processing fees and adjustments from the total successful payment amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments about the payment run, including any issues encountered, special handling instructions, or audit observations.']
  payment_file_format varchar [note: 'The technical format of the payment file: NACHA for ACH, ISO20022 for international standards, BAI2 for bank reconciliation, MT940 for SWIFT, proprietary for custom bank formats.']
  payment_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the payment file generated for bank transmission (e.g., NACHA file for ACH, SWIFT file for wire transfers).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument used for disbursement: ACH for automated clearing house transfers, wire for same-day transfers, check for paper instruments, card for credit/debit payments, cash for on-premise disbursements, virtual_card for single-use card numbers.']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged by payment processors, banks, or payment networks for executing this payment run.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when this payment run was successfully reconciled against bank statements.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank reconciliation for this payment run: pending awaiting bank confirmation, reconciled when all payments match bank records, discrepancy if mismatches found, under_review during investigation.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this payment run has been retried after initial failure, used for monitoring and escalation thresholds.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payment run, formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment run based on its purpose and scheduling: scheduled for regular cycles, ad_hoc for one-time payments, emergency for urgent disbursements, payroll for employee compensation, vendor for supplier payments, refund for guest reimbursements.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned date when the payment run is scheduled to execute and disburse funds.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system that initiated this payment run (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, property management system, payroll system).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run: draft for initial creation, pending_approval awaiting authorization, approved ready for execution, processing during disbursement, completed when all payments successful, failed if errors occurred, cancelled if terminated before completion.']
  successful_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount successfully disbursed from payments that completed without errors.']
  successful_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that were successfully processed and disbursed.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of all payments included in this run before any adjustments or fees.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.capex_request {
  capex_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for capex_request']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the capital expenditure request. Null if not yet approved.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or hotel location for which the capital expenditure is requested.']
  requestor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initiated the capital expenditure request.']
  revised_capex_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on capex_request (revised_capex_request_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary vendor or contractor selected to execute the capital expenditure project. Null if not yet selected.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the capital expenditure project was completed and the asset placed in service. Null if not yet completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the capital expenditure project commenced. Null if not yet started.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of management authority required to approve this capital expenditure request based on amount thresholds and policy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the capital expenditure request was approved by authorized management. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure amount approved by management, which may differ from the requested amount. Null if not yet approved.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who approved the capital expenditure request. Null if not yet approved.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset type being acquired or improved through this capital expenditure.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the capital expenditure is budgeted and planned.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Rationale and business case for the capital expenditure, including strategic alignment and expected benefits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital expenditure request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the requested and approved amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department or cost center requesting the capital expenditure, aligned with USALI departmental structure.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method to be used for depreciating the capital asset over its useful life.']
  environmental_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment level of environmental impact associated with the capital expenditure project, supporting sustainability reporting.']
  expected_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years for depreciation calculation purposes.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the capital expenditure will be capitalized, following the chart of accounts structure.']
  is_multi_year_project boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capital expenditure project spans multiple fiscal years requiring phased budgeting and approval.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital expenditure request record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, notes, or supplementary information related to the capital expenditure request.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Estimated number of months required to recover the capital expenditure investment through generated cash flows or savings.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date for the capital expenditure project to be completed and the asset placed in service.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the capital expenditure project to commence.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the capital expenditure request based on urgency and strategic importance.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the capital expenditure project, including scope, objectives, and expected outcomes.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the capital expenditure project or initiative.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number issued for the capital expenditure procurement. Null if not yet issued.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a capital expenditure request is rejected by approving authority. Null if not rejected.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the capital expenditure request was formally submitted for review.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the capital expenditure request, formatted as CAPEX-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capital expenditure request in the approval workflow.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital expenditure request: CapEx (general capital expenditure), FF&E (furniture, fixtures, and equipment), renovation, expansion, maintenance, technology, or equipment.']
  requested_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure amount requested for the project in the property\'s functional currency.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who initiated the capital expenditure request.']
  roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated return on investment percentage for the capital expenditure project, used in financial justification.']
  sox_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital expenditure request is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements for publicly traded entities.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary vendor or contractor selected to execute the capital expenditure project. Null if not yet selected.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for capex_request. Referenced by capex_request_id.'
}

Table finance.recurring_entry_template {
  recurring_entry_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recurring_entry_template']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this template to specific SOX financial controls for publicly traded entities.']
  superseded_recurring_entry_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recurring_entry_template (superseded_recurring_entry_template_id)']
  amount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the journal entry amount is a fixed value, variable (entered at generation time), or calculated using a formula.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether entries generated from this template require explicit approval before posting, supporting SOX segregation of duties controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this recurring entry template for use, supporting SOX segregation of duties.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recurring entry template was approved, establishing the audit trail for template authorization.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Role or position authorized to approve journal entries generated from this template (e.g., Controller, Finance Manager, Property Accountant).']
  auto_post_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether journal entries generated from this template are automatically posted to the general ledger (true) or require manual review and approval (false).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and supporting documentation for this recurring entry template, required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  calculation_formula varchar [note: 'Formula or calculation logic used to determine the journal entry amount when amount_type is formula_based. May reference other GL accounts, percentages, or system variables.']
  capex_ffe_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template relates to capital expenditure or FF&E reserve tracking, critical for hotel asset management and lender covenant compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recurring entry template record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts in this template (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Department or cost center code associated with this recurring entry template for departmental P&L tracking per USALI standards.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this recurring entry template expires and stops generating journal entries. Null indicates an open-ended template.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this recurring entry template becomes active and begins generating journal entries.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry type that this template generates (standard operating entry, period-end adjusting entry, accrual, reversal, cost allocation, or account reclassification).']
  fixed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount to be used when generating journal entries from this template. Only applicable when amount_type is fixed.']
  gaap_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to specific GAAP or IFRS accounting standard that governs this recurring entry (e.g., ASC 606, IFRS 15).']
  gl_account_credit varchar [note: 'General ledger account number to be credited when this template generates a journal entry. Must conform to the USALI chart of accounts structure.']
  gl_account_debit varchar [note: 'General ledger account number to be debited when this template generates a journal entry. Must conform to the USALI chart of accounts structure.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template generates intercompany journal entries requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements.']
  last_generated_date date [note: 'Date when this template last generated a journal entry, used for tracking and scheduling next occurrence.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this recurring entry template, supporting change control and audit requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recurring entry template record was last modified, critical for SOX audit trail and version control.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date when this template is scheduled to generate its next journal entry based on recurrence frequency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or comments related to this recurring entry template for operational guidance and knowledge transfer.']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Property or hotel identifier for property-level financial tracking and consolidated reporting.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this template generates journal entries (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, or annually).']
  reversal_period_offset varchar [note: 'Number of accounting periods after the original entry when the reversal should occur. Typically 1 for next-period reversals.']
  reversal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether entries generated from this template require automatic reversal in the subsequent accounting period (true for accruals and certain adjusting entries).']
  sap_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template is integrated with SAP S/4HANA for automated journal entry posting and synchronization.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recurring entry template indicating whether it is in draft, actively generating entries, temporarily suspended, permanently inactive, or archived.']
  tax_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether entries from this template have income tax implications requiring coordination with tax accounting and reporting.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the recurring entry template, used for external reference and reporting.']
  template_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the recurring entry template including its purpose, business rationale, and any special instructions for processing.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the recurring entry template that clearly identifies its business purpose (e.g., Monthly Depreciation - FF&E, Quarterly Accrued Interest).']
  usali_category varchar [note: 'USALI financial statement category or line item that this recurring entry impacts (e.g., Rooms Revenue, F&B Cost of Sales, GOP, EBITDA).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this recurring entry template, supporting audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for recurring_entry_template. Referenced by recurring_entry_template_id.'
}

Table finance.allocation_run {
  allocation_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation_run']
  allocation_rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predefined set of allocation rules and parameters applied in this run.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this allocation run for execution, ensuring segregation of duties.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated or triggered this allocation run.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fiscal period for which this allocation run is executed.']
  gl_batch_id varchar [note: 'The batch identifier in the general ledger system where allocation journal entries were posted.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity under which this allocation run is processed for consolidated financial reporting.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this allocation run is executed. Null for corporate-level allocations.']
  reversal_allocation_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_run (reversal_allocation_run_id)']
  reversed_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original allocation run that this run is reversing. Populated only when reversal_flag is True.']
  source_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating cost center from which costs are being allocated.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period in YYYY-MM format for which allocations are being processed.']
  actual_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the allocation run processing was initiated.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the allocation basis or driver used for cost distribution (e.g., occupied room nights, revenue percentage, employee count, square footage).']
  allocation_line_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual allocation line items generated in this run.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to distribute costs across departments, properties, or cost centers (direct assignment, step-down, reciprocal, activity-based costing, revenue-based, headcount-based, or square footage-based).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the allocation run was approved for execution.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the allocation run processing was completed successfully or terminated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allocation run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amounts.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error or failure message if the allocation run encountered processing errors. Null for successful runs.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The effective date when allocation entries were posted to the general ledger.']
  gl_posting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation results have been posted to the general ledger system.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation run was executed automatically by the system (True) or manually triggered by a user (False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allocation run record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this allocation run, including business justification, special circumstances, or manual adjustments.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the total allocated amounts reconcile to the source amounts (balanced) or if discrepancies exist (unbalanced).']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation run is a reversal of a previously posted allocation (True) or an original allocation (False).']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the allocation run, formatted as AR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation run based on the scope and nature of cost distribution (departmental, property-level, corporate, intercompany, overhead, or capital expenditure).']
  scheduled_execution_date date [note: 'The planned date for executing this allocation run as part of the monthly close calendar.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which cost data was extracted for this allocation run (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Financials).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation run indicating its processing state.']
  target_cost_center_count varchar [note: 'The number of distinct cost centers or departments that received allocated costs in this run.']
  total_amount_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of costs distributed in this allocation run across all target cost centers or departments.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes or warnings from the validation process, including any exceptions or manual overrides applied.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation run passed pre-execution validation checks (balancing, rule completeness, data integrity).']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary difference between source amounts and allocated amounts, indicating rounding differences or allocation errors.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_run. Referenced by allocation_run_id.'
}

Table finance.hma_contract {
  hma_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hma_contract']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel brand under which the property operates as specified in the management agreement.']
  operator_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel operator party responsible for managing the property under this agreement.']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property owner party who is the counterparty to this hotel management agreement contract.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property or resort covered under this management agreement contract.']
  superseded_hma_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hma_contract (superseded_hma_contract_id)']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'Financial accounting standard framework required for property financial reporting under the contract, such as USALI, US GAAP, or IFRS.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments or modifications executed since the original contract signing.']
  assignment_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether either party has the right to assign or transfer the contract to a third party without consent.']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner has contractual rights to audit the operators books and records related to property operations and fee calculations.']
  base_management_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross operating revenue or other defined base that the operator receives as base management fee under the contract terms.']
  budget_approval_authority varchar [note: 'Party or governance structure with authority to approve annual operating budgets and capital expenditure plans for the property.']
  capital_expenditure_reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross revenue required to be set aside annually for capital expenditure and furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) reserves.']
  confidentiality_period_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years that confidentiality obligations survive after contract termination or expiration.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the hotel management agreement contract, typically including property name and agreement type.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special provisions, or contextual information about the hotel management agreement contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the hotel management agreement contract, typically formatted as HMA- followed by numeric sequence.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hotel management agreement contract indicating its operational state.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hotel management agreement type defining the operational and financial relationship structure between parties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which contract fees and financial obligations are denominated.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the contract data indicating sensitivity and access control requirements per organizational data governance policy.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Primary method specified in the contract for resolving disputes between owner and operator parties.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the hotel management agreement contract becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the hotel management agreement contract term ends and obligations cease, unless renewed or extended. Nullable for evergreen contracts.']
  fee_calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The financial metric or basis upon which management fees are calculated, such as gross operating revenue, gross operating profit, or adjusted gross operating profit.']
  force_majeure_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes force majeure provisions that excuse performance obligations during extraordinary events beyond parties control.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law specified in the contract for dispute resolution and contract interpretation.']
  incentive_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross operating profit (GOP) or adjusted gross operating profit (AGOP) that the operator receives as incentive fee when performance thresholds are met.']
  indemnification_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum liability amount for indemnification obligations between parties under the contract terms, if capped.']
  insurance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for procuring and maintaining required property and liability insurance coverage under the contract.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the original contract terms, if any amendments have been executed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was most recently updated or modified in the system.']
  minimum_annual_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Guaranteed minimum annual management fee amount payable to the operator regardless of property performance, if applicable.']
  non_compete_period_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of non-compete restrictions applicable to parties after contract termination, if specified.']
  performance_test_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes performance test provisions that allow the owner to terminate if specified performance metrics are not met.']
  renewal_option_count varchar [note: 'Number of renewal option periods available to extend the contract beyond the initial term.']
  renewal_option_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of each renewal option period if the contract is extended.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for financial and operational reporting from operator to owner under the contract terms.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the hotel management agreement contract was executed and signed by all parties.']
  term_years varchar [note: 'Initial term duration of the hotel management agreement contract expressed in years.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the contract under standard termination provisions.']
  working_capital_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial working capital amount required to be provided or maintained for property operations under the contract terms.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for hma_contract. Referenced by hma_contract_id.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fiscal_period']
  prior_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)']
  prior_year_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding fiscal period in the prior year. Used for year-over-year variance analysis and comparative financial reporting.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'The budget version or scenario associated with this fiscal period (e.g., Original Budget, Revised Budget, Forecast). Used for budget vs actual variance analysis.']
  business_days_in_period varchar [note: 'Total number of business days (excluding weekends and holidays) in this fiscal period. Used for operational metrics and productivity calculations.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month number (1-12) in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based reporting and seasonality analysis.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter number (1-4) in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based comparisons and external benchmarking.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the period starts (e.g., 2024). May differ from fiscal_year for companies with non-calendar fiscal years.']
  close_date date [note: 'The actual date when the fiscal period was closed for transaction posting. Null if period is still open. Used for financial close tracking and SOX compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in this fiscal period. Used for daily average calculations (e.g., Average Daily Rate, Revenue Per Available Room).']
  description varchar [note: 'Additional descriptive text or notes about this fiscal period, including special circumstances, adjustments, or reporting considerations.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this fiscal period. Defines the end boundary for transaction posting and financial reporting.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month number within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods. Used for monthly financial close and management reporting.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number within the fiscal year (1-4). Null for non-quarterly periods. Used for quarterly financial reporting and SEC filings.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Null for non-weekly periods. Used for weekly operational and revenue reporting in hospitality operations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Used for year-over-year comparisons and annual financial statement consolidation.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (e.g., Period 13) used for year-end adjustments and accruals. True if adjustment period, False if regular operating period.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fiscal period falls within a leap year. True if leap year, False otherwise. Used for accurate daily average calculations.']
  lock_date date [note: 'The date when the fiscal period was locked and finalized. Null if period is not yet locked. Used for audit trail and financial statement finalization.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fiscal period record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the fiscal period, typically formatted as YYYY-MM or YYYY-Pnn (e.g., 2024-01, 2024-P01). Used in financial reports and external communications.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., January 2024, Q1 2024, FY2024-P01). Used for display in reports and dashboards.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential period number within the fiscal year (e.g., 1-12 for monthly periods, 1-4 for quarterly periods). Used for period-to-period comparisons and trend analysis.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fiscal period granularity: month (standard monthly period), quarter (quarterly period), year (annual fiscal year), week (weekly period for operational reporting), or custom (non-standard period for special reporting needs).']
  reopen_date date [note: 'The date when a previously closed period was reopened for adjustments. Null if period has never been reopened. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance monitoring.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency consolidation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this fiscal period. Defines the beginning of the period for transaction posting and financial reporting.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Open: period is active and transactions can be posted. Closed: period is closed for regular posting but can be reopened. Locked: period is finalized and cannot accept new transactions without special authorization. Archived: period is archived for historical reference only.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fiscal_period. Referenced by fiscal_period_id.'
}

Table finance.gl_batch {
  gl_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for gl_batch']
  reversal_gl_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gl_batch (reversal_gl_batch_id)']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period to which this GL batch applies, typically in format YYYY-MM or period number (e.g., \'2024-01\', \'Period 3 FY2024\'). Used for period-end closing and financial reporting alignment.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL batch requires formal approval before it can be posted to the general ledger. True if approval workflow is required, False if batch can be posted without approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved the GL batch for posting. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail. Null if batch has not been approved or does not require approval.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GL batch was approved for posting. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance. Null if batch has not been approved.']
  batch_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title assigned to the GL batch to identify its purpose or content (e.g., \'Monthly Accruals - January 2024\', \'Payroll Posting - Week 12\').']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the GL batch, typically assigned by the accounting system or finance team for tracking and reference purposes.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL batch: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (authorized for posting), posted (committed to the general ledger), rejected (not approved), or cancelled (voided before posting).']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the GL batch based on its accounting purpose: standard (regular transactions), recurring (automated periodic entries), adjustment (correcting entries), accrual (period-end accruals), reversal (reversing prior entries), or closing (period/year-end closing entries).']
  control_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Optional control total amount provided by the batch creator for validation purposes. Used to verify that the sum of batch entries matches the expected total before posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GL batch record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for tracking batch lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the GL batch amounts are denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department or cost center associated with this GL batch (e.g., \'ROOMS\', \'F&B\', \'ADMIN\'). Used for departmental accounting and GOP calculation per USALI.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the GL batch purpose, content, or context. Provides additional information beyond the batch name for audit trail and user reference.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date or transaction date that the GL batch entries represent. May differ from posting_date when backdating or forward-dating entries for accruals or adjustments.']
  entry_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual journal entries or line items contained within this GL batch. Used for validation and reconciliation purposes.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier from the source system or business process that originated this GL batch (e.g., invoice number, payroll run ID, bank statement reference). Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this GL batch belongs (e.g., 2024). Used for annual financial reporting and year-end closing processes.']
  is_balanced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GL batch is in balance (total debits equal total credits). True if balanced, False if out of balance. Out-of-balance batches cannot be posted.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL batch is part of a recurring batch template that is automatically generated on a scheduled basis (e.g., monthly rent accruals, depreciation entries).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the GL batch. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance. Null if batch has never been modified after creation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GL batch was last modified. Part of the audit trail for tracking changes to the batch. Null if batch has never been modified after creation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments added by users regarding the GL batch. Used for additional context, special instructions, or documentation of unusual circumstances.']
  original_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original GL batch that this batch reverses, if this is a reversal batch. Used to track the relationship between reversal entries and their original accruals. Null if this is not a reversal batch.']
  posted_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who posted the GL batch to the general ledger. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance. Null if batch has not been posted.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GL batch was posted to the general ledger. Represents the moment the batch entries were committed to the GL. Null if batch has not been posted.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the GL batch was or will be posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and impacts the accounting period in which transactions are recognized.']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the hotel property, resort, or business unit to which this GL batch applies. Used for property-level P&L and departmental accounting per USALI standards.']
  recurring_frequency varchar [note: 'For recurring batches, specifies the frequency at which the batch is automatically generated: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually. Null for non-recurring batches.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation provided by the approver when rejecting a GL batch. Documents why the batch was not approved for posting. Null if batch has not been rejected.']
  reversal_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL batch that reverses this batch, if applicable. Used to track the relationship between original accrual entries and their subsequent reversals. Null if batch has not been reversed.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which this GL batch should be automatically reversed, if applicable. Commonly used for accrual entries that need to be reversed in the subsequent period.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or sub-system within the source system that generated the batch (e.g., \'Accounts Payable\', \'Accounts Receivable\', \'Fixed Assets\', \'Payroll\').']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the originating system that created or submitted the GL batch (e.g., \'SAP S/4HANA\', \'Property Management System\', \'Payroll System\', \'Manual Entry\').']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all credit entries in the GL batch. Must equal total_debit_amount for the batch to be in balance per double-entry accounting principles.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all debit entries in the GL batch. Must equal total_credit_amount for the batch to be in balance per double-entry accounting principles.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created or initiated the GL batch. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance and internal controls.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for gl_batch. Referenced by gl_batch_id.'
}

Table finance.allocation_rule_set {
  allocation_rule_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation_rule_set']
  superseded_allocation_rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_rule_set (superseded_allocation_rule_set_id)']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'The business driver or metric used as the basis for allocation calculations (e.g., room nights, square footage, headcount, revenue, labor hours).']
  allocation_frequency varchar [note: 'The cadence at which this allocation rule set is executed in the financial close process.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The calculation methodology used by this rule set to distribute costs or revenues across entities (direct assignment, step-down, reciprocal, percentage-based, driver-based, or activity-based costing).']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed percentage used for allocation when the allocation method is percentage-based. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  approval_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval workflow configuration that governs the review and approval process for allocations generated by this rule set.']
  approved_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this allocation rule set for active use in financial processing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation rule set was approved for active use.']
  audit_trail_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether detailed audit trail logging is required for all allocations performed using this rule set.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and justification for this allocation rule set including regulatory requirements, management reporting needs, or operational drivers.']
  created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this allocation rule set record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation rule set record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which threshold amounts and allocation calculations are denominated.']
  department_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or pattern of department codes to which this allocation rule set applies. Used to scope the rule set to specific departments.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the allocation rule set including its purpose, scope, and any special considerations for its application.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation rule set ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended rule set with no planned expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this allocation rule set becomes active and applicable to financial transactions.']
  external_reference_id varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number for this allocation rule set used for integration and reconciliation with source financial systems.']
  gl_account_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or pattern of GL account codes to which this allocation rule set applies. Used to scope the rule set to specific account ranges.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation rule set executes automatically during the financial close process or requires manual triggering.']
  is_reciprocal_allocation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this rule set supports reciprocal allocation where departments can allocate costs to each other iteratively.']
  last_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who most recently modified this allocation rule set record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation rule set record was most recently modified.']
  maximum_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount for which this allocation rule set applies. Transactions above this threshold may use different allocation logic.']
  minimum_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount required for this allocation rule set to be applied. Transactions below this threshold are excluded from allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about this allocation rule set including implementation details, special handling instructions, or historical context.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking that determines the order of execution when multiple allocation rule sets apply to the same transaction or entity. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  property_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or pattern of property codes to which this allocation rule set applies. Used to scope the rule set to specific properties or property groups.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether allocation results from this rule set require management approval before posting to the general ledger.']
  rule_set_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the allocation rule set used for external reference and reporting.']
  rule_set_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the allocation rule set that clearly identifies its purpose and scope.']
  rule_set_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation rule set indicating what type of financial data it allocates (cost, revenue, expense, overhead, departmental, or property-level).']
  source_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of organizational entity from which costs or revenues are being allocated (property, department, cost center, profit center, division, region, or brand).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this allocation rule set was created or integrated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Financials, custom allocation engine).']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation rule set is subject to SOX financial controls and requires enhanced audit trail and segregation of duties.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation rule set indicating whether it is actively being used in financial processing.']
  target_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of organizational entity to which costs or revenues are being allocated (property, department, cost center, profit center, division, region, or brand).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this allocation rule set configuration used to track changes and maintain historical versions for audit and rollback purposes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_rule_set. Referenced by allocation_rule_set_id.'
}

Table procurement.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key for the employee entity. System of record identifier used across all workforce management systems.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or supervisor to whom this employee reports. Enables organizational hierarchy analysis and reporting structure visualization. Self-referential foreign key to employee table.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational department to which the employee is assigned (e.g., Front Desk, Housekeeping, Food and Beverage, Sales, Engineering). Critical for USALI labor cost allocation and departmental GOP analysis.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Employee occupies an authorized position. Position is the authoritative source for job definitions, titles, codes, and levels. This normalizes job attributes and links workforce planning (position = h']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property where the employee is primarily assigned. Links to the property master data for location-based workforce analytics and USALI labor cost reporting by property.']
  ada_accommodation_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specific workplace accommodations provided to the employee under ADA or equivalent legislation. Highly sensitive health-related information. Used for accommodation implementation and compliance documentation.']
  ada_accommodation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the employee requires workplace accommodations under ADA or equivalent disability legislation. Used for compliance tracking and accommodation management. Confidential but not direct health information.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the employee\'s labor costs are allocated. Used for financial reporting, budgeting, and USALI compliance. Links to the finance cost center master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the employee. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, retirement planning, and compliance with labor laws regarding minimum working age.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address assigned to the employee for business communications, system access, and official correspondence. Critical for Oracle HCM Cloud and internal communication systems.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person to contact in case of employee emergency. Used for employee safety and crisis management. Confidential employee information.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person. Used for employee safety and crisis management. Confidential employee information.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend). Used for emergency response prioritization and communication.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable employee number or badge number assigned by the organization. Used for timekeeping, access control, payroll identification, and operational reference. May differ from system employee_id.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee record indicating whether the employee is actively working, on leave, suspended, terminated, retired, or deceased. Used for workforce planning and payroll processing.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment arrangement indicating whether the employee is full-time, part-time, seasonal, contract, temporary, or intern. Critical for labor cost analysis, benefits eligibility, and USALI reporting.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee as recorded in official employment records and payroll systems. Used for identification, communication, and compliance with employment regulations.']
  food_safety_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s food safety certification expires. Null if not applicable to role. Used for proactive recertification scheduling and F&B compliance management.']
  food_safety_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the employee holds a current food safety certification (e.g., ServSafe, ISO 22000). Required for Food and Beverage department employees. Used for compliance with health department regulations.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-Time Equivalent percentage representing the employee\'s work commitment relative to a full-time position. 1.0 = 100% full-time, 0.5 = 50% part-time. Critical for workforce capacity planning and labor cost analysis.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was originally hired by the organization. Used for calculating tenure, seniority, benefits eligibility, and anniversary recognition. Critical for workforce analytics and retention analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname/family name) of the employee as recorded in official employment records and payroll systems. Used for identification, communication, and compliance with employment regulations.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the employee if applicable. Optional field used for complete legal identification in employment records.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting, payroll processing, and employment verification. Highly sensitive PII.']
  oracle_hcm_employee_code varchar [note: 'External employee identifier from Oracle HCM Cloud system. Used for integration and reconciliation between lakehouse and source HCM system. Critical for maintaining data lineage from system of record.']
  osha_training_current_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the employee\'s required OSHA safety training is current and up-to-date. Used for workplace safety compliance, audit readiness, and training program management.']
  osha_training_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s current OSHA safety training certification expires. Used for proactive training renewal scheduling and compliance management.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to the employee\'s position. Used for salary administration, compensation benchmarking, and pay equity analysis. Business-confidential but not direct PII.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the employee is compensated (hourly wages, salaried, commission-based, or contract). Critical for payroll processing, labor cost calculation, and USALI reporting.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee. Used for emergency contact, scheduling notifications, and operational communications. May be mobile or landline.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day operations, which may differ from legal name. Used for name badges, internal communications, and guest-facing interactions.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of hours the employee is scheduled to work per week based on their employment type. Used for FTE calculation, scheduling, and labor cost forecasting. Typically 40 for full-time, variable for part-time.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for turnover analysis, exit processing, and historical workforce reporting. Critical for calculating turnover rates and retention metrics.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for employment termination. Used for turnover analysis, exit trend identification, and workforce planning. Null for active employees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary-resignation|involuntary-termination|retirement|end-of-contract|layoff|death|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific labor union to which the employee belongs, if applicable. Null for non-union employees. Used for contract administration and labor relations tracking.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the employee is a member of a labor union. Used for labor relations management, collective bargaining compliance, and union dues processing.']
  work_authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s work authorization expires (if applicable). Null for citizens and permanent residents. Used for proactive renewal tracking and compliance management.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s legal authorization to work in the jurisdiction. Critical for compliance with immigration and employment laws. Used for I-9 compliance tracking in USA and equivalent requirements in other jurisdictions.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of where the employee primarily performs their work. Indicates whether the employee works on-property at a hotel/resort, remotely, in a hybrid arrangement, at corporate office, or across multiple properties.']

  Note: 'Master record for all hotel and resort employees across properties. Captures full employment profile including personal details, employment type (full-time, part-time, seasonal, contract), job classification, department assignment, property assignment, hire date, termination date, employment status, OSHA compliance flags, ADA accommodation requirements, work authorization status, and Oracle HCM Cloud employee identifier. SSOT for employee identity across the workforce domain. Supports USALI labor cost reporting by department.'
}

Table procurement.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the authorized position within the hotel organizational structure. Primary key.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit or department to which this position belongs within the property structure.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where this position is authorized.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisory position to which this position reports in the organizational hierarchy. Enables organizational chart construction.']
  ada_accommodation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position has been identified as requiring reasonable accommodation under ADA compliance guidelines.']
  budgeted_annual_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned annual base salary for this position as defined in the labor budget. Used for CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) labor cost calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this position record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budgeted salary amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
