  payroll_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payroll group or employee segment (e.g., hourly, salaried, executive, union) to which this period applies.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the payroll period, typically used in payroll systems and reporting (e.g., PP202401, 2024-W01).']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the payroll period (e.g., January 2024 - Period 1, Week Ending 2024-01-07).']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this payroll period within the fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 1-52 for weekly, 1-26 for bi-weekly, 1-12 for monthly).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll period frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  processing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when payroll processing was completed for this period.']
  processing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when payroll processing began for this period.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this payroll period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll period (draft, open, processing, closed, locked, archived).']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The tax year to which this payroll period belongs for tax reporting purposes.']
  total_days_count varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in this payroll period.']
  working_days_count varchar [note: 'Number of standard working days (typically Monday-Friday) within this payroll period, excluding holidays.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_period. Referenced by payroll_period_id.'
}

Table procurement.work_order {
  work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for work_order']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific asset or equipment that is the subject of the work order.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor contract governing pricing, terms, and service levels for this work order.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel, resort, or property where the work order is executed.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated purchase order that authorizes procurement for this work order.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor assigned to fulfill this work order.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the work order was completed and closed.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred for the work order upon completion, including all charges.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when work commenced on the work order.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the work order was formally approved.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order requires formal approval before execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the work order.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, team, or vendor responsible for executing the work order.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Budget or cost center code against which the work order costs are charged.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order was formally closed and marked as complete.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting work performed, issues encountered, and final outcomes upon completion.']
  contract_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order adheres to the terms, pricing, and service levels defined in the vendor contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this work order.']
  department varchar [note: 'Property department or cost center responsible for the work order (e.g., Engineering, Housekeeping, Food & Beverage).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope, purpose, and requirements of the work order.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total cost for completing the work order, including labor, materials, and services.']
  expenditure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of spend as Capital Expenditure (CapEx) for PIP projects or Operating Expenditure (OpEx) for routine operations.']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for financial posting and accounting integration.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is part of a recurring maintenance or service schedule.']
  labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of labor hours expended on the work order.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order record was last updated or modified.']
  location varchar [note: 'Specific location within the property where work is to be performed (e.g., Room 305, Lobby, Kitchen).']
  material_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of materials, parts, and supplies consumed in executing the work order.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and resource allocation: critical, high, medium, or low.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'High-level procurement category for spend classification: F&B supplies, housekeeping, FF&E, MRO, CapEx, or services.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of recurrence for scheduled recurring work orders: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or department who initiated the work order request.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Date when the work order was originally requested or initiated.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM module document number for integration and traceability with the ERP system.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which the work order is expected to be completed.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when work is scheduled to begin.']
  service_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of external services or contractor fees associated with the work order.']
  warranty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work performed is covered under an existing warranty or service agreement.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the work order, typically displayed on documents and used for tracking.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order: draft, submitted, approved, in progress, on hold, completed, or cancelled.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order by nature of work: maintenance, repair, installation, inspection, project, or emergency.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_order. Referenced by work_order_id.'
}

Table procurement.project {
  project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for project']
  parent_project_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project (parent_project_id)']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred on the project to date, including all purchase orders, invoices, and expenses.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when project execution was completed or closed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when project execution commenced.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the project was formally approved by the sponsoring authority or governance board.']
  approved_by_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or executive who granted final approval for the project.']
  brand_standard_compliance boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the project deliverables must comply with specific brand or franchise standards.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for the project in the property\'s operating currency.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business case and rationale explaining why the project is being undertaken and the expected benefits.']
  committed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount committed through approved purchase orders and contracts that have not yet been invoiced or paid.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of project completion based on milestones, deliverables, or earned value, ranging from 0.00 to 100.00.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master vendor contract or agreement governing procurement for this project, if applicable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting cost center code to which project expenses are charged for budgeting and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this project.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the project scope, objectives, and deliverables.']
  is_pip_project boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this project is part of a Property Improvement Plan mandated by brand standards or franchise agreements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to the project.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when project execution is planned to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when project execution is planned to begin.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  project_category varchar [note: 'Procurement category classification for the project. F&B for food and beverage supplies, housekeeping for cleaning and linen supplies, FF&E for furniture fixtures and equipment, technology for IT and systems, facility for building and infrastructure, guest experience for amenities and services.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique alphanumeric code for the project, used for external reference and reporting.']
  project_manager_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing and overseeing the project execution.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the project.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the project indicating its stage in the project management workflow.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project by financial and operational category. CapEx for capital expenditure projects, OpEx for operational expenditure, PIP for Property Improvement Plan projects, renovation for property upgrades, new build for new construction, maintenance for ongoing upkeep.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel, resort, or vacation property where this project is being executed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for the project based on complexity, dependencies, and potential impact.']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executive or business unit sponsoring and funding the project.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Environmental sustainability rating or certification target for the project, such as LEED or Green Key levels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the project record was last modified in the system.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Hierarchical work breakdown structure element code used for project planning, tracking, and cost allocation in SAP Project System.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for project. Referenced by project_id.'
}

Table procurement.delivery_address {
  delivery_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery_address']
  parent_delivery_address_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on delivery_address (parent_delivery_address_id)']
  address_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the delivery address, used for procurement and logistics operations.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including street number, street name, and building identifier for the delivery location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as suite, floor, room, loading dock, or department.']
  address_line_3 varchar [note: 'Tertiary address line for additional location details or special delivery instructions.']
  address_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for the delivery address (e.g., \'Main Kitchen Loading Dock\', \'Housekeeping Storage Entrance\').']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery address type indicating the nature of the location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name where the delivery address is located.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the delivery address contact person or receiving department for delivery notifications and coordination.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the delivery address for receiving shipments and coordinating deliveries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the delivery address contact person or receiving department.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the delivery address is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery address record was first created in the system.']
  default_shipping_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or default shipping method for deliveries to this address.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this delivery address becomes active and available for use in procurement operations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this delivery address remains active. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  forklift_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether forklift equipment is required for unloading deliveries at this address.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary or default delivery address for the associated property or entity.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery address has been verified through postal validation services or successful delivery confirmation.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery address for mapping and route optimization.']
  loading_dock_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a loading dock is available at this delivery address for freight deliveries.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery address for mapping and route optimization.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the delivery address record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery address record was last modified or updated.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery address used for mail and package routing.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'Primary procurement category or material group typically delivered to this address (e.g., \'Food & Beverage\', \'Housekeeping Supplies\', \'Furniture Fixtures & Equipment\').']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or facility associated with this delivery address.']
  receiving_days varchar [note: 'Days of the week when deliveries are accepted at this address (e.g., \'Monday-Friday\', \'Monday,Wednesday,Friday\').']
  receiving_hours_end varchar [note: 'End time for receiving deliveries at this address in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  receiving_hours_start varchar [note: 'Start time for receiving deliveries at this address in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM) plant code associated with this delivery address for procurement integration.']
  sap_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA storage location code within the plant for goods receipt and inventory management.']
  special_delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions or notes for delivery personnel regarding access, parking, unloading procedures, or security requirements.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the delivery address is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery address indicating whether it is available for use in procurement operations.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the delivery address location, used for scheduling deliveries and coordinating logistics.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the delivery address was last verified or validated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the delivery address record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for delivery_address. Referenced by delivery_address_id.'
}

Business justification: Marketing campaigns operate under master service agreements or annual contracts with agencies, media vendors, platforms. Linking campaign to contract enables contract compliance verification, spend tr']

Business justification: Campaigns target specific guest journey touchpoints (pre-arrival, check-in, in-stay, checkout, post-stay) for personalized engagement. Marketing teams design campaigns around touchpoint moments to max']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Marketing campaigns engage external vendors (creative agencies, media buyers, email platforms, event production). Linking campaign to vendor enables budget reconciliation, invoice matching, vendor per']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on this campaign to date, including all invoiced and paid expenses.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the campaign approval workflow indicating whether the campaign has been reviewed and authorized for execution.']

  budget_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or department responsible for managing and authorizing spend against this campaign budget.']

  budget_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Variance between planned budget and actual spend, calculated as total budget minus actual spend. Positive indicates under-budget, negative indicates over-budget.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the campaign used in external systems and reporting.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|paid_search|social|ota_coop|direct_mail|display|affiliate|content|influencer — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM parameter identifying paid search keywords for search engine marketing campaigns.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing campaigns executed by Travel Hospitality, including brand campaigns, promotional drives, seasonal offers, and targeted guest acquisition initiatives. Captures campaign name, type (email, paid search, social, OTA co-op, direct mail), objective (acquisition, retention, upsell, reactivation), status (draft, active, paused, completed), start and end dates, target segment, owning brand/property scope, campaign owner, Salesforce CRM campaign ID, UTM parameters, approval workflow state, and integrated budget management including budget period (monthly, quarterly, annual), total budget allocated, budget by channel breakdown, actual spend to date, committed spend (POs raised), remaining budget, budget owner, cost center reference, and variance to plan. SSOT for all campaign-level metadata and financial planning.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_offer {
  campaign_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign offer. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign that this offer belongs to.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Promotional offers frequently specify or override cancellation policies (e.g., non-refundable flash sales, flexible cancellation promotions). Marketing teams must define which cancellation terms apply']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that a reservation must be made to qualify for this offer. Null indicates no advance booking requirement.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges or specific dates when this offer is not valid (e.g., \'2024-12-24:2024-12-26,2024-12-31:2025-01-01\'). Null indicates no blackout dates.']
  bonus_points_flat varchar [note: 'Flat number of bonus loyalty points awarded upon qualifying activity completion. Used for fixed-point promotional offers. Null for non-points-based offers.']
  bonus_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base loyalty points earning for bonus point offers. For example, 2.00 represents double points, 1.50 represents 50% bonus points. Null for non-points-based offers.']
  booking_window_end_date date [note: 'Latest date on which reservations can be made using this offer code. After this date, the offer code cannot be used for new bookings even if stay dates are within valid_from_date and valid_to_date.']
  booking_window_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date on which reservations can be made using this offer code. Controls when the offer becomes bookable, distinct from stay validity.']
  channel_restrictions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of booking channels where this offer is valid (e.g., \'direct_web,mobile_app,call_center\'). Null or \'ALL\' indicates offer is available across all channels.']
  combinable_with_other_offers_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer can be combined with other promotional offers in a single reservation. True allows stacking. False requires exclusive use.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this campaign offer record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign offer record was first created in the system.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the rate discount is applied. Percentage applies a percentage reduction. Fixed amount applies a currency value reduction. BAR override replaces the Best Available Rate with a specific promotional rate.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount. For percentage discounts, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). For fixed amount discounts, this is the currency amount. For BAR override, this is the override rate amount.']
  eligible_property_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes where this offer is valid. Used when eligible_property_scope is \'property_list\'. Null indicates scope is defined by eligible_property_scope alone.']
  eligible_property_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic or organizational scope of properties where this offer is valid. All properties indicates portfolio-wide. Brand specific limits to specific brand(s). Region specific limits to geographic region(s). Property list indicates specific property codes are defined.']
  eligible_rate_plans varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate plan codes that this offer can be combined with or applied to. Null or \'ALL\' indicates offer applies to all rate plans.']
  eligible_room_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of room type codes eligible for this offer. Null or \'ALL\' indicates offer applies to all room types.']
  eligible_tier_levels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty tier levels eligible for this offer (e.g., \'Silver,Gold,Platinum\'). Null or \'ALL\' indicates offer is available to all tiers including non-members.']
  enrollment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest must explicitly enroll or opt-in to this offer before redemption. True requires enrollment. False allows immediate redemption with offer code.']
  marketing_message varchar [note: 'Short promotional message or tagline used in marketing communications and guest-facing materials to describe this offer.']
  maximum_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive nights allowed under this offer. Null indicates no maximum LOS restriction.']
  member_exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer is exclusively available to loyalty program members. True restricts to members only. False allows non-member access.']
  minimum_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of consecutive nights required to qualify for this offer. Null indicates no minimum LOS requirement.']
  minimum_spend_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum total spend amount required to qualify for this offer. Applies to total reservation value or qualifying spend category. Null indicates no minimum spend requirement.']
  minimum_spend_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the minimum spend amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this campaign offer record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign offer record was last modified.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used by guests to redeem this promotional offer. This is the externally-known identifier used in booking channels, CRS, and PMS systems.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the promotional offer including terms, conditions, and guest benefits.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the offer displayed to guests and used in promotional materials.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional offer. Draft indicates offer is being configured. Active indicates offer is live and bookable. Paused indicates temporary suspension. Expired indicates validity period has ended. Cancelled indicates offer was terminated before expiration.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional offer mechanism. Percentage discount applies a percentage reduction to BAR. Flat discount applies a fixed currency amount reduction. Free night provides complimentary accommodation. Room upgrade provides category enhancement. F&B credit provides food and beverage spending credit. Bonus points provides additional loyalty points. Tier accelerator provides multiplied tier qualifying activity. Partner earn provides bonus points from partner programs. Member rate provides loyalty member exclusive pricing. Birthday offer provides member birthday recognition benefit. Package deal bundles multiple benefits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_discount|flat_discount|free_night|room_upgrade|fnb_credit|bonus_points|tier_accelerator|partner_earn|member_rate|birthday_offer|package_deal — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current count of how many times this offer has been redeemed. Incremented with each qualifying reservation or transaction.']
  redemption_limit_per_guest varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single guest can redeem this offer. Null indicates unlimited redemptions per guest.']
  redemption_limit_total varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of redemptions allowed across all guests for this offer. Used to cap offer exposure. Null indicates unlimited total redemptions.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions text governing this promotional offer. Includes eligibility requirements, restrictions, cancellation policies, and disclaimers.']
  tier_credit_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to tier qualifying credits for tier accelerator campaigns. For example, 2.00 represents double tier credits. Null for non-tier-accelerator offers.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this offer becomes active and can be redeemed. Reservations with arrival dates on or after this date are eligible.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date after which this offer is no longer active. Reservations with arrival dates on or before this date are eligible. Null indicates open-ended validity.']

  Note: 'Promotional offers and rate-based incentives associated with marketing campaigns, including discount codes, complimentary night offers, F&B credits, package deals, BAR override offers, and all loyalty-specific marketing promotions (bonus point offers, tier accelerator campaigns, partner earn promotions, member-exclusive rate offers, birthday offers). Captures offer code, offer type (percentage discount, flat discount, free night, upgrade, bonus points, tier accelerator, partner earn, member rate, birthday offer), discount value, eligible tier levels, bonus multiplier, qualifying activity, minimum LOS/spend threshold, blackout dates, eligible rate plans, eligible property scope, redemption limit, redemption count, offer validity window, enrollment required flag, promotion period, and linkage to parent campaign. SSOT for all marketing promotional constructs including loyalty-specific promotions. Distinct from revenue domain rate plans — this entity tracks the marketing promotional construct, not the PMS rate. Complements the loyalty domain tier structure with marketing-driven promotional constructs.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_execution {
  campaign_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for a single campaign execution instance. Primary key. Represents one deployment or send event of a campaign across a specific channel and audience segment.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign. Links this execution to the overarching campaign strategy and plan.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: campaign_execution currently has offer_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to campaign_offer. Adding offer_id FK to campaign_offer.campaign_offer_id normalizes this relationship and allows']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Account-based marketing campaigns target specific corporate accounts with customized offers (e.g., Acme Corp Q4 incentive). Sales and marketing teams measure account-level campaign performance, track ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual campaign executions (email sends, paid media buys, OTA co-op) represent actual marketing spend that must be posted to cost centers for budget consumption tracking, variance analysis, and fi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing user or system account that created this execution record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  execution_touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Campaign executions are deployed at specific touchpoints in the guest journey (e.g., post-checkout email, in-room tablet message). Tracks which touchpoint triggered the marketing action, enabling touc']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest audience segment targeted by this execution. Links to the specific customer cohort or persona group.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Campaign executions frequently promote specific loyalty offers (e.g., double points promotion, tier upgrade challenge). Links marketing delivery to the loyalty promotion being advertised, enabling per']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific hotel property or resort this execution is promoting. Null for brand-level or multi-property campaigns.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Individual campaign executions (media buys, email sends, creative deliverables) tied to specific POs for invoice matching and budget tracking. Enables three-way match (PO-execution-invoice), accrual a']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_group_block. Business justification: Targeted email/digital campaigns are executed to drive pickup for specific group blocks (e.g., reminding attendees to book within a conference block). Marketing operations track execution-to-block per']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: When executing email/paid search campaigns, marketers promote specific rate plans. Linking the rate plan enables analysis of which rate plans drive best response rates and conversion performance.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Each campaign execution (email send, paid search flight, social boost) is delivered by specific vendor. Enables granular cost reconciliation, three-way matching (PO-invoice-execution), channel vendor ']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifies which test variant this execution represents in an A/B or multivariate test. Control represents the baseline version.']
  attribution_model_type varchar [note: 'The attribution methodology used to assign conversion credit to this execution. Determines how revenue and conversions are attributed across the customer journey.']
  audience_size varchar [note: 'Total number of guests or contacts in the target audience segment for this execution. Represents the potential reach.']
  bounce_count varchar [note: 'Number of messages that failed to deliver due to invalid addresses, full mailboxes, or other delivery issues.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which this execution was deployed. Represents the medium used to reach the target audience. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|push_notification|paid_social|display|gds|ota — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the marketing channel. Owned channels are company-controlled (email, SMS), paid channels require media spend (display, social), earned channels are organic (PR, reviews).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific channel configuration or platform used for this execution. Used for system integration and tracking.']
  channel_cost_model varchar [note: 'Pricing model used for this channel execution. CPC (Cost Per Click), CPM (Cost Per Mille/Thousand Impressions), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), flat fee, or revenue share arrangement.']
  click_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique clicks on links or calls-to-action within the campaign content. Measures engagement level.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution completed all send and delivery activities. Marks the end of the deployment process.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'Number of conversions or desired actions completed as a result of this execution. Examples: bookings made, loyalty enrollments, event registrations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  creative_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the creative assets used in this execution. Enables A/B testing and creative performance analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this execution record. Example: USD, EUR, GBP.']
  delivery_count varchar [note: 'Number of messages successfully delivered to recipients. Excludes bounces and delivery failures.']
  execution_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for this specific execution instance. Includes media spend, agency fees, content production, and technology costs.']
  execution_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for this execution instance. Typically includes campaign name, channel, and wave identifier for easy recognition by marketing teams.']
  execution_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for this execution instance. Used for operational tracking and reporting. Example: CAMP-2024-Q1-EMAIL-001.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this campaign execution. Tracks progression from planning through completion or failure.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign execution was deployed or sent. The principal business event timestamp representing when the campaign reached the audience.']
  invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vendor invoice associated with this execution cost. Links to accounts payable for reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after execution during which conversions are attributed to this campaign. Defines the attribution time window.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this execution. Used by marketing teams to document special circumstances, issues, or observations.']
  open_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique opens or views of the campaign content. Applicable to email, push notifications, and display channels.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment for this execution. Tracks financial settlement with vendors and partners.']
  revenue_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to this campaign execution based on the attribution model. Represents the financial impact of this execution.']
  sap_cost_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP S/4HANA cost accounting document that records this execution expense. Used for financial reconciliation and USALI reporting.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this execution was originally scheduled to deploy. May differ from actual execution timestamp if delays occurred.']
  send_count varchar [note: 'Total number of messages or impressions sent or served in this execution. Represents actual deployment volume.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the execution spend by type. Used for budget allocation tracking and financial reporting per USALI standards.']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Email subject line or message headline used in this execution. Applicable to email and push notification channels.']
  tracking_capability varchar [note: 'Level of tracking and attribution capability available for this execution. Full tracking includes all metrics, partial tracking has limited visibility, none indicates no tracking available.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a single campaign deployment or send event — one execution instance of a campaign across a specific channel and audience segment. Captures execution date/time, channel (email, SMS, push notification, paid social, display, GDS, OTA), channel category, channel cost model (CPC, CPM, CPA, flat fee), audience segment ID, audience size, send count, delivery count, bounce count, open count, click count, conversion count, revenue attributed, cost of execution, execution status, vendor/media partner, invoice reference, spend category (media buy, agency fee, content production, technology, events/trade shows, print), amount, currency, payment status, SAP S/4HANA cost document reference, channel code, tracking capability, attribution model type (last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, data-driven), and lookback window. SSOT for campaign execution activity, per-execution spend tracking, and channel performance metrics. Enables multi-wave and multi-channel campaign performance tracking with integrated cost-per-execution for direct ROI calculation, CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) analysis, and marketing spend governance.'
}

Table marketing.guest_segment {
  guest_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest segment definition. Primary key.']
  segment_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Guest segment ownership requires clear accountability for segment definition maintenance, refresh execution, and campaign targeting decisions. Marketing analysts and managers need assigned ownership f']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Guest segments are often defined by behavior at specific touchpoints (e.g., \'mobile check-in users\', \'spa bookers\', \'late checkout requesters\'). Enables touchpoint-based segmentation strategy, allowin']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the marketing manager or governance authority who approved this segment for operational use. Null for draft or unapproved segments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition was approved for operational use. Null for draft or unapproved segments.']
  campaign_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests in this segment are eligible for marketing campaign communications (True) or excluded due to opt-out, regulatory restrictions, or business rules (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary operational system or combination of systems from which segment membership data is derived (Salesforce CRM for profiles and campaigns, OPERA PMS for booking history, loyalty system for tier status, Medallia for satisfaction scores, or multi-source for composite segments).']
  definition_criteria varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text representation of the business rules and criteria used to assign guests to this segment (e.g., ADR > $300 AND stays >= 5 per year AND loyalty tier = Platinum).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or archived. Null for currently active segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this segment definition became active and available for campaign targeting and personalization.']
  estimated_size varchar [note: 'Approximate count of unique guests currently assigned to this segment, updated at each refresh cycle.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic boundary or market scope for this segment (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, USA, specific cities or regions). Null for non-geographic segments.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when segment membership was last recalculated and updated, enabling point-in-time segment membership queries.']
  loyalty_tier_scope varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier(s) included in this segment definition (e.g., Platinum, Gold, Silver, or combinations). Null if loyalty tier is not a defining criterion. Distinct from loyalty tier master data — this is the marketing filter.']
  ml_model_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the machine learning model used to generate this segment, if applicable. Null for rule-based or manually defined segments.']
  ml_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the machine learning model used to generate this segment. Null for rule-based or manually defined segments.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the marketing user or system that last modified this segment definition.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition record was last modified.']
  next_refresh_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next segment membership recalculation cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this segment definition, including historical changes, business rationale, or special handling instructions.']
  occasion_type_scope varchar [note: 'Travel occasion type(s) included in this segment: business (corporate travel), leisure (vacation), MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions), group (group bookings), FIT (Free Independent Traveler), or all occasions. Null if occasion type is not a defining criterion.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the marketing team or business unit responsible for defining, maintaining, and using this segment (e.g., Brand Marketing, Guest Acquisition, Loyalty Marketing, Regional Marketing).']
  personalization_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this segment for personalized guest communications and offers: high (premium personalization), medium (standard personalization), low (generic communications).']
  property_tier_scope varchar [note: 'Property service tier scope for this segment: luxury (high-end resorts), premium (full-service hotels), select-service (limited amenities), or all tiers. Null if property tier is not a defining criterion.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which segment membership is recalculated and refreshed to reflect current guest behavior and attributes.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment for operational use in campaigns and reporting (e.g., LUX_FREQ, BUS_TRVL, LEISURE_FAM).']
  segment_creation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this segment was created: rule-based (business rules engine), ml_model (machine learning algorithm), manual (analyst-defined), or hybrid (combination of methods).']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment definition, including business rationale, target guest characteristics, and intended use cases for campaign targeting and personalization.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the guest segment (e.g., Luxury Frequent Travelers, Business Travelers, Leisure Families).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition: active (in use for campaigns), inactive (paused), draft (under development), archived (retired).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology used: behavioral (booking patterns, stay frequency), demographic (age, income), geographic (region, country), RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary), loyalty tier-based (elite status), or occasion-based (business, leisure, events).']
  target_adr_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum Average Daily Rate threshold used in segment definition criteria for revenue-based segmentation. Null if ADR is not a defining criterion.']
  target_adr_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum Average Daily Rate threshold used in segment definition criteria for revenue-based segmentation. Null if ADR is not a defining criterion.']
  target_gss_min decimal(3,1) [note: 'Minimum Guest Satisfaction Score threshold used in segment definition criteria for satisfaction-based segmentation. Null if GSS is not a defining criterion.']
  target_ltv_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum guest Lifetime Value threshold used in segment definition criteria for high-value guest identification. Null if LTV is not a defining criterion.']
  target_nps_min varchar [note: 'Minimum Net Promoter Score threshold used in segment definition criteria for satisfaction-based segmentation. Null if NPS is not a defining criterion.']
  target_stay_frequency_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of stays per year used in segment definition criteria for frequency-based segmentation. Null if stay frequency is not a defining criterion.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the marketing user or system that created this segment definition.']

  Note: 'Marketing-defined guest segmentation taxonomy and individual membership tracking used to target campaigns and personalize communications. Captures segment name, segment code, segment type (behavioral, demographic, geographic, RFM, loyalty tier-based, occasion-based), definition criteria, estimated segment size, last refresh date, data source (Salesforce CRM, PMS, loyalty), active flag, owning marketing team, and individual guest membership records including guest ID, membership start/end dates, membership source (rule-based, manual, ML-model), confidence score, and active flag. SSOT for both segment definitions and guest-to-segment membership associations. Enables point-in-time segment membership queries and supports campaign audience list generation. Distinct from loyalty tier — this is the marketing segmentation layer used for campaign targeting and personalization strategy.'
}

Table marketing.guest_communication {
  guest_communication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each guest communication record. Primary key for the guest communication entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign under which this communication was sent. Links to the campaign master record.']
  cancellation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation. Business justification: Service recovery and win-back communications are triggered by cancellations. Marketing tracks cancellation-specific outreach for sentiment analysis, re-booking campaigns, and loyalty retention. Essent']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: When guest communications are involved in data breaches (email list exposure, unauthorized sends to opted-out guests), linking communication records to privacy incidents enables impact assessment, sub']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who received this communication. Links to the guest master record in the Guest domain.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or brand associated with this communication. Used for property-specific or brand-specific messaging.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reservation associated with this communication, if applicable. Used for pre-arrival, in-stay, and post-stay communications.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the communication template used for this message. Links to the template library for reusable content governance.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'The variant identifier if this communication was part of an A/B or multivariate test. Used for campaign optimization and performance analysis. Null if not part of a test.']
  body_content varchar [note: 'The full text or HTML content of the communication message. Contains the complete message body including personalized merge fields and dynamic content.']
  bounce_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code or message explaining why the communication bounced. Examples include invalid email address, mailbox full, spam filter rejection, or server error. Null if no bounce occurred.']
  bounced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the communication bounced or failed delivery. Null if successfully delivered.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The delivery channel through which the communication was sent. Email is the primary digital channel; SMS for text messages; push for mobile app notifications; direct mail for physical correspondence; in-app for in-application messages; WhatsApp for messaging app communications.']
  clicked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest first clicked a link within the communication. Null if no clicks occurred or tracking not available.']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the communication content type. Promotional includes offers and campaigns; transactional includes booking confirmations and receipts; loyalty includes program updates; survey includes NPS and GSS feedback requests; event includes meeting and conference invitations; service recovery includes apology and compensation messages.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the guest completed the desired action after receiving this communication (e.g., made a booking, redeemed an offer, completed a survey). True if conversion occurred; False otherwise.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest completed the conversion action attributed to this communication. Null if no conversion occurred.']
  conversion_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the conversion attributed to this communication, typically in the property\'s base currency. Used for calculating campaign ROI and guest acquisition cost. Null if no conversion or non-monetary conversion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'The unique activity or task identifier in Salesforce CRM that corresponds to this communication. Used for cross-system reconciliation and guest interaction history.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the communication was successfully delivered to the recipient\'s mail server or device. Null if delivery failed or is pending.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery status of the communication. Sent indicates the message left the system; delivered confirms receipt by the guest\'s mail server or device; bounced indicates delivery failure; failed indicates system error; unsubscribed indicates the guest opted out; blocked indicates spam filter rejection.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the communication was sent. Examples: en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French, de for German, zh for Chinese.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Locale code combining language and region for localization purposes. Format: language_REGION (e.g., en_US, en_GB, es_MX, fr_CA). Used for region-specific content and formatting.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest first opened or viewed the communication. Tracked via pixel or read receipt. Null if not opened or tracking not available.']
  opt_in_status varchar [note: 'The guest\'s consent status for receiving this type of communication at the time of send. Opted_in indicates explicit consent; opted_out indicates withdrawal of consent; pending indicates consent requested but not confirmed; not_applicable for transactional communications that do not require opt-in.']
  personalization_fields varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited string containing the dynamic merge fields and their values used to personalize this communication. Examples: guest name, loyalty tier, reservation dates, property name.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'The email address to which the communication was sent. Captured at send time to preserve historical accuracy even if the guest\'s current email changes.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number to which the communication was sent, applicable for SMS and WhatsApp channels. Captured at send time to preserve historical accuracy.']
  reply_to_email varchar [note: 'The email address to which guest replies should be directed. May differ from sender email to route responses to appropriate teams.']
  send_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this communication for send queue processing. High priority for time-sensitive transactional messages; normal for standard campaigns; low for bulk promotional sends.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'The email address from which the communication was sent. Used for reply-to and sender identification. Must comply with CAN-SPAM sender requirements.']
  sender_name varchar [note: 'The display name of the sender as shown to the recipient. Examples: property name, brand name, or generic sender like \'Guest Services\' or \'Reservations Team\'.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the communication was sent from the marketing system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the communication lifecycle.']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'The subject line or headline of the communication. For email, this is the email subject; for SMS, this may be the first line; for push notifications, this is the notification title.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why this communication was suppressed or not sent, if applicable. Examples: guest on do-not-contact list, invalid contact information, frequency cap exceeded, unsubscribed from category. Null if communication was sent.']
  tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether open and click tracking was enabled for this communication. True if tracking pixels and link tracking were included; False if tracking was disabled for privacy or technical reasons.']
  unsubscribed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest unsubscribed or opted out from this communication or communication type. Null if guest remains subscribed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication record was last modified. Updated when delivery status changes or engagement events occur.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every outbound marketing communication sent to a guest, including pre-arrival emails, promotional offers, loyalty newsletters, post-stay surveys, win-back messages, and event invitations. Also serves as the master library for reusable communication templates. Captures communication date/time, channel (email, SMS, push, direct mail), subject line, delivery status (sent, delivered, bounced, unsubscribed), open timestamp, click timestamp, campaign linkage, Salesforce CRM activity ID, language/locale, and template metadata including template name, content type (promotional, transactional, loyalty, survey, event), body content, dynamic merge fields, brand/property scope, version number, approval status, and active flag. SSOT for individual guest-level communication history AND template governance in the marketing domain.'
}

Table marketing.attribution_event {
  attribution_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing attribution event record.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Marketing attribution events need to link to the booking source for complete channel attribution analysis. This FK enables tracking of marketing touchpoints through to final booking channel, essential']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated this touchpoint event.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional offer or rate plan associated with this attribution event, if applicable.']
  content_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific ad creative (banner, video, text ad) displayed during this touchpoint event. Used for creative performance analysis.']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Attribution tracking involves cookies, device IDs, and IP addresses subject to privacy regulations. Privacy incidents may involve tracking data breaches or unauthorized tracking. Link enables impact a']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest associated with this attribution event. Links to the guest master record when the visitor is identified.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reservation that resulted from this attribution path, if a conversion occurred.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Attribution tracking must capture which rate plan was ultimately booked after a marketing touchpoint. This is essential for marketing mix modeling, channel ROI analysis, and understanding rate plan co']
  ad_platform varchar [note: 'The advertising platform or network that served the ad or content for this touchpoint (e.g., Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Bing Ads, programmatic exchange).']
  attribution_credit decimal(5,4) [note: 'The fractional credit assigned to this touchpoint event under the selected attribution model. Values range from 0.0000 to 1.0000, where 1.0000 represents full credit.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'The attribution model applied to this event for credit allocation (e.g., last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, position-based multi-touch attribution).']
  browser varchar [note: 'The web browser used by the guest during this touchpoint event (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which the guest interacted with the brand (e.g., paid search, organic search, display advertising, social media, email, direct, referral). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_search|organic_search|display|social_media|email|direct|referral — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the conversion value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this touchpoint event resulted in a conversion (booking, reservation, or other defined conversion action).']
  conversion_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the conversion attributed to this touchpoint event, if a conversion occurred. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) calculation.']
  cookie_code varchar [note: 'Browser cookie identifier used to track anonymous guest behavior across sessions before guest identification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this attribution event record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the device (mobile, tablet, desktop) used by the guest during this touchpoint interaction.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The category of device used by the guest during this touchpoint (desktop, mobile, tablet, other).']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or platform that captured and reported this attribution event (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, booking engine).']
  event_status varchar [note: 'The processing status of this attribution event within the marketing analytics pipeline (captured, processed, attributed, excluded).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the guest touchpoint interaction occurred, in ISO 8601 format.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'The city derived from the IP address, providing granular geographic attribution for campaign performance analysis.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code derived from the IP address, indicating the guest\'s geographic location at the time of the event.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'The state, province, or region derived from the IP address, providing sub-country geographic attribution.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the guest device at the time of the touchpoint event. Used for geographic attribution and fraud detection.']
  keyword varchar [note: 'The search keyword or phrase that triggered this touchpoint event in paid or organic search campaigns.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'The full URL of the landing page where the guest arrived during this touchpoint event.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'The operating system of the guest device during this touchpoint event (e.g., Windows, macOS, iOS, Android).']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The full URL of the referring page that directed the guest to this touchpoint. Used for attribution analysis when UTM parameters are not present.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the web or mobile session during which this attribution event occurred. Used to group touchpoints within a single browsing session.']
  time_to_conversion_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours elapsed between this touchpoint event and the eventual conversion, if a conversion occurred. Used for conversion lag analysis.']
  touchpoint_sequence varchar [note: 'The ordinal position of this touchpoint within the guest\'s attribution path (1 for first touch, incrementing for each subsequent touchpoint).']
  touchpoint_type varchar [note: 'The type of marketing touchpoint interaction captured in this event (e.g., ad impression, click, landing page visit, booking engine entry, offer redemption, email open).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this attribution event record was last updated in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'The UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific marketing campaign name or code. Part of the standard UTM tracking taxonomy.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'The UTM content parameter used to differentiate similar content or links within the same campaign (e.g., A/B test variants). Part of the standard UTM tracking taxonomy.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'The UTM medium parameter identifying the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, email, banner, social). Part of the standard UTM tracking taxonomy.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'The UTM source parameter identifying the traffic source (e.g., google, facebook, newsletter). Part of the standard UTM tracking taxonomy.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'The UTM term parameter identifying paid search keywords or targeting criteria. Part of the standard UTM tracking taxonomy.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a guest touchpoint interaction that contributes to a marketing attribution model — including ad impressions, clicks, landing page visits, booking engine entries, and offer redemptions. Captures event timestamp, guest/cookie/device identifier, touchpoint type, channel, campaign linkage, offer linkage, session ID, UTM source/medium/campaign/content/term, IP-based geography, device type, and conversion flag. Feeds multi-touch attribution (MTA) and last-click attribution models for campaign ROI calculation.'
}

Table marketing.content_asset {
  content_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing content asset. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand or brand segment (luxury, premium, select-service) that this content asset is associated with.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that this content asset is linked to or created for.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Content creation and licensing costs (photography, video production, stock imagery, creative agency fees) require cost center allocation for budget tracking, usage rights amortization, and capitalizat']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific property (hotel, resort, vacation property) that this content asset is associated with, if applicable.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Content assets are deliverables against specific POs (photography shoots, video production, design work). Linking asset to PO enables goods receipt confirmation, invoice matching, asset inventory reco']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Marketing content assets are created for specific touchpoints (pre-arrival email creative, in-room tablet content, mobile app check-in screen). Content teams organize assets by touchpoint to ensure co']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Content assets (photography, video, design) are created by external vendors (photographers, videographers, agencies). Linking to vendor enables rights management, invoice matching, usage rights verifi']
  alt_text varchar [note: 'Alternative text description of the content asset for accessibility compliance (screen readers) and Search Engine Optimization (SEO).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the content asset was approved for use in marketing campaigns and brand materials.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the content asset in the brand compliance and content governance workflow (e.g., draft, pending review, approved, rejected, archived).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role who approved the content asset for use.']
  asset_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or SKU assigned to the content asset for cataloging and reference across marketing campaigns and brand materials.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the content asset used for identification and search within the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content asset by format and medium (e.g., image, video, banner ad, brochure, landing page copy, social media post, email template, infographic, presentation, in-property collateral). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: image|video|banner_ad|brochure|landing_page|social_media_post|email_template|infographic|presentation|collateral — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the content asset, including its purpose, visual elements, messaging, and intended use cases.']
  content_theme varchar [note: 'Thematic category or focus of the content asset (e.g., seasonal promotion, loyalty program, Food and Beverage (F&B), Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE), destination marketing, wellness, family, business travel, romance, adventure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seasonal|loyalty|fnb|mice|destination|wellness|family|business_travel|romance|adventure — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  copyright_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that holds the copyright or intellectual property rights to the content asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content asset record was first created in the DAM system.']
  creator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, agency, or vendor who created or produced the content asset.']
  download_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the content asset has been downloaded from the DAM system by marketing users or external partners.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Technical file format of the content asset (e.g., JPG, PNG, MP4, PDF, HTML, PSD, AI, DOCX, PPTX). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: jpg|png|gif|svg|mp4|mov|pdf|html|psd|ai|docx|pptx — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_kb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the content asset file in kilobytes, used for storage management and performance optimization.']
  file_url varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Locator (URL) or storage path where the content asset file is hosted or stored (e.g., cloud storage bucket, CDN endpoint, internal DAM repository).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the content asset is currently active and available for use in marketing campaigns (True) or has been retired or archived (False).']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags associated with the content asset for search, discovery, and SEO optimization within the DAM system and marketing platforms.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the primary language of the content asset (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French).']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Locale code combining language and region (e.g., en_US, en_GB, es_MX) for localized content targeting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the content asset record was last modified or updated in the DAM system.']
  publish_end_date date [note: 'Date when the content asset should no longer be published or used in marketing channels, typically for time-sensitive or seasonal campaigns.']
  publish_start_date date [note: 'Date when the content asset becomes available for publication and use in marketing channels.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience or guest segment for this content asset (e.g., loyalty members, business travelers, families, millennials, luxury guests).']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL or path to a thumbnail preview image of the content asset for quick visual identification in the DAM system.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the content asset has been used or deployed across marketing campaigns, channels, and properties.']
  usage_rights_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the usage rights or license for the content asset expire, after which the asset may no longer be legally used.']
  usage_rights_type varchar [note: 'Type of usage rights or licensing model for the content asset (e.g., owned, licensed, royalty-free, rights-managed, Creative Commons, public domain).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the content asset, used to track revisions and updates over time (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0).']

  Note: 'Master record for digital and physical marketing content assets managed by the marketing team, including images, videos, banner ads, brochures, landing page copy, social media posts, and in-property collateral. Captures asset name, asset type, file format, file URL/storage path, brand/property association, campaign linkage, content theme (seasonal, loyalty, F&B, MICE, destination), language/locale, usage rights expiry date, approval status, and version. Supports digital asset management (DAM) and brand compliance across global properties.'
}

Table marketing.social_post {
  social_post_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the social media post record. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand this post represents (luxury, premium, select-service segment).']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this post is associated with, if applicable.']
  content_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.content_asset. Business justification: Social posts use content assets from the marketing content library. Adding content_asset_id FK to marketing.content_asset.content_asset_id allows social_post to reference the authoritative content ass']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Boosted social media posts represent marketing spend (boost_budget_amount) that must be allocated to cost centers for financial reporting, budget tracking, and reconciliation with social platform invo']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property this post is promoting or associated with, if applicable.']
  actual_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the post was successfully published to the platform.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the post before publication.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the post was approved for publication.']
  boost_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of budget allocated to boost or promote this post, in the campaign currency.']
  boost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the boost budget amount.']
  comments_count varchar [note: 'Number of comments the post received.']
  content_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the primary language of the post content.']
  content_text varchar [note: 'The text content or caption of the social media post.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this social post record was first created in the system.']
  deleted_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or explanation for why the post was deleted (e.g., policy violation, campaign ended, error correction).']
  deleted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the post was deleted or removed from the platform.']
  engagement_count varchar [note: 'Total number of user engagements with the post (sum of likes, comments, shares, saves).']
  engagement_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Calculated engagement rate as a percentage (engagement_count / reach_count), stored as decimal (e.g., 0.0523 for 5.23%).']
  hashtags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of hashtags included in the post for discoverability and campaign tracking.']
  impressions_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the post was displayed to users, regardless of engagement.']
  is_boosted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this post was promoted with paid advertising budget.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this social post record was last modified.']
  likes_count varchar [note: 'Number of likes or reactions the post received.']
  link_clicks_count varchar [note: 'Number of clicks on any links included in the post.']
  metrics_last_refreshed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the engagement metrics (impressions, reach, likes, etc.) were last refreshed from the social media platform API.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The social media platform where this post was published.']
  post_external_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by the social media platform to this post (e.g., Instagram post ID, Facebook post ID, tweet ID).']
  post_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the social media post.']
  post_type varchar [note: 'The type or format of the social media post. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|paid|story|reel|carousel|video|live — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  post_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL link to the published post on the social media platform.']
  reach_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique users who saw the post.']
  saves_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the post was saved or bookmarked by users (platform-dependent).']
  scheduled_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the post was scheduled to be published.']
  sentiment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated sentiment score derived from comments and reactions, ranging from -1.00 (negative) to +1.00 (positive).']
  shares_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the post was shared or retweeted by users.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience segment for this post (e.g., loyalty members, business travelers, millennials, regional market).']
  video_views_count varchar [note: 'Number of video views if the post contains video content.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of social media posts published or scheduled by Travel Hospitality across platforms including Instagram, Facebook, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, WeChat, and TikTok, along with the master registry of all official social media accounts. Captures post date/time, platform, post type (organic, paid, story, reel, carousel), content text, media asset linkage, campaign linkage, property association, hashtags, target audience, boosted flag, boost budget, impressions, reach, engagements (likes, comments, shares, saves), link clicks, and account governance metadata including account handle, account type (brand, property, regional, F&B outlet), follower count, following count, account status (active, suspended, archived), account manager, linked property ID, brand ID, verification status, and last audit date. SSOT for social media publishing activity AND account governance across the portfolio.'
}

Table marketing.survey_program {
  survey_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey_program']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: survey_program currently has brand_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to brand. Adding brand_id FK to brand.marketing_brand_id normalizes this relationship and allows proper referential i']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent marketing campaign if this survey is part of a broader campaign initiative. Null for standalone survey programs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Guest satisfaction survey programs (Medallia, Qualtrics) have vendor subscription costs, incentive expenses, and operational costs that must be allocated to marketing cost centers for budget tracking ']
  medallia_program_id varchar [note: 'External system identifier for the survey program in Medallia platform. Used for API integration and response data synchronization.']
