  primary_survey_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the marketing user or team responsible for managing this survey program. Links to workforce/user management system.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific property when property_scope is \'property\'. Null for multi-property or enterprise-wide surveys.']
  tertiary_survey_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user who last modified the survey program record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  trigger_touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Marketing survey programs are triggered at specific experience touchpoints (post-checkout, post-spa visit, post-dining). The touchpoint determines survey timing, content, and relevance. Critical for t']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Guest satisfaction surveys administered by third-party platforms (Medallia, Qualtrics). Linking survey program to vendor enables contract compliance tracking, invoice reconciliation, platform vendor p']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survey program is currently active and distributing surveys. True = active and sending surveys; False = inactive/paused.']
  auto_case_creation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether low-score survey responses automatically create service recovery cases in the experience domain. True = auto-create cases; False = manual review required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey program record was first created in the system. Audit trail for program lifecycle tracking.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days to retain individual survey response data before anonymization or deletion. Aligns with GDPR data minimization and retention policies.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the survey is delivered to guests: email (post-stay email), SMS (text message link), in-app (mobile app notification), web (website pop-up), kiosk (property self-service terminal), qr-code (printed QR code on receipt/collateral).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the survey program stops distributing surveys. Null for open-ended programs. Trigger events after this date will not send surveys.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the survey program begins distributing surveys. Trigger events occurring on or after this date will initiate survey sends.']
  estimated_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes for a guest to complete the survey. Displayed to guests to set expectations and improve response rates.']
  incentive_description varchar [note: 'Description of the incentive offered for survey completion (e.g., \'500 loyalty points\', \'Entry into monthly $500 prize draw\', \'10% off next stay\'). Null if incentive_offered_flag is False.']
  incentive_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a reward or incentive is offered for survey completion (e.g., loyalty points, prize draw entry, discount code). True = incentive offered; False = no incentive.']
  language_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes supported by this survey (e.g., \'en,es,fr,de,zh\'). Enables multi-language guest experience.']
  max_reminders varchar [note: 'Maximum number of reminder messages to send to a non-respondent. Null if reminder_enabled_flag is False. Typically 1-2 to avoid guest fatigue.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey program record was last modified. Audit trail for configuration change tracking.']
  privacy_notice_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy notice presented to guests with this survey. Ensures GDPR/CCPA compliance and tracks consent framework changes.']
  property_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the property coverage of the survey program: all (enterprise-wide across all brands and properties), brand (specific brand segment - luxury/premium/select), region (geographic region), property (single property), portfolio (custom property grouping).']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions in the survey instrument. Used for survey length optimization and completion rate analysis.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code when property_scope is \'region\' (e.g., \'NA-EAST\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Null for non-regional surveys.']
  reminder_delay_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours after initial survey send to wait before sending a reminder. Null if reminder_enabled_flag is False.']
  reminder_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated reminder messages are sent to non-respondents. True = reminders enabled; False = no reminders.']
  send_delay_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours to wait after the trigger event before sending the survey. Allows guests time to complete their experience (e.g., 24 hours post-checkout).']
  service_recovery_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score threshold below which a survey response triggers an automatic service recovery case in the experience domain (e.g., NPS detractor score <= 6, GSS score < 7). Enables real-time guest recovery.']
  survey_category varchar [note: 'Measurement framework category: NPS (Net Promoter Score - likelihood to recommend), GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score - overall satisfaction rating), CSAT (Customer Satisfaction - specific touchpoint satisfaction), transactional (event-triggered feedback), relationship (periodic loyalty/engagement survey).']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the survey program (e.g., \'Post-Stay Guest Satisfaction Q1 2024\', \'Luxury Segment NPS Wave 3\').']
  survey_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey program: draft (design phase, not yet launched), active (live and distributing), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended, no longer distributing), archived (historical record only).']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey based on guest journey touchpoint: post-stay (after checkout), post-event (after MICE event completion), post-fnb (after Food and Beverage visit), loyalty (loyalty program member feedback), digital (website/app experience), pre-arrival (pre-stay expectations), in-stay (mid-stay pulse check). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: post-stay|post-event|post-fnb|loyalty|digital|pre-arrival|in-stay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  survey_url_template varchar [note: 'URL template for the survey with placeholder tokens for personalization (e.g., \'https://survey.medallia.com/program/{program_id}?guest={guest_id}&res={reservation_id}\'). Used for survey link generation.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the survey instrument (e.g., \'v2.1\', \'2024-Q1\'). Tracks survey design iterations and question set changes for longitudinal analysis.']
  target_gss_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target GSS score for the survey program (typically 0-100 or 0-10 scale). Used for goal setting and performance evaluation. Null for non-GSS surveys.']
  target_nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target NPS score for the survey program (range -100 to +100). Used for goal setting and performance evaluation. Null for non-NPS surveys.']
  target_response_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target response rate percentage for the survey program (e.g., 25.00 for 25%). Used for performance tracking and optimization.']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Guest segmentation criteria for survey targeting (e.g., \'Luxury Guests\', \'Business Travelers\', \'Loyalty Elite Members\', \'Group Bookers\', \'FIT Leisure\'). Aligns with CRM segmentation taxonomy.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'The operational event that initiates survey distribution (e.g., \'checkout\', \'event_completion\', \'fnb_visit\', \'loyalty_tier_change\', \'reservation_cancellation\', \'complaint_resolution\'). Maps to PMS or CRM event codes.']

  Note: 'Master record for NPS (Net Promoter Score) and GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score) survey programs administered through Medallia. Captures survey name, survey type (post-stay, post-event, post-F&B, loyalty, digital), trigger event (checkout, event completion, F&B visit), distribution channel (email, SMS, in-app), target segment, property scope, survey version, active flag, and Medallia program ID. SSOT for survey program definition and distribution configuration within the marketing domain. Distinct from experience domain service recovery and operational feedback — marketing owns survey program design, distribution targeting, and NPS/GSS score aggregation; experience domain owns individual service recovery actions triggered by survey responses.'
}

Table marketing.survey_response {
  survey_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey_response']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Survey responses contain guest PII and verbatim comments. If exposed in a data breach, linking survey responses to privacy incidents enables precise subject identification for mandatory GDPR/CCPA brea']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who submitted this survey response. Links to the guest master data product.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel, resort, or property where the guest experience occurred that this survey response evaluates.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reservation or booking associated with this survey response. Nullable for non-stay surveys (e.g., event-only, F&B-only).']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service recovery case created in response to this survey feedback. Nullable if no case was created.']
  stay_history_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.stay_history. Business justification: Survey responses must link to the exact stay_history record (not just reservation) to analyze NPS trends by actual room type delivered, service quality by specific staff on duty, and operational perfo']
  survey_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the survey program or campaign under which this response was collected (e.g., Post-Stay NPS, Event Feedback, F&B Experience).']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the reservation was originally booked (e.g., Direct, OTA, GDS, Corporate). Used for satisfaction analysis by booking source.']
  checkout_date date [note: 'Date when the guest checked out or completed their visit. Used to calculate survey timing and response lag.']
  contact_permission_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the guest granted permission to be contacted regarding their feedback. Required for GDPR and privacy compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey response record was first created in the data product. Used for data lineage and audit trails.']
  csat_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score measuring satisfaction with specific touchpoints or services. May differ from GSS in scope and scale.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the total spend amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this response requires follow-up action from guest services or management (typically true for detractors or critical feedback).']
  gss_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall guest satisfaction score, typically on a 0-100 scale or 1-5 scale depending on survey design. Measures overall satisfaction with the experience.']
  guest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guest\'s visit purpose (leisure, business, group, event). Used for satisfaction segmentation by travel purpose.']
  length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of nights the guest stayed at the property. Used for satisfaction correlation analysis with stay duration.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Guest\'s loyalty program tier at the time of the survey response (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum). Used for satisfaction analysis by loyalty segment.']
  medallia_response_code varchar [note: 'External unique identifier assigned by the Medallia survey platform for this response. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails.']
  nps_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the guest based on NPS score: Promoter (9-10), Passive (7-8), Detractor (0-6). Used for NPS calculation and segmentation.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Guest\'s likelihood-to-recommend rating on a 0-10 scale. Core metric for NPS calculation. 0=Not at all likely, 10=Extremely likely.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the rate plan or pricing tier under which the guest booked (e.g., BAR, Corporate, Loyalty Member). Used for satisfaction segmentation.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest submitted the survey response (e.g., email link, SMS, mobile app, in-property kiosk).']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the survey response was fully completed, partially completed, or abandoned by the guest.']
  response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours elapsed between survey invitation sent and survey response submitted. Used for engagement analysis.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the guest submitted the survey response. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  room_type varchar [note: 'Code representing the type of room the guest stayed in (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite). Used for satisfaction analysis by room category.']
  sentiment_classification varchar [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis classification of the verbatim comment. Derived from natural language processing of guest feedback text.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric sentiment score derived from text analytics, typically ranging from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive).']
  stay_date date [note: 'Date of the guest stay or visit that this survey response evaluates. Used for temporal analysis and linking to operational data.']
  survey_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the survey was presented and completed by the guest.']
  survey_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey invitation was sent to the guest. Used to calculate response time and survey engagement metrics.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used for this response. Used for tracking survey design changes and ensuring consistent analysis.']
  total_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount spent by the guest during the stay or visit, including room, F&B, and ancillary charges. Used for satisfaction-revenue correlation analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey response record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment provided by the guest explaining their rating or describing their experience. Critical for qualitative analysis and service recovery.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual guest responses to NPS and GSS surveys administered via Medallia. Captures response date/time, survey program ID, guest ID, stay/event/visit reference, NPS score (0-10), promoter/passive/detractor classification, GSS score, verbatim comment text, sentiment flag, follow-up required flag, service recovery case linkage, property ID, and Medallia response ID. SSOT for guest satisfaction measurement data in the marketing domain, feeding CSAT and NPS KPI tracking.'
}

Table marketing.brand {
  brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for brand']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Brand P&L accountability requires linking brand manager to employee master for org chart reporting, succession planning, performance tracking, and compensation decisions. Current brand_manager_name an']
  average_daily_rate_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target average daily rate for properties under this brand. Used for revenue management strategy and brand positioning in competitive set analysis.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the brand for operational and system identification. Used across property management systems, central reservation systems, and marketing platforms.']
  brand_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative description of the brand identity, positioning, value proposition, and target guest experience. Used for marketing content and brand communications.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the hotel or resort brand as recognized in the market and used in all guest-facing communications.']
  brand_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the brand in the portfolio. Determines whether the brand is actively marketed and accepting new property affiliations.']
  brand_tier varchar [note: 'Market positioning tier of the brand indicating service level, amenities, and target guest segment. Aligns with STR competitive set classifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the marketing system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  geographic_focus varchar [note: 'Primary geographic markets or regions where the brand operates or targets expansion (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Global). Informs regional marketing strategies.']
  guest_satisfaction_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target guest satisfaction score for the brand measured through post-stay surveys. Key performance indicator for brand experience quality.']
  guidelines_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the comprehensive brand guidelines document containing visual identity standards, usage rules, and creative specifications.']
  is_featured_brand boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this brand is currently featured in primary marketing campaigns and promotional activities. Used to prioritize marketing resource allocation.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the brand was officially launched in the market. Used for brand anniversary campaigns and historical reporting.']
  logo_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the official brand logo asset stored in the digital asset management system. Used for marketing campaigns and content distribution.']
  loyalty_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loyalty or rewards program associated with the brand. Used in marketing campaigns promoting member benefits and enrollment.']
  marketing_budget_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual marketing budget allocated to the brand for campaigns, advertising, content creation, and promotional activities. Used for marketing ROI analysis.']
  net_promoter_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target net promoter score for the brand indicating guest loyalty and likelihood to recommend. Critical metric for brand health and marketing effectiveness.']
  parent_company varchar [note: 'Name of the parent hospitality company or holding entity that owns and operates the brand. Used for corporate reporting and brand portfolio management.']
  positioning_statement varchar [note: 'Strategic statement defining how the brand differentiates itself in the market and the unique value it delivers to target guests. Foundation for all marketing messaging.']
  primary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Primary brand color in hexadecimal format used in digital marketing assets, website design, and visual identity materials.']
  property_count varchar [note: 'Total number of hotel and resort properties operating under this brand globally. Key metric for brand scale and market presence.']
  secondary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Secondary brand color in hexadecimal format used as accent color in marketing materials and digital assets.']
  social_media_handle_facebook varchar [note: 'Official Facebook page handle or username for the brand. Used for social media marketing campaigns and guest engagement tracking.']
  social_media_handle_instagram varchar [note: 'Official Instagram account handle or username for the brand. Used for visual content marketing and influencer partnership campaigns.']
  social_media_handle_linkedin varchar [note: 'Official LinkedIn company page handle for the brand. Used for corporate communications, talent acquisition marketing, and B2B engagement.']
  social_media_handle_twitter varchar [note: 'Official Twitter account handle or username for the brand. Used for real-time guest service, announcements, and brand conversations.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or sustainability certifications held by the brand (e.g., LEED, Green Key, EarthCheck). Used in marketing messaging to eco-conscious travelers.']
  tagline varchar [note: 'Official marketing tagline or slogan associated with the brand used in advertising campaigns and promotional materials.']
  target_guest_segment varchar [note: 'Primary guest demographic and psychographic segment the brand is designed to attract (e.g., business travelers, leisure families, luxury seekers, millennials). Informs marketing strategy and campaign targeting.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last updated the brand record. Used for audit trail and accountability in brand data management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  voice varchar [note: 'Defined tone and style of communication for the brand (e.g., sophisticated and refined, warm and welcoming, modern and energetic). Guides content creation across all marketing channels.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the brand where guests can explore properties, make reservations, and access brand information.']

  Note: 'Master record for hotel and resort brand identity metadata managed by the marketing function within the Travel Hospitality portfolio, including luxury, premium, and select-service brand tiers. Captures brand name, brand code, brand tier (luxury, premium, select-service, extended-stay), brand tagline, brand color palette, brand guidelines URL, parent company, active flag, launch date, and number of properties under brand. SSOT for marketing-specific brand identity metadata (visual identity, creative guidelines, brand voice) used across campaigns, content assets, and channel distribution. Distinct from property domain brand master which governs operational brand-property affiliation and contractual brand standards.'
}

Table marketing.offer_redemption {
  offer_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each offer redemption transaction. Primary key for the offer redemption record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated this promotional offer. Enables campaign-level performance tracking and ROI analysis.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing promotional offer that was redeemed by the guest.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest loyalty program membership if the redemption was tied to a loyalty member benefit or points redemption.']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POS transaction if the offer was redeemed at point of sale (restaurant, bar, banquet, spa). Null if redeemed at booking.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who redeemed the promotional offer.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where the offer was redeemed.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation associated with this offer redemption, if redeemed at point of booking. Null if redeemed at point of sale.']
  travel_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the travel agent or OTA partner if the offer was redeemed through an intermediary channel.']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The marketing attribution source that led to this offer redemption. Tracks the touchpoint that drove conversion (e.g., email campaign, social media ad, search ad).']
  booking_date date [note: 'The date when the reservation was booked, if this redemption occurred at point of booking. Used for lead time and booking window analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the redemption transaction amounts.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the guest to redeem the offer. Enables device-specific campaign optimization.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied through the promotional offer redemption. Calculated as original rate minus final rate.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied if the offer type is percentage-based. Null for non-percentage offers.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The type of discount or benefit provided by the promotional offer. Enables segmentation of offer performance by benefit type.']
  final_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The final rate or price after the promotional offer discount was applied. This is the amount charged to the guest.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the offer redemption was initiated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis.']
  length_of_stay varchar [note: 'The number of nights for the reservation associated with this offer redemption. Used for LOS restriction compliance and performance analysis.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'The market segment classification for this redemption transaction. Used for segmentation analysis and revenue management.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'The promotional code entered or applied by the guest at redemption. Used for tracking campaign performance and fraud prevention.']
  original_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The original rate or price before the promotional offer discount was applied. Used to calculate discount value and campaign ROI.']
  points_earned varchar [note: 'The number of loyalty points earned from this transaction after offer redemption, if the offer includes a points multiplier benefit.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'The number of loyalty points redeemed as part of this offer, if applicable. Null for non-points-based offers.']
  promo_terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest explicitly accepted the promotional offer terms and conditions at redemption. Required for compliance and dispute resolution.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The rate plan code applied to the reservation after offer redemption. Links to the promotional rate plan in the PMS.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the guest redeemed the offer. Enables channel attribution analysis for marketing campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_web|mobile_app|call_center|front_desk|ota|gds|email|social_media — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  redemption_location_latitude decimal(10,8) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate where the offer was redeemed, if captured. Used for location-based campaign analysis.']
  redemption_location_longitude decimal(11,8) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate where the offer was redeemed, if captured. Used for location-based campaign analysis.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the offer was redeemed by the guest. Primary business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation if the offer redemption was rejected. Includes reasons such as expired offer, invalid code, terms not met, or fraud detection.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'The room type code for the reservation associated with this offer redemption. Used to track offer performance by room category.']
  source_code varchar [note: 'The booking source code indicating where the guest originated. Used for attribution and channel performance analysis.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'The check-in date for the reservation associated with this offer redemption. Null if redeemed at point of sale.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'The check-out date for the reservation associated with this offer redemption. Null if redeemed at point of sale.']
  terms_acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest accepted the promotional offer terms and conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was last modified. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string captured at redemption. Used for technical analytics and fraud detection.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'The validation status of the offer redemption. Used for fraud prevention and ensuring offer terms compliance.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the offer redemption was validated or rejected by the system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each instance of a marketing promotional offer being redeemed by a guest at point of booking or point of sale. Captures redemption date/time, offer code, guest ID, reservation or POS transaction reference, property ID, channel of redemption (direct web, OTA, call center, front desk), discount amount applied, original rate, final rate, and redemption validation status. Enables offer performance tracking, fraud prevention on promotional codes, and campaign conversion attribution.'
}

Table marketing.consent {
  consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consent']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel brand or sub-brand for which this consent applies. Allows brand-specific consent management in multi-brand hospitality groups.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that prompted this consent capture. Null if consent was not captured in the context of a specific campaign.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system user who captured or recorded the consent on behalf of the guest (e.g., front desk agent, call center representative). Null if self-service.']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Privacy incidents often involve consent violations (marketing sent after opt-out, consent records exposed in breach). Direct link enables root cause analysis, regulatory reporting of consent-related b']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this consent record applies. Links to the guest master record in Salesforce CRM and OPERA PMS guest profiles.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program associated with this consent. Consent may be tied to loyalty membership enrollment or benefits.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific property or hotel where the consent was captured. Null if consent applies across all properties or was captured centrally.']
  communication_frequency_preference varchar [note: 'The guest preferred frequency for receiving marketing communications. Allows guests to control volume of messages even when opted in.']
  consent_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest provided or updated their consent for this communication type. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails.']
  consent_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of data usage covered by this consent. Specifies what data will be used and for what marketing purposes. Free-text field for transparency.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the guest provided their consent. Examples include booking engine during reservation, loyalty program enrollment, front desk check-in, website form, mobile app, email preference link, call center interaction, or property self-service kiosk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: booking_engine|loyalty_enrollment|front_desk|web_form|mobile_app|email_link|call_center|property_kiosk — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the guest consent for this communication channel. Opted-in indicates active consent, opted-out indicates explicit refusal, pending indicates consent request awaiting response, expired indicates consent that has lapsed per retention policy, and withdrawn indicates previously granted consent that has been revoked.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The type of marketing communication or data usage for which consent is being recorded. Includes email marketing, SMS marketing, push notifications, direct mail, third-party sharing, and phone call marketing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.']
  data_processing_agreement_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has accepted the data processing agreement or privacy policy associated with this consent. Required for GDPR compliance.']
  double_opt_in_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest confirmed their consent through a double opt-in process (e.g., clicking a confirmation link in an email). True if confirmed, false if single opt-in only. Best practice for email marketing compliance.']
  double_opt_in_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest confirmed their consent via double opt-in process. Null if double opt-in was not used or not yet confirmed.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this consent expires and must be re-confirmed. Some jurisdictions require periodic re-consent. Null if consent does not expire.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the consent was provided. Used for fraud detection and consent verification. May be considered PII under GDPR.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record is currently active and should be honored for marketing operations. False if superseded by a newer consent record or if consent has been withdrawn.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction or country under which this consent record is governed. Determines applicable privacy regulations (GDPR for EU, CCPA for California, etc.). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|DEU|FRA|CAN|AUS|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|MEX|ESP|ITA|NLD|SWE — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  language varchar [note: 'The language in which the consent was presented to and acknowledged by the guest. Uses ISO 639-3 language codes. Required for GDPR compliance to demonstrate informed consent. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eng|spa|fra|deu|jpn|zho|ita|por|rus|ara|kor|nld|swe|dan|nor — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_communication_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent marketing communication sent to the guest under this consent. Used to track engagement and enforce quiet periods.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last updated. Tracks all modifications to consent status, preferences, or metadata.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The legal basis under GDPR for processing this guest data for marketing purposes. Consent is most common for marketing; legitimate interest may apply for existing customer communications.']
  minor_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent is required for this guest due to age restrictions under COPPA or GDPR. True if guest is a minor and special consent rules apply.']
  opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest opted out or withdrew their consent for this communication type. Null if consent has never been withdrawn.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or categorized reason provided by the guest for opting out of marketing communications. Used for service recovery and marketing strategy refinement.']
  parental_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent has been verified for a minor guest. True if verified, false if not yet verified or not applicable.']
  preference_center_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the preference center or web form where the guest can update their consent preferences. Supports self-service consent management.']
  proof_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or storage path to the archived proof of consent (e.g., screenshot of form submission, signed document, recorded call). Required for regulatory audits.']
  salesforce_consent_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the corresponding consent record in Salesforce CRM. Enables bi-directional synchronization between lakehouse and operational CRM system.']
  third_party_processor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party data processor or marketing platform that will process guest data under this consent (e.g., email service provider, SMS gateway). Required for GDPR transparency.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string captured at the time of consent. Provides technical context for consent capture and helps verify authenticity.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent policy or terms and conditions that the guest agreed to. Tracks changes to privacy policies and consent language over time.']

  Note: 'Master record of guest marketing communication consent and opt-in/opt-out preferences, capturing GDPR, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM compliance status per guest and channel. Records guest ID, consent type (email marketing, SMS marketing, push notifications, direct mail, third-party sharing), consent status (opted-in, opted-out, pending), consent date, consent source (booking engine, loyalty enrollment, front desk, web form), opt-out date, opt-out reason, jurisdiction, and Salesforce CRM consent record ID. SSOT for marketing consent governance.'
}

Table marketing.experiment {
  experiment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing experiment. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand under which this experiment is conducted. Supports brand-level test-and-learn strategies.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this experiment is associated with. Links experiment to broader campaign context for performance attribution.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: A/B tests and marketing experiments with budget implications require cost center tracking for spend authorization, budget consumption monitoring, and ROI calculation against estimated_annual_impact_am']
  experiment_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the experiment design and launch. Typically a marketing manager or director with authority to allocate resources and traffic.']
  experiment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing team member responsible for designing, executing, and analyzing this experiment. Accountable for experiment outcomes and learnings.']
  experiment_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the experiment record. Supports accountability and audit trail for experiment changes.']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest segment targeted by this experiment. Enables segment-specific testing and personalization strategies.']
  parent_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on experiment (parent_experiment_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property where this experiment is being conducted. Enables property-level experiment tracking and localized optimization.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: A/B tests in marketing often test different rate plans (e.g., promoting BAR vs. advance purchase). Linking the rate plan(s) being tested is essential for experiment analysis and measuring rate plan me']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: A/B tests and experiments are run at specific touchpoints to optimize guest experience and conversion (e.g., testing check-in messaging, upsell offers at booking confirmation). Experimentation framewo']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'The actual number of days the experiment ran from start to end. May differ from planned duration due to early stopping or extension for statistical significance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the experiment was approved for launch. Used for audit trail and governance compliance.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The primary marketing channel where the experiment is being conducted. Enables channel-specific optimization and cross-channel performance comparison. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|paid_search|social|display|ota|website|mobile_app|sms — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conclusion_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the experiment results, key learnings, business implications, and recommendations for future tests or implementation. Critical for organizational learning and knowledge management.']
  confidence_level_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The confidence level required for declaring a winner, typically 95% or 99%. Represents the probability that the observed difference is not due to random chance.']
  control_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of experiment traffic allocated to the control variant. Typically 50% for A/B tests but may vary for multivariate or risk-averse tests.']
  control_variant_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control variant (baseline) used as the comparison benchmark in the experiment. Represents the current or default experience.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the experiment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and experiment lifecycle tracking.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the experiment concluded or is scheduled to conclude. May be null for ongoing experiments or extended indefinitely until statistical significance is reached.']
  estimated_annual_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The projected annual revenue or cost impact of implementing the winning variant at scale. Calculated by extrapolating lift percentage across full traffic volume and annualizing.']
  experiment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the experiment in operational systems and reporting dashboards. Must be unique and human-readable.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the experiment that clearly communicates the test objective to stakeholders and marketing teams.']
  experiment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the experiment. Draft indicates planning phase, approved indicates ready to launch, running indicates active test, paused indicates temporary suspension, concluded indicates completed with results, archived indicates historical record, cancelled indicates terminated without completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|running|paused|concluded|archived|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  experiment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the experiment methodology. A/B tests compare two variants, multivariate tests multiple variables simultaneously, holdout tests control vs treatment groups, sequential tests run variants in sequence, and split tests divide traffic across variants.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'The testable hypothesis statement that defines the expected outcome of the experiment. Captures the business assumption being validated through the test.']
  impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated annual impact amount. Typically USD for global reporting but may vary by property or region.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'The date when the winning variant was implemented as the standard experience. Used to track time-to-value from experiment conclusion to full rollout.']
  implementation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the winning variant has been implemented as the new standard experience. True means the test results have been rolled out to 100% of traffic.']
  lift_percentage decimal(7,2) [note: 'The percentage improvement of the winning variant over the control on the primary metric. Positive values indicate treatment outperformed control; negative values indicate control outperformed treatment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the experiment record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  p_value decimal(10,8) [note: 'The probability that the observed difference between variants occurred by chance. Values below the significance threshold indicate statistically significant results.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the experiment in days, based on sample size calculations and business requirements. Used for resource planning and timeline management.']
  primary_metric varchar [note: 'The primary success metric used to evaluate experiment performance, such as conversion rate, click-through rate, booking rate, revenue per visitor, or guest satisfaction score.']
  sample_size_actual varchar [note: 'The actual number of observations collected during the experiment. Used to assess whether sufficient data has been gathered for valid statistical inference.']
  sample_size_target varchar [note: 'The target number of observations required to achieve statistical significance based on power analysis. Calculated using expected effect size, significance level, and statistical power.']
  secondary_metrics varchar [note: 'Additional metrics tracked to understand broader impact of the experiment. May include engagement metrics, downstream conversion, average daily rate (ADR), or net promoter score (NPS). Stored as comma-separated list.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the experiment began running and traffic allocation started. Used to calculate experiment duration and time-based performance metrics.']
  statistical_significance_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The p-value threshold required to declare a statistically significant result. Industry standard is typically 0.05 (95% confidence) or 0.01 (99% confidence).']
  test_variable varchar [note: 'The specific element being tested in the experiment, such as email subject line, landing page layout, offer amount, creative imagery, call-to-action text, or pricing structure.']
  traffic_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total traffic allocated to this experiment. Typically ranges from 10% to 100% depending on risk tolerance and sample size requirements.']
  treatment_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of experiment traffic allocated to treatment variant(s). Sum of control and treatment allocations should equal 100% of experiment traffic.']
  treatment_variant_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the treatment variant(s) being tested against the control. May include multiple variants separated by delimiters for multivariate tests.']
  winning_variant varchar [note: 'The variant that achieved statistically significant superior performance on the primary metric. Set to inconclusive if no significant difference was found, or ongoing if experiment is still running.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing A/B tests and multivariate experiments conducted across channels, landing pages, email subject lines, creative variants, and offer structures. Captures experiment name, hypothesis, experiment type (A/B, multivariate, holdout), test variable, control variant description, treatment variant(s), traffic allocation percentage, statistical significance threshold, start/end dates, campaign linkage, channel, sample size, winning variant, lift percentage, and status (draft, running, concluded, archived). Enables data-driven optimization of marketing campaigns and supports test-and-learn culture across digital marketing operations.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_calendar {
  marketing_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing calendar entry. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the primary campaign associated with this calendar entry. Nullable for non-campaign entries (tentpoles, blackouts, brand moments).']
  parent_calendar_entry_marketing_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent calendar entry if this is a recurring instance or sub-entry. Nullable for standalone entries.']
  parent_marketing_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on marketing_calendar (parent_marketing_calendar_id)']
  primary_marketing_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who approved this calendar entry. Nullable if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  tertiary_marketing_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified this calendar entry.']
  actual_campaign_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of campaigns executed during this calendar window. Updated as campaigns are launched and completed.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this calendar entry requires executive or cross-functional approval before confirmation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this calendar entry was approved. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  blackout_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for blackout period if entry_type is blackout_period (e.g., \'System Maintenance\', \'Brand Refresh Quiet Period\', \'Competitive Event Avoidance\', \'Regulatory Restriction\').']
  brand_moment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the brand moment if entry_type is brand_moment (e.g., \'New Brand Launch\', \'Rebranding Announcement\', \'Sustainability Initiative Launch\', \'Partnership Announcement\').']
  brand_scope varchar [note: 'Brand or brand tier this entry applies to (e.g., \'All Brands\', \'Luxury Tier\', \'Premium Tier\', specific brand codes). Supports brand-level planning and prevents brand collision.']
  collision_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this calendar entry overlaps with other high-priority entries, creating potential campaign collision or audience fatigue risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this calendar entry record was first created in the system.']
  cross_functional_dependencies varchar [note: 'Description of dependencies on other business functions (e.g., \'Requires Revenue Management rate load\', \'Dependent on Property Renovation completion\', \'Requires IT system upgrade\').']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Calculated duration of the calendar entry in days (end_date - start_date + 1). Used for planning and capacity analysis.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this calendar entry ends. For campaign windows, this is the latest campaign close date; for blackout periods, the last restricted day. Nullable for open-ended entries.']
  entry_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the calendar entry, used for cross-system reference and reporting.']
  entry_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing calendar entry (e.g., \'Summer Travel Campaign Window\', \'Q4 Holiday Blackout\', \'Brand Refresh Launch\').']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar entry: planned (initial planning stage), confirmed (approved and locked), in_flight (currently active), completed (finished), cancelled (no longer happening), postponed (delayed to future date).']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the calendar entry: campaign_window (planned campaign execution period), tentpole_event (major seasonal or industry event), blackout_period (no marketing activity allowed), brand_moment (brand-level milestone or announcement), property_milestone (property-specific event), seasonal_promotion (recurring seasonal offer period).']
  expected_campaign_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of campaigns planned to execute during this calendar window. Used for capacity planning and collision detection.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this calendar entry is currently active and should be included in planning views. False for archived or deleted entries.']
  linked_campaign_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional campaign IDs associated with this calendar entry. Supports multi-campaign coordination within a single calendar window.']
  market_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or market scope of the calendar entry (e.g., \'Global\', \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'USA\', \'UK\', \'Multi-Market\'). Supports regional planning and coordination.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this calendar entry record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and additional context for this calendar entry. Used for planning commentary, coordination notes, and historical context.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'The marketing team or department responsible for this calendar entry (e.g., \'Brand Marketing\', \'Digital Marketing\', \'Revenue Marketing\', \'Regional Marketing EMEA\').']
  planning_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter this entry belongs to (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) for quarterly marketing planning and review cycles.']
  planning_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal or calendar year this entry belongs to for annual planning cycles (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of this calendar entry: critical (must-execute, top-tier events), high (important strategic initiatives), medium (standard campaigns), low (opportunistic or filler activities).']
  property_milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the property milestone if entry_type is property_milestone (e.g., \'Grand Opening\', \'Renovation Completion\', \'Anniversary Celebration\', \'Award Recognition\').']
  property_scope varchar [note: 'Property or property group this entry applies to (e.g., \'All Properties\', \'Resort Portfolio\', \'Urban Hotels\', specific property codes). Supports property-level milestone tracking.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Indicates if this calendar entry recurs: one_time (single occurrence), annual (repeats yearly), quarterly (repeats every quarter), monthly (repeats monthly), weekly (repeats weekly), custom (custom recurrence rule).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when this calendar entry begins. For campaign windows, this is the earliest campaign launch date; for blackout periods, the first restricted day.']
  tentpole_category varchar [note: 'Category of tentpole event: holiday (Christmas, New Year, etc.), seasonal (summer, winter travel periods), conference (major business events), industry_event (trade shows, expos), cultural_moment (local festivals, celebrations), sporting_event (Olympics, major tournaments).']
  tentpole_event_name varchar [note: 'Name of the tentpole event if entry_type is tentpole_event (e.g., \'Summer Travel Season\', \'Holiday Season\', \'Spring Break\', \'Major Conference\', \'Industry Trade Show\').']
  visibility_scope varchar [note: 'Access level for this calendar entry: public (visible to all stakeholders), internal (visible to marketing team), restricted (visible to specific teams), confidential (executive visibility only).']

  Note: 'Master planning record for the marketing activity calendar, capturing planned campaign windows, seasonal tentpole events (holidays, school breaks, major conferences), blackout periods, brand moments, and property-level marketing milestones. Records calendar entry name, entry type (campaign window, tentpole, blackout, brand moment), start/end dates, market scope, brand/property scope, priority level, owning team, status (planned, confirmed, in-flight, completed), and linked campaign IDs. Enables cross-functional visibility into marketing activity density, prevents campaign collision, and supports annual/quarterly marketing planning cycles.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_treatment_promotion {
  campaign_treatment_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign-treatment promotional relationship. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that is promoting this treatment.']
  treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa treatment being promoted in this campaign.']
  booking_count varchar [note: 'Number of bookings generated for this treatment attributed to this campaign, tracked via UTM parameters, promo codes, or campaign tracking mechanisms. Performance metric for campaign effectiveness. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional pricing for this treatment expires within this campaign. May differ from the overall campaign end date if treatments are phased. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment becomes available at the promotional price within this campaign. May differ from the overall campaign start date if treatments are phased. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this treatment was added to the campaign.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to this treatment within this campaign, calculated from the standard recommended retail price. Used for promotional messaging and guest communications. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  featured_position varchar [note: 'Display order or prominence ranking for this treatment within the campaign (e.g., 1 for hero treatment, 2 for secondary feature). Used for email layout, landing page design, and promotional material sequencing. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Campaign-specific promotional code that guests use to receive the promotional price for this treatment. Enables tracking and attribution of bookings to this campaign-treatment combination.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this treatment promotion within the campaign lifecycle. Allows individual treatments to be activated, paused, or removed from campaigns independently.']
  promotional_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Campaign-specific promotional price for this treatment, overriding the standard recommended retail price during the campaign period. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated from bookings of this treatment attributed to this campaign. Performance metric for ROI analysis and campaign optimization. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that added this treatment to the campaign.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the promotional relationship between marketing campaigns and spa treatments. It captures campaign-specific pricing, positioning, and performance metrics for each treatment featured in a campaign. Each record links one campaign to one treatment with attributes that exist only in the context of this promotional relationship, enabling marketing teams to track treatment-level performance within campaigns and spa operations to understand which campaigns drive treatment bookings.. Existence Justification: Marketing campaigns in travel hospitality routinely promote multiple spa treatments simultaneously (e.g., a summer wellness campaign featuring massage, facial, and body treatments), and individual spa treatments are promoted across multiple campaigns throughout the year (e.g., signature massage featured in Valentine\'s campaign, summer promotion, and holiday campaign). The business actively manages these promotional relationships by setting campaign-specific pricing, featured positioning, tracking performance metrics, and managing promotional terms for each treatment-campaign combination.'
}

Table marketing.communication_template {
  communication_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for communication_template']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel brand or property segment this template is associated with.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this template is associated with, if applicable.']
  parent_communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on communication_template (parent_communication_template_id)']
  a_b_test_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template is part of an A/B testing experiment.']
  a_b_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifies which variant this template represents in an A/B test.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved the template for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template was approved for production use.']
  body_content varchar [note: 'Main body content of the communication template, may include merge field placeholders.']
  call_to_action_text varchar [note: 'Primary call-to-action button or link text in the template.']
  call_to_action_url varchar [note: 'Target URL for the primary call-to-action link.']
  compliance_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template has been reviewed and approved for regulatory compliance.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes or comments from compliance review process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the templates purpose, use case, and intended audience.']
  design_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the template design for change tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the template is no longer active and should not be used in new campaigns.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the template becomes active and available for use in campaigns.']
  footer_text varchar [note: 'Standard footer text including legal disclaimers, privacy policy links, and contact information.']
  html_content varchar [note: 'HTML-formatted version of the template body for rich email rendering.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 country code) for template localization.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the template.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template record was last updated.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template was last used to send a communication.']
  merge_fields varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of available merge field placeholders for personalization (e.g., guest_name, property_name, reservation_number).']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template supports dynamic personalization with guest data merge fields.']
  plain_text_content varchar [note: 'Plain text version of the template body for fallback rendering and accessibility.']
  preheader_text varchar [note: 'Preview text displayed in email client inbox before opening the email.']
  reply_to_email varchar [note: 'Email address where recipient replies should be directed.']
  segment_criteria varchar [note: 'Target audience segment criteria or rules for this template (e.g., loyalty tier, booking history, geographic region).']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address used as the sender for email communications.']
  sender_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the sender shown to recipients.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the communication template.']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Subject line or headline text for the communication template. Applicable to email and push notifications.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or keywords for template categorization and search.']
  template_category varchar [note: 'Business category classification of the template purpose.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the template used in external systems and integrations.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the communication template.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Type of communication channel this template is designed for.']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL to a thumbnail preview image of the template for visual reference in template selection interfaces.']
  unsubscribe_link_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an unsubscribe link must be included per regulatory requirements.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this template has been used in communications sent to guests.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for communication_template. Referenced by template_id.'
}

Table experience.guest_feedback {
  guest_feedback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest feedback submission record. Primary key for the guest feedback entity.']
  channel_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_booking. Business justification: Post-stay feedback must link to originating channel booking to enable channel performance analysis by guest satisfaction, NPS by distribution partner, and channel mix optimization based on experience ']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Guest feedback tracks F&B outlet-specific ratings (fnb_rating attribute exists). Links feedback to specific restaurant/bar for outlet-level performance analysis, service recovery targeting, and operat']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Guest satisfaction analysis requires linking feedback to specific room type products for product-level quality metrics, defect tracking, and renovation prioritization. Replaces denormalized room_type_']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Event planners provide separate feedback on meeting spaces. Sales teams require space-level satisfaction scores for proposals and contract renewals. Real business process: meeting space performance da']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Guest feedback analysis by loyalty tier drives service recovery prioritization, tier-specific satisfaction benchmarking, and retention campaign targeting. Hospitality operations require immediate memb']
  nps_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.nps_survey. Business justification: Guest feedback submissions should reference the specific NPS survey instrument used for the response. This links the feedback record to the survey definition, enabling analysis by survey version, camp']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Guest feedback is routed to specific departments (Housekeeping, Front Desk, F&B) for service recovery and performance improvement. Hotels track feedback by org_unit for departmental scorecards, manage']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who submitted the feedback. Links to the guest master profile for loyalty and experience tracking.']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Guest feedback routinely references specific facilities (pool, gym, restaurant). Operations teams need facility-level satisfaction tracking to prioritize maintenance and route complaints to facility m']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the guest experience occurred. Essential for property-level satisfaction benchmarking and operational improvement.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation associated with this feedback submission. Links feedback to the specific stay or booking that prompted the response.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Facility-specific feedback (noise, cleanliness, maintenance issues) must link to exact room for targeted corrective action, out-of-order decisions, and room-level quality tracking. Critical for operat']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service recovery case created in Salesforce CRM if this feedback triggered a complaint or service recovery workflow. Links feedback to resolution tracking.']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Guest feedback is collected at specific touchpoints in the journey (post-checkout survey, post-stay email, in-app feedback). Linking feedback to touchpoint enables touchpoint-level satisfaction analys']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Guests frequently mention specific vendor-provided services in feedback (restaurant quality, spa experience, shuttle service, concierge partnerships). Linking vendor enables correlation of vendor perf']
  amenities_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating of property amenities including pool, fitness center, business center, and other facilities. Informs capital expenditure and FF&E decisions.']
  cleanliness_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating specific to property cleanliness standards across all areas. Essential metric for housekeeping operations and health compliance.']
  complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this feedback contains a complaint requiring service recovery action. Triggers case management workflow in Salesforce CRM.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this feedback record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  csat_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Transaction-specific satisfaction rating typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Measures satisfaction with a specific interaction or service touchpoint.']
  feedback_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the feedback submission. Used for case tracking and guest communication reference.']
  fnb_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating specific to food and beverage quality, variety, and service. Key metric for F&B operations and outlet performance.']
  gss_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall guest satisfaction score typically on a 0-100 scale or 1-5 scale. Composite metric aggregating multiple satisfaction dimensions. Key performance indicator for property operations and service quality.']
  guest_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification of the guest. FIT (Free Independent Traveler), MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions). Used for segment-specific satisfaction analysis and targeted service improvements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Transient|Group|Contract|Wholesale|FIT|Corporate|Leisure|MICE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code indicating the language in which the feedback was submitted. Used for multilingual sentiment analysis and regional experience tracking.']
  location_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating of property location convenience, accessibility, and surrounding area. Influences market positioning and guest segment targeting.']
  nps_classification varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the NPS score. Promoter (9-10) indicates strong loyalty, Passive (7-8) indicates satisfaction without enthusiasm, Detractor (0-6) indicates dissatisfaction and churn risk.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Guest rating on a 0-10 scale indicating likelihood to recommend the property. 0-6 are detractors, 7-8 are passives, 9-10 are promoters. Core metric for loyalty measurement and SALT program reporting.']
  overall_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'General overall rating provided by the guest, typically on a 1-5 star scale. Used for aggregated property ratings and guest experience benchmarking.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Rate plan code under which the guest booked. Used for analyzing satisfaction by rate segment and pricing strategy effectiveness.']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of hours between survey invitation and guest response submission. Metric for survey engagement and recency of experience recall.']
  room_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating specific to room quality, cleanliness, comfort, and amenities. Key operational metric for housekeeping and room standards.']
  sentiment_indicator varchar [note: 'Automated or manual classification of the overall sentiment expressed in the feedback. Derived from verbatim comments and ratings through natural language processing or analyst review.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical sentiment score typically ranging from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive). Generated through natural language processing of verbatim comments.']
  service_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest rating specific to staff service quality, responsiveness, and professionalism. Critical metric for training and service excellence programs.']
  service_recovery_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this feedback requires formal service recovery intervention. Used for GRR (Guest Recovery Rate) calculation and case prioritization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this feedback was captured. Medallia is the primary experience analytics platform, with additional sources including OPERA PMS direct feedback, Salesforce case submissions, manual entry, and third-party review aggregators.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'Start date of the guest stay associated with this feedback. Used for temporal analysis and linking feedback to operational conditions during the stay period.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'End date of the guest stay associated with this feedback. Used for calculating length of stay context and temporal correlation with feedback timing.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the guest submitted the feedback. Critical for recency analysis and service recovery response time tracking.']
  survey_completion_status varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the guest completed the entire survey, partially completed it, or abandoned it. Used for survey design optimization and response quality assessment.']
  survey_invitation_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey invitation was sent to the guest. Used for calculating response time and survey engagement metrics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this feedback record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks enrichment activities such as sentiment scoring updates or case linkage.']
  value_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Guest perception of value for money received. Critical metric for pricing strategy and revenue management decisions.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment provided by the guest describing their experience in their own words. Rich qualitative data for sentiment analysis, theme extraction, and service recovery prioritization.']
  would_recommend_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the guest explicitly stated they would recommend the property to others. Complementary metric to NPS for loyalty measurement.']
  would_return_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the guest explicitly stated they would return to the property. Key metric for repeat business potential and guest retention forecasting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record of guest feedback submissions captured across all touchpoints including post-stay surveys, in-stay pulse checks, real-time feedback, and NPS survey responses. Sourced primarily from Medallia and integrated with OPERA PMS stay data. Captures NPS scores (0-10 with promoter/passive/detractor classification), GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score), CSAT ratings, verbatim comments, sentiment indicators, survey type discriminator (NPS, CSAT, pulse, general), response channel (email, SMS, kiosk, app), stay reference, property, and submission timestamp. Serves as the single authoritative source for all guest voice data including NPS responses in the experience domain. Drives SALT program reporting and trend analysis by property, segment, and time period.'
}

Table experience.nps_survey {
  nps_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NPS survey instrument and campaign definition. Primary key.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: NPS surveys can be linked to specific experience programs (SALT programs) to measure program-specific guest satisfaction. The nps_survey already has salt_program_linked_flag (boolean) but no FK to ide']
  survey_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master survey template or design framework from which this survey instance was created. Supports template-based survey design and standardization.']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: NPS surveys are deployed at specific touchpoints in the guest journey (post-checkout, post-stay, post-service). This FK links the survey definition to the touchpoint where it is triggered, enabling to']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the A/B test variant if this survey is part of a controlled experiment to optimize survey design, question wording, or distribution strategy (e.g., \'Control\', \'Variant_A\', \'Variant_B\'). Null if not part of an A/B test.']
  anonymous_response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest responses to this survey are collected anonymously (True) without linking to guest profiles, or are attributed to specific guests (False) for follow-up and service recovery.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'The date when this survey was last reviewed for compliance with data privacy and guest communication regulations. Null if not yet reviewed.']
  compliance_reviewed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey has been reviewed and approved for compliance with data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA) and guest communication standards (True) or is pending review (False).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who created this survey instrument definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this survey instrument record was first created in the system.']
  detractor_threshold_max varchar [note: 'The maximum NPS score (typically 6 on a 0-10 scale) for a guest response to be classified as a Detractor. Standard NPS methodology uses 0-6 as detractors.']
  distribution_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this survey is distributed: one_time (single campaign), daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly (scheduled intervals), or event_triggered (distributed based on business events).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this survey campaign ends and stops distributing to guests. Null for ongoing campaigns without a defined end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this survey campaign becomes active and begins distribution to guests. Supports scheduled campaign launches.']
