  participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of participants included in this enrollment (guest plus accompanying guests).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Payment status for the experience program enrollment (not required, pending, authorized, paid, refunded, waived).']
  post_program_csat_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score provided by the guest after completing the experience program, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00.']
  post_program_gss_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) provided by the guest after completing the experience program, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00 or 1.00 to 10.00.']
  post_program_nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the guest after completing the experience program, ranging from 0 to 10.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guest\'s participation in the experience program (enrolled, confirmed, active, in progress, completed, cancelled, no show). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|confirmed|active|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the experience program is set to conclude for the guest.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the experience program is set to begin for the guest.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special requests or preferences the guest has for the experience program (dietary restrictions, accessibility needs, activity preferences).']
  total_program_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged to the guest for the experience program enrollment.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific guest and reservation to an experience program, capturing enrollment date, enrollment channel, program status (enrolled, active, completed, cancelled), fulfillment progress, assigned experience host, and guest satisfaction outcome post-program. Enables tracking of which guests are participating in which curated experience programs and measures program effectiveness through post-program NPS and GSS scores.'
}

Table experience.online_review {
  online_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the online review record. Primary key for the online review entity.']
  channel_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_booking. Business justification: Reviews reference specific stays; linking to channel booking enables verified stay validation, channel attribution for reputation management, and identification of review patterns by distribution sour']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Online reviews frequently mention specific restaurants/bars by name. Links review sentiment to outlet for reputation management, outlet-level sentiment analysis, competitive benchmarking, and identify']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Event reviews on platforms like Cvent specifically rate meeting spaces. Sales teams aggregate space-level reviews for RFP responses and contract negotiations. Real business process: meeting space repu']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Linking reviews to loyalty members enables program effectiveness analysis (do higher tiers give better reviews?), targeted recovery for high-LTV detractors, and member review sentiment tracking for re']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Reviews posted on OTA platforms must link to partner entity for partner-specific reputation tracking, content quality monitoring, and management response SLA compliance by partner. Essential for OTA c']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile who authored this review, if the guest can be matched to internal records.']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Online reviews routinely mention specific facilities by name (pool, restaurant, gym). Reputation management teams need facility-level sentiment analysis to prioritize capital improvements and respond ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property that this review is about. Links to the property master data.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation associated with this guest stay, if available. Links to the reservation record in the PMS.']
  amenities_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Sub-rating for property amenities and facilities on a normalized 0-5 scale, if provided by the platform.']
  cleanliness_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Sub-rating for cleanliness on a normalized 0-5 scale, if provided by the platform. Key dimension for housekeeping performance.']
  competitive_set_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review is part of the competitive set analysis for benchmarking. True if included in competitive reputation tracking.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this review record was ingested into the unified reputation management system from the external platform.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this review record was last updated in the system, typically when management response or platform metadata changed.']
  location_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Sub-rating for property location on a normalized 0-5 scale, if provided by the platform.']
  management_response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of management response to this review. Tracks service recovery workflow completion.']
  management_response_text varchar [note: 'Full text of the management response posted to the review platform. Documents service recovery communication.']
  management_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the management response was posted to the review platform. Tracks response time performance.']
  normalized_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Overall rating normalized to a standard 0-5 scale for cross-platform comparison and aggregation. Enables unified reputation scoring.']
  platform_native_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall rating score as provided by the guest on the platforms native scale. Stored in original format before normalization.']
  platform_rating_scale varchar [note: 'The native rating scale used by the review platform (e.g., 1-5 stars, 1-10 points). Enables interpretation of platform_native_rating.']
  platform_review_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the external review platform to this review. Used for deduplication and tracking.']
  response_author_name varchar [note: 'Name or title of the property representative who authored the management response (e.g., General Manager, Guest Relations Manager).']
  review_body_text varchar [note: 'Full text content of the review written by the guest. Contains detailed feedback, experiences, and opinions.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the review was published on the external platform. This is the primary business event timestamp for reputation tracking.']
  review_helpfulness_count varchar [note: 'Number of users on the review platform who marked this review as helpful. Indicates review influence and visibility.']
  review_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code of the review text. Supports multilingual reputation management.']
  review_source_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL link to the original review on the external platform. Enables quick access for verification and response.']
  review_title varchar [note: 'Title or headline of the review as provided by the guest. Summarizes the main sentiment or topic.']
  review_visibility_status varchar [note: 'Current visibility status of the review on the external platform. Tracks whether review is publicly visible.']
  reviewer_alias varchar [note: 'Public username or alias of the reviewer as displayed on the review platform. May be pseudonymous.']
  reviewer_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location of the reviewer as provided by the review platform, typically city and country.']
  sentiment_classification varchar [note: 'AI-derived sentiment classification of the review text. Supports automated reputation monitoring and alert triggering.']
  sentiment_score decimal(4,3) [note: 'Numeric sentiment score ranging from -1.0 (most negative) to +1.0 (most positive) derived from natural language processing of review text.']
  service_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Sub-rating for service quality on a normalized 0-5 scale, if provided by the platform. Key dimension for guest experience.']
  stay_end_date date [note: 'End date of the guest stay period referenced in the review, if provided by the platform.']
  stay_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the guest stay period referenced in the review, if provided by the platform.']
  traveler_type varchar [note: 'Type of traveler as indicated by the reviewer or inferred from the review. Supports segmented reputation analysis.']
  value_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Sub-rating for value for money on a normalized 0-5 scale, if provided by the platform. Key dimension for pricing perception.']
  verified_stay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review platform verified that the reviewer actually stayed at the property. True if verified, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Record of guest reviews published on external OTA and review platforms (TripAdvisor, Google, Booking.com, Expedia, Yelp) captured and ingested for unified reputation management. Captures platform name, review rating (normalized and platform-native scales), review title, review body text, reviewer alias, review date, property, stay period reference, management response status, management response text, response timestamp, and AI-derived sentiment classification. Enables benchmarking of online reputation scores alongside direct Medallia survey feedback and supports competitive set reputation analysis.'
}

Table experience.reputation_alert {
  reputation_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reputation alert record. Primary key.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Reputation alerts triggered by F&B issues (food quality complaints, service delays, health concerns) require outlet-level accountability. Essential for escalation routing to outlet managers, tracking ']
  guest_feedback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.guest_feedback. Business justification: Reputation alerts can be triggered by internal guest feedback when NPS/GSS scores breach thresholds (e.g., detractor score, low CSAT). This FK links the alert to the specific feedback submission that ']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Product-level reputation issues (all ocean view rooms overpriced, suites don\'t meet expectations) require room type linkage for portfolio-wide response, pricing adjustments, and identifying systemic p']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Reputation alerts for loyalty members trigger tier-appropriate escalation paths and recovery budgets. Real process: platinum member negative review alerts route to GM immediately with higher compensat']
  online_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.online_review. Business justification: Reputation alerts are often triggered by negative online reviews on OTA platforms (TripAdvisor, Google, Booking.com). This FK links the alert to the specific review that triggered it, enabling review-']
  primary_reputation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or manager assigned to investigate and resolve the alert. Links to workforce management system.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest associated with the feedback or review that triggered the alert. May be null for anonymous reviews.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the alert was triggered. Links to the property master data.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reservation associated with the feedback event. May be null if feedback is not tied to a specific reservation.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Reputation alerts triggered by room-specific issues in reviews (room 305 has mold, noisy HVAC in corner rooms) require room linkage for immediate investigation, out-of-order decisions, and tracking re']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service recovery case created in Salesforce CRM in response to this alert. Null if no case has been created yet.']
  whistleblower_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.whistleblower_report. Business justification: Reputation alerts involving ethical violations, harassment, discrimination, or misconduct may escalate to or originate from formal whistleblower reports. Operations require linking public reputation i']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assigned user acknowledged receipt of the alert. Used to track response time SLA compliance.']
  actual_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes from alert trigger to acknowledgment. Calculated as difference between triggered_timestamp and acknowledged_timestamp.']
  alert_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the alert, formatted as ALT-YYYYMMDD sequence. Used for tracking and communication.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the alert. Tracks progression from initial trigger through acknowledgment, investigation, resolution, and closure.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alert based on the triggering condition. Values: low_nps_threshold_breach (NPS score below threshold), negative_ota_review (negative review on OTA platform), repeat_complaint_pattern (multiple complaints from same guest or about same issue), gss_drop (Guest Satisfaction Score drop), csat_threshold_breach (Customer Satisfaction below threshold), social_media_escalation (negative social media mention).']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was assigned to a specific user for action. Null if alert has not yet been assigned.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was formally closed after verification and documentation. Final lifecycle timestamp.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of the guest complaint or concern identified in the feedback. Examples: room_cleanliness, staff_service, noise, amenities, billing, food_quality. Supports root cause analysis and trend identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_cleanliness|staff_service|noise|amenities|billing|food_quality|maintenance|check_in_process|check_out_process|reservation_issue|loyalty_program|parking|wifi|temperature_control|bedding_comfort|bathroom_condition — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this alert record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Hotel department identified as responsible for addressing the issue that triggered the alert. Used for routing and accountability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_desk|housekeeping|food_and_beverage|maintenance|reservations|guest_services|management|sales|events — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert was escalated to senior management or corporate level due to severity or lack of timely resolution. True if escalated, false otherwise.']
  escalated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was escalated. Null if alert was not escalated.']
  guest_contact_method varchar [note: 'Method used to contact the guest for service recovery. Null if guest was not contacted.']
  guest_contacted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest was directly contacted by management as part of the resolution process. True if contact was made, false otherwise.']
  guest_satisfaction_post_recovery varchar [note: 'Guest satisfaction level measured after service recovery action was taken. Used to calculate Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) effectiveness.']
  loyalty_points_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points awarded to the guest as part of service recovery. Null if no points were awarded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, investigation findings, or special circumstances related to the alert. Used for knowledge capture and future reference.']
  recovery_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of service recovery compensation provided to the guest (room credit, refund, amenity value, loyalty points cash equivalent). Used to calculate Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) cost.']
  recovery_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the recovery cost amount. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP.']
  resolution_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action or service recovery measure taken to address the alert. Examples: guest contacted and apology issued, room upgraded, refund processed, staff retraining scheduled, maintenance work order created.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was marked as resolved after corrective action was completed. Used to calculate resolution time metrics.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'AI-generated sentiment analysis score of the review text, ranging from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive). Generated by Medallia natural language processing engine.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the alert indicating urgency of management response. Critical requires immediate action, high requires same-day response, medium within 24 hours, low within 48 hours.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert response met the SLA target. True if actual_response_minutes <= sla_response_minutes, false otherwise.']
  sla_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Target response time in minutes based on alert severity level, as defined by SALT program SLA standards. Used to measure compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or channel that originated the feedback or review that triggered the alert. Identifies the data source for root cause analysis.']
  threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Configured threshold value that was breached to trigger the alert. Represents the minimum acceptable performance level for the metric.']
  triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was triggered by the Medallia alert engine based on threshold breach or pattern detection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this alert record was last modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes throughout the alert lifecycle.']
  variance_from_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated difference between the metric value and threshold value. Negative values indicate below-threshold performance. Helps prioritize alert severity.']

  Note: 'Operational alert record triggered when guest feedback, NPS scores, or online reviews breach defined thresholds requiring management attention. Captures alert type (low NPS threshold breach, negative OTA review, repeat complaint pattern, GSS drop), severity level, triggering metric value, threshold value, property, alert timestamp, assigned recipient, acknowledgment status, and resolution action taken. Sourced from Medallia alert engine and integrated with Salesforce CRM for case creation.'
}

Table experience.gss_score {
  gss_score_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GSS score measurement record.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this GSS score was measured.']
  bottom_box_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who gave the lowest satisfaction ratings (bottom-box scores), typically 1-2 on a 5-point scale or 0-6 on a 10-point scale.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand identifier for the property (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service segment), used for brand-level GSS aggregation and QA reviews.']
  brand_qa_review_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this GSS score has been flagged for brand-level quality assurance review due to performance concerns or significant variance.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GSS score was calculated and recorded in the system.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the GSS score, typically at 95% confidence level.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the GSS score, typically at 95% confidence level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GSS score record was first created in the data platform.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system from which the GSS score data was derived (Medallia, Salesforce CRM, Manual Entry, Integrated Multi-Source).']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Department or touchpoint area for which this GSS score was calculated (e.g., Front Desk, Housekeeping, F&B, Overall Property). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FRONT_DESK|HOUSEKEEPING|FNB|CONCIERGE|SPA|MAINTENANCE|OVERALL|EVENTS|VALET|ROOM_SERVICE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  detractor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents classified as detractors (score 0-6 on likelihood-to-recommend scale).']
  gm_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this GSS score requires General Manager review and action plan due to falling below acceptable thresholds or being classified as Critical.']
  grr_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Guest Recovery Rate percentage for the measurement period, calculated as the percentage of service recovery cases that resulted in successful guest satisfaction restoration.']
  gss_score_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated GSS score value for the measurement period, typically on a 0-100 scale derived from aggregated Medallia survey responses.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment of guests included in this GSS measurement (Transient, Group, Contract, Wholesale, Complementary).']
  measurement_end_date date [note: 'End date of the measurement period for this GSS score.']
  measurement_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or granularity of the GSS measurement period (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  measurement_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the measurement period for this GSS score.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this GSS score measurement, including context for anomalies, data quality issues, or special circumstances affecting the score.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score calculated for the same measurement period and scope, derived from likelihood-to-recommend survey responses.']
  passive_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents classified as passives (score 7-8 on likelihood-to-recommend scale).']
  prior_period_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'GSS score value from the immediately preceding measurement period of the same type, used for sequential trend comparison.']
  prior_period_variance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Variance between current GSS score and prior period score, calculated as (gss_score_value - prior_period_score).']
  promoter_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents classified as promoters (score 9-10 on likelihood-to-recommend scale).']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this GSS score has been published and made available to property management and brand QA teams (True if published, False if draft or under review).']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GSS score was published and made available to stakeholders.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the property, used for regional GSS benchmarking and performance comparison.']
  response_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of surveys that received responses, calculated as (sample_size / surveys_sent_count) * 100.']
  salt_target_attained_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the GSS score met or exceeded the SALT program target (True if gss_score_value >= salt_target_score).']
  salt_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target GSS score defined by the SALT program for this property, department, and measurement period.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of survey responses included in the GSS score calculation for this measurement period.']
  score_band varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the GSS score into performance bands (Excellent, Good, Needs Improvement, Critical) based on brand-defined thresholds.']
  service_recovery_case_count varchar [note: 'Number of service recovery cases opened during the measurement period for this property and department, sourced from Salesforce CRM case management.']
  surveys_sent_count varchar [note: 'Total number of surveys sent to guests during the measurement period.']
  top_box_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who gave the highest satisfaction rating (top-box score), typically 5 on a 5-point scale or 9-10 on a 10-point scale.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GSS score record was last updated in the data platform.']
  yoy_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'GSS score value from the same measurement period in the prior year, used for year-over-year comparison.']
  yoy_variance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Variance between current GSS score and year-over-year score, calculated as (gss_score_value - yoy_score).']

  Note: 'Periodic GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score) measurement record at property and department level, derived from aggregating Medallia survey responses. Captures GSS score value, measurement period (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly), property, department or touchpoint area, sample size, response rate, confidence interval, prior period score for trend comparison, YoY comparison, score band classification (excellent, good, needs improvement, critical), and SALT program target attainment flag. Serves as the operational scorecard record driving GM accountability, brand QA reviews, and property-level experience performance management.'
}

Table experience.quality_audit {
  quality_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality audit record. Primary key for the quality audit entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Quality audits (brand standards, service excellence) often run concurrently with or trigger regulatory compliance audits (health, safety, accessibility). Hospitality operations track which compliance ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality audit costs (auditor fees, corrective action expenses, re-inspection costs) must be tracked by cost center for departmental budget accountability and quality assurance program cost management.']
  follow_up_quality_audit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_audit (follow_up_quality_audit_id)']
  inventory_room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Brand quality audits inspect room type compliance with standards (suite amenities, deluxe finishes, ADA features). Room type linkage enables product-level scoring, deficiency tracking, and certificati']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Meeting space audits cover fire safety, AV functionality, accessibility compliance. Brand standards require space-level audit documentation for insurance and liability purposes. Real business process:']
  property_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Quality audits inspect individual facilities (restaurant health scores, pool safety certifications, spa licensing). Compliance teams track facility-level audit scores for regulatory reporting and bran']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where the quality audit was conducted. Links to the property master record.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Audits sample specific rooms for inspection. Room linkage documents which units were audited, tracks deficiencies by room, and enables follow-up re-inspection scheduling. Critical for audit trail and ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Quality audits assess vendor-delivered services (F&B providers, housekeeping suppliers, maintenance contractors, spa operators). Audit findings directly impact vendor performance scorecards, contract ']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the audit fieldwork concluded. Captures the precise moment the audit inspection was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the audit fieldwork commenced. Captures the precise moment the audit inspection began.']
  amenity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score evaluating the quality, availability, and condition of property amenities including fitness centers, pools, business centers, and guest conveniences.']
  audit_date date [note: 'The date on which the quality audit was conducted or completed. Represents the primary business event timestamp for the audit inspection.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional context, observations, commendations, or special circumstances noted during the audit that provide qualitative insight beyond numerical scores.']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'Web link or file path to the detailed audit report document containing full findings, deficiency descriptions, photographic evidence, and corrective action recommendations.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality audit. Scheduled indicates planned future audit, in progress means audit is actively being conducted, completed means audit fieldwork is done, under review indicates findings are being validated, closed means all corrective actions are complete, and cancelled means audit was terminated before completion.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality audit indicating the auditing body or program. Forbes Travel Guide evaluates luxury service standards, AAA Diamond assesses overall quality, brand standard ensures compliance with corporate guidelines, mystery shop provides anonymous guest perspective, internal QA validates operational adherence, and health safety covers regulatory compliance inspections.']
  auditor_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or credential number of the auditor, validating their qualification to conduct quality audits in the hospitality industry.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary auditor or inspector who conducted the quality audit. May represent an individual evaluator or lead inspector for team audits.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or entity that the auditor represents, such as Forbes Travel Guide, AAA, brand quality assurance team, or third-party mystery shopping firm.']
  brand_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property met all mandatory brand standards and compliance requirements during the audit. True means full compliance achieved, false indicates brand standard violations requiring remediation.']
  certification_level_achieved varchar [note: 'The quality classification or rating level awarded based on audit results, such as Forbes 5-Star, Forbes 4-Star, AAA 5-Diamond, AAA 4-Diamond, or brand-specific tier designations. Drives market positioning and guest expectations.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when all required corrective actions were completed and verified. Used to measure remediation cycle time and compliance with deadlines.']
  corrective_action_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of corrective action implementation. Not started means no work has begun, in progress indicates active remediation, completed means property reports actions done, verified means auditor confirmed completion, and overdue means deadline has passed without completion.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'The date by which all required corrective actions must be completed and verified. Used to track remediation timelines and ensure accountability for quality improvement.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit findings require formal corrective action plans to be developed and implemented. True means corrective actions are mandatory, false means audit was clean or findings are informational only.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quality audit record was first created in the system. Represents the initial data capture event for audit trail purposes.']
  critical_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or high-severity deficiencies identified that pose immediate risk to guest safety, brand reputation, or regulatory compliance. Requires urgent remediation.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of deficiencies, non-conformances, or findings identified during the audit. Provides a quantitative measure of quality gaps requiring corrective action.']
  facility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score assessing physical property condition, maintenance standards, cleanliness, and facility upkeep. Evaluates the tangible aspects of the guest experience.']
  fnb_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score assessing food and beverage operations including restaurant service, menu quality, culinary execution, bar service, and dining experience standards.']
  gm_accountability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit results are tied to General Manager performance evaluation and accountability metrics. True means GM is formally accountable for audit outcomes and corrective actions.']
  housekeeping_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score assessing room cleanliness, housekeeping standards, turndown service, linen quality, and overall room presentation. Critical component for guest satisfaction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quality audit record was most recently updated. Used to track data currency and audit record lifecycle changes.']
  major_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of major or medium-severity deficiencies identified that significantly impact guest experience or operational standards but do not pose immediate critical risk.']
  minor_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor or low-severity deficiencies identified that represent opportunities for improvement but do not materially impact guest experience or compliance.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality score representing the overall audit result. Scale and interpretation vary by audit type (e.g., Forbes uses 0-100, AAA uses point system). This is the primary metric for property quality classification.']
  pass_fail_determination varchar [note: 'Final audit outcome indicating whether the property met the required quality standards. Pass means all standards met, fail indicates significant deficiencies, conditional pass requires minor corrective actions, and pending means determination is under review.']
  prior_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the previous quality audit of the same type conducted at this property. Used to calculate audit frequency and track historical score trends.']
  prior_certification_level varchar [note: 'The quality classification or rating level held by the property before this audit. Used to track certification progression or regression over time.']
  prior_overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overall quality score from the previous audit of the same type. Used to calculate score variance and track quality improvement or degradation trends over time.']
  re_inspection_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual date of the follow-up audit conducted to verify that corrective actions have been effectively implemented and quality standards are now met.']
  re_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit or re-inspection is required to verify corrective action effectiveness and validate quality improvement. True means re-inspection is mandatory, false means no follow-up audit needed.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'The planned date when the audit is scheduled to conclude. Used for audit planning and resource allocation.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned date when the audit is scheduled to begin. Used for audit planning and resource allocation.']
  score_variance decimal(5,2) [note: 'The change in overall score compared to the prior audit, calculated as current score minus prior score. Positive values indicate quality improvement, negative values indicate degradation.']
  service_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score evaluating service quality, staff professionalism, guest interaction, and service delivery standards. Key component of overall audit assessment.']
  spa_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Category score evaluating spa and wellness facilities, treatment quality, therapist professionalism, and spa service standards. Applicable only to properties with spa amenities.']

  Note: 'Master record for property quality inspections and audits conducted by Forbes Travel Guide evaluators, AAA Diamond inspectors, brand QA teams, and mystery shoppers. Captures audit type (Forbes, AAA, brand standard, mystery shop, internal QA), audit date, property, auditor identity, overall score, category scores (service, facility, amenity, F&B, spa, housekeeping), pass/fail determination, deficiency findings with severity classification, corrective action requirements, corrective action deadlines, corrective action completion status, re-inspection date, and historical score trend. Drives property classification maintenance (Forbes 5-Star, AAA 5-Diamond), brand compliance enforcement, and GM accountability for service standard adherence. Supports the quality improvement cycle from inspection through remediation to re-certification. Integrates with property domain for facility-related deficiencies and workforce domain for staff training gaps identified during audits.'
}

Table experience.touchpoint {
  touchpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest journey touchpoint. Primary key.']
  parent_touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent touchpoint if this touchpoint is a sub-touchpoint or variant of a broader interaction (self-referential hierarchy).']
  accessibility_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint meets Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility requirements.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'The degree of automation at this touchpoint (fully automated, semi-automated, manual, hybrid).']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand code if this touchpoint is brand-specific (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service brand identifiers).']
  compliance_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint requires compliance review for regulatory adherence (e.g., General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)).']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'The currency code for cost per interaction using ISO 4217 three-letter codes (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cost_per_interaction decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated or actual cost per guest interaction at this touchpoint, used for operational efficiency analysis.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who created this touchpoint definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this touchpoint record was created in the system.']
  data_privacy_sensitive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint involves collection or processing of privacy-sensitive guest data requiring special handling.']
  default_language_code varchar [note: 'The default language code for this touchpoint using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh).']
  department_owner varchar [note: 'The department or functional area responsible for managing and delivering this touchpoint (e.g., Front Office, Housekeeping, Food and Beverage (F&B), Concierge, Guest Services, Revenue Management).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this touchpoint definition expires or is no longer effective (null for indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this touchpoint definition became or will become effective.']
  feedback_collection_method varchar [note: 'The method used to collect guest feedback at this touchpoint (survey, direct feedback, observation, system log, social media, review platform).']
  guest_effort_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target Guest Effort Score for this touchpoint, representing the desired ease of interaction (lower scores indicate less effort required).']
  guest_segment_applicability varchar [note: 'The guest segments to which this touchpoint applies (e.g., all guests, loyalty members only, VIP guests, business travelers, leisure travelers, group bookings). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_guests|loyalty_members|vip_guests|business_travelers|leisure_travelers|group_bookings|families|couples — promote to reference product]']
  interaction_channel varchar [note: 'The specific channel through which the touchpoint interaction occurs (e.g., email, mobile app, front desk, in-room tablet, phone, SMS, chatbot, kiosk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|mobile_app|web_portal|front_desk|concierge|phone|sms|chatbot|kiosk|in_room_tablet|social_media — promote to reference product]']
  is_csat_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) survey measurement.']
  is_gss_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) measurement.']
  is_mandatory_touchpoint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint is mandatory for all guests (e.g., check-in, check-out) or optional.']
  is_nps_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey measurement.']
  journey_stage varchar [note: 'The stage of the guest journey lifecycle where this touchpoint occurs (pre-arrival, arrival, in-stay, departure, post-stay).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who last modified this touchpoint definition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this touchpoint record was last modified.']
  medallia_touchpoint_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding touchpoint identifier in the Medallia experience analytics platform for integration and feedback mapping.']
  multi_language_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint supports multiple languages for international guests.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The business priority level assigned to this touchpoint for guest experience management (critical, high, medium, low).']
  property_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether this touchpoint applies to all properties, specific brands, individual properties, or regions.']
  salesforce_touchpoint_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding touchpoint identifier in Salesforce Customer Relationship Management (CRM) for case management and guest interaction tracking.']
  salt_program_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this touchpoint is linked to the Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) program for performance measurement.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'The typical sequence order of this touchpoint within its journey stage, used for journey mapping and visualization.']
  service_recovery_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether negative feedback or issues at this touchpoint automatically trigger service recovery workflows.']
  staff_training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff training is required to deliver this touchpoint effectively.']
  touchpoint_category varchar [note: 'The category or modality of the touchpoint (digital, physical, voice, self-service, staff-assisted, automated).']
  touchpoint_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code identifying the touchpoint type (e.g., PRE_ARRIVAL_EMAIL, CHECK_IN_DESK, IN_ROOM_DINING, CHECKOUT_KIOSK).']
  touchpoint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the touchpoint, its purpose, and the guest interaction it represents.']
  touchpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the touchpoint (e.g., Pre-Arrival Welcome Email, Front Desk Check-In, In-Room Dining Order).']
  touchpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the touchpoint (active, inactive, pilot, deprecated, seasonal).']
  typical_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The typical or expected duration of the guest interaction at this touchpoint, measured in minutes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of this touchpoint definition, incremented when the touchpoint configuration is updated.']

  Note: 'Master definition of guest journey touchpoints across the stay lifecycle (pre-arrival, arrival, in-stay, departure, post-stay) used to map feedback and interactions to specific moments.'
}

Table experience.program_treatment_inclusion {
  program_treatment_inclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this program-treatment inclusion record. Primary key.']
  experience_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to experience_program']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the experience program that includes this treatment']
  treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spa treatment included in this experience program']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Specific dates or date ranges when this treatment is not available as part of this experience program (e.g., seasonal restrictions, therapist availability).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program-treatment inclusion record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment is no longer available for inclusion in this experience program. Null indicates ongoing availability.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment becomes available for inclusion in this experience program.']
  inclusion_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which this treatment should be delivered within the experience program journey. Used for scheduling and guest communication.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this treatment inclusion in the program (active, suspended, discontinued). Allows temporary removal without deleting the inclusion record.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment is mandatory (must be taken) or optional (guest can choose) within the experience program.']
  quantity_included varchar [note: 'Number of sessions or instances of this treatment included in the experience program (e.g., 2 massages, 1 facial).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program-treatment inclusion record was last modified.']
  upgrade_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest can upgrade this treatment to a premium version within the program (e.g., upgrade 60-min massage to 90-min).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this inclusion record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion relationship between experience programs and spa treatments. It captures which treatments are included in which experience programs, along with sequencing, quantities, upgrade eligibility, and temporal availability. Each record links one experience program to one treatment with attributes that exist only in the context of this program-treatment pairing.. Existence Justification: In hospitality operations, experience programs (wellness retreats, romance packages, SALT programs) are curated bundles that include multiple spa treatments, and each treatment can be included in multiple different programs. The business actively manages these inclusions with specific rules about sequencing, quantities, upgrade eligibility, and seasonal availability. This is an operational product composition relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table experience.program_fitness_inclusion {
  program_fitness_inclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this program-fitness class inclusion relationship. Primary key.']
  experience_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to experience_program']
  fitness_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fitness class included in this experience program']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the experience program that includes this fitness class']
  advance_booking_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests enrolled in this program must book this fitness class in advance or can attend walk-in. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase relationship data.']
  class_selection_flexibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether guests must attend specific scheduled sessions (fixed), can choose any available session (flexible), or can choose from a subset (choice_of_n). This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program-fitness class inclusion relationship was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this fitness class is no longer included in this experience program. Nullable for ongoing inclusions. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this fitness class becomes available as part of this experience program. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase relationship data.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this fitness class inclusion in the experience program, allowing program managers to activate or deactivate specific class offerings without removing the relationship.']
  priority_booking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests enrolled in this program receive priority booking access for this fitness class over non-program guests.']
  sessions_included varchar [note: 'Number of fitness class sessions included in the experience program for each enrolled guest. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program-fitness class inclusion relationship was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion relationship between experience programs and fitness classes. It captures which fitness classes are included in which experience programs, along with the number of sessions guests receive, booking requirements, and selection flexibility. Each record links one experience program to one fitness class with attributes that define the terms of inclusion.. Existence Justification: In travel hospitality operations, experience programs are curated packages that bundle multiple amenities and services, including fitness classes. A single experience program (e.g., \'Wellness Journey\' or \'VIP Welcome Package\') includes multiple fitness class types (yoga, pilates, HIIT) to give guests variety and choice. Conversely, a popular fitness class like \'Morning Yoga\' can be included in multiple experience programs (Wellness Journey, SALT Recovery Program, Anniversary Package) with different terms for each program. Program managers actively configure and manage these inclusions, specifying how many sessions are included, booking rules, and guest selection flexibility for each program-class pairing.'
}

Table experience.survey_template {
  survey_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey_template']
  superseded_survey_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survey_template (superseded_survey_template_id)']
  allows_anonymous_responses boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests can submit responses to this survey without providing identifying information, supporting privacy preferences.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this survey template for production use, supporting governance and quality control.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this survey template was approved for production use, supporting governance workflow tracking.']
  auto_case_creation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether low-scoring survey responses automatically create service recovery cases in Salesforce CRM for immediate guest outreach.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when this survey template was last reviewed for regulatory compliance, supporting governance and audit requirements.']
  compliance_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey template has been reviewed for compliance with privacy regulations and data protection requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this survey template record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and version history.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the survey template purpose, target audience, and intended use cases within the guest experience management program.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the survey is delivered to guests, used for channel-specific optimization and response tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this survey template is retired or replaced, supporting template lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this survey template becomes active and available for distribution to guests, supporting version control and rollout planning.']
  estimated_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes for a guest to complete the survey, used for guest communication and response rate optimization.']
  incentive_offered varchar [note: 'Type of incentive offered to guests for completing this survey, used for response rate optimization and program cost tracking.']
  incentive_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary or points value of the incentive offered for survey completion, used for program cost analysis and ROI measurement.']
  includes_csat_question boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey template includes a Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) rating question for overall satisfaction measurement.']
  includes_nps_question boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey template includes the standard NPS question for measuring guest loyalty and likelihood to recommend.']
  includes_open_feedback boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this survey template includes open-ended text fields for qualitative guest feedback and comments.']
  is_default_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default survey template for its type and segment, used for automated survey distribution logic.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the primary language of the survey template for localization and guest communication.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date this survey template was distributed to a guest, used for template lifecycle management and archival decisions.']
  medallia_template_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this survey template in the Medallia experience analytics platform, used for system integration and data synchronization.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this survey template record, supporting change tracking and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this survey template record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or context about this survey template for internal reference and knowledge management.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department or function responsible for maintaining and governing this survey template, supporting organizational accountability.']
  property_tier varchar [note: 'Hotel property tier or brand segment for which this survey template is designed, enabling tier-specific experience measurement.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions included in the survey template, used for completion time estimation and survey design optimization.']
  response_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the survey remains open for guest responses after distribution, balancing recency and response opportunity.']
  salt_program_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether responses from this survey template are eligible for inclusion in SALT program metrics and service recovery workflows.']
  service_recovery_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum satisfaction score below which a service recovery case is automatically triggered in Salesforce CRM for guest follow-up.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey template indicating its availability for use in guest experience programs.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or keywords for categorizing and searching survey templates, supporting template library organization.']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Intended guest segment or audience for this survey template, used for targeted experience measurement and personalized feedback collection.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the survey template, used for external references and system integration.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the survey template used for identification and selection purposes.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey template by its primary purpose: Net Promoter Score (NPS), Customer Satisfaction (CSAT), Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS), post-stay feedback, pre-arrival expectations, or service recovery assessment.']
  trigger_delay_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours to wait after the trigger event before sending the survey to the guest, optimizing response timing and quality.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Guest journey event or touchpoint that triggers the distribution of this survey template, enabling timely feedback collection.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this survey template has been distributed to guests, used for template effectiveness analysis and optimization.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the survey template following semantic versioning to track template evolution and changes over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this survey template record, supporting audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for survey_template. Referenced by survey_template_id.'
}

Table spa.treatment {
  treatment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spa treatment. Primary key for the treatment master catalog.']
  content_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.content_asset. Business justification: Individual treatments are promoted through content assets (treatment videos, photos, descriptions, brochures). Links treatment inventory to marketing content, enables content performance tracking, and']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Treatments may be brand-signature offerings (Ritz-Carlton signature treatments, Mandarin Oriental therapies). Links treatment inventory to brand identity, supports brand differentiation, and enables b']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Spa treatments consume retail products (massage oils, lotions, linens) tracked in material master. Essential for COGS calculation, inventory planning, and treatment profitability analysis in spa opera']
  parent_treatment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on treatment (parent_treatment_id)']
  advance_booking_required_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice required to book this treatment. Used for scheduling rules and guest communication.']
  age_restriction varchar [note: 'Minimum age requirement or age-related restrictions for this treatment (e.g., 18+, 16+ with parental consent). Used for compliance and liability management.']
  cancellation_policy_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before the appointment that a guest can cancel without penalty. Used for revenue protection and scheduling optimization.']
  commission_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether therapists or spa staff earn commission on sales of this treatment. Used for payroll and incentive compensation calculations.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission rate paid to therapists or staff on sales of this treatment. Used for payroll processing and labor cost analysis.']
  contraindications varchar [note: 'Medical conditions, allergies, or circumstances under which the treatment should not be performed or requires physician approval. Critical for guest safety and liability management.']
  cost_of_goods decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard cost of consumable products, oils, lotions, and supplies used in delivering this treatment. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this treatment record was first created in the master catalog system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard duration of the treatment in minutes, used for scheduling, therapist allocation, and capacity planning.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates no planned end date. Supports seasonal offerings and menu rotation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment becomes available for booking and display in guest-facing systems. Supports seasonal launches and menu updates.']
  gender_preference varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment is designed for specific gender or can be offered to any guest. Used for marketing segmentation and therapist assignment.']
  gratuity_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gratuity is included in the treatment price or added separately. Used for pricing transparency and billing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this treatment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points a guest earns when purchasing this treatment. Used for loyalty program integration and guest engagement.']
  minimum_certification varchar [note: 'Minimum professional certification, license, or credential required to perform this treatment (e.g., Licensed Massage Therapist, Esthetician License, Cosmetology License).']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this treatment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  package_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment can be included in spa packages or bundled offerings. Used for package configuration and revenue management.']
  pregnancy_safe_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment is safe for pregnant guests. Critical for guest safety screening and liability management.']
  recommended_retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Corporate-recommended retail price for this treatment in the base currency. Individual properties may adjust based on local market conditions and positioning.']
  required_equipment varchar [note: 'List of specialized equipment, tools, or facilities required to deliver this treatment (e.g., hydrotherapy tub, vichy shower, hot stone warmer, facial steamer).']
  retail_products_used varchar [note: 'List of retail product SKUs or product lines used during the treatment. Supports cross-sell opportunities and inventory planning.']
  revenue_center varchar [note: 'Revenue center to which this treatment is assigned for financial reporting and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) analysis per USALI standards.']
  room_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of treatment room or facility required to deliver this service (e.g., single room, couples suite, wet room, outdoor cabana). Used for facility scheduling and capacity planning.']
  seasonal_availability varchar [note: 'Indicates specific seasons or months when this treatment is available (e.g., summer only for outdoor treatments, winter for specific wellness programs).']
  skill_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum skill level classification required for a therapist to perform this treatment. Used for therapist assignment and training planning.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular segmentation within the primary category (e.g., Swedish Massage, Deep Tissue, Hot Stone within Massage category).']
  treatment_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the spa treatment type. Used for menu organization, revenue reporting, and therapist skill matching.']
  treatment_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric business identifier for the treatment used across all properties and systems. This is the externally-known catalog code used in PMS, POS, and booking systems.']
  treatment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the treatment including techniques, benefits, and guest experience. Used in spa menus, websites, and booking confirmations.']
  treatment_name varchar [note: 'Full marketing name of the spa treatment as displayed to guests in menus, booking systems, and promotional materials.']
  treatment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the treatment in the master catalog. Controls availability for booking and display in guest-facing systems.']
  upsell_treatments varchar [note: 'List of complementary treatment codes that can be recommended as add-ons or follow-up services. Supports revenue optimization and guest experience enhancement.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all spa and wellness treatments offered across properties, including massages, facials, body wraps, hydrotherapy, and beauty services. Captures treatment name, treatment code, category (massage, facial, body, nail, fitness), duration in minutes, treatment description, contraindications, required equipment, skill level required, minimum therapist certification, recommended retail price, cost of goods, revenue center classification, and active status. This is the SSOT for spa treatment definitions across the portfolio.'
}

Table spa.treatment_menu {
  treatment_menu_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the property-level spa treatment menu. Primary key.']

  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Treatment menus are designed for specific market segments (luxury leisure, corporate wellness, local membership). Business process: spa menu strategy aligns with property market segmentation for targe']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Treatment menus are brand-specific (Ritz-Carlton Spa vs. Westin Heavenly Spa vs. Six Senses Spa). Essential for brand-specific menu management, brand standards enforcement, and supporting brand differ']
  primary_treatment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (spa manager, revenue manager, or property director) who approved this treatment menu for publication.']

  superseded_treatment_menu_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on treatment_menu (superseded_treatment_menu_id)']

  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the cancellation policy applicable to treatments booked from this menu (e.g., hours notice required, penalty fees).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this treatment menu record was first created in the system.']

  display_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering value controlling the presentation sequence of this menu in guest-facing channels and staff systems.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this treatment menu becomes active and available for guest bookings at the property.']

  menu_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the primary marketing image or hero image representing this treatment menu in digital channels.']
