  dpo_notification_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Data Protection Officer was notified about the privacy incident.']
  dpo_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization\'s Data Protection Officer was notified about the incident.']
  estimated_subjects_affected varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individual data subjects (guests, employees, or other persons) whose personal data was affected by the incident.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Actual or estimated date when the privacy incident occurred or the breach event took place.']
  incident_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or context about the privacy incident not captured in other structured fields.']
  incident_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the privacy incident for tracking and regulatory reporting purposes. Format: PI-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the privacy incident in the investigation and remediation workflow.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the privacy incident type indicating the nature of the data protection violation or breach event.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the internal investigation into the privacy incident was completed.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal investigation into the privacy incident.']
  jurisdictions_impacted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of jurisdictions or regulatory regions where affected data subjects reside, determining applicable data protection laws (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, FRA, CAN).']
  legal_counsel_engaged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether internal or external legal counsel was engaged to advise on the incident response and regulatory obligations.']
  litigation_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether civil litigation or class action lawsuits were filed by affected data subjects as a result of the incident.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of regulatory penalty or fine imposed by the supervisory authority.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  regulatory_authority_notified varchar [note: 'Name of the supervisory authority or data protection authority that was notified about the breach (e.g., ICO, CNIL, California Attorney General).']
  regulatory_notification_deadline timestamp [note: 'Deadline timestamp by which the organization must notify the relevant supervisory authority about the breach (typically 72 hours from discovery under GDPR).']
  regulatory_notification_sent_date timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when notification was sent to the regulatory supervisory authority.']
  regulatory_penalty_imposed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory penalty or fine was imposed by a supervisory authority as a result of the incident.']
  remediation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Description of remediation measures implemented to address the root cause, restore security, and prevent recurrence of similar incidents.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed risk assessment narrative describing the potential consequences and risks to the rights and freedoms of affected data subjects.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of the underlying root cause of the privacy incident including technical, procedural, or human factors that contributed to the breach.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity level of the privacy incident based on the nature of data affected, number of subjects, and potential harm.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or application where the incident was recorded or from which the incident data originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS).']
  subject_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct notification to affected data subjects is required under applicable data protection laws.']
  subject_notification_sent_date timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when notification was sent to affected data subjects.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy incident record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Data privacy breach and incident record tracking GDPR, CCPA, and other data protection regulation events at Travel Hospitality. Captures incident reference number, incident type (data breach, unauthorized access, data loss, improper disclosure, subject rights violation), discovery date, incident date, data categories affected (PII, payment card, health data, biometric), estimated number of data subjects affected, jurisdictions impacted, breach notification required flag, regulatory notification deadline, notification sent date, regulatory authority notified, and containment actions taken. Distinct from guest.privacy_request which tracks individual data subject rights requests.'
}

Table compliance.dpia {
  dpia_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Data Protection Impact Assessment record. Primary key.']
  dpia_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this DPIA record, supporting audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  dpia_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated this DPIA record, supporting audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or business unit to which this DPIA applies. Links to the property master data.']
  superseded_dpia_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dpia (superseded_dpia_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the DPIA was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the DPIA within the organization\'s governance workflow.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved the DPIA (typically a senior privacy officer, legal counsel, or executive).']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the DPIA was conducted or completed.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or team responsible for conducting the DPIA.']
  assessor_role varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the assessor (e.g., Data Protection Officer, Privacy Manager, Compliance Analyst).']
  automated_decision_logic varchar [note: 'Description of the logic involved in automated decision-making processes, if applicable.']
  automated_decision_making_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the processing involves automated decision-making, including profiling, that produces legal or similarly significant effects.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DPIA record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the processing involves transfer of personal data to third countries or international organizations.']
  data_subject_consultation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data subjects or their representatives were consulted during the DPIA process, as recommended under GDPR Article 35(9).']
  data_subject_consultation_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of feedback or views obtained from data subjects or their representatives during the DPIA process.']
  dpo_consultation_date date [note: 'Date on which the Data Protection Officer was consulted regarding the DPIA.']
  dpo_consultation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Protection Officer was consulted during the DPIA process as required under GDPR Article 35(2).']
  dpo_recommendation varchar [note: 'Formal recommendation or advice provided by the Data Protection Officer regarding the processing activity and identified risks.']
  identified_privacy_risks varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of privacy risks identified during the assessment, including risks to data subject rights and freedoms.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review or update of this DPIA.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis under GDPR Article 6 that justifies the processing activity being assessed.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Detailed description of technical and organizational measures implemented or planned to mitigate identified privacy risks.']
  necessity_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the data processing is necessary to achieve the stated purpose, documenting the business justification and legal basis.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this DPIA to reassess risks and mitigation measures.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context relevant to the DPIA that do not fit into other structured fields.']
  processing_activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the data processing activity, including purpose, scope, data subjects involved, and data categories processed.']
  processing_activity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the personal data processing activity being assessed (e.g., Guest Loyalty Program Enrollment, Employee Background Screening, CCTV Surveillance in Public Areas).']
  proportionality_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the data processing is proportionate to the purpose, evaluating whether less intrusive alternatives exist.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the DPIA, typically formatted as DPIA-YYYY-NNNN for tracking and audit purposes.']
  residual_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level remaining after mitigation measures have been applied, determining whether additional action or supervisory authority consultation is required.']
  retention_period varchar [note: 'Documented retention period for personal data processed under this activity, aligned with data minimization principles.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the DPIA should be reviewed and updated to ensure continued accuracy and compliance.']
  risk_likelihood varchar [note: 'Likelihood rating of the identified privacy risks materializing before mitigation measures are applied.']
  risk_severity varchar [note: 'Overall severity rating of the identified privacy risks before mitigation measures are applied.']
  special_category_data_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the processing involves special categories of personal data as defined in GDPR Article 9 (e.g., health data, biometric data, racial or ethnic origin).']
  special_category_justification varchar [note: 'Justification and legal basis for processing special category data, if applicable, under GDPR Article 9(2).']
  supervisory_authority_consultation_date date [note: 'Date on which the supervisory authority was consulted regarding the high-risk processing activity.']
  supervisory_authority_consultation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consultation with the supervisory authority is required due to high residual risk that cannot be adequately mitigated, as per GDPR Article 36.']
  supervisory_authority_response varchar [note: 'Summary of the response or guidance received from the supervisory authority following consultation.']
  transfer_safeguards varchar [note: 'Description of safeguards in place for cross-border data transfers (e.g., Standard Contractual Clauses, Binding Corporate Rules, adequacy decision).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DPIA record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) record documenting formal privacy risk assessments conducted for high-risk personal data processing activities as required under GDPR Article 35. Captures DPIA reference number, processing activity assessed, assessment date, assessor name, necessity and proportionality assessment, identified privacy risks, risk severity (high, medium, low), risk mitigation measures, residual risk level, DPO consultation flag, DPO recommendation, supervisory authority consultation required flag, and DPIA approval status. Supports GDPR accountability obligations and privacy-by-design governance.'
}

Table compliance.food_safety_cert {
  food_safety_cert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the food safety certification record. Primary key.']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific F&B outlet, kitchen, or catering operation covered by this certification.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Food safety certifications are regulatory permits/licenses. The food_safety_cert table has certification_number, certifying_body, status, and lifecycle dates that duplicate permit master data. Adding ']
  primary_food_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager responsible for maintaining this certification and ensuring ongoing compliance.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this food safety certification applies.']
  renewed_food_safety_cert_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on food_safety_cert (renewed_food_safety_cert_id)']
  tertiary_food_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last updated this certification record in the system.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this certification record to detailed audit logs, inspection reports, or compliance documentation.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'The full name or title of the certification as issued by the certifying body.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of operations covered by this certification (e.g., full-service restaurant, banquet kitchen, bar service, catering operations).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of food safety certification (e.g., HACCP, ServSafe, local health department grade, ISO 22000, food handler card, manager certification). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HACCP|ServSafe|Local Health Department|ISO 22000|Food Handler|Manager Certification|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  corrective_actions_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required corrective actions have been completed (True) or are still pending (False).']
  corrective_actions_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which all required corrective actions were completed and verified.']
  corrective_actions_required varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions required to address violations or deficiencies identified during the last inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system.']
  critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'The number of critical food safety violations identified during the last inspection. Critical violations pose immediate health risks.']
  health_inspection_grade varchar [note: 'The letter grade or pass/fail result assigned by the health inspector during the last inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|Pass|Fail|Satisfactory|Unsatisfactory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  health_inspection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The numeric score assigned during the last health inspection (typically 0-100 scale, where higher is better).']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between required inspections as mandated by the certifying body or regulatory authority.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The code or identifier for the regulatory jurisdiction (city, county, state, country) under which this certification is issued and enforced.']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., Los Angeles County Health Department, New York State Department of Health).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent health inspection conducted at the outlet or facility covered by this certification.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent food safety training was completed for staff covered by this certification.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next health inspection or audit of the outlet or facility.']
  next_training_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next food safety training session must be completed to maintain certification compliance.']
  non_critical_violations_count varchar [note: 'The number of non-critical food safety violations identified during the last inspection. Non-critical violations do not pose immediate health risks but require correction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to this food safety certification.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'The assessed risk level of the outlet or facility based on food safety compliance history, violation severity, and operational complexity.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing food safety training is required to maintain this certification (True) or not (False).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Food safety certification and inspection record for F&B outlets, kitchens, and catering operations at Travel Hospitality properties. Captures certification type (HACCP, ServSafe, local health department grade, ISO 22000), certifying body, property and outlet reference, certification number, issue date, expiration date, certification status (active, expired, suspended, pending), last health inspection date, health inspection grade or score, critical violations count, non-critical violations count, and corrective actions required. Complements fnb.food_safety_inspection (which captures individual inspection events) by serving as the authoritative certification master.'
}

Linking safety records to asset register enables maintenance cost tracking, depreciation schedules, insurance documentation,']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Fire safety certificates are a type of permit/license. The fire_safety_record table has certificate_number, issuing_authority, certificate dates, and status that duplicate permit master data. Adding p']
  primary_fire_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this fire safety record.']

  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required fire safety inspections as mandated by local regulations.']
  insurance_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property insurance carrier must be notified of fire safety status changes.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent fire safety inspection conducted by the fire authority or certified inspector.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required fire safety inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the property\'s fire safety compliance status.']
  outstanding_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved fire code violations currently recorded for the property.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which outstanding fire code violations must be remediated to maintain compliance.']

  suppression_system_next_test_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required test of the fire suppression system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fire safety record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Fire safety compliance master record for each property capturing the status of all fire safety systems, inspections, and certifications. Captures fire safety certificate number, issuing fire authority, property reference, certificate issue date, expiration date, certificate status, last fire inspection date, fire suppression system type and last test date, fire alarm system last test date, emergency evacuation plan last review date, fire drill last conducted date, sprinkler coverage percentage, fire extinguisher count and last inspection date, and any outstanding fire code violations. Single source of truth for property-level fire safety compliance posture.'
}

Table compliance.ada_assessment {
  ada_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ADA accessibility compliance assessment record.']
  primary_ada_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created this ADA assessment record.']
  prior_ada_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ada_assessment (prior_ada_assessment_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel, resort, or vacation property being assessed for ADA compliance.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the ADA accessibility assessment was conducted at the property.']
  assessment_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard frequency in months between scheduled ADA assessments for this property (e.g., 12, 24, 36 months).']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or recommendations from the assessor regarding ADA compliance, best practices, or areas of concern.']
  assessment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business reference number for the ADA assessment, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  assessment_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed ADA assessment report document containing findings, photographs, and recommendations.']
  assessment_scope_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the specific scope, limitations, or special considerations of the ADA assessment (e.g., areas excluded, phased approach, weather constraints).']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ADA assessment based on the trigger or purpose: initial baseline assessment, periodic scheduled review, complaint-triggered investigation, renovation-triggered evaluation, acquisition due diligence, or post-remediation verification.']
  assessor_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional credentials, certifications, or qualifications of the assessor (e.g., Certified Access Specialist, ADA Coordinator, licensed architect).']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or firm who conducted the ADA accessibility assessment.']
  assessor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or consulting firm that the assessor represents.']
  barriers_pending_remediation_count varchar [note: 'Count of accessibility barriers that remain outstanding and require remediation to achieve full ADA compliance.']
  barriers_remediated_count varchar [note: 'Count of accessibility barriers that have been successfully remediated and brought into ADA compliance as of the current date.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the ADA compliance certification, after which a new assessment is required to maintain certified status.']
  certification_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal ADA compliance certification or letter of compliance was issued by the assessor following the assessment.']
  complaint_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the guest complaint, legal claim, or regulatory inquiry that triggered this ADA assessment, if applicable.']
  compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of ADA compliance based on the ratio of compliant elements to total elements assessed, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall ADA compliance status of the property based on the assessment findings: compliant (no barriers), substantially compliant (minor barriers only), partially compliant (moderate barriers present), non-compliant (significant barriers present), or under remediation (active remediation plan in progress).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ADA assessment record was first created in the system.']
  entrances_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether building entrances, accessible routes, and entry points were included in the ADA assessment.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in the property\'s operating currency to remediate all identified ADA accessibility barriers and achieve full compliance.']
  fitness_facilities_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fitness centers, gyms, and exercise facilities were included in the ADA assessment.']
  guest_rooms_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest rooms and sleeping accommodations were included in the scope of the ADA assessment.']
  high_priority_barriers_count varchar [note: 'Count of accessibility barriers classified as high priority due to significant impact on guest safety, access, or legal risk.']
  legal_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or docket number of any legal case, lawsuit, or Department of Justice (DOJ) investigation related to this ADA assessment.']
  meeting_spaces_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether meeting rooms, conference spaces, ballrooms, and event facilities were included in the ADA assessment for accessibility compliance.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic ADA accessibility assessment to ensure ongoing compliance and identify any new barriers.']
  parking_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parking facilities and accessible parking spaces were included in the ADA assessment.']
  pools_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether swimming pools, spas, and aquatic facilities were included in the ADA assessment for accessibility features such as lifts and sloped entries.']
  public_areas_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether public areas such as lobbies, corridors, elevators, and common spaces were included in the ADA assessment.']
  remediation_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated remediation cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  remediation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the formal remediation plan document that outlines corrective actions, timelines, and responsibilities for addressing identified ADA barriers.']
  responsible_party_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual responsible for ADA compliance and remediation at the property.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department responsible for overseeing ADA compliance and remediation efforts at the property (e.g., General Manager, Facilities Director, Compliance Officer).']
  restaurants_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Food and Beverage (F&B) outlets, restaurants, bars, and dining areas were included in the ADA assessment.']
  restrooms_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether public restrooms and toilet facilities were included in the ADA assessment for accessibility features.']
  signage_evaluated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether directional, informational, and accessible signage (including Braille and tactile signs) were included in the ADA assessment.']
  total_barriers_identified varchar [note: 'Total count of accessibility barriers, deficiencies, or non-compliant elements identified during the ADA assessment across all evaluated areas.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ADA assessment record was last modified or updated in the system.']

  Note: 'ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) and accessibility compliance assessment record for Travel Hospitality properties. Captures assessment reference number, property assessed, assessment type (initial, periodic, triggered by complaint), assessment date, assessor name and credentials, accessibility areas evaluated (guest rooms, public areas, parking, pools, restaurants, meeting spaces), total barriers identified, barriers remediated count, barriers pending remediation count, ADA compliance status (compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant), remediation plan reference, and next assessment due date. Supports ADA Title III compliance and accessibility improvement planning.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_training_completion {
  compliance_training_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training completion record. Primary key for this entity.']
  learning_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance training requirement that this completion record satisfies. Links to the training requirement master.']
  loyalty_program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Compliance training on loyalty program rules (anti-fraud procedures, points liability accounting, tier qualification criteria) must reference which program configuration version the training covered. ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Compliance policies often require mandatory training (e.g., Code of Conduct training, Anti-Harassment training, Data Privacy training). One policy can require one training course. This FK allows track']
  primary_compliance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who completed the training. Links to the workforce employee master record.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the employee is assigned at the time of training completion. Enables property-level compliance tracking.']
  retake_compliance_training_completion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compliance_training_completion (retake_compliance_training_completion_id)']
  tertiary_compliance_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or process that last modified this training completion record. Used for audit trail and change management.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which attempt this represents for the employee to complete this training requirement. First attempt is 1. Used to track retakes and learning effectiveness.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking this training completion to external audit documentation, regulatory filings, or compliance case files. Used for regulatory audit response.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certificate was officially issued. May differ from completion date for certifications requiring external validation. Null if no certificate issued.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued upon successful completion. Used for verification with regulatory bodies and external auditors. Null if no certificate issued.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee successfully completed the training course. Used to calculate compliance currency and expiration dates.']
  completion_record_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the training completion record. Used for audit trails and external reporting to regulatory bodies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the training for this employee. Current indicates valid certification; expiring_soon indicates approaching expiration within threshold; expired indicates past due; not_started indicates requirement not yet initiated; in_progress indicates training underway; waived indicates exemption granted.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the training course. Used for system integration and reporting consistency across properties.']
  course_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance training course completed by the employee. Examples include Food Safety Certification, ADA Accessibility Training, Fire Safety Procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training completion record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the training cost amount. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP. Enables multi-currency cost tracking across global properties.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training was delivered to the employee. Online indicates e-learning; in_person indicates classroom; blended indicates combination; virtual_instructor_led indicates live remote; self_paced indicates asynchronous; on_the_job indicates practical training.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department to which the employee belonged at the time of training completion. Used for department-level compliance reporting and cost allocation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certification expires and requires renewal. Null for training that does not expire. Critical for proactive compliance monitoring.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the instructor or facilitator who delivered the training. Null for self-paced or automated training. Used for quality tracking and instructor effectiveness analysis.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body, certification authority, or organization that issued the certificate. Examples include ServSafe, OSHA, state liquor boards. Null if internally certified.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employees job role at the time of training completion. Used to track role-specific training compliance and identify training gaps by position.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal jurisdiction (country, state, province, municipality) whose regulations this training satisfies. Critical for multi-jurisdictional compliance tracking.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training is mandatory for the employees role and location. True for required compliance training; false for optional professional development.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, or compliance officer comments related to this training completion. Used for audit documentation and exception tracking.']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the training assessment. Pass indicates successful completion; fail indicates unsuccessful attempt; incomplete indicates training started but not finished; waived indicates exemption from assessment.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the training assessment. Used to determine pass/fail result. Null if training does not have a scored assessment.']
  regulatory_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of regulatory compliance that this training addresses. Used for compliance dashboard reporting and regulatory audit preparation by compliance domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health_safety|food_safety|data_privacy|accessibility|fire_safety|liquor_licensing|financial_compliance|hr_compliance|environmental|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals for this training. Null if renewal is not required. Common values include 12, 24, 36 months depending on regulatory requirements.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training requires periodic renewal. True for certifications that expire; false for one-time training. Used to trigger renewal workflows.']
  score_achieved decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or percentage achieved by the employee on the training assessment. Null if training does not include scored assessment. Range typically 0-100.']
  training_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for this training completion, including course fees, materials, instructor costs, and employee time. Used for training ROI analysis and budget tracking.']
  training_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the employee spent completing the training. Used for workforce planning, cost analysis, and regulatory reporting where contact hours are required.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or vendor that provided the training. May be internal (company name) or external third-party provider. Important for audit and vendor management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training completion record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer or manager who approved the training waiver. Null if no waiver granted. Required for audit trail.']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the training waiver expires and standard requirement resumes. Null if waiver is permanent or no waiver granted. Used to trigger compliance re-assessment.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver or exemption from this training requirement was granted to the employee. True if waived; false if standard requirement applies. Requires documented justification.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting a training waiver. Examples include prior certification from another employer, role exemption, or temporary assignment. Null if no waiver granted.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual employee completion of mandatory compliance training requirements. Captures completion record number, employee reference, training requirement reference, training course name, completion date, expiration date, pass/fail result, score achieved, training delivery method, training provider, certificate number issued, and compliance status (current, expiring soon, expired, not started). Enables compliance officers to track training currency across the workforce and demonstrate regulatory compliance to auditors. Complements workforce.learning_course by focusing on compliance-mandated training completion status.'
}

Table compliance.risk_register {
  risk_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk register entry. Primary key for the risk register product.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings (especially critical and major findings) represent identified compliance risks that must be tracked and mitigated. One finding can be tracked as one risk entry. This FK allows linking r']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this risk register entry. Links to user or employee master data for audit trail purposes.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Compliance obligations represent operational and regulatory risks that must be tracked in the enterprise risk register. Failure to meet an obligation can result in fines, license suspension, or operat']
  parent_risk_register_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_register (parent_risk_register_id)']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit expiration, suspension, or non-renewal represents significant operational risk (e.g., liquor license expiration shuts down bar operations, health permit suspension closes F&B outlets). One perm']
  primary_risk_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property to which this risk applies. Links to the property master data for property-specific risks.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated this risk register entry. Links to user or employee master data for audit trail purposes.']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the risk was closed or mitigated to an acceptable level.']
  control_effectiveness varchar [note: 'Assessment of how well the current control measures are functioning to mitigate the risk. Effective means controls are working as designed; partially effective means controls reduce but do not eliminate risk; ineffective means controls are not adequately addressing the risk.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Description of existing controls, policies, procedures, and mitigation actions currently in place to manage and reduce the risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk register record was first created in the system.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was escalated to senior management or board level for review and decision.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk currently requires escalation to senior management based on residual risk score exceeding the escalation threshold.']
  escalation_threshold_score varchar [note: 'Residual risk score threshold above which the risk must be escalated to senior management or board level. Typically set at 15 or 20 on the 1-25 scale.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact in monetary terms if the risk materializes, including potential revenue loss, penalty costs, remediation costs, or legal settlement amounts.']
  financial_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  identification_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was first identified and entered into the risk register.']
  impact_rating varchar [note: 'Severity assessment of the risk impact if it occurs on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is negligible impact and 5 is catastrophic impact affecting multiple properties or enterprise reputation.']
  inherent_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk score before considering control measures, computed as likelihood rating multiplied by impact rating. Range is 1 to 25.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-letter code identifying the legal jurisdiction or geographic region where this risk applies (e.g., US, CA, EU, UK).']
  last_incident_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent incident or event related to this risk.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was most recently reviewed and assessed by the risk owner or compliance team.']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Probability assessment of the risk occurring on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is rare (less than 10% probability) and 5 is almost certain (greater than 90% probability).']
  mitigation_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in monetary terms to implement the mitigation plan and controls to reduce the risk.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed action plan outlining specific steps, resources, and timeline for reducing the risk to an acceptable level.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review and reassessment of this risk.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context about the risk for internal reference and knowledge sharing.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of specific regulation, law, standard, or compliance requirement associated with this risk (e.g., GDPR Article 32, OSHA 1910.38, PCI DSS 3.2.1).']
  related_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of actual incidents or near-miss events that have occurred related to this risk, providing evidence of risk materialization.']
  residual_impact_rating varchar [note: 'Revised severity assessment after considering the effect of control measures, on a scale of 1 to 5.']
  residual_likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Revised probability assessment after considering the effect of control measures, on a scale of 1 to 5.']
  residual_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk score after considering control measures, computed as residual likelihood rating multiplied by residual impact rating. Range is 1 to 25.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of this risk. High-priority risks typically have shorter review cycles (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  risk_appetite_alignment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the residual risk level aligns with the organizations stated risk appetite. Within appetite means acceptable; exceeds appetite means requires additional mitigation; below appetite means over-controlled.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the risk type. Regulatory includes compliance with laws and regulations; health and safety covers guest and employee safety; data privacy includes GDPR and CCPA compliance; reputational covers brand and guest satisfaction; financial covers revenue and cost risks; operational covers business process and service delivery risks.']
  risk_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the risk following the format AAA-9999 where AAA represents risk category prefix and 9999 is sequential number.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the risk including potential causes, scenarios, and business context.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk. Open means newly identified; in progress means mitigation actions underway; mitigated means controls have reduced risk to acceptable level; accepted means risk acknowledged and no further action planned; closed means risk no longer applicable; escalated means risk requires senior management attention.']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the primary risk category (e.g., fire safety, food safety, PCI DSS, ADA compliance).']
  risk_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the identified risk for quick reference and reporting.']
  scope_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level at which the risk applies. Property for single-property risks; brand for brand-specific risks; region for geographic area risks; portfolio for multi-property segment risks; enterprise for company-wide risks.']
  target_closure_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the risk is expected to be mitigated to an acceptable level or closed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk register record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Enterprise compliance and operational risk register for Travel Hospitality capturing identified risks across all properties and business functions. Captures risk ID, risk title, risk category (regulatory, health and safety, data privacy, reputational, financial, operational), risk description, property or portfolio scope, likelihood rating (1-5), impact rating (1-5), inherent risk score, control measures in place, residual risk score, risk owner, risk status (open, mitigated, accepted, closed), risk review date, and escalation threshold. Serves as the authoritative risk inventory for compliance governance and executive risk reporting.'
}

  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the filing was submitted to the regulatory body.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory filing record was last updated or modified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of regulatory filings, submissions, and reports submitted by Travel Hospitality to government agencies and regulatory bodies. Covers occupancy tax filings, liquor license renewals, health department reports, OSHA 300 log submissions, GDPR supervisory authority notifications, CCPA annual reports, fire marshal reports, and environmental compliance filings. Captures filing reference number, filing type, regulatory body, jurisdiction, property or entity scope, filing period, submission date, filing status (draft, submitted, acknowledged, accepted, rejected, under review), filing agent, and any regulatory response received.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance policy record. Primary key.']
  policy_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this policy record in the system.']
  policy_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executive or department head responsible for policy governance, maintenance, and enforcement.']
  policy_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this policy record.']
  superseded_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous policy version that this policy replaces. Null if this is the first version.']
  acknowledgment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees must formally acknowledge receipt and understanding of this policy. True if acknowledgment is mandatory.']
  applicable_scope varchar [note: 'Organizational reach of the policy. Enterprise-wide applies to all entities; brand-specific applies to a hotel brand; property-specific applies to individual properties; regional applies to geographic regions; departmental applies to specific functions.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name and title of the executive, board, or committee that formally approved this policy for implementation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this policy to audit logs, compliance assessments, and enforcement actions for traceability.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the policy owner or compliance officer for questions, clarifications, and policy-related inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the policy owner or compliance officer for urgent policy-related matters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full policy document stored in the enterprise document management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy becomes enforceable and binding across the applicable scope.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Degree of compliance obligation. Mandatory policies require strict adherence with consequences for non-compliance; recommended policies are best practices; advisory policies provide guidance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the policy ceases to be in force. Null for policies without a defined end date.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary legal jurisdiction governing this policy (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was most recently reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and regulatory alignment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory policy review to ensure continued compliance and relevance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, implementation guidance, exceptions, or special considerations related to this policy.']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the policy domain. Categories include data privacy (GDPR/CCPA compliance), health and safety (OSHA, fire safety), anti-bribery (anti-corruption), code of conduct (ethics), liquor service (licensing), food safety (ISO 22000), ADA (accessibility), environmental (sustainability), and information security (PCI DSS).']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive summary of the policy purpose, scope, and key requirements. Provides context for policy application and interpretation.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique policy identifier used for reference and communication across the organization.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy. Draft indicates policy is being developed; active indicates policy is in force; under review indicates policy is being revised; retired indicates policy is no longer applicable; suspended indicates temporary hold.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework, statute, or industry standard that mandates or informs this policy (e.g., GDPR Article 32, OSHA 1910.38, PCI DSS 3.2.1, ISO 22000:2018).']
  related_policy_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of policy IDs that are related or cross-referenced by this policy for comprehensive compliance understanding.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard interval in months between mandatory policy reviews as defined by governance requirements.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the business and regulatory risk associated with non-compliance with this policy. Critical indicates severe legal or operational consequences.']
  scope_entity_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific brand, property, region, or department to which this policy applies when scope is not enterprise-wide. Null for enterprise-wide policies.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the policy category (e.g., guest data protection, employee safety, vendor conduct).']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the compliance policy as approved by governance authority.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees within the applicable scope must complete formal training on this policy. True if training is mandatory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy record was last modified.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the policy document (e.g., 1.0, 2.3) to track revisions and updates over time.']

  Note: 'Internal compliance policy and procedure master record for Travel Hospitality. Captures policy number, policy title, policy category (data privacy, health and safety, anti-bribery, code of conduct, liquor service, food safety, ADA, environmental, information security), policy owner, effective date, review date, next review date, policy status (draft, active, under review, retired), applicable scope (enterprise-wide, brand-specific, property-specific), regulatory basis, version number, and approval authority. Single source of truth for all internal compliance policies and their lifecycle management.'
}

  policy_acknowledgment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy acknowledgment record. Primary key for the policy acknowledgment transaction.']
  compliance_training_completion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the e-learning or training completion record if acknowledgment was captured through training system attestation. Null for non-training acknowledgments.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Loyalty program terms & conditions acknowledgments by members (required for tier benefits eligibility, points redemption authorization, data sharing consent) are policy acknowledgments. Essential for ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance policy that was acknowledged. Links to the compliance policy master record.']
  primary_policy_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who acknowledged the policy. Links to the workforce employee master record.']
  prior_policy_acknowledgment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on policy_acknowledgment (prior_policy_acknowledgment_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the employee was assigned at the time of acknowledgment. Supports property-level compliance reporting and jurisdiction-specific policy tracking.']
  quaternary_policy_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system process that created this acknowledgment record. Typically the HR system or compliance workflow engine.']
  quinary_policy_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system process that last modified this acknowledgment record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  tertiary_policy_escalated_to_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or compliance officer to whom the overdue acknowledgment was escalated. Null if no escalation has occurred.']
  acknowledgment_channel varchar [note: 'The digital or physical channel through which the acknowledgment was captured. Distinguishes between self-service, facilitated, and system-driven acknowledgments.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee acknowledged the policy. Represents the business event timestamp when the acknowledgment was completed.']
  acknowledgment_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the employee was required to acknowledge the policy. Used to identify overdue acknowledgments and trigger escalations.']
  acknowledgment_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism through which the employee acknowledged the policy. Indicates whether acknowledgment was captured electronically, physically, or through training completion.']
  acknowledgment_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the acknowledgment transaction. Externally visible unique reference number used in audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy acknowledgment. Tracks whether the employee has completed, is pending, or has missed the acknowledgment requirement.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the employee completed the policy acknowledgment. Used for audit trail and legal defensibility.']

  compliance_officer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes added by compliance officers regarding this acknowledgment. Used to document special circumstances, follow-up actions, or audit findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this acknowledgment record was first created in the system. Represents the initial capture of the acknowledgment requirement.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The department or division to which the employee belonged at the time of acknowledgment. Enables department-level compliance tracking and reporting.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used to submit the acknowledgment. Supports user experience analysis and fraud detection.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The current escalation level for overdue acknowledgments. 0 indicates no escalation, higher numbers indicate progressive escalation to management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this acknowledgment expires and requires renewal. Supports periodic re-acknowledgment requirements for ongoing compliance.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The Internet Protocol address from which the acknowledgment was submitted. Captured for audit trail and fraud prevention purposes.']
  job_role_code varchar [note: 'The job role or position code of the employee at the time of acknowledgment. Supports role-based policy assignment and compliance analysis.']
  last_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent reminder notification was sent to the employee. Null if no reminders were sent.']
  policy_version_acknowledged varchar [note: 'The specific version of the policy that the employee acknowledged. Critical for demonstrating that employees acknowledged the current policy version in effect at the time.']
  re_acknowledgment_frequency_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between required re-acknowledgments. Null if re-acknowledgment is not required. Common values include 365 (annual) or 730 (biennial).']
  re_acknowledgment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy requires periodic re-acknowledgment. True if the employee must re-acknowledge at regular intervals.']
  reminder_sent_count varchar [note: 'The number of reminder notifications sent to the employee before acknowledgment was completed. Used to track engagement and escalation effectiveness.']
  signature_image_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored digital or scanned signature image file. Null for non-signature acknowledgment methods. Points to secure document storage system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this acknowledgment record was last modified. Tracks changes to status, notes, or other attributes.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'The date when the waiver was approved. Null if no waiver was granted.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver was granted exempting the employee from acknowledging this policy. True if waiver was approved by compliance officer.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting a waiver. Null if no waiver was granted. Required for audit trail when waiver_granted_flag is true.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of employee acknowledgment of compliance policies and codes of conduct. Captures acknowledgment record number, employee reference, policy reference, acknowledgment date, acknowledgment method (electronic signature, wet signature, e-learning attestation), acknowledgment status (pending, completed, overdue, waived), policy version acknowledged, and expiration date for re-acknowledgment. Enables compliance officers to demonstrate that employees have read and accepted key compliance policies, supporting regulatory audits and legal defensibility.'
}

  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Links due diligence to corporate account for credit risk management, regu']
  primary_third_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this due diligence assessment record.']
  prior_third_party_due_diligence_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on third_party_due_diligence (prior_third_party_due_diligence_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or corporate entity conducting the due diligence assessment.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Hotels perform third-party due diligence on vendors before contracting, screening for sanctions, financial stability, data processing agreements, and cybersecurity. This is a mandatory pre-contracting']
  adverse_media_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether adverse media (negative news, legal issues, corruption allegations) was identified during the assessment.']
  adverse_media_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of adverse media findings, including sources and nature of concerns identified during screening.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the third-party relationship was formally approved following due diligence assessment.']
  approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current approval expires and re-assessment is required.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the third-party relationship based on due diligence findings (approved, rejected, conditional approval, pending approval, under review).']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or authority who granted final approval for the third-party relationship.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the due diligence assessment was conducted or completed.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and observations from the due diligence assessment, including specific findings, concerns, and recommendations.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of due diligence assessment conducted (anti-bribery/FCPA, sanctions screening, data processor assessment under GDPR Article 28, modern slavery, environmental, financial stability, cybersecurity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: anti_bribery_fcpa|sanctions_screening|data_processor_assessment|modern_slavery|environmental|financial_stability|cybersecurity — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessor_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit of the assessor (e.g., Legal, Compliance, Procurement, Risk Management).']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for conducting the due diligence assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this due diligence assessment record was first created in the system.']
  cybersecurity_assessment_result varchar [note: 'Result of cybersecurity and data security assessment for third-parties with access to Travel Hospitality systems or guest data.']
  data_processing_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place as required by GDPR Article 28.']
  data_processor_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the third-party acts as a data processor under GDPR Article 28, requiring specific contractual safeguards and assessments.']
  document_repository_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or path to supporting documentation stored in the document management system (contracts, certificates, screening reports).']
  dpa_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the Data Processing Agreement was executed with the third-party data processor.']
  due_diligence_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this due diligence assessment for tracking and audit purposes.']
  financial_stability_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the third-party\'s financial stability and viability (excellent, good, fair, poor, not assessed).']
  insurance_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether required insurance coverage (liability, professional indemnity, cyber insurance) has been verified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic due diligence review or re-assessment of the third-party relationship.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the third-party or its principals are identified as Politically Exposed Persons (PEP), requiring enhanced due diligence.']
  remediation_actions varchar [note: 'Description of specific remediation actions or corrective measures required to address identified risks or deficiencies.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all required remediation actions were completed and verified.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation actions must be completed.']
  remediation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether remediation actions or corrective measures are required before the third-party relationship can proceed or continue.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which periodic due diligence reviews must be conducted based on risk rating (e.g., 12 months for low risk, 6 months for high risk).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the third-party based on the due diligence assessment (high, medium, low, critical).']
  sanctions_list_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against international sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, EU) indicating whether the third-party or its principals appear on any sanctions list.']
  screening_result varchar [note: 'Overall result of the due diligence screening (pass, fail, conditional approval, pending review).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this due diligence assessment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Compliance due diligence assessment record for third-party vendors, contractors, and business partners engaged by Travel Hospitality. Captures due diligence reference number, third-party name and type (vendor, contractor, franchise partner, joint venture), assessment type (anti-bribery/FCPA, sanctions screening, data processor assessment, modern slavery, environmental), assessment date, assessor, risk rating (high, medium, low), screening result (pass, fail, conditional), sanctions list check result, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) flag, adverse media flag, remediation required flag, and approval status. Supports FCPA, UK Bribery Act, and GDPR Article 28 processor obligations.'
}

  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case is subject to legal hold requirements for litigation or regulatory proceedings (True/False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to the whistleblower report and investigation process.']

  reporter_contact_method varchar [note: 'Contact information provided by the reporter (email, phone, or callback code) for follow-up communication. Null if anonymous.']
  reporter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who submitted the report, if not anonymous. Null if reporter chose anonymity.']
  reporter_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the reporter to the organization (employee, contractor, vendor, guest, former employee, other).']
  retaliation_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporter expressed concerns about potential retaliation (True/False). Triggers additional monitoring and protection measures.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the whistleblower report record is eligible for archival or deletion per records retention policy, typically 7 years from case closure.']

  Note: 'Confidential whistleblower and ethics hotline report record for Travel Hospitality. Captures report reference number, report channel (hotline, web portal, in-person, email), report date, allegation category (fraud, bribery, harassment, safety violation, data misuse, discrimination, retaliation, code of conduct breach), property or business unit implicated, reporter anonymity flag, case status (received, under review, investigation opened, closed substantiated, closed unsubstantiated), assigned investigator, investigation outcome, and regulatory escalation required flag. Supports Sarbanes-Oxley Section 301 whistleblower obligations and internal ethics governance.'
}

Table compliance.sanction_screening {
  sanction_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sanction screening record. Primary key.']

  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: AML/sanctions screening must be performed on bookings from high-risk jurisdictions, sanctioned entities, or flagged guests. Hotels need to link screening results to specific bookings for compliance do']

Business justification: AML/sanctions screening of high-value loyalty members (large points transfers, suspicious redemption patterns, partner program transfers) requires member reference for risk assessment and regulatory r']

Business justification: Sanctions screening is a component of third-party due diligence for vendors, contractors, and business partners. One due diligence assessment includes one or more sanctions screenings. This FK allows ']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the system user or automated process that last updated this screening record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor if the screened entity is a vendor or supplier. Null for non-vendor entities.']
  business_relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the business relationship with the screened entity following the screening result: active (approved to continue), suspended (temporarily halted), terminated (permanently ended), or pending approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanction screening record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or module from which the entity data was extracted for screening (e.g., OPERA PMS Guest Profiles, Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the screening result was escalated to senior compliance management or legal counsel. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this screening result requires escalation to senior compliance management or legal counsel due to high risk or complexity.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or jurisdiction identifier where the screening was performed or where the entity operates, relevant for jurisdiction-specific sanctions compliance.']
  match_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the likelihood that the screened entity matches a watchlist entry, generated by the screening system algorithm.']
  match_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition status of the screening result after review: cleared (approved to proceed), escalated (sent to compliance officer), blocked (transaction/relationship prohibited), under review, or false positive.']
  match_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening indicating whether the entity matched any watchlist entries: no match, potential match requiring review, or confirmed match.']
  matched_entry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the specific watchlist entry that matched or potentially matched the screened entity. Null if no match.']
  matched_list_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific sanctions or watchlist where a match or potential match was identified. Null if no match.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic re-screening of this entity against updated sanctions and watchlists, as required by AML compliance policy.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the screening result was reported to regulatory authorities. Null if no reporting was required or not yet reported.']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory report filed (e.g., SAR filing number). Null if no report was filed.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this screening result requires reporting to regulatory authorities (e.g., FinCEN SAR filing for confirmed matches).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the manual review of the screening result was completed. Null if no manual review was performed.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and rationale documented by the reviewer explaining the disposition decision, including any supporting evidence or justification for clearing or blocking the entity.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance officer or employee who reviewed the screening result. Null if no manual review was performed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level assigned to this screening result based on match confidence, entity type, and business context: low, medium, high, or critical.']
  screened_by_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the automated screening system or platform that performed the sanction check (e.g., Salesforce CRM screening module, third-party AML platform).']
  screened_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual, corporate account, vendor, or business partner being screened against sanctions and watchlists.']
  screened_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity being screened: guest, corporate account, vendor, employee, business partner, or third-party.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction screening was performed against regulatory watchlists.']
  screening_lists_checked varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory watchlists checked during this screening event (e.g., OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, UN Security Council Sanctions, INTERPOL, national lists).']
  screening_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the screening: automated system, manual review, or hybrid approach combining both.']
  screening_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this sanction screening event for audit trail and regulatory reporting purposes.']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the screening record: completed (final disposition reached), in progress (screening underway), pending review (awaiting manual review), or expired (requires re-screening).']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the sanction screening was executed, including time zone information.']
  screening_trigger varchar [note: 'Event or condition that triggered this sanction screening: new entity onboarding, periodic review, transaction threshold exceeded, regulatory watchlist update, or manual compliance request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanction screening record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Sanctions and watchlist screening record for guests, corporate accounts, vendors, and business partners screened against OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, UN Security Council Sanctions, and other regulatory watchlists. Captures screening reference number, screened entity name and type (guest, corporate account, vendor, employee), screening date, screening list(s) checked, match result (no match, potential match, confirmed match), match confidence score, match disposition (cleared, escalated, blocked), screened by (automated system, manual review), reviewer name, and regulatory reporting required flag. Supports AML, OFAC compliance, and anti-terrorism financing obligations in hospitality.'
}

Business justification: Regulatory filing deadlines appear on the compliance calendar as obligations with due dates. One filing has one calendar entry. This FK allows tracking which calendar obligations represent filing dead']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the compliance obligation was fulfilled or completed. Null for upcoming or in-progress obligations.']

  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the compliance obligation must be completed to avoid regulatory lapse or penalty. Critical for proactive compliance management.']

  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete this compliance obligation. Used for resource planning and workload management.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction code under which this compliance obligation falls. May be country, state, province, or local authority code.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this compliance calendar entry was last updated. Tracks most recent change for audit and synchronization purposes.']
